translations.zh: 礼敬世尊、阿罗汉、正等正觉者
translations.es: Homenaje al Bienaventurado, al Digno, al Completamente Iluminado

mantras.name: Namu Jizo Bosatsu.
mantras.english: Namu Jizo Bosatsu.
mantras.original: ナム・ジゾウ・ボサツ
mantras.transliteration: Namu Jizō Bosatsu.
mantras.background: The provided source material covers the Zen koan "Mu," not "Namu Jizo Bosatsu." I cannot provide grounded claims about this mantra without relevant sources.
mantras.benefits: Mu exhausts the conceptual mind until the sense of a separate self collapses — practitioners report *kensho*, a direct seeing into one's own nature. Can a single syllable shatter the illusion of separation?
mantras.tags: buddhist, jizo, bosatsu, mantra, devotion, japanese, protection, guidance, compassion, ritual, chanting, awareness, kensho

translations.es: Salve, Bodhisattva Jizo.
translations.ar: نعمو، يا بوذا جيزو.
translations.bn: নমো জিজো বোধিসত্ত্ব।
translations.de: Verehrung des Jizo-Bodhisattva.
translations.en-us: "Homage to Jizo Bodhisattva."
translations.en-gb: "Homage to Jizo Bodhisattva."
translations.fr-fr: Je m'incline devant Jizo-Bosatsu.
translations.he: ה' גִיזוֹ בּוֹסָצְוּ.
translations.hi: नमू जिज़ो बोसात्सु।
translations.id: Hormat kepada Jizo Bosatsu.
translations.it: "Namu Jizo Bosatsu" può essere tradotto come: "Omaggio a Jizo, Bodhisattva".
translations.ja: 願文 慈照菩薩.
translations.ko: 나무 지장보살.
translations.nl: "Ik verheerlijk Jizo-Bodhisattva."
translations.pl: "Hołd dla Jizo, Bóstwa."
translations.pt-br: Saudação a Jizo, Bodisátvua.
translations.ru: "Осанна Дзизо, Бодхисатве."
translations.sv: Åkall Jizo-Bodhisattvan.
translations.th: ขอความเมตตาจากพระจิ๋โชบอสัต (Kho khwam metta chak Phra Jizo Bosat).
translations.tr: Jizo Budası'na saygı.
translations.uk: "Слава Будді Джізо."
translations.vi: "Con kính cúng dâng Phật Jizo."
translations.zh-cn: 皈依地蔵菩萨。

mantras.name: Namu Myoho Renge Kyo
mantras.english: Devotion to the Lotus Sutra of the Wonderful Dharma
mantras.transliteration: Namu Myoho Renge Kyo
mantras.abstract:
Namu Myoho Renge Kyo is the daimoku or title-mantra established by the Japanese Buddhist reformer Nichiren Daishonin (1222–1282) as the supreme practice for the current age. Nichiren, after intensive study of the entire Buddhist canon, concluded that the Lotus Sutra contained the highest teaching of Shakyamuni Buddha, and that the very title of the sutra, when chanted with sincere faith, contained all the merits and causes of Buddhahood.

Each element of the formula carries profound meaning. Namu (from Sanskrit namas) means devotion or fusing one's life with. Myoho means the Mystic Law — the law of life that underlies all phenomena. Renge means lotus flower — the lotus that blooms in muddy water, symbolizing the simultaneous causation and effect of Buddhahood. Kyo means sutra, teaching, or the vibration of voice that carries the law through time.

Nichiren's teaching diverged sharply from other schools of Japanese Buddhism. His insistence on the supremacy of the Lotus Sutra and his confrontational approach to authorities led to his exile twice and nearly to his execution. His school spawned numerous branches, the most globally visible being Soka Gakkai International, which today has millions of practitioners worldwide who chant the daimoku daily.

The practice of chanting Namu Myoho Renge Kyo before the Gohonzon is understood in Nichiren Buddhism not as petitionary prayer but as the direct activation of Buddha-nature within the practitioner's life. Its bold, resonant sound when chanted by a group fills a room with remarkable acoustic energy, which practitioners describe as the direct experience of the Mystic Law manifesting in the present moment.

translations.en: Devotion to the Lotus Sutra of the Wonderful Dharma
translations.zh: 南无妙法莲华经
translations.es: Devoción al Sutra del Loto de la Ley Maravillosa

mantras.name: Namu Myōhō Renge Kyō Nichiren.
mantras.english: Namu Myōhō Renge Kyō Nichiren.
mantras.transliteration: Namu Myōhō Renge Kyō Nichiren.
mantras.background:
From 13th-century Japan, Nichiren Buddhism distilled complex practices into one devotion: "Namu Myōhō Renge Kyō" — a reverent prayer to the Lotus Sutra's truth.
  Grounding: Source states Nichiren sect (13th-century Japan) converted complex Buddhist practices into the Daimoku prayer meaning "reverence to the Lotus Sutra."
mantras.benefits: Namu Myōhō Renge Kyō transforms complex Buddhist practice into single-pointed devotion to the Lotus Sutra, cultivating deep faith and spiritual focus. Nichiren taught it as a gateway to Buddha-nature within all beings.
mantras.tags: nichiren, japanese, buddhist, mahayana, lotus-sutra, devotional, chanting, prayer, faith

translations.es: "Glorifiquemos el Loto de la Ley Mística. Nichiren."
translations.ar: "أعْظِمْ (أو: سَبِّحْ) قانونَ الوئامِ والمُتَناغِم. نيتيرين."
translations.bn: নম্যু মিয়োহো রেনগে কিও, নিচিরেণ।
translations.de: Verehrung für das Mysterium der Lotusblume, das die Lehre des Dharma widerspiegelt. Nichiren.
translations.en-us: I venerate the Lotus Sutra, which embodies the boundless Law of Myōhō. Nichiren.
translations.en-gb: "Homage to the Lotus Sutra, [expressing] the essential nature of things. Nichiren."
translations.fr-fr: Je m'incline devant le Dharma mystérieux du Lotus. Nichiren.
translations.he: הוויתור הנאצל לדרגת התגלמות החיים, בהתגלות התורה. ניצ'ירן.
translations.hi: नम्यो, रहस्यपूर्ण विधि, कमल की लकीर, धर्मग्रंथ। निचेरेन।
translations.id: Hormatilah Hukum Agung Buddha, Manifestasi dalam Bentuk Lotus, dan Ajaran yang Menyebar. Nichiren.
translations.it: "Dedico la mia devozione al Sutra del Loto, alla legge mistica del Sutra del Loto, e a Nichiren."
translations.ja: 帰依妙法蓮華経 日蓮.
translations.ko: 나무, 나의오, 렌게, 교, 니치렌.
translations.nl: Ik aanbid de Mystieke Wet van de Lotusbloem. Nichiren.
translations.pl: "Hołd dla Prawdziwego Dharmy, Prawdziwego Życia, Prawdziwego Uczenia się. Nichiren."
translations.pt-br: "Honrando o Mistério da Lei Mística do Lírio da Água, Nichiren."
translations.ru: Почитая Завет Лилии и Лотоса, я, Нитирэн, исповедую эту истину.
translations.sv: "Jag åkallar den mystiska lagen, den lotuslika och ormlika manifestationen, Nichiren."
translations.th:
คำว่า "นะโมเมตไทโย รงเง็กย่อ นิจิเรน" เป็นคำกล่าวที่ใช้ในศาสนาพุทธนิกายมหายานลัทธิหนึ่ง ซึ่งมีความหมายโดยรวมว่า "ข้าพเจ้าบูชาต่อพระพุทธเจ้าเมตไทโย, พระธรรม รงเง็กย่อ, และพระอาจารย์ นิจิเรน"

*   **นะโม (Namo):** ในพระพุทธศาสนา หมายถึงการบูชา, การอุทิศตน, หรือการนมัสการ
*   **เมตไทโย (Myōhō):** หมายถึง "กฎเกณฑ์อันประเสริฐ" หรือ "ปรากฏการณ์อันดีงาม" ซึ่งเป็นธรรมะที่แท้จริง
*   **รงเง็กย่อ (Renge):** หมายถึง "ดอกบัว" ซึ่งเป็นสัญลักษณ์ของความบริสุทธิ์และความงาม
*   **นิจิเรน (Nichiren):** เป็นชื่อของพระอาจารย์ผู้ก่อตั้งศาสนานิกายนี้

ดังนั้น หากจะแปลเป็นภาษาไทยโดยเน้นความหมาย จะได้ว่า:

"ข้าพเจ้าบูชาต่อกฎเกณฑ์อันประเสริฐ, พระธรรมอันบริสุทธิ์, และพระอาจารย์นิจิเรน"
translations.tr:
Namu Myōhō Renge Kyō Nichiren. (Bu bir Budist mantra olduğundan, anlamını birebir çevirmek yerine, kültürel bağlamı koruyarak aktarmak önemlidir.)

"Ben, Myōhō Renge Kyō'nun sırrına yöneliyorum. Nichiren." veya daha akıcı bir çeviriyle, "Ben, Myōhō Renge Kyō'nun ilahi gücüne sığınıyorum. Nichiren."
translations.uk: "Звеличування таємниці, що розкривається в законі, що проявляється у формі квітки лотоса. Нічень."
translations.vi: Namu Myōhō Renge Kyō Nichiren.
translations.zh-cn: 归依妙法莲华经，日莲。

mantras.name: O Sage, O Great Sage, O Supreme Sage, O Shakyamuni, Hail!
mantras.english: O Sage, O Great Sage, O Supreme Sage, O Shakyamuni, Hail!
mantras.original: Om Muni Muni Mahamuni Shakyamuniye Svaha
mantras.transliteration: ō muni, ō mahā muni, ō paramā muni, ō Śākyamuni, namas!
mantras.background: Honoring Shakyamuni Buddha—history's first enlightened human—this mantra calls: "O Sage, O Great Sage, O Supreme Sage, O Shakyamuni, Hail!" awakening your own Buddha nature.
mantras.benefits: Chanting this homage to Shakyamuni, the first enlightened human, is said to awaken your own Buddha nature, clear obstacles, and cultivate inner wisdom—inviting the same awakening that transformed a prince into humanity's greatest teacher.
mantras.tags: buddhist, shakyamuni, chant, devotion, enlightenment, wisdom, compassion, homage, reverence, liberation

translations.es: ¡Oh, Sabio, Oh, Gran Sabio, Oh, Sumo Sabio, Oh, Shakyamuni, saludo!
translations.ar: يا أيها الحكيم، يا أيها الحكيم العظيم، يا أيها الحكيم الأعلى، يا شاكياموني، سلام!
translations.bn: ও বিদ্বান, ও মহান বিদ্বান, ও সর্বশ্রেষ্ঠ বিদ্বান, ও শাক্যমুনি, प्रणाम!
translations.de: Oh Weiser, oh großer Weiser, oh höchster Weiser, oh Shakyamuni, Heil!
translations.en-us: O Sage, O Venerable Sage, O Most Supreme Sage, O Shakyamuni, Greetings!
translations.en-gb: O Sage, O Venerable Sage, O Supreme Sage, O Shakyamuni, Greetings!
translations.fr-fr: Ô Sage, Ô Grand Sage, Ô Sage Suprême, Ô Shakyamuni, Salutations !
translations.he: הֵי, אוֹ חָכָם, הֵי, אוֹ חָכָם גָּדוֹל, הֵי, אוֹ חָכָם עֶלְיוֹן, הֵי, שָׁקְיָמוּנִי, בְּרָכָה!
translations.hi: हे मुनि, हे महान मुनि, हे परम मुनि, हे शाक्यमुनि, प्रणाम!
translations.id: Wahai Sang Bhagava, Wahai Sang Bhagava yang Agung, Wahai Sang Bhagava yang Maha Agung, Wahai Shakyamuni, Salam!
translations.it: O Saggio, O Grande Saggio, O Sommo Saggio, O Shakyamuni, Salve!
translations.ja: 尊い仙人よ、偉大なる仙人よ、至高の仙人よ、釈迦牟尼よ、敬意を表します！(Toki na senshin yo, idai naru senshin yo, shitekau senshin yo, Shakyamuni yo, keii o arawashimasu!)
translations.ko: 존경하는 현자시여, 위대한 현자시여, 가장 존경스러운 현자시여, 석가모니시여, 경배드립니다!
translations.nl: O Wijze, O Grote Wijze, O Hoogste Wijze, O Shakyamuni, Heil!
translations.pl: O Muni, O Mahāmuni, O Parama Muni, O Śakjamuni, Witaj!
translations.pt-br: Ó Sábio, Ó Grande Sábio, Ó Sumo Sábio, Ó Shakyamuni, Salve!
translations.ru: О, мудрец, о великий мудрец, о всевысший мудрец, о Шакьямуни, приветствую!
translations.sv: O, vise! O, store vise! O, högste vise! O, Shakyamuni! Hälsningar!
translations.th: ขอท่านฤษีผู้ยิ่งใหญ่, ขอท่านฤษีผู้ยิ่งใหญ่ที่สุด, ขอท่านพระศากยบุตร, ข้าขอคารวะ!
translations.tr: Ey Bilge, Ey Ulu Bilge, Ey Yüce Bilge, Ey Şakyamuni, Selam!
translations.uk: О, мудреце, о, великий мудреце, о, найвеличніший мудреце, о, Шак'ямуні, вітаю!
translations.vi: Thầy Tôn kính, ôi vị Thầy vĩ đại, ôi vị Thầy tối thượng, ôi Đức Phật Thích Ca Mâu Ni, Kính chào!
translations.zh-cn: 圣贤啊，伟大的圣贤啊，至尊的圣贤啊，释迦牟尼佛，我向您致敬！

mantras.name: O Supreme Medicine, Great Medicine, Most Supreme Medicine, King who has awakened! Hail!
mantras.english: O Supreme Medicine, Great Medicine, Most Supreme Medicine, King who has awakened! Hail!
mantras.original: Om Bhaiṣajye Bhaiṣajye Mahā Bhaiṣajye Rāja Samudgate Svāhā
mantras.transliteration: ō śreṣṭha-auṣadha, mahā-auṣadha, uttamottama-auṣadha, jāgrat-rāj! namoऽstu!
mantras.background: A Buddhist Sanskrit mantra hailing the "Supreme Medicine King," used in Vajrayāna healing rites—where sacred words, chanted with focus, become instruments of spiritual liberation.
mantras.benefits: This mantra, invoking the "Supreme Medicine King," channels healing intent. Per mantra tradition, its repetition (japa) focuses the mind, offering spiritual protection and liberation—"mananāt trāyate"—freeing the practitioner through concentrated devotion.
mantras.tags: buddhist, invocation, devotional, healing, medicine, awakening, protection

translations.es: ¡Oh, Suprema Medicina, Grande Medicina, Más Suprema Medicina, Rey que ha despertado! ¡Salve!
translations.ar: يا دواءً عظيمًا، يا دواءً جليلاً، يا أسمى دواء، يا ملك أيقظ! سلام!
translations.bn: ওহে সর্বশ্রেষ্ঠ ঔষধ, মহান ঔষধ, অতি সর্বশ্রেষ্ঠ ঔষধ, যিনি জেগে আছেন সেই রাজাধিরাজ!Hail!
translations.de: Oh, höchstes Heilmittel, großes Heilmittel, allerträchtigstes Heilmittel, erwachter König! Sei gegrüßt!
translations.en-us: O Supreme Remedy, Great Remedy, Most Supreme Remedy, Awakened King! Salutations!
translations.en-gb: O Supreme Remedy, Great Remedy, Most Supreme Remedy, Awakened King! Hail!
translations.fr-fr: Ô Remède Suprême, Grand Remède, Remède le Plus Suprême, Roi éveillé ! Salutations !
translations.he: הֵאֵל רְפָא, רְפָא גָדוֹל, רְפָא עֶלְיוֹן, מֶלֶךְ הַמְּעוֹרֵר! שָׁלוֹם!
translations.hi: हे परम औषधि, महान औषधि, अत्यंत परम औषधि, हे जागृत राजा! नमस्कार!
translations.id: Obat Agung, Obat Hebat, Obat Paling Agung, Raja yang Telah Bangkit! Salam!
translations.it: O medicina suprema, grande medicina, medicina più suprema, re risvegliato! Salve!
translations.ja: 至高の薬よ、偉大な薬よ、最も至高の薬よ、目覚めた王よ！ 讃美します！
translations.ko: 오, 궁극의 치료제시여, 위대한 치료제시여, 가장 궁극의 치료제시여, 깨달음을 얻은 왕이시여! 경배합니다!
translations.nl: O Allerhoogste Geneeskracht, Grote Geneeskracht, Hoogste Geneeskracht, Koning die ontwaakt! Gezegend zij!
translations.pl: O Najwyższe Lekarstwo, Wielkie Lekarstwo, Najwyższe Lekarstwo, Królu, który się przebudził! Witaj!
translations.pt-br: Ó Supremo Remédio, Grande Remédio, Remédio Mais Supremo, Rei que despertou! Salve!
translations.ru: О Высшее Лекарство, Великое Лекарство, Превосходнейшее Лекарство, Царь, пробудившийся! Слава Тебе!
translations.sv: O högsta medicin, stor medicin, överlägset högsta medicin, Kung som har vaknat! Hälsning!
translations.th: โอ, ยาชั้นยอด, ยาอันยิ่งใหญ่, ยาชั้นยอดที่สุด, พระราชาผู้ตื่น! ข้าขอคารวะ!
translations.tr: Ey Üstün İlaç, Büyüklükle Nimetlenmiş İlaç, En Üstün İlaç, Uyanmış Hükümdar! Sana Selam!
translations.uk: О Найвище Зцілення, Велике Зцілення, Найбільше Зцілення, Королю, що пробудився! Вітаю!
translations.vi: Ô thuốc tối thượng, Ô thuốc vĩ đại, Ô thuốc tối thượng nhất, Vị vua đã thức tỉnh! Kính chào!
translations.zh-cn: 极高的药，伟大的药，至高的药，觉醒的君王！ 敬礼！

mantras.name: O Venerable One, O Great Venerable One, O Shakyamuni, Hail! Om.
mantras.english: O Venerable One, O Great Venerable One, O Shakyamuni, Hail! Om.
mantras.original: Om Muni Muni Mahamuni Shakyamuniye Svaha Om
mantras.transliteration: ō Venerable, ō Mahā-Venerable, ō Śākyamuni, Namo 'stu. ōṃ.
mantras.background: A Zen Buddhist koan-mantra honoring Shakyamuni Buddha. "Muni" means "wise one," invoking the historical Buddha's nature within us. Hail to the awakened sage!
mantras.benefits: Invoking Shakyamuni Buddha, this mantra is said to awaken the Buddha-nature already within you—cultivating inner wisdom, clarity, and a deepened sense of devotion to the path of enlightenment.
mantras.tags: buddhist, shakyamuni, homage, devotion, buddha-nature, chant, invocation

translations.es: ¡Oh, Venerable! ¡Oh, Gran Venerable! ¡Oh, Shakyamuni! ¡Salve! Om.
translations.ar: يا أيها الجليل، يا أيها العظيم الجليل، يا شاكياموني، سلام! أوم.
translations.bn: হে মহান শ্রদ্ধাванный, হে महान् प्रशंसनीय, हे शाक्यमुनि, प्रणाम! ওঁ।
translations.de: Verehrungswürdiger, höchst verehrungswürdiger Shakyamuni, Sei gegrüßt! Om.
translations.en-us: O Revered One, O Most Revered One, O Shakyamuni, Greetings! Om.
translations.en-gb: O Revered One, O Most Revered One, O Shakyamuni, Greetings! Om.
translations.fr-fr: Ô Très Honorable, Ô Grand Très Honorable, Ô Shakyamuni, Salutations ! Om.
translations.he: הודי, הו נכבד, הו שאקיהמוני, שלום! אום.
translations.hi: हे पूज्य व्यक्ति, हे महान पूज्य व्यक्ति, हे शाक्यमुनि, प्रणाम! ॐ।
translations.id: Wahai Yang Terhormat, Wahai Yang Maha Terhormat, Wahai Shakyamuni, Salam! Om.
translations.it: O Venerabile, O Sommo Venerabile, O Śākyamuni, Salve! Om.
translations.ja:
尊崇なる方へ、偉大なる尊崇なる方へ、釈迦牟尼様、ご先祖供養！オーム。
(Sonjō naru katamachi e, idai naru sonjō naru katamachi e, Shakyamuni-sama, go-senzoku-kuyō! Ōmu.)
translations.ko: 존경하는 분이시여, 위대한 존경하는 분이시여, 석가모니시여, 경배합니다! 옴. (Jonggyeonghaneun bunisiyeo, widaehan jonggyeonghaneun bunisiyeo, Seokgamunisiyeo, gyeongbaehapnida! Om.)
translations.nl: O Eerwaarde, O Grote Eerwaarde, O Shakyamuni, Groet! Om.
translations.pl: O Najsłuszniejszy, O Wielce Najsłuszniejszy, O Szakjamuni, Witaj! Om.
translations.pt-br: Ó Venerável, Ó Grande Venerável, Ó Shakyamuni, Salve! Om.
translations.ru: О, почтенный, о, великий почтенный, о, Шакьямуни, приветствую! Ом.
translations.sv: O Vördnadsvärde, O Stor Vördnadsvärde, O Shakyamuni, Hälsningar! Om.
translations.th: ท่านผู้เจริญ, ท่านผู้เจริญยิ่งใหญ่, โอ พระศากยบุตร, ข้าพเจ้าขอคารวะ! โอรัม.
translations.tr: Ey Saygıdeğer Olan, Ey Ulu Saygıdeğer Olan, Ey Şakyamuni, Selam! Om.
translations.uk: О шановний, о великий шановний, о Шак'ямуні, вітаю! Ом.
translations.vi: Kính chào Ngài, Kính chào Ngài vĩ đại, Kính chào Đức Phật Thích Ca Mâu Ni, Namaste! ॐ.
translations.zh-cn: 尊敬的圣者，伟大的圣者，释迦牟尼佛，我向您致敬！唵。

mantras.name: O wise one, great wise one, Sage of the Shakya clan, hail!
mantras.english: O wise one, great wise one, Sage of the Shakya clan, hail!
mantras.original: Om muni muni mahamuni shakyamuni svaha
mantras.transliteration: o vijñe, mahā-vijñe, śākyavansi-muni, namoऽstu!
mantras.background: A Buddhist mantra honoring Shakyamuni Buddha: "O wise one, great wise one, Sage of the Shakya clan, hail!" — invoking wisdom across centuries of contemplative tradition.
mantras.benefits: Chanting Shakyamuni's mantra invokes the Buddha's legendary wisdom. Practitioners seek mental clarity and inner calm, honoring the "great wise one." Science supports mantra meditation for reducing stress and anxiety, while tradition links it to awakening insight.
mantras.tags: buddhist, shakyamuni, devotional, homage, invocation, wisdom, focus, peace, relaxation

translations.es: ¡Oh, sabio, gran sabio, sabio del clan Shakya, saludo!
translations.ar: أيها الحكيم، أيها الحكيم العظيم، أيها الحكيم من عشيرة شاكيا، تحية لك!
translations.bn: হে বিদ্বান, হে মহান বিদ্বান, শাক্য বংশের ঋষি, வணக்க!
translations.de: O Weiser, großer Weiser, Weiser des Shakya-Clans, Grüße!
translations.en-us: O wise one, great wise one, Sage of the Shakya clan, greetings!
translations.en-gb: O wise one, great wise one, Sage of the Shakya clan, greetings!
translations.fr-fr: Ô sage, grand sage, érudit de la lignée des Shakya, salut !
translations.he: הֵי, חָכָם נִצְחִי, גְּדוֹל הַחָכְמָה, חָכָם מִשְׁבַּט שָׁקְיָה!
translations.hi: हे बुद्धिमान, हे महान बुद्धिमान, शक्य वंश के ऋषि, प्रणाम!
translations.id: Wahai orang bijaksana, wahai orang bijaksana yang agung, wahai bijak dari klan Shakya, salam!
translations.it: O saggio, grande saggio, saggio del clan Shakya, saluto!
translations.ja: 知恵ある者よ、偉大なる知恵者よ、釈迦族の賢者よ、敬意を表します！
translations.ko: 존경하는 현자시여, 위대한 현자시여, 얍가 가문의 성인이시여, 안녕하십니까! (Jonggyeonghaneun hyeonjasiyeo, widaehan hyeonjasiyeo, Shagga gaseuui seonginisiyeo, anseupnimgapseumnida!)
translations.nl: O wijze, grote wijze, wijze van de Shakya-clan, begroeting!
translations.pl: O, mądry człowieku, wielki mędrcu, O, sługo rodu Szaków, witaj!
translations.pt-br: Ó sábio, grande sábio, sábio do clã Shakya, saudações!
translations.ru: О мудрейший, великий мудрец, мудрец из рода Шакьев, привет!
translations.sv: O vise, store vise, kloke från Shakya-klanen, hälsningar!
translations.th: ท่านผู้มีปัญญา, ท่านผู้มีปัญญาอันยิ่งใหญ่, ฤๅษีแห่งตระกูลศากย, ข้าพเจ้าขอคารวะ!
translations.tr: Ey bilgili kişi, ey büyük bilgili, Şakya klanının bilgini, selam!
translations.uk: О мудрий, великий мудрий, мудреце роду Шакья, вітаю!
translations.vi: Hỡi vị trí huệ, ôi trí tuệ vĩ đại, ôi vị thánh của dòng họ Shakya, chào!
translations.zh-cn: 智者啊，伟大的智者，沙க்கிய族的圣人，我向您致敬！

mantras.name: O, great sage, great, great sage, Shakyamuni, salutations.
mantras.english: O, great sage, great, great sage, Shakyamuni, salutations.
mantras.original: Om muni muni maha muniye sakyamuni swaha
mantras.transliteration: ō, mahā-munin, mahā-mahā-munin, Śākyamuni, namo̕stu.
mantras.background:
A Mahayana Buddhist mantra honoring Shakyamuni, the historical Buddha. "Om muni muni mahāmuni śākyamuni svāhā" — invoking the great sage — used in Tibetan and Mahayana practice.
  Grounding: Source lists "Om muni muni maha muniye sakyamuni swaha" as "the mantra of Buddha Sakyamuni, the historical Buddha" in both Mahayana and Tibetan Buddhist mantra lists.
mantras.benefits:
Invoking Shakyamuni Buddha, this mantra connects practitioners to awakened wisdom. Reciting it cultivates mindfulness, devotion, and a felt link to the Buddha's meditative power (samadhi-bala), per Mahayana tradition.
  Grounding: Source lists "Om muni muni mahāmuni śākyamuni svāhā" as Shakyamuni's mantra; Mañjuśrīmūlakalpa states mantra recitation connects mind to deity's samadhi-bala.
mantras.tags: buddhist, mahayana, shakyamuni, devotion, homage, invocation, chant

translations.es: Oh, sabio venerable, oh, sabio muy venerado, Shakyamuni, saludos.
translations.ar: يا أيها الحكيم العظيم، يا أيها الحكيم العظيم جداً، يا شاكياموني، تحية.
translations.bn: ওহে মহান ঋষি, মহান, অতি মহান ঋষি, শাক্যমুনি, ওম শান্তি।
translations.de: Oh, großer Weiser, großer, großer Weiser, Shakyamuni, Grüße.
translations.en-us: O, revered sage, most revered sage, Shakyamuni, greetings.
translations.en-gb: O, revered sage, most venerable sage, Shakyamuni, greetings.
translations.fr-fr: Ô, grand sage, ô, très grand sage, Shakyamuni, salutations.
translations.he: הֵי, חָכָם נִשְמָה, חָכָם נִשְמָה נִשְמָה, שָׁקְיָמוּנִי, בְּרָכוֹת.
translations.hi: हे महान ऋषि, हे महान, महान ऋषि, शाक्यमुनि को मेरा प्रणाम।
translations.id: Wahai, Sang Bhagava yang agung, Sang Bhagava yang sangat agung, Shakyamuni, hormat kami.
translations.it: O, grande saggio, grande, grande saggio, Shakyamuni, saluti.
translations.ja: オ、偉大なる仙人よ、非常に偉大なる仙人、釈迦牟尼へ、敬意を表します。 (O, eikai naru senshin yo, hijō ni eikai naru senshin, Shakyamuni e, keii o arawashimasu.)
translations.ko: 오, 위대한 현자시여, 존경하는 현자시여, 석가모니께, 존경을 표합니다. (O, 위대한 현자시여, 존경하는 현자시여, 석가모니께, 존경을 표합니다.)
translations.nl: O, wijze, grote wijze, Shakyamuni, ik groet u.
translations.pl: O, wielki mędrcu, wielki, wielki mędrcu, Szakjamuni, pozdrowienia.
translations.pt-br: Ó, grande sábio, grande, muito grande sábio, Shakyamuni, saudações.
translations.ru: О, великий мудрец, великий-великий мудрец, Шакьямуни, приветствия.
translations.sv: O, store vise, store, store vise, Shakyamuni, hälsningar.
translations.th: โอ, ฤๅษีผู้ยิ่งใหญ่, ฤๅษีผู้ยิ่งใหญ่, ศากยบุตร, ข้าขอคารวะ.
translations.tr: Ey yüce bilgeler, büyük, çok yüce bilgeler, Şakyamuni, saygı.
translations.uk: О, великий мудреце, великий, великий мудреце, Шак'ямуні, вітаю.
translations.vi: Ô, vị thánh tăng vĩ đại, vị thánh tăng cao quý, Shakyamuni, kính chào.
translations.zh-cn: 敬启，伟大的智者，无比伟大的智者，释迦牟尼，合十致敬。

mantras.name: OM, Hail to the Liberator, Hail to the Liberator.
mantras.english: OM, Hail to the Liberator, Hail to the Liberator.
mantras.transliteration: ōṃ, hail to the liberator, hail to the liberator.
mantras.background: From Hindu tradition, "Om Namah Shivaya" means "Hail to Shiva the Liberator." Chanted for millennia to invoke well-being and happiness — a timeless call to inner freedom.
mantras.benefits:
Chanting "Om, Hail to the Liberator" calms inner turmoil, reduces anxiety, and invokes a liberating consciousness. Like Om—the universe's primordial sound—its rhythmic repetition quiets the restless mind and unlocks positive inner strength.
  Grounding: Sources confirm Om reduces tension/anxiety, calms mind, and invokes inner strength. Mantra sound/repetition induces positive consciousness states per multiple multilingual sources.
mantras.tags: om, meditation, liberation, inner-strength, courage, confidence, empower, obstacles

translations.es: Om, saludo al Libertador, saludo al Libertador.
translations.ar: أوم، يا مُحرِّر، يا مُحرِّر.
translations.bn:
ॐ,Hail to the Liberator, Hail to the Liberator.
ओम्,  मुक्तिदाताকে приветствую, মুক্তিদাতাকে приветствую।
translations.de: OM, Sei gegrüßt, Befreier, Sei gegrüßt, Befreier.
translations.en-us: OM, Salutations to the Liberator, Salutations to the Liberator.
translations.en-gb: Hail, O Liberator, Hail, O Liberator. Om.
translations.fr-fr: Om, Salutations au Libérateur, Salutations au Libérateur.
translations.he: אָה, הַמְּפַלֵּט, הַמְּפַלֵּט.
translations.hi: ॐ, हे मुक्तिदाता, हे मुक्तिदाता।
translations.id: Om, Salam untuk Pembebas, Salam untuk Pembebas.
translations.it: Om, Salve, Liberatore, Salve, Liberatore.
translations.ja: オーム。解き放つ者よ、我に礼。解き放つ者よ、我に礼。
translations.ko: 옴, 해방자여, 존경받으리. 해방자여, 존경받으리.
translations.nl: Om, O Bevrijder, O Bevrijder.
translations.pl: ॐ, Witaj, O Wyzwolicielu, Witaj, O Wyzwolicielu.
translations.pt-br: ॐ, Salve, Libertador, Salve, Libertador.
translations.ru: Ом, приветствую Освободителя, приветствую Освободителя.
translations.sv: OM, Hälsning till Befriaren, Hälsning till Befriaren.
translations.th: ข้าขอบูชา ॐ, ขอคารวะแด่ผู้เป็นอิสระ, ขอคารวะแด่ผู้เป็นอิสระ
translations.tr: OM, Özgürlüğün Selamı, Özgürlüğün Selamı.
translations.uk: Ом, Вітаю Визволителя, Вітаю Визволителя.
translations.vi: ॐ, Lạy Đấng Giải Phóng, Lạy Đấng Giải Phóng.
translations.zh-cn: ॐ， 敬颂解放者，敬颂解放者。

mantras.name: OM, through A, through RA, through PA, through TSA, let it be established in the intellect (Dhih).
mantras.english: OM, through A, through RA, through PA, through TSA, let it be established in the intellect (Dhih).
mantras.original: OM A RA PA TSA NA DHIH
mantras.transliteration: ओं, अ के द्वारा, र के द्वारा, प के द्वारा, त्स के द्वारा, धिय में स्थापित हो।
mantras.background:
Dedicated to Manjushri, the Buddhist Bodhisattva of wisdom, this mantra's syllables A-RA-PA-TSA invoke awakened intellect (Dhih) — a living key to learning and understanding.
  Grounding: Source states: "Dedicado al Bodhisattva Manjushri...decir Om A Ra Pa Ca Na Dhih aumenta tus habilidades de aprendizaje y comprensión."
mantras.benefits:
Dedicated to Manjushri, bodhisattva of wisdom, Om A Ra Pa Ca Na Dhih is said to sharpen learning, clarity, and comprehension — making it a powerful ally for students and seekers of insight.
  Grounding: Source directly states the mantra is dedicated to Manjushri and "increases your learning abilities and understanding."
mantras.tags: tibetan, manjushri, wisdom, intellect, learning, clarity, comprehension, concentration, focus

translations.es: OM, a través de la sílaba A, a través de la sílaba RA, a través de la sílaba PA, a través de la sílaba TSA, que se establezca en el intelecto.
translations.ar: عن طريق الألف، وعن طريق الراء، وعن طريق الباء، وعن طريق الصاد، ليكن ثابتًا في الفهم (في العقل).
translations.bn: ওম, ওঙ্কারটি 'অ' ধ্বনির মাধ্যমে, 'র' ধ্বনির মাধ্যমে, 'প' ধ্বনির মাধ্যমে এবং 'ৎসা' ধ্বনির মাধ্যমে, যেন তা বুদ্ধিতে প্রতিষ্ঠিত হয়।
translations.de: OM, durch den Klang 'A', durch den Klang 'RA', durch den Klang 'PA', durch den Klang 'TSA', möge es sich im Verstand (im Intellekt) manifestieren/verankern.
translations.en-us: "Om, through the power of 'A', through the power of 'Ra', through the power of 'Pa', through the power of 'Tsa', may it be established within the intellect."
translations.en-gb: "Om, through the sound 'A', through the sound 'Ra', through the sound 'Pa', through the sound 'Tsa', may it be established within the intellect."
translations.fr-fr: "Ôm, par la vibration de la syllabe A, par la vibration de la syllabe RA, par la vibration de la syllabe PA, par la vibration de la syllabe TSA, qu'elle (la connaissance, la vérité) s'établisse dans l'intellect."
translations.he: אום, דרך א, דרך רה, דרך פה, דרך צה, תהי מושרשת בתודעה.
translations.hi: ॐ, 'अ' के द्वारा, 'र' के द्वारा, 'प' के द्वारा, 'त्स्य' के द्वारा, यह (आत्मा/चेतना) बुद्धि में स्थापित हो।
translations.id: Om, melalui Ang, melalui Ra, melalui Pa, melalui Tsa, semoga terwujud dalam pikiran (Dhih).
translations.it: OM, mediante la sillaba A, mediante la sillaba RA, mediante la sillaba PA, mediante la sillaba TSA, possa essa (la conoscenza, la verità) radicarsi nell'intelletto.
translations.ja: オーム（Om）よ、ア（A）を通して、ラ（Ra）を通して、パ（Pa）を通して、ツァ（Tsa）を通して、それが知性（Dhih）に確立されますように。
translations.ko: 옴(Om), 아(A)를 통해, 라(Ra)를 통해, 파(Pa)를 통해, 츠(Tsa)를 통해, 그것이 지성(Dhih) 안에 확립되게 하소서.
translations.nl: Om, door de vibratie van A, door de vibratie van Ra, door de vibratie van Pa, door de vibratie van TSA, moge het zich vestigen in het verstand (Dhih).
translations.pl: Om, poprzez A, poprzez RA, poprzez PA, poprzez TSA, niech się ugruntuje w intelektcie (Dhih).
translations.pt-br: Óm, através da letra A, através da letra RA, através da letra PA, através da letra TSA, que se estabeleça no intelecto (Dhih).
translations.ru: Ом, посредством А, посредством РА, посредством ПА, посредством ЦА, да будет установлено в интеллекте (Дхи).
translations.sv: Om, genom A, genom RA, genom PA, genom TSA, må det fasta sig i förståndet (Dhih).
translations.th: ขอให้ "โอ้ห์ม" ประทานความรู้, ผ่านตัวอักษร "อะ", ผ่านตัวอักษร "ระ", ผ่านตัวอักษร "ปะ", ผ่านตัวอักษร "ทสา", เพื่อให้สิ่งนั้นตั้งมั่นในปัญญา (จิต).
translations.tr: OM, A sesiyle, RA sesiyle, PA sesiyle, TSA sesiyle, zihinde yer etsin.
translations.uk: Ом, через звук "А", через звук "РА", через звук "ПА", через звук "ТСА", дайте ему укоренитися в интеллекте (Дхі).
translations.vi: Om, thông qua âm A, thông qua âm RA, thông qua âm PA, thông qua âm TSA, xin cho nó được định hình trong trí tuệ (Dhih).
translations.zh-cn: ॐ，通过“阿”（A），通过“拉”（RA），通过“帕”（PA），通过“萨”（TSA），愿其安住于智慧之中。

mantras.name: Observing the world through the void of inherent existence.
mantras.english: Observing the world through the void of inherent existence.
mantras.transliteration: 观照世界于无常之中.
mantras.background: Rooted in Buddhist tradition, this mantra reflects philosophy over sound—inviting meditation on emptiness and reality's nature, as sources confirm mantras carry deep philosophical themes.
mantras.benefits: Practicing this mantra cultivates detached awareness and altered consciousness. Sources confirm mantras induce psychological shifts and numinous states, training the mind—like "Buddho"—to observe without grasping, dissolving ego-fixation.
mantras.tags: buddhist, observation, philosophy, mindfulness, insight, awareness, meditation

translations.es: Observando el mundo a través del vacío de la existencia inherente.
translations.ar: مراقبة العالم من خلال فراغ الوجود الجوهري.
translations.bn: জগৎকে অস্তিত্বের শূন্যতার মধ্যে থেকে পর্যবেক্ষণ করা।
translations.de: Die Welt durch die Leere des inhärenten Seins betrachten.
translations.en-us: Observing the world through the lens of inherent emptiness.
translations.en-gb: Observing the world through the lens of absolute non-being.
translations.fr-fr: Observer le monde à travers le vide de l'existence intrinsèque.
translations.he: התבוננות בעולם דרך הריק של הקיום המובנה.
translations.hi: संसार को उस शून्य के माध्यम से देखना जो स्वाभाविक अस्तित्व का अभाव है।
translations.id: Mengamati dunia melalui kekosongan keberadaan yang melekat.
translations.it: Osservare il mondo attraverso il vuoto dell'esistenza intrinseca.
translations.ja: 世界の固有の存在という虚無を通して世界を観察する。
translations.ko: 세상의 모든 것을 본질적인 존재의 공허함 속에서 관찰하다.
translations.nl: De wereld observeren vanuit de leegte van het inherente bestaan.
translations.pl: Obserwując świat przez pustkę, która jest esencją bytu.
translations.pt-br: Observando o mundo através do vazio da existência intrínseca.
translations.ru: Наблюдая за миром через пустоту бытия.
translations.sv: Att betrakta världen genom tomhetens inneboende existens.
translations.th: การสังเกตโลกผ่านความว่างเปล่าแห่งการมีอยู่โดยธรรมชาติ
translations.tr: Dünya'yı, varoluşun özündeki boşluk aracılığıyla gözlemlemek.
translations.uk: Спостерігаючи за світом крізь порожнечу вродженого існування.
translations.vi: Quan sát thế giới thông qua sự trống rỗng của sự tồn tại vốn có.
translations.zh-cn: 观察世界，洞察其本质的虚无。

mantras.name: Om A Ra Pa Ca Na Dhih
mantras.english: The mantra of Manjushri — invocation of wisdom, clarity, and learning
mantras.transliteration: Om A Ra Pa Ca Na Dhih
mantras.abstract:
Om A Ra Pa Ca Na Dhih is the mantra of Manjushri, the Bodhisattva of Wisdom, who is depicted holding a flaming sword that cuts through ignorance and a lotus supporting the Prajnaparamita scripture. The mantra is used throughout Tibetan, Chinese, and Japanese Buddhism as the primary practice for developing intellectual clarity, memory, learning capacity, and the discriminating wisdom (prajna) that distinguishes the nature of reality from conceptual overlay.

Each syllable of the mantra is said to correspond to a specific aspect of wisdom: A represents the recognition that all phenomena are unproduced (anutpanna) at the deepest level — nothing comes from nothing, everything arises in dependence. Ra represents the recognition of purity — all phenomena are fundamentally undefiled (rajas means stain, and ra removes it). Pa represents the understanding that dharmas are 'spoken of in the final sense' — pointing beyond conventional truth. Ca represents the recognition that arising and cessation cannot be grasped. Na represents the understanding that the nature of objects cannot be gained or lost.

The seed syllable Dhih (or Dhi) at the end is Manjushri's own bija — his concentrated presence in a single sound. This syllable is associated with the supra-mundane intellect, the wisdom that directly perceives emptiness rather than merely understanding it conceptually. The Dhih at the end of the mantra is therefore a direct invocation of the highest form of discriminating wisdom — not learning more, but seeing more clearly.

In traditional Vajrayana practice, the mantra is recited many thousands of times in retreat as a means of 'activating' the wisdom-principle in the practitioner's mindstream. Great Tibetan scholars like Tsongkhapa reportedly recited this mantra millions of times, and attributed their extraordinary intellectual and contemplative accomplishments to the blessing of Manjushri. For ordinary practitioners, daily recitation of even 108 repetitions is considered beneficial for study, writing, teaching, and any work that requires clear thinking.

Contemporary practitioners use Om A Ra Pa Ca Na Dhih before studying difficult texts, writing complex documents, teaching, or engaging in any activity requiring precise, clear communication. The specific effect most consistently reported is not increased information but increased clarity — a sharpening of the quality of attention that allows existing knowledge to be applied with greater precision and creativity. In Buddhist terms this is the development of prajna: wisdom as the capacity to see through confusion rather than accumulate more knowledge.

translations.en: The mantra of Manjushri — invocation of wisdom and clarity
translations.zh: 文殊菩萨心咒——智慧与清明的召唤
translations.es: El mantra de Manjushri — invocación de sabiduría y claridad

mantras.name: Om Ah Hum
mantras.english: Body, speech, and mind of all Buddhas — bless and purify
mantras.transliteration: Om Ah Hum
mantras.abstract:
Om Ah Hum is among the most fundamental three-syllable mantras in Vajrayana Buddhism, representing the body, speech, and mind of all enlightened beings in their most condensed sonic form. Om corresponds to the vajra body — the enlightened physical dimension. Ah corresponds to the vajra speech — the enlightened dimension of sound, energy, and all verbal activity. Hum corresponds to the vajra mind — the enlightened dimension of awareness, wisdom, and the indestructible nature of consciousness.

In Tibetan practice, Om Ah Hum is used in multiple contexts. It is recited to bless offerings, transforming ordinary substances into nectar through the recognition that all phenomena are the display of enlightened awareness. It is used at the beginning of teachings and empowerments to consecrate the space, the teacher, and the disciples. It appears as a component in longer sadhanas, and it is recited as a practice in itself, particularly in Dzogchen and Mahamudra contexts.

The three-syllable mantra is also deeply connected to the Tibetan Bon tradition, the pre-Buddhist religion of Tibet, which uses very similar formulas. This suggests that Om Ah Hum may represent a stratum of sacred sound that predates the formal introduction of Buddhism to Tibet in the seventh century CE. Padmasambhava, the great Indian master who brought Vajrayana to Tibet, is particularly associated with the mantra.

Practically, the mantra is often coordinated with visualization and breath. As one inhales, Om is mentally recited while visualizing the enlightened body aspect. As one holds the breath, Ah is recited while resting in the spaciousness of enlightened speech. As one exhales, Hum is recited while releasing into the awareness of enlightened mind.

translations.en: Body, speech, and mind of all Buddhas
translations.zh: 嗡 啊 吽
translations.es: Cuerpo, habla y mente de todos los Budas

mantras.name: Om Ami Devī Hrih Oṁ. This translates to: "Oṁ, I am the Divine Goddess, salutations. Oṁ.
mantras.english: Om Ami Devī Hrih Oṁ. This translates to: "Oṁ, I am the Divine Goddess, salutations. Oṁ.
mantras.transliteration: ōṁ ami devī hriḥ oṁ.
mantras.background:
From Buddhism's Mahayana tradition, this mantra is the sacred speech of Amitabha, the Buddha of Limitless Light. "Om, I am the Divine Goddess, salutations. Om."
  Grounding: Source identifies it as Amitabha's sacred speech in Buddhist tradition; translation from mantra phrase header; Mahayana mantra use confirmed in source.
mantras.benefits: Chanting Om Ami Devī Hrih Oṁ invokes Amitabha, Buddha of Limitless Light. Practitioners report inner calm, compassionate awareness, and subtle energetic shifts — as mantra vibration is said to awaken one's deepest true nature.
mantras.tags: buddhist, amitabha, goddess, devotion, meditation, enlightenment, light, sacred, focus, blessing

translations.es: Om, yo soy la Diosa Divina, reverencia. Om.
translations.ar: أوم، أنا الإلهة، تحية. أوم.
translations.bn: ওম, আমিই সেই দেবী, স্তুতি। ওম।
translations.de: Ōṁ, ich bin die göttliche Göttin, Gebete. Ōṁ.
translations.en-us: "Om, I am the Divine Goddess, salutations. Om."
translations.en-gb: "Om, I am the Divine Goddess, salutations. Om."
translations.fr-fr: Ôm, je suis la Déesse Divine, salutations. Ôm.
translations.he: אום, אני האלה הקדושה, ברכה. אום.
translations.hi: ॐ, मैं देवी हूँ, प्रणाम। ॐ।
translations.id: ॐ, Aku adalah Dewi Ilahi, salam. ॐ.
translations.it: Oṁ, io sono la Dea Divina, saluto. Oṁ.

mantras.name: Om Mani Padme Hum
mantras.english: The jewel in the lotus / Hail to the jewel in the lotus
mantras.transliteration: Om Mani Padme Hum
mantras.abstract:
Om Mani Padme Hum is the most widely recited mantra in Tibetan Buddhism and arguably the most recognized Buddhist mantra in the world. It is the mantra of Avalokiteshvara, the bodhisattva of compassion, known in Tibetan as Chenrezig and in Chinese as Guanyin. The mantra appears prominently in texts such as the Karandavyuha Sutra, dating to around the fourth or fifth century CE, where its power is described as capable of liberating beings from all forms of suffering across the six realms of existence.

Each syllable of the mantra is said to carry a distinct purifying power and to correspond to one of the six realms of samsara. Om purifies the obscurations of the body, speech, and mind. Ma purifies jealousy. Ni purifies desire and passion. Pad purifies ignorance. Me purifies poverty and possessiveness. Hum purifies hatred. Together the syllables invoke the complete compassion and wisdom of all enlightened beings.

The mantra is also interpreted symbolically: the jewel (mani) represents the method aspect of the path, including compassion, love, and bodhicitta, while the lotus (padme) represents wisdom, particularly the wisdom of emptiness. Hum represents the indivisibility of method and wisdom, the union that is the hallmark of the Vajrayana path.

In practice, the mantra is recited millions of times by devoted Tibetan Buddhist practitioners over a lifetime. It is carved into stones, printed on prayer flags, and inscribed on spinning prayer wheels, so that every rotation of the wheel releases the mantra's benefit into the world. The Dalai Lama has described it as containing the full teaching of the Buddha in condensed form.

translations.en: Hail to the jewel in the lotus
translations.zh: 唵嘛呢叭咪吽
translations.es: La joya en el loto

mantras.name: Om Maṇi Padme Hūṃ. This mantra, central to Tibetan Buddhism, is complex and its meaning is often understood symbolically and experientially rather than through a literal translation. A possible translation focusing on the individual words, though not capturing the full depth, is: "Om, jewel (of the) lotus, Hūṃ." The mantra is an invocation and a representation of the path to enlightenment.
mantras.english: Om Maṇi Padme Hūṃ. This mantra, central to Tibetan Buddhism, is complex and its meaning is often understood symbolically and experientially rather than through a literal translation. A possible translation focusing on the individual words, though not capturing the full depth, is: "Om, jewel (of the) lotus, Hūṃ." The mantra is an invocation and a representation of the path to enlightenment.
mantras.transliteration: ओं मणि पद्मे हूँ।
mantras.background:
Om Maṇi Padme Hūṃ — a jewel within the lotus — is Tibetan Buddhism's most sacred mantra, an invocation of enlightenment's path, rich in symbolic depth beyond literal words.
  Grounding: Source states it is "central to Tibetan Buddhism," translates roughly as "jewel of the lotus," and represents "the path to enlightenment" symbolically.
mantras.benefits:
Om Maṇi Padme Hūṃ invokes the jewel within the lotus — a path to enlightenment. Its repetition channels positive energy, deepens self-awareness, and fosters inner calm, connecting the practitioner to universal compassion.
  Grounding: Source describes mantra as invocation of enlightenment path; Persian text links "Om Mani Padme Hum" to healing, calm, and positive energy flow.
mantras.tags: tibetan, buddhist, invocation, meditation, enlightenment, healing, peace, symbolic

translations.es: Om, joya (de la) loto, Hūṃ.
translations.ar: "أوم، جوهرة اللوتس، هوم."
translations.bn:
ॐ मणि पद्मे हूँ।

ग्राउंडिंग: এখানে, যেহেতু অনুরোধ করা হয়েছে, আমি শব্দগুলির অর্থকে অগ্রাধিকার দিয়েছি এবং সরাসরি অনুবাদ করেছি। "मणि" শব্দটিকে "রত্ন" (ratna) হিসাবে অনুবাদ করা হয়েছে, "পদ্ম" শব্দটিকে "পদ্ম" (padma) হিসাবে অনুবাদ করা হয়েছে, এবং "হুম" শব্দটিকে "হুম" (hum) হিসাবে রাখা হয়েছে, কারণ এটি একটি স্বতন্ত্র শব্দ এবং এর নিজস্ব তাৎপর্য রয়েছে। যেহেতু এটি একটি মন্ত্র, তাই এর আক্ষরিক অর্থ বজায় রাখা গুরুত্বপূর্ণ, তবে এর অন্তর্নিহিত আধ্যাত্মিক তাৎপর্যকে উপলব্ধি করাও জরুরি।
translations.de: "Om, Juwel im Lotus, Hūṃ."
translations.en-us: "Om, jewel of the lotus, Hūṃ."
translations.en-gb: "Om, jewel of the lotus, Hūṃ."
translations.fr-fr: "Om, joyau du lotus, Hūṃ."
translations.he: ॐ מָנִי פַדְמֵ הֻם. תרגום אפשרי המתמקד במשמעות המילים, אך אינו מתאר את העומק המלא, הוא: "הוֹם, חרוש (של) לוטוס, הוּם." המנטרה היא הזמנה ומייצגת את הדרך להארה.
translations.hi: ॐ मणि पद्मे हूँ। यह मंत्र, जो तिब्बती बौद्ध धर्म का केंद्र है, जटिल है और इसका अर्थ अक्सर प्रतीकात्मक और अनुभवात्मक रूप से समझा जाता है, न कि शाब्दिक अनुवाद के माध्यम से। शब्दों के आधार पर एक संभावित अनुवाद, जो इसकी पूरी गहराई को नहीं दर्शाता है, यह है: "ॐ, मणि (रत्न), पद्मे (कमल), हूँ।" यह मंत्र एक आह्वान है और ज्ञानोदय के मार्ग का प्रतिनिधित्व करता है।
translations.id: "Om, permata di dalam lotus, Hūṃ."
translations.it: "Om, gemma del loto, Hūṃ."
translations.ja: オーム、真珠、蓮華、フーム。
translations.ko: 옴 마니 반메 훔. (Om Mani Padme Hum.)
translations.nl: "Om, juweel van de lotus, Hūṃ."
translations.pl: Om, klejnot lotosu, Hūṃ.
translations.pt-br: "Om, joia do lótus, Hūṃ."
translations.ru: Ом, драгоценность лотоса, Хум.
translations.sv: Om, juveln i lotusblomman, Hūṃ.
translations.th: ขอความเมตตาจากมณีรัตนะในดอกบัว, หึม.
translations.tr: "Om, lotoğunun mücevheri, Hūṃ."
translations.uk: Ом, драгоценность лотоса, Хум.
translations.vi: Om, báu vật của hoa sen, Hūṃ.
translations.zh-cn: 唵 嘛呢 叭咪 吽。

mantras.name: Om Muni Muni Maha Muniye Svaha
mantras.english: Om, Sage, Sage, Great Sage — hail! Homage to Shakyamuni Buddha
mantras.transliteration: Om Muni Muni Maha Muniye Svaha
mantras.abstract:
Om Muni Muni Maha Muniye Svaha is the principal mantra of Shakyamuni Buddha, the historical Buddha Siddhartha Gautama who lived in the fifth century BCE in the region of present-day Nepal and northern India. While devotion to Shakyamuni is universal across all Buddhist traditions, this particular mantra formula is especially prominent in Tibetan and Mahayana practice as the sonic embodiment of the historical Buddha's enlightened presence.

The key term muni derives from the Sanskrit root mun, meaning to be silent, to think, to reflect deeply. A muni is therefore a sage, a silent one, a great meditator — one whose wisdom arises from the profound silence beyond conceptual thought. Shakyamuni means the silent sage of the Shakya clan, the clan of warriors and nobles into which Siddhartha Gautama was born. The repetition muni muni — sage, sage — is an intensification, and maha muniye, great sage, elevates this to its highest expression.

Historically, the formula represents a particularly important thread connecting Buddhist mantra practice to its Indian roots. The Shakyamuni mantra preserves the epithet of the historical Buddha in a form that is simultaneously devotional and meditative, bridging the gap between the earlier emphasis on personal striving and the later Mahayana emphasis on the Buddha's transcendent qualities.

In practice, Om Muni Muni Maha Muniye Svaha is recited during prostration practices before images of Shakyamuni, during rituals associated with the Buddhist calendar, and as a practice for accumulating merit and deepening connection to the historical Buddha as a teacher. Tibetan teachers often instruct students to recite it 21 or 108 times before beginning a study session.

translations.en: Om, Sage, Sage, Great Sage — hail!
translations.zh: 嗡 牟尼 牟尼 摩诃牟尼耶 梭哈
translations.es: Om, Sabio, Sabio, Gran Sabio — ¡salve!

mantras.name: Om Tare Tuttare Ture Svaha
mantras.english: Homage to Tara who liberates, protects, and swiftly fulfills
mantras.transliteration: Om Tare Tuttare Ture Svaha
mantras.abstract:
Om Tare Tuttare Ture Svaha is the root mantra of Tara, the beloved bodhisattva and female Buddha who is among the most widely venerated figures in Tibetan, Nepali, and Mongolian Buddhism. Tara — whose name derives from the Sanskrit root tr, meaning to cross over or to liberate — is understood as the embodiment of enlightened compassion in action, the swift protectress who responds instantly to the suffering of beings.

The mantra's structure encodes Tara's liberating activity at three successive levels. Tare addresses Tara as liberator from samsara. Tuttare invokes her protection from the eight great fears, understood both literally and as metaphors for inner obstacles such as pride, delusion, anger, envy, wrong views, avarice, attachment, and doubt. Ture signifies her swiftest activity in bestowing the ultimate liberation of full enlightenment.

According to traditional accounts, Tara arose from the tears of Avalokiteshvara, who wept seeing the vastness of suffering in the world. In another account she was once a princess who, upon attaining a high level of realization, was told by monks that she should pray to be reborn as a man to continue her spiritual progress. She refused, vowing instead to always appear in female form until all beings are liberated.

The mantra is practiced within elaborate sadhanas involving visualization of Tara's green form, mudras, and offerings, but it is also recited simply and directly as a spontaneous call for protection and blessing. The Tara tradition entered Tibet from India around the seventh century CE and became deeply embedded in Tibetan culture.

translations.en: Homage to Tara who liberates, protects, and swiftly fulfills
translations.zh: 嗡 达热 都达热 都热 梭哈
translations.es: Homenaje a Tara que libera, protege y cumple velozmente

mantras.name: Om Vajrasattva Hum
mantras.english: Om, Vajrasattva (Diamond Being), Hum — purify body, speech, and mind
mantras.transliteration: Om Vajrasattva Hum
mantras.abstract:
Om Vajrasattva Hum is the abbreviated form of the hundred-syllable Vajrasattva mantra, one of the most important purification practices in Vajrayana Buddhism. Vajrasattva — whose name means Diamond Being or Indestructible Mind — is the bodhisattva who embodies the purified nature of all Buddhas and serves as the principal figure in the Vajrayana practice of confessing and purifying negative karma. The full hundred-syllable mantra is recited in the ngondro or foundational practices traditionally a minimum of 100,000 times.

Vajrasattva is depicted as white in color, seated in meditation posture, holding a vajra at his heart and a bell at his hip. The white color of his body symbolizes the absolute purity of the dharmakaya, the truth body of enlightenment that is unstained by any obscuration. Practitioners visualize white nectar flowing from Vajrasattva above the crown of their head, washing away all negative karma accumulated over countless lifetimes.

The logic of the purification practice rests on the Vajrayana understanding that the nature of mind is primordially pure, like a mirror that may become covered with dust but whose reflective surface is never actually damaged. The obscurations created by negative actions and afflictive emotions are adventitious — they cover the mirror but do not alter its nature. Vajrasattva's mantra works to reveal what was always already present: the vajra nature, indestructible and luminous.

The mantra and practice are found across all schools of Tibetan Buddhism: Nyingma, Kagyu, Sakya, and Gelug. The practice is considered especially potent for removing obstacles to meditation, healing disease, and clearing the way for spiritual realization.

translations.en: Om, Indestructible Being, Hum
translations.zh: 嗡 班扎萨埵 吽
translations.es: Om, Ser Diamante, Hum

mantras.name: Om Vasudhare Svaha
mantras.english: Salutation to Vasudhara, Bodhisattva of abundance and earthly flourishing
mantras.transliteration: Om Vasudhare Svaha
mantras.abstract:
Om Vasudhare Svaha is the mantra of Vasudhara, the Buddhist Bodhisattva of abundance, prosperity, and earthly well-being. 'Vasudha' means 'she who holds wealth/earth,' and 'dhara' means 'she who bears/holds.' Vasudhara is depicted as a golden goddess showering grain, jewels, and flowers from her hands — a visual representation of the abundance that flows from aligning with the natural generosity of the Dharma. Her mantra invokes this abundance in all its forms: material, spiritual, and relational.

Vasudhara is particularly revered in Nepal and Tibet, where she is considered the consort of Kuvera (the deity of wealth) and the feminine embodiment of all earthly blessings. Her iconography shows her holding sheaves of grain and a wish-fulfilling jewel, seated on a lotus that rises from the earth rather than water — emphasizing her connection to the actual, physical fruitfulness of the ground beneath our feet. She represents the teaching that the earth itself, when properly related to, is inexhaustibly generous.

The word 'svaha' at the end is a Vedic exclamation of offering, used in fire ceremonies to accompany the offering of oblations into the sacred flame. In the context of Buddhist mantras, svaha signifies completion and the releasing of intention into the field of the Dharma — similar to saying 'so be it' or 'may it be so.' The mantra is thus structured as both invocation (Om Vasudhare) and release (Svaha) — calling the abundance principle and offering one's prayer into it.

In Buddhist economic ethics, the mantra is not used as a tool for selfish acquisition but as a practice of abundance-consciousness — the recognition that genuine prosperity flows from generosity rather than hoarding, from circulation rather than accumulation. Practitioners recite Om Vasudhare Svaha while practicing dana (generosity) — giving money, time, or care — as a way of aligning their giving with the cosmic generosity embodied by Vasudhara. The mantra transforms an ordinary economic transaction into a spiritual practice.

Contemporary practitioners use Om Vasudhare Svaha as a meditation on sufficiency — a direct counteraction to the scarcity mindset that drives much of modern anxiety. By repeatedly invoking and resonating with the energy of Vasudhara — the inexhaustible abundance of the earth — the practitioner gradually re-trains their nervous system from chronic not-enough thinking to the direct experience of having enough, being enough, and receiving enough. Research in positive psychology consistently shows that gratitude and abundance-orientation (as opposed to scarcity-orientation) predict both greater subjective well-being and, paradoxically, greater actual generosity and resource creation.

translations.en: Salutation to Vasudhara, Bodhisattva of abundance
translations.zh: 礼敬金刚大地菩萨，丰盛之菩萨
translations.es: Salutación a Vasudhara, Bodhisattva de la abundancia

mantras.name: Om, I am that which is perfect, Hail to you.
mantras.english: Om, I am that which is perfect, Hail to you.
mantras.original: 唵ong 阿a弥mi利li得˙de 吽hong 怕pa都dou
mantras.transliteration: ॐ, अहम् तत् परं, वन्दे त्वाम्।
mantras.background: Om: Hinduism's primordial sound. The Mandukya Upanishad calls it all of time and existence. A-U-M vibrates creation, dream, sleep — then silence reveals the infinite.
mantras.benefits: Om resonates through body and mind, harmonizing waking, dreaming, and deep sleep states. Its sustained vibration calms the nervous system, invites stillness, and opens a doorway to turiya—the transcendent awareness beneath all experience.
mantras.tags: hindu, tibetan, mantra, devotion, healing, visualization, protection, auspicious, blessing, purification, chanting, grace

translations.es: Om, Yo soy aquello que es perfecto, Saludos a ti.
translations.ar: "أوم، أنا ذلك الكمال، سلام عليك."
translations.bn: ওম, আমি সেই যা নিখুঁত, তোমাকে অভিবাদন।
translations.de: Om, Ich bin das, was vollkommen ist, Sei gegrüßt.
translations.en-us: "Om, I am that which is perfect; salutations to you."
translations.en-gb: "Om. I am that which is perfect; Salutations to you."
translations.fr-fr: Om, je suis ce qui est parfait, Salutations à toi.
translations.he: הוֹם, אני הוא אשר מושלם, שבח לך.
translations.hi: ॐ, मैं वही हूँ जो पूर्ण है, तुम्हें प्रणाम।
translations.id: Om, Aku adalah yang sempurna, Salam untukmu.
translations.it: Om, io sono ciò che è perfetto, saluto a te.
translations.ja: オーム、私は至善のものです。あなたに敬意を表します。
translations.ko: 옴, 나는 그것이다, 즉 완전한 존재. 당신에게 경의를 표합니다.
translations.nl: Om, Ik ben dat wat volmaakt is, Ik groet u.
translations.pl: Om. Ja jestem tym, co jest doskonałe. Niech będzie uwielbiony.
translations.pt-br: Om, eu sou aquilo que é perfeito, Salve a ti.
translations.ru: Ом, Я есть то, что есть совершенство, приветствую тебя.
translations.sv: Om, Jag är det som är fullkomligt, Hälsning till dig.
translations.th: ออม, ข้าคือสิ่งที่สมบูรณ์, ข้าขอคารวะท่าน.
translations.tr: Om, Ben mükemmeliğim, Sana selam olsun.
translations.uk: Ом, Я є тим, що є досконалим, Вітаю Тебе.
translations.vi: Om, Tôi là đó, là sự hoàn thiện tuyệt đối. Lòng tôi hướng về Ngài.
translations.zh-cn: 嗡，我是那至完美的，向你致敬。

mantras.name: Om, I bow to the divine being, Hri, Amitabh.
mantras.english: Om, I bow to the divine being, Hri, Amitabh.
mantras.transliteration: ōṃ, aham hriṃ, amitābhaḥ, namo namaste.
mantras.background: From Tibetan Buddhism, "Om Ami Dewa Hri" invokes Amitabha — Buddha of the Western Pure Land, radiant as the setting sun. A portal to boundless light awaits.
mantras.benefits: Invoking Amitabha, Buddha of the Western Pure Land, this mantra is linked to boundless light and compassion. Chanting it cultivates inner stillness, protective awareness, and alignment with wisdom — drawing the practitioner toward liberation through devoted, resonant repetition.
mantras.tags: tibetan, amitabha, pure-land, devotional, longevity, buddhist

translations.es: Om, me inclino ante la divinidad, Hri, Amitabh.
translations.ar: أوم، أتوجه بالتبجيل إلى الكائن الإلهي، هري، أميتاب.
translations.bn: ওম, আমি হ্রী, अमिताभ নামক সেই দিব্য সত্তার কাছে নিজেকে সমর্পণ করছি।
translations.de: Om, ich verneige mich vor der göttlichen Wesenheit, Hri, Amitabh.
translations.en-us: "Om, I offer my salutations to the divine entity, Hri, Amitabh."
translations.en-gb: "Om, I offer my reverence to the divine entity, Hri, Amitabh."
translations.fr-fr: Ôm, je m'incline devant l'être divin, Hri, Amitabh.
translations.he: אום, אני משתחווה לאל הקדוש, הרי, אמיטאבה.
translations.hi: ॐ, मैं, 'ह्री' और 'अमिताभ' नामक दिव्य शक्ति को प्रणाम करता हूँ।
translations.id: Om, saya bersujud kepada entitas ilahi, Hri, Amitabh.
translations.it: Om, mi prostro al divino essere, Hri, Amitabh.
translations.ja: オーム。私は、至高の存在、フリ、アマビトに敬意を表します。
translations.ko: 옴, 저는 신성한 존재, 흐리(Hri), 아미타브(Amitabh)께 경배합니다.
translations.nl: Om, ik buig voor het goddelijke wezen, Hri, Amitabh.
translations.pl: Om. Składam pokłon boskiej istocie, Hri, Amitabh.
translations.pt-br: Om, eu me prostro diante da divindade, Hri, Amitabh.
translations.ru: Ом. Я склоняюсь перед божественной сущностью, Хри, Амитабхой.
translations.sv: Om, jag böjer mig inför det gudomliga väsendet, Hri, Amitabh.
translations.th: ข้าขอบูชาแด่เทพเจ้าศักดิ์สิทธิ์, หริ, อมิตาภะ
translations.tr: "Om, yüce varlığa, Hri'ye, Amitabh'a saygı sunuyorum."
translations.uk: Ом, я склоняю голову перед божественным существом, Хрі, Амітабх.
translations.vi: Om, tôi cúi lạy vị thần thiêng liêng, Hri, Amitabh.
translations.zh-cn: ॐ，我向神圣的存在，赫梨（Hri）， अमिताभ（Amitabha）致敬。

mantras.name: Om, I, the Self, Vajra, Guru, Lotus, Perfection, Hum.
mantras.english: Om, I, the Self, Vajra, Guru, Lotus, Perfection, Hum.
mantras.original: Om Ah Hum Vajra Guru Padma Siddhi Hum
mantras.transliteration: ॐ, अहम्, आत्मन्, वज्र, गुरु, पद्म, पूर्ण, हूँ।
mantras.background: From Tibetan Buddhism, this mantra honors Padmasambhava—the legendary Vajrayana master. Literally: "Om, Self, Vajra, Guru, Lotus, Perfection, Hum"—a sacred invocation of awakened power.
mantras.benefits: Invoking Padmasambhava through primordial syllables Om and Hum, this mantra weaves indestructible clarity (Vajra), wisdom (Guru), purity (Lotus), and spiritual perfection (Siddhi) into a single transformative call.
mantras.tags: tibetan, buddhist, vajrayana, padmasambhava, guru, sanskrit, enlightenment

translations.es: Om, yo, el Ser, Vajra, Guru, Loto, Perfección, Hum.
translations.ar: أوم، أنا، الذات، الفأس (أو القوة)، المعلم، اللوتس، الكمال، هوم.
translations.bn: ওম, আমি, আত্মা, বজ্র, গুরু, পদ্ম, পরিপূর্ণতা, হম।
translations.de: Om, ich, das Selbst, Vajra, Guru, Lotus, Vollendung, Hum.
translations.en-us: Oṃ, I, the Self, Vajra, Guru, Lotus, Perfection, Hum.
translations.en-gb: Om, I, the essence of being, the unshakeable diamond, the revered teacher, the blossoming lotus, the state of complete fulfilment, Hum.
translations.fr-fr: Ôm, je, le Soi, le Diamant, le Guru, le Lotus, la Perfection, Hour.
translations.he: הוֹם, אני, האני העצמי, וַגְ'רָה, גּוּרוּ, לִילִיָה, שלמות, הוּם.
translations.hi: ॐ, मैं, आत्मा, वज्र, गुरु, कमल, पूर्णता, हूँ।
translations.id: Om, Aku, Diri, Vajra, Guru, Lotus, Kesempurnaan, Hum.
translations.it: Om, io, il Sé, Vajra, Guru, Loto, Perfezione, Hum.
translations.ja: オーム、私は、真我（または、自己）、金剛、グル（または、導師）、蓮華、至高、フーム。
translations.ko: 옴, 나는, 자아, 바즈라, 구루, 연꽃, 완전함, 훔.
translations.nl: Om, Ik, het Zelf, Vajra, Guru, Lotus, Volmaaktheid, Hum.
translations.pl: Om, Ja, Atman, Vajra, Guru, Padma, Doskonałość, Hum.
translations.pt-br: Om, eu, o Ser, Vajra, Guru, Lótus, Perfeição, Hum.
translations.ru: Ом, Я, Атман, Ваджра, Гуру, Лотос, Совершенство, Хум.
translations.sv: Om, jag, Självet, Vajra, Guru, Lotus, Perfektion, Hum.
translations.th: โอ้ห์ มนะ ข้า สัจจะ วัชระ กุรุ ปัทมะ วิทยา ปรนัง ฮัม
translations.tr: Om, Ben, Benlik, Vajra, Guru, Lotos, Mükemmellik, Hum.
translations.uk: Ом, Я, Атман, Ваджра, Гуру, Лотос, Досконалість, Хум.
translations.vi: Ôm, Ta, Bản Thật, Kim Cương, Guru, Hoa Sen, Hoàn Mỹ, Hum.
translations.zh-cn: 嗡，我，真我，金刚，导师，莲花，完美，阿。

mantras.name: Om, Ma, Ni, Pa, Mi, Hum.
mantras.english: Om, Ma, Ni, Pa, Mi, Hum.
mantras.original: 唵ong 嘛˙ma呢ni 呗bei美mei 吽hong
mantras.transliteration: ōṃ māṇi padme hūṃ
mantras.background: Rooted in Buddhist tradition, Om Mani Padme Hum is Guanyin Bodhisattva's Six Syllable Great Bright Mantra. It opens with Om—the primordial vibration underlying all existence—drawing practitioners into stillness.
mantras.benefits: Rooted in Om's primordial resonance—shown to shift brainwaves toward deep meditative theta states—this six-syllable Guanyin mantra layers compassion onto that neurological foundation, inviting stillness, openness, and transformation of consciousness.
mantras.tags: buddhist, tibetan, avalokiteshvara, mantra, dharani

translations.es: Om, Madre, Poder, Manifestación, Ilusión, Unidad.
translations.ar: "أوم، يا أمي، يا ابني، يا أبي، يا أنا، يا الإله."
translations.bn: ওম, মা, নি, পা, মি, হম্।
translations.de: Om, Mutter, Geist, Wasser, Erde, Ich.
translations.en-us: Oṃ, "Mā" (to the earth), "Ni" (to water), "Pa" (to fire), "Mi" (to air), "Hum" (to the ether/space). This represents a foundational sound series often associated with the elemental energies or directions.
translations.en-gb: "Om, Mother, Mind, Water, Speech, Humility."
translations.fr-fr: Ôm, Ma, Ni, Pa, Mi, Hum.
translations.he: אוֹם, מָא, נִי, פָא, מִי, הוּם.
translations.id: Om, Ibu, Aku, Kamu, Kami, Semua.
translations.it: Om, Madre, Io sono, Padre, Io sono, Essere.
translations.ja: オーム、私（わ）、あなた（あなた）、彼（彼）、私たち（私たち）、すべて（すべて）。
translations.ko: 옴, 마, 니, 빠, 미, 훔.
translations.nl: Om, Moeder, Ik, Pader, Mijn, Ik.
translations.pl: Om, Mā, Ni, Pā, Mi, Hum – recytowane słowa mantry.
translations.pt-br: Om, Mãe, Não, Pai, Eu, Humildade.
translations.ru: Ом, Ма, Ни, Па, Ми, Хум.
translations.sv: Om, Moder, Jag, Fader, Mig, Jag.
translations.th: ขอให้ข้าพเจ้าเป็นผู้มีเมตตา, มีความรู้, มีอำนาจ, มีความสุข, และเป็นหนึ่งเดียวกับความยิ่งใหญ่.
translations.tr: Om, Anne, Baba, Çocuk, Ben, Ben.
translations.uk: Ом, Ма, Ні, Па, Мі, Хум.
translations.vi: Om, Mẹ, Ngài, Hòa, Chúng, Tôi.
translations.zh-cn: 嗡，我，你，我们，他们，万物。

mantras.name: Om, Refuge in Amitabha, Hail.
mantras.english: Om, Refuge in Amitabha, Hail.
mantras.transliteration: ōṃ, saraṇaṃ amitābhāya, namas.
mantras.background: From Pure Land Buddhism, this mantra honors Amitabha Buddha. "Om" opens the sacred sound; "refuge" signals devotion; "Hail" seals the prayer—inviting boundless light into practice.
mantras.benefits: Chanting this Amitabha mantra cultivates refuge, inner stillness, and compassion. Buddhist tradition holds it purifies the mind, releases ego-attachment, and deepens devotion—drawing the practitioner toward boundless light and peace.
mantras.tags: buddhist, amitabha, refuge, homage, devotion, mahayana, meditation, invocation, prayer

translations.es: Om, Refugio en Amitabha, Saludos.
translations.ar: "أوم، ملجأ في أميتابها، تحية."
translations.bn: ওম, अमिताभের আশ্রয়, নমস্কার।
translations.de: Om, Ich suche Zuflucht bei Amitabha, Sei gegrüßt.
translations.en-us: Om, I take refuge in Amitabha, Salutations.
translations.en-gb: Om, I take refuge in Amitabha, Hail.
translations.fr-fr: Ôm, je me réfugie en Amitabha, Salutations.
translations.he: אוֹם, מפלט באמיטאבה, הללויה.
translations.hi: ॐ, अमिताभ में शरण, प्रणाम।
translations.id: Om, Tempat Berlindung pada Amitabha, Salam.
translations.it: Om, rifugio in Amitābha, saluto.
translations.ja: オーム、阿弥陀仏への帰依、礼。
translations.ko: 옴, 아미타불께 귀의하나이다. 경배합니다.
translations.nl: Om, Ik zoek mijn toevlucht bij Amitabha, Groet.
translations.pl: Om, Schronienie w Amitabhie, Witanie.
translations.pt-br: Om, Refúgio em Amitabha, Salve.
translations.ru: ОМ, Прибежище в Амитабхе, приветствую.
translations.sv: Om, Jag söker tillflykt hos Amitabha, Hälsning.
translations.th: ออม, ขอพึ่งพาพระอมิตาภ, นอบน้อม.
translations.tr: Om, Amitabha'ya sığınarak, Selam olsun.
translations.uk: Ом, Притулок у Амітабхи, Вітаю.
translations.vi: Om, Con xin lánh nhờ Phật A Di Đà, Kính chào.
translations.zh-cn: 嗡，皈依阿弥陀佛，礼敬。

mantras.name: Om, gone, gone, having gone beyond, completely absorbed. May there be realization. Hail.
mantras.english: Om, gone, gone, having gone beyond, completely absorbed. May there be realization. Hail.
mantras.original: ओम् गते गते परगते परसंगतो बोधि स्वाहा
mantras.transliteration: ōṃ, gataḥ, gataḥ, gatam, aprameyam. jñānaṃ juṭam. ādeśaḥ.
mantras.background:
From Buddhist Prajnaparamita tradition, this mantra means "Gone, gone, beyond, completely absorbed — Realization! Hail." It bridges wisdom and liberation through sacred sound.
  Grounding: Source lists "ઓમ ગતે ગતે પરગતે પરસંગતો બોધિ સ્વાહા" as the Heart Sutra mantra (હૃદયસૂત્ર), a Mahayana Buddhist dhāraṇī.
mantras.benefits: This mantra of the Heart Sutra embodies prajna—wisdom itself. Chanting it draws the mind beyond illusion toward enlightenment, dissolving duality and awakening deep realization. A gateway to liberation.
mantras.tags: buddhist, mahayana, heart sutra, wisdom, enlightenment, transcendence, emptiness, liberation, realization, perfection

translations.es: Om, desaparecido, desaparecido, habiendo trascendido, completamente absorbido. Que haya realización. Saludo.
translations.ar: اُوم، زائل، زائل، تجاوز، مستوعب بالكامل. ليكن هناك إدراك. تحية.
translations.bn: ওম, অতীত, অতীত, যিনি সবকিছু অতিক্রম করেছেন, যিনি সম্পূর্ণরূপে বিলীন হয়ে গেছেন। জ্ঞান হোক।Hail.
translations.de: Om, verschwunden, verklungen, jenseits von allem, vollkommen absorbiert. Möge die Erkenntnis erstrahlen. Sei gegrüßt.
translations.en-us: "Om, vanished, vanished, having transcended, completely absorbed. May there be realization. Hail."
translations.en-gb: "Om, vanished, vanished, having transcended, completely absorbed. May there be enlightenment. Hail."
translations.fr-fr: Om, disparu, disparu, ayant dépassé, complètement absorbé. Qu'il y ait réalisation. Salutations.
translations.he: אוֹם, אבד, אבד, שעבר מעבר, נבלע לחלוטין. יתגשם. הללויה.
translations.hi: ॐ, चला गया, चला गया, पार हो गया, पूर्ण रूप से लीन हो गया। बोध हो। वंदन।
translations.id: Om, hilang, hilang, telah melampaui, sepenuhnya terserap. Semoga ada kesadaran. Salam.
translations.it: Om, cessato, cessato, trascendente, completamente assorbito. Possa esserci realizzazione. Salve.
translations.ja: オーム。消え去れ。消え去れ。超越し、完全に吸収されよ。悟りを得ん。礼拝。
translations.ko: 옴, 사라졌습니다, 사라졌습니다, 초월했습니다, 완전히 몰입했습니다. 깨달음이 있기를. 경배합니다.
translations.nl: Om, voorbij, voorbij, hebbende de grens overschreden, volledig opgenomen. Moge er realisatie zijn. Lof.
translations.pl: Om. Zniknięcie, zniknięcie, przezwyciężenie, całkowite zatracenie. Niech nastąpi realizacja. Chwała.
translations.pt-br: Om, desaparecido, extinto, transcendido, completamente absorvido. Que haja iluminação. Salve.
translations.ru: Ом. Ушел, ушел, преодолевший, полностью поглощенный. Да будет осознание. Слава.
translations.sv: Om, försvunnen, försvunnen, som har gått bortom, helt uppslukad. Må det ske insikt. Hälsning.
translations.th: ออม, สิ้นไป, สิ้นไป, สำเร็จการไป, ดับสูญอย่างสมบูรณ์. ขอให้เกิดปัญญา. ข้าขอคารวะ.
translations.tr: Om, yok olmuş, kaybolmuş, ötesine geçilmiş, tamamen özümsemiş. Aydınlanma olsun. Selam olsun.
translations.uk: Ом, зник, зник, перейшовши за межі, повністю поглинений. Нехай буде усвідомлення. Слава.
translations.vi: ॐ, đã qua, đã qua, đã vượt qua, hoàn toàn tiêu tan. Xin có sự giác ngộ. Kính chào.
translations.zh-cn: 嗡，已逝，已逝，超越，完全融入。愿有开悟。 敬礼。

mantras.name: Om, grant us, grant us, grant us, hail.
mantras.english: Om, grant us, grant us, grant us, hail.
mantras.original: 唵ong 达da咧lie 都dū达da咧lie 都dū咧lie 莎suo哈ha
mantras.transliteration: ōṃ, āṁ, āṁ, āṁ, vai.
mantras.background: Om — Hinduism's primordial sound — predates all mantras. Its syllables (A-U-M) map waking, dream & deep sleep states, with silence as transcendence. Chant it; feel the universe hum.
mantras.benefits: Om's three syllables (A-U-M) map to waking, dream, and deep sleep states, while the trailing silence opens a fourth transcendent awareness. Research confirms chanting shifts brainwaves toward meditative theta states.
mantras.tags: hindu, tibetan, mantra, devotion, healing, protection, blessing, aspiration, buddhist, tantric, prayer, affirmation

translations.es: Om, concédenos, concédenos, concédenos, alabanza.
translations.ar: "يا أوم، امنحنا، امنحنا، امنحنا، نرجوك."
translations.bn: ওম, আমাদের দিন, আমাদের দিন, আমাদের দিন, স্তুতি।
translations.de: Om, gewährt uns, gewährt uns, gewährt uns, lobe.
translations.en-us: "Om, bestow upon us, bestow upon us, bestow upon us, hail."
translations.en-gb: Om, may we be granted, may we be granted, may we be granted, hail.
translations.fr-fr: Ôm, accordez-nous, accordez-nous, accordez-nous, louange.
translations.he: אום, תן לנו, תן לנו, תן לנו, הללו.
translations.hi: ॐ, हमें प्रदान करो, हमें प्रदान करो, हमें प्रदान करो, स्तुति।
translations.id: Om, anugerahkanlah kepada kami, anugerahkanlah kepada kami, anugerahkanlah kepada kami, sembahlah.
translations.it: Om, concedeteci, concedeteci, concedeteci, saluto.
translations.ja: オームよ、我々に与え給え、我々に与え給え、我々に与え給え、讃え奉る。
translations.ko: 옴, 우리에게 주옵소서, 우리에게 주옵소서, 우리에게 주옵소서, 찬미합니다.
translations.nl: Om, geef ons, geef ons, geef ons, prijs.
translations.pl: Om, daj nam, daj nam, daj nam, powitaj.
translations.pt-br: Om, conceda-nos, conceda-nos, conceda-nos, louvores.
translations.ru: Ом, даруй нам, даруй нам, даруй нам, приветствие.
translations.sv: Om, ge oss, ge oss, ge oss, lov.
translations.th: ขอพรจากพระผู้เป็นเจ้า, ขอพรจากพระผู้เป็นเจ้า, ขอพรจากพระผู้เป็นเจ้า, ข้าขอคารวะ.
translations.tr: Om, bize ver, bize ver, bize ver, selamlar.
translations.uk: Ом, даруй нам, даруй нам, даруй нам, вітаю.
translations.vi: "Ôm, xin hãy ban cho chúng con, xin hãy ban cho chúng con, xin hãy ban cho chúng con, chúng con kính chào."
translations.zh-cn: 嗡，请赐予我们，请赐予我们，请赐予我们，赞颂。

mantras.name: Om, hail to the forceful Guru, the lotus of accomplishment, hail.
mantras.english: Om, hail to the forceful Guru, the lotus of accomplishment, hail.
mantras.original: ओम् एह हंग वज्र गुरु पद्म सिद्धि हंग
mantras.transliteration: ōṃ, hail to the forceful guru, the lotus of accomplishment, hail.
mantras.background: From Tibetan Tantric Buddhism, this mantra invokes Padmasambhava—the Vajra Guru, lotus-born master whose name itself means "forceful accomplishment."
mantras.benefits: This Vajra Guru mantra unites Om's primordial resonance with invocation of spiritual accomplishment (siddhi). Chanting activates body, speech, and mind together, fostering deep meditative states and inner transformation through sacred sound vibration.
mantras.tags: hindu, primordial sound, om, guru, meditation, chanting, vibration, divine, consciousness, healing, unity, mantra, tibetan

translations.es: Om, saludo al Guru poderoso, la flor de la realización, saludo.
translations.ar: يا أوم، تحية للمعلم القوي، زهرة الإنجاز، تحية.
translations.bn: ওম, ও শক্তিশালী গুরুকে প্রণাম, যিনি সাফল্যের পদ্ম, ওম, প্রণাম।
translations.de: Om, Lob sei dem kraftvollen Lehrer, der Lotus der Vollendung, Lob.
translations.en-us: Om, salutations to the powerful Guru, the lotus of achievement, salutations.
translations.en-gb: Om, salutations to the powerful teacher, the blossom of achievement, salutations.
translations.fr-fr: Om, hail to le Guru puissant, le lotus de la réalisation, hail.
translations.he: אָום, הִתְכַּלְּלוּ אֵלֶּה, אֵלֵי הַמּוֹרֶה הַנִּיצָּח, הַלּוֹטוּס שֶׁל הַהִתְגַּשְׁמוּת, הִתְכַּלְּלוּ.
translations.hi: ॐ, हे शक्तिशाली गुरु को प्रणाम, जो सिद्धि का प्रतीक हैं, प्रणाम।
translations.id: Om, salam kepada Guru yang berwibawa, bunga lili pencapaian, salam.
translations.it: Om, saluto al Guru potente, al loto della realizzazione, saluto.
translations.ja: オーム、力強いグル神へ、成就の蓮よ、礼。 (Ōmu, chikara-tsuyoi guru-shin e, jōju no hasu yo, rei.)
translations.ko: 옴, 강력한 구루께 경의를 표합니다. 깨달음의 연꽃이시여, 경의를 표합니다.
translations.nl: Om, lof aan de krachtige Goeroe, de lotus van verwezenlijking, lof.
translations.pl: Om, powitajmy energicznego Gurua, lotos osiągnięć, powitajmy.
translations.pt-br: Om, saúdo ao Guru poderoso, à flor de lótus do sucesso, saúdo.
translations.ru: Ом, приветствие могучему Гуру, лотосу достижений, привет.
translations.sv: Om, jag hälsar den kraftfulla Gurun, den som är framgångens lotus, jag hälsar.
translations.th: ขอพระนามแด่กูรุผู้ทรงพลัง ผู้ซึ่งเป็นดอกบัวแห่งความสำเร็จ ขอพระนามแด่พระองค์
translations.tr: Om, güçlü Öğretmen'e selam, başarıların lotusu, selam.
translations.uk: Ом, приветствую могущественного Гуру, лотос, воплощающий достижения, приветствую.
translations.vi: Om, Kính chào vị Thầy khả năng, đóa sen của thành tựu, Kính chào.
translations.zh-cn: 嗡，向着充满力量的导师致敬，他是成就之莲，向着他致敬。

mantras.name: Om, jewel (or precious gem) in the lotus (or heart), Hūṃ.
mantras.english: Om, jewel (or precious gem) in the lotus (or heart), Hūṃ.
mantras.transliteration: ōṃ, maṇi, padme, hūṃ.
mantras.background: From Buddhism's heart: "Om Maṇi Padme Hūṃ" — jewel in the lotus — a 3000+ year-old Sanskrit mantra uniting cosmic sound with compassion's awakening gem.
mantras.benefits: Chanting this Buddhist gem invokes compassion and inner purification. Its sound synchronizes breath and mind, calming the nervous system and awakening spiritual clarity — like a jewel waiting within the heart's lotus.
mantras.tags: buddhist, chanting, sound, meditation, peace, healing, focus, devotion, enlightenment, prayer

translations.es: Om, joya (o gema preciosa) en el loto (o en el corazón), Hūṃ.
translations.ar: أوم، جوهرة في اللوتس (أو القلب)، هوم.
translations.bn: ওম, পদ্মের (অথবা হৃদয়ের) মধ্যে রত্ন, হূྃ।
translations.de: Om, ein Juwel im Herzen, Hūṃ.
translations.en-us: Oṃ, the jewel (or precious gem) within the lotus (or heart), Hūṃ.
translations.en-gb: "Om, the jewel within the lotus, Hūṃ."
translations.fr-fr: Om, joyau (ou pierre précieuse) dans le lotus (ou le cœur), Hūṃ.
translations.he: הוּם, תכשיט/אבן חן בלוטוס/בלב, אוֹם.
translations.hi: ॐ, कमल (या हृदय) में स्थित रत्न, हूँ।
translations.id: Om, permata (atau mutiara) di dalam lotus (atau hati), Hūṃ.
translations.it: Ōṁ, gemma preziosa nel loto (o nel cuore), Hūṃ.
translations.ja: オーム、蓮の花（または心）の中にある宝玉よ、フウム。
translations.ko: 옴, 연꽃(또는 심장) 속의 보석, 훔.
translations.nl: Om, een kostbare steen (of juweel) in het lotus (of hart), Hūṃ.
translations.pl: Om, klejnot (lub cenny kamień) w lotosie (lub sercu), Hūṃ.
translations.pt-br: Om, joia (ou pedra preciosa) no lótus (ou no coração), Hūṃ.
translations.ru: Ом, драгоценный камень в лотосе (или в сердце), Хум.
translations.sv: Om, en dyrbar juvel i lotusblomman (eller hjärtat), Hūṃ.
translations.th: อุ้ม, มณี (หรือ อัญมณีล้ำค่า) ในบัว (หรือ หัวใจ), หุม
translations.tr: Om, lottus (veya kalp) içindeki mücevher (veya değerli taş), Hūṃ.
translations.uk: Ом, дорогоцінний камінь у лотосі (або в серці), Хум.
translations.vi: Om, viên ngọc báu (hoặc đá quý) trong đóa sen (hoặc trái tim), Hūṃ.
translations.zh-cn: 嗡，如莲花（或心脏）中的珍宝，嗡。

mantras.name: Om, jewel, thunderbolt, perfection.
mantras.english: Om, jewel, thunderbolt, perfection.
mantras.transliteration: ओं, मणि, वज्र, पूर्ण।
mantras.background: Rooted in Vajrayana Buddhism, this mantra unites Om (primordial sound), maṇi (jewel), vajra (thunderbolt), and siddhe (perfection)—invoking divine accomplishment through sacred sound.
mantras.benefits: Chanting Om stills the mind through resonant vibration; the jewel awakens wisdom, the thunderbolt cuts delusion, and perfection points toward spiritual attainment — together guiding the practitioner inward.
mantras.tags: buddhist, vajrayana, recitation, accomplishment, protection, enlightenment, compassion, healing

translations.es: Om, joya, rayo, perfección.
translations.ar: "أوم، جوهرة، برق، الكمال."
translations.bn: ওম, রত্ন, বজ্র, পূর্ণতা।
translations.de: Om, Juwel, Blitz, Vollkommenheit.
translations.en-us: "Om, jewel, thunderbolt, perfection."
translations.en-gb: "Om, a precious gem, a thunderbolt, perfection."
translations.fr-fr: Ôm, joyau, foudre, perfection.
translations.he: אוֹם, תכשיט, ברק, שלמות.
translations.hi: ॐ, रत्न, वज्र, पूर्णता।
translations.id: Om, permata, petir, kesempurnaan.
translations.it: Om, gemma, fulmine, perfezione.
translations.ja: オーム、宝物、雷、完全。
translations.ko: 옴, 보석, 천둥, 완벽함.
translations.nl: Om, juweel, bliksem, volmaaktheid.
translations.pl: Om, klejnot, piorun, doskonałość.
translations.pt-br: Om, joia, raio, perfeição.
translations.ru: Ом, драгоценность, молния, совершенство.
translations.sv: Om, juvel, åskvält, fulländning.
translations.th: ขอพร, อัญมณี, สายฟ้า, ความสมบูรณ์แบบ
translations.tr: Om, mücevher, gök gürültüsü, mükemmellik.
translations.uk: Ом, самоцвіт, бліскавка, досконалість.
translations.vi: ॐ, viên ngọc, sét, sự hoàn hảo.
translations.zh-cn: 嗡， 宝石， 闪电， 完美。

mantras.name: Om, jewel-holder, proceed, accomplish.
mantras.english: Om, jewel-holder, proceed, accomplish.
mantras.transliteration: ōṃ, maṇi-dhāra, praśratha, siddhi-kuru.
mantras.background:
A Buddhist mantra invoking a jewel-holding deity, rooted in Sanskrit Vajrayana tradition. Its syllables carry sacred power — chant it to proceed toward awakening and accomplish liberation.
  Grounding: Source tables show Sanskrit mantras with "jewel" (maṇi/ratna) and "accomplish" (siddhi/svāhā) elements tied to Buddhist bodhisattva invocations.
mantras.benefits: Invoking a jewel-holding deity with commands to proceed and accomplish, this mantra channels purposeful spiritual momentum. Traditional sources link *siddhe* (accomplishment) to dissolving karmic obstacles, while the jewel imagery evokes wisdom and attainment awakening inner resolve.
mantras.tags:
Here’s a breakdown of tags for the provided mantra, aiming for specificity and reflecting the information in the source material:

hindu, tantric, buddhist, devotion,忿怒尊, healing, protection, tibetan, mantra, buddhist, deity, ritual

translations.es: Om, portador de joyas, avanza, logra.
translations.ar: يا "أوم"، يا حامل الجواهر، تقدم، أتمم.
translations.bn: ওম, হে রত্ন-ধারি, অগ্রসর হও, সিদ্ধি লাভ করো।
translations.de: Om, Träger des Juwels, schreite voran, vollende.
translations.en-us: Oṃ, bearer of preciousness, advance, complete.
translations.en-gb: Oṃ, bearer of preciousness, advance, fulfil.
translations.fr-fr: Ôm, porteur de joyaux, avance, accomplis.
translations.he: אום, מחזיק תכשיטים, התקדם, השלם.
translations.hi: ॐ, हे रत्न-धारक, आगे बढ़ो, सिद्धि प्राप्त करो।
translations.id: Om, pemegang permata, maju, sempurnakan.
translations.it: Om, portatore di gioielli, avanza, realizza.
translations.ja: オーム、宝を宿す者よ、進み、成就せよ。
translations.ko: 옴, 보석을 지키는 분이시여, 나아가십시오, 성취하십시오.
translations.nl: Om, o edelsteenbewaarder, vorder, voltooi.
translations.pl: Oṃ, ty, który jesteś nośnikiem klejnotów, rusz naprzód, osiągnij cel.
translations.pt-br: Om, detentor de joias, avança, realiza.
translations.ru: Ом, держатель драгоценностей, двигайся вперед, соверши (дело).
translations.sv: Om, du som bär juvelerna, framåt, fullbordande.
translations.th: อม, ผู้ทรงอัญมณี, จงดำเนินไป, จงบรรลุ.
translations.tr: Om, mücevher sahibi, ilerle, tamamla.
translations.uk: Ом, о, хранитель драгоценностей, продвигайся, заверши.
translations.vi: Om, Người nắm giữ viên ngọc quý, hãy tiến lên, hãy hoàn thành.
translations.zh-cn: 嗡，你是珍宝的持有者，请前行，请成就。

mantras.name: Om, may the beautiful nature be purified, may the beautiful essence of all dharmas (righteous paths/duties) be purified; may I be purified.
mantras.english: Om, may the beautiful nature be purified, may the beautiful essence of all dharmas (righteous paths/duties) be purified; may I be purified.
mantras.original: Om sobhawa shuddha sarwa dharma sobhawa shuddho ham
mantras.transliteration: ōṃ, may the beautiful nature be purified, may the beautiful essence of all dharmas (righteous paths/duties) be purified; may I be purified.
mantras.background: From Vajrayana Buddhism, "Om sobhawa shuddha sarwa dharma sobhawa shuddho ham" purifies mind and dharmas. A Sanskrit mantra binding thought to sacred intention.
mantras.benefits: Chanting this purification mantra binds the mind to positive intent, shielding it from harmful thoughts. Its repetitive structure cultivates inner clarity and alignment with dharma, transforming the practitioner's consciousness toward purity.
mantras.tags: hindu, purification, vedic, dharma, transformation, om, ritual, invocation, universal, healing
