mantras.benefits: Chanting Om anchors the practitioner in primordial vibration, while bowing to the Divine Mother cultivates devotion and inner opening—together fostering the stillness, wisdom, and peace described across Vedic and Tara traditions.
mantras.tags: hindu, vedic, goddess, shakti, devotional, prayer, primordial, chanting, healing

translations.es: Om, me inclino ante la Madre Divina, saludos.
translations.ar: أوم، أُسَلِّم إلى الأم الإلهية، تحية.
translations.bn: ওম, আমি ঐশ্বরিক মাতৃ goddesses-এর কাছে নতজানু, प्रणाम।
translations.de: OM, ich verneige mich vor der Göttlichen Mutter, Gebete.
translations.en-us: "Om, I offer my reverence to the Divine Mother, salutations."
translations.en-gb: "Om, I offer my reverence to the Divine Mother, and offer my salutations."
translations.fr-fr: Ôm, je m'incline devant la Mère Divine, salutations.
translations.he: אוֹם, אני משתחווה לאם האלוהית, ברכות.
translations.hi: ॐ, मैं दिव्य माता को प्रणाम करता हूँ, नमस्कार।
translations.id: Om, saya bersujud kepada Ibu Ilahi, salam.
translations.it: Om, mi prostro alla Dea Madre divina, saluti.
translations.ja: オーム。私は至高の女神に敬意を払い、礼します。 (Ōmu. Watashi wa shitekino megami ni keii o hanai, rei shimasu.)
translations.ko: 옴, 저는 신성한 어머니께 경배를 올립니다. (Om, jeoneun sinsanghan eomeonije kke gyeongbaereul ollimnida.)
translations.nl: Om, ik buig voor de goddelijke Moeder, groeten.
translations.pl: Om, składam pokłon Bożej Matce, pozdrowienia.
translations.pt-br: Om, eu me prostro diante da Mãe Divina, saudações.
translations.ru: Ом. Я склоняюсь перед Божественной Матерью. Приветствия.
translations.sv: Om, jag böjer mig inför den gudomliga modern, hälsningar.
translations.th: ข้าขอน้อมนวไมตรีต่อพระแม่ divine, ขอแสดงความเคารพ
translations.tr: Om, İlahi Ana'ya saygı duyuyorum, selamlar.
translations.uk: Ом. Я вклоняюся Божественній Матері, вітаю.
translations.vi: Ôm, con cúi lạy Mẹ Thiêng Liêng, kính chào.
translations.zh-cn: ॐ，我向神圣的母亲致敬，合十。

mantras.name: Obeisance to the luminous one, the sun, whose brilliance is like the lotus, who is of the lineage of Kashyapa, who possesses immense splendor, who dispels all sins. I bow down to the illuminator.
mantras.english: Obeisance to the luminous one, the sun, whose brilliance is like the lotus, who is of the lineage of Kashyapa, who possesses immense splendor, who dispels all sins. I bow down to the illuminator.
mantras.original: Om Japakusuma Sankasham Kashyapeyam Mahadyutim, Dhantari Sarvapapaghnam Pranato’smi Divakaram.
mantras.transliteration: obeisance to the luminous one, the sun, whose brilliance is like the lotus, who is of the lineage of Kashyapa, who possesses immense splendor, who dispels all sins. I bow down to the illuminator.
mantras.background: A Hindu morning sun mantra honoring the solar deity of Kashyapa's lineage—chanted to dispel sins and awaken the mind with divine, lotus-bright brilliance.
mantras.benefits: Chanting this solar mantra cultivates inner radiance, purification of sins, and mental clarity — mirroring the sun's light. Tradition links it to strength, spiritual growth, and emotional balance at dawn.
mantras.tags: hindu, sun, devotion, purification, illumination, kashyapa, splendor, sin-removal, gratitude, morning, mantra, praise

translations.es: "Salutations al ser luminoso, al sol, cuyo resplandor es como el loto, que es descendiente de Kashyapa, que posee un esplendor inmenso, y que disipa todo pecado. Me inclino ante el iluminador."
translations.ar: نُسَجِّلُ الاحترام للوهّاج، الشمس، الذي لمعانه يشبه اللوتس، والذي ينتمي إلى سلالة كاشيابا، والذي يمتلك بهجة عظيمة، والذي يبدد كل الذنوب. أُسَلِّمُ إلى المُنوِّر.
translations.bn: "হে উজ্জ্বল সত্তা, হে সূর্য, যাঁর ঔজ্জ্বল্য পদ্মের মতো, যিনি কাশ্যপের বংশধর, যাঁর বিশাল আভা আছে, যিনি সকল পাপ দূর করেন। আমি সেই আলোকময় সত্তার প্রতি নতজানু।"
translations.de: Vere der strahlende, die Sonne, deren Leuchtkraft der eines Lotusblatts gleicht, der aus der Linie von Kashyapa stammt, der unermesslichen Glanz besitzt und alle Sünden vertreibt. Ich verneige mich vor dem Erleuchter.
translations.en-us: Salutations to the radiant one, the sun, whose brilliance resembles the lotus, who descends from the lineage of Kashyapa, who possesses immense splendor, and who eradicates all sins. I prostrate before the illuminator.
translations.en-gb: Salutations to the radiant one, the sun, whose brilliance resembles the lotus flower, who is descended from the lineage of Kashyapa, who possesses boundless glory, and who eradicates all sins. I offer my reverence to the illuminator.
translations.fr-fr:
Voici une traduction en français :

"Salut à la lumière divine, au soleil, dont l'éclat est comparable à celui du lotus, qui est issu de la lignée de Kashyapa, qui possède une splendeur immense et qui dissipe tous les péchés. Je m'incline devant celui qui illumine."
translations.he: אנוחה לאור המאיר, השמש, שהזוהר שלו דומה ללוטוס, השייך לשושלת של קאשיאפה, בעל תפארת עצומה, המבטל את כל החטאים. אני משתחווה למאיר.
translations.hi: "मैं उस तेजस्वी, प्रकाशमान, सूर्य देव को प्रणाम करता हूँ, जिनकी आभा कमल के समान है, जो कश्यप गोत्र के हैं, जिनकी भव्यता असीम है, और जो सभी पापों को दूर करते हैं। मैं उस प्रकाशक को प्रणाम करता हूँ।"
translations.id: Salam hormat kepada Sang Cahaya, Matahari, yang kecerahannya bagaikan bunga teratai, yang berasal dari keturunan Kashyapa, yang memiliki kemegahan yang luar biasa, yang menghapus segala dosa. Aku bersujud kepada Sang Pemberi Cahaya.
translations.it: Om, prostratiamo a quella luce splendente, al Sole, la cui luminosità è paragonabile al loto, nato dalla stirpe di Kashyapa, che possiede una magnificenza immensa e che dissolve ogni peccato. Mi prostro all'illuminatore.
translations.ja: 太陽神、カシャパの血統に連なる、輝きが蓮の花のような、そしてあらゆる罪を払う、光り輝く存在、あなたに礼拝します。光をもたらすものに、私はひざまずく。
translations.ko: 빛나는 태양, 연꽃처럼 아름다운, 가슈여파의 혈통을 잇는, 엄청난 광채를 가진, 모든 죄를 없애는 존재께 경배합니다. 나는 그 빛을 비추는 존재께 굴복합니다.
translations.nl: Ik buig voor de stralende, de zon, wier helderheid gelijk is aan die van een lotus, die behoort tot de lijn van Kashyapa, die immense glorie bezit en alle zonden wegneemt. Ik verneem de verlichter.
translations.pl: Skłaniam się przed emanującym światłem, przed Słońcem, którego blask jest jak lotos, pochodzącym z rodu Kashyapy, obdarzonym ogromną chwałą, który rozprasza wszelkie grzechy. Czczę ten, który oświeca.
translations.pt-br: Reverência àquele que é a luz, ao sol, cujo brilho é semelhante ao lótus, que pertence à linhagem de Kashyapa, que possui imensa magnificência e que dissipa todos os pecados. Eu me prostro perante o iluminador.
translations.ru: Я склоняюсь перед сияющим, перед Солнцем, чей блеск подобен лотосу, происходящим из рода Кашьяпы, обладающим огромным великолепием, рассеивающим все грехи. Я поклоняюсь Дарителю света.
translations.sv: Jag böjer mig för den strålande, den lysande solen, vars glans liknar lotusblomman, som är av Kashyapas släkt, som besitter en enorm prakt och som skingrar all synd. Jag vördar den som upplyser.
translations.th: ขอคารวะแด่พระอาทิตย์ ผู้ส่องสว่าง ดุจดวงบัว ที่มีรัศมีอันยิ่งใหญ่ สืบเชื้อสายจากกัศยปะ ผู้มีพระเมตตาอันประเสริฐ ผู้ขจัดบาปทั้งปวง ข้าพเจ้าขอถวายบังคมแด่ผู้ให้แสงสว่าง
translations.tr: Güneş'e, parlak olan, nilüfer çiçeği gibi ışıltısı olan, Kaşyapa'nın soyundan gelen, muazzam bir ihtişama sahip, tüm günahları yok eden, aydınlatıcı güneşe saygı. Aydınlatıcıya secde ediyorum.
translations.uk: Низький уклін світлу, сонячному сяйву, чия яскравість подібна до лотоса, який є з роду Кашьяпи, що володіє величезним блиском і розсіює всі гріхи. Я поклоняюся Тобі, що даруєш просвітлення.
translations.vi: Kính cúng Tỳ Ni, vầng thái dương rực rỡ, ánh sáng tựa đóa sen, thuộc dòng dõi của Kashyapa, mang trong mình vầng sáng vô tận, xua tan mọi tội lỗi. Con nguyện cúi lạy Đấng Giác Ngộ.
translations.zh-cn: 敬礼光芒万丈的太阳神，其光辉如莲花般纯洁，乃卡希叶帕的后裔，拥有无尽的荣耀，能消除一切罪恶。我向这位启明者致敬。

mantras.name: Obeisances to Narayana, the golden one, Hari, Obeisances.
mantras.english: Obeisances to Narayana, the golden one, Hari, Obeisances.
mantras.transliteration: ōṃ nārāyaṇa-nāmaḥ, suvarṇa-vignam, hari-nāmaḥ, ōṃ nārāyaṇa-nāmaḥ.
mantras.background: The source material does not contain information about the mantra "Obeisances to Narayana, the golden one, Hari" — its origin, tradition, or literal meaning cannot be grounded in the provided text.
mantras.benefits: Practicing this mantra of obeisance to Narayana/Hari may help dissolve accumulated Sanchita Karma, easing life's miseries and roadblocks, potentially guiding the practitioner toward liberation (mukti) through devoted daily repetition.
mantras.tags: hindu, ram, narayana, hari, mantra, devotion, bhakti, karma, liberation, empower

translations.es: Salutations a Narayana, el de tez dorada, Hari. Salutations.
translations.ar: تحيةٌ لنارايانا، الذهبي، هاري. تحية.
translations.bn: নாராயণের, সোনালী সত্তার, হরিকে, প্রণাম।
translations.de: Verehrung für Narayana, den Goldenen, Hari, Verehrung.
translations.en-us: Salutations to Narayana, the golden one, Hari; salutations.
translations.en-gb: Salutations to Narayana, the golden one, Hari. Salutations.
translations.fr-fr: Salutations à Narayana, l'être d'or, Hari. Salutations.
translations.he: "נפילה חסד לנריינה, זהב, הרי, נפילה חסד."
translations.hi: नारायण, स्वर्ण स्वरूप वाले, हरि को प्रणाम। प्रणाम।
translations.id: Salam hormat kepada Narayana, Sang Emas, Hari. Salam hormat.
translations.it: Om, venerazione a Narayana, colui che è dorato, Hari, venerazione.
translations.ja: ナラヤナ神、黄金の御姿を持つハリーへ、敬意を表します。敬意を表します。 (Narayana-shin, kōgon no mi-sugata o motsu Hari e, keii o taisemasu. Keii o taisemasu.)
translations.ko: 나라인 나라이에게 경배를, 황금빛인 주님, 하리께 경배를.
translations.nl: Groeten aan Narayana, de gouden, Hari. Groeten.
translations.pl: Składam pokłon Narayani, Złotemu, Hari, Składam pokłon.
translations.pt-br: Saudações a Narayana, o dourado, Hari; Saudações.
translations.ru: Низкий поклон Нараяне, Златоликому, Хари. Поклон.
translations.sv: Hälsningar till Narayana, den gyllene, Hari. Hälsningar.
translations.th: ขอคำนับนารายณ์, ผู้ซึ่งมีลักษณะเป็นสีทอง, พระหริ. ขอคำนับ.
translations.tr: Narayana'ya, altın renkli olan, Hari'ye saygılar.
translations.uk: Поклоніння Нараяні, Золотому, Харі. Поклоніння.
translations.vi: Kính cúng Narayana, đấng Vàng, Hari. Kính cúng.
translations.zh-cn: 敬礼纳伽纳，这位金色的主；敬礼哈利；敬礼。

mantras.name: Oh, Lord of the Kali Yuga, I take refuge in you, Ganesha.
mantras.english: Oh, Lord of the Kali Yuga, I take refuge in you, Ganesha.
mantras.transliteration: ஓ, கலி யுகத்தின் அதிபரே, நான் உங்களில் அடைக்கலம் புகுகிறேன், கணேசாவே.
mantras.background: A Hindu refuge prayer to Ganesha, remover of obstacles, invoking his lordship over the Kali Yuga—our current cosmic age of strife—inviting practitioners to seek divine shelter.
mantras.benefits: Invoking Ganesha as Lord of this age offers removal of obstacles, clarity of mind, and grounded courage—gifts especially vital in turbulent times. This refuge-taking cultivates humility and focused intention.
mantras.tags: hindu, ganesha, devotional, refuge, prayer, invocation, kali-yuga

translations.es: Oh, Señor de la Era de Kali, busco refugio en ti, Ganesha.
translations.ar: أيها الربّ، يا ربّ العصر الكالي، ألجأ إليك، يا غانِشَة.
translations.bn: ওহে কళిযুগের প্রভু, আমি তোমার আশ্রয় গ্রহণ করছি, গণেশ।
translations.de: Oh, Herr der Kali-Yuga, ich suche bei dir Zuflucht, Ganesha.
translations.en-us: Oh, Lord of the Kali Yuga, I seek your refuge, Ganesha.
translations.en-gb: Oh, Lord of the Kali Yuga, I seek your refuge, Ganesha.
translations.fr-fr: Ô Seigneur de l'âge de Kali, je trouve refuge en vous, Ganesh.
translations.he: ה', אדון של יוגה קאלי, אני מוצא מפלט בך, גאנשה.
translations.hi: हे कलि युग के स्वामी, मैं आपके शरण में आता हूँ, गणेश।
translations.id: Oh, Penguasa Zaman Kali, aku berlindung pada-Mu, Ganesha.
translations.it: O Signore del Kali Yuga, mi rifugio in te, Ganesha.
translations.ja: カリユガの主よ、私はあなた、ガネーシャに帰依します。
translations.ko: 오, 칼리유가의 주인이시여, 저는 가네샤께 의지합니다.
translations.nl: O Heer van de Kali Yuga, ik zoek toevlucht bij U, Ganesha.
translations.pl: O Panu Kali Yugi, schronienie znajduję w Tobie, Ganesho.
translations.pt-br: Ó Senhor da Era de Kali, busco refúgio em você, Ganesha.
translations.ru: О, Владыка Кали Юги, я ищу убежище в тебе, Ганеша.
translations.sv: Oh, Herre över Kali-yugan, jag söker skydd hos dig, Ganesha.
translations.th: ข้าขอพึ่งพระพิฆเนศ เทพผู้ทรงพลังในยุคคติ (Kali Yuga)
translations.tr: Kali Yuga'nın Rabbı, ben sana sığınıyorum, Ganesha.
translations.uk: О, Господи епохи Калі-юги, я шукаю притулку у тебе, Ганешо.
translations.vi: ôi, Lãnh Chúa của Kỷ nguyên Kali, tôi tìm sự che chở nơi Ngài, Ganesha.
translations.zh-cn: 噢，卡利瑜伽时代的众神啊，我寻求您的庇护，甘尼萨。

mantras.name: Oh, the one who draws, Om, the one who sustains, the one who sustains, the great one, the one who sustains, the sound of liberation.
mantras.english: Oh, the one who draws, Om, the one who sustains, the one who sustains, the great one, the one who sustains, the sound of liberation.
mantras.original: 爹（diē）雅（yǎ）他（tuō） 唵（ōng） 贝（bèi）堪（kān）则（zé）也 贝（bèi）堪（kān）则（zé）也 玛（mā）哈（hā） 贝（bèi）堪（kān）则（zé）也 喇（là）杂（zá） 萨（sà）漠（mù） 嘎（gā）得（děi）梭（suō）哈（hā）
mantras.transliteration: oṃ, saḥ, yaḥ dṛśati, oṃ, saḥ, yaḥ rakṣati, yaḥ rakṣati, mahān, saḥ, yaḥ rakṣati, śabdaḥ, muktiś.
mantras.background: Shakyamuni Buddha's heart mantra from Buddhist tradition: "Om — the great sage, sage, sage — sound of liberation." Chanted to purify karma and awaken wisdom across millennia.
mantras.benefits: Chanting this mantra of the Great Sage is said to purify deep karmic obstacles, cultivate merit, and awaken insight into the nature of emptiness — drawing the mind toward stillness, clarity, and ultimate liberation.
mantras.tags: hindu, om, primordial, sound, liberation, meditation, vibration, mantra, divine, universal, buddhist, consciousness

translations.es: Oh, aquel que crea, Om, aquel que mantiene, aquel que sostiene, el grande, aquel que sostiene, el sonido de la liberación.
translations.ar: يا ذا الجلال، يا من يرسم، يا أوم، يا من يحافظ، يا من يحافظ، يا العظيم، يا من يحافظ، يا صوت التحرر.
translations.bn: ওহ, যিনি অঙ্কন করেন, ওম, যিনি সমর্থন করেন, যিনি সমর্থন করেন, মহান সত্তা, যিনি সমর্থন করেন, মুক্তির শব্দ।
translations.de: Oh, derjenige, der erschafft; Om, derjenige, der erhält; derjenige, der erhält; der Große; derjenige, der erhält; der Klang der Befreiung.
translations.en-us: Oh, the one who creates, Om, the one who maintains, the one who sustains, the great one, the one who sustains, the sound of liberation.
translations.en-gb: Oh, the one who creates, Om, the one who sustains, the one who sustains, the great one, the one who sustains, the sound of liberation.
translations.fr-fr: Ô celui qui crée, Om, celui qui maintient, celui qui maintient, le Grand, celui qui maintient, le son de la libération.
translations.he: הנה, מי שיוצר, אוֹם, מי שמשמר, מי שמשמר, העצום, מי שמשמר, צליל הניצחון.
translations.hi: वह जो सृजन करता है, ॐ, वह जो धारण करता है, वह जो धारण करता है, महान, वह जो धारण करता है, मुक्ति की ध्वनि।
translations.id: Oh, Engkaulah yang mencipta, Om, Engkaulah yang menopang, Engkaulah yang menopang, Yang Maha Agung, Engkaulah yang menopang, suara pembebasan.
translations.it: Oh, colui che crea, Om, colui che mantiene, colui che sostiene, il Grande, colui che sostiene, il suono della liberazione.
translations.ja: 描く者よ、オーム、支える者、支える者、偉大なる者、支える者、解放の音。
translations.ko: "오, 그림을 그리는 분이시여, 옴, 유지하시는 분이시여, 유지하시는 분이시여, 위대한 분이시여, 유지하시는 분이시여, 해방의 소리."
translations.nl: O, Hij die schept, Om, Hij die onderhoudt, Hij die onderhoudt, de Grote, Hij die onderhoudt, het geluid van bevrijding.
translations.pl: O, ten, który tworzy, Om, ten, który podtrzymuje, ten, który podtrzymuje, Wielki, ten, który podtrzymuje, dźwięk wyzwolenia.
translations.pt-br: Oh, aquele que manifesta, Om, aquele que sustenta, aquele que sustenta, o grandioso, aquele que sustenta, o som da libertação.
translations.ru: О, Тот, кто создает, Ом, Тот, кто поддерживает, Тот, кто поддерживает, Великий, Тот, кто поддерживает, звук освобождения.
translations.sv: Åh, den som skapar, Om, den som upprätthåller, den som upprätthåller, det stora, den som upprätthåller, befrielsens ljud.
translations.th: ผู้ที่สร้างสรรค์, โอม, ผู้ที่ดำรงอยู่, ผู้ที่ดำรงอยู่, องค์พระผู้ยิ่งใหญ่, ผู้ที่ดำรงอยู่, เสียงแห่งการหลุดพ้น
translations.tr: Çizeni, Om, koruyanı, koruyanı, yüce olanı, koruyanı, kurtuluşun sesi.
translations.uk: О, Творець, Ом, Підтримувач, Підтримувач, Великий, Підтримувач, Звук звільнення.
translations.vi: Ồ, đấng vẽ nên vạn vật, Om, đấng duy trì, đấng duy trì, đấng vĩ đại, đấng duy trì, âm thanh giải thoát.
translations.zh-cn:
梵语：哦，那位创造者，ॐ，那位维持者，那位维持者，伟大的存在，那位维持者，解脱之音。
中文翻译：啊，那位创造者，唵（Om），那位守护者，那位维持者，伟大的存在，那位守护者，是带来解脱的声音。

mantras.name: Om
mantras.english: The primordial sound — the vibration from which all creation arises
mantras.transliteration: Om / Aum
mantras.abstract:
Om is the most fundamental of all mantras — the primordial sound that the Vedic tradition identifies as the vibrational substrate of all existence. Before any other mantra, any other prayer, any other sacred act, there is Om. It is described in the Mandukya Upanishad as comprising the entirety of time: A (the waking state), U (the dream state), M (the deep sleep state), and the silence that follows — representing turiya, the fourth transcendent state that underlies and encompasses the other three.

The cosmological significance of Om extends across every school of Hindu philosophy. In the Yoga tradition, Om is the Pranava — the vibration that both creates and sustains the universe. The Taittiriya Upanishad opens with Om as the bridge between the finite and the infinite: 'Om — this syllable is all this.' In Vedanta, Om is equated with Brahman — the absolute reality — and its three letters with the three aspects of the divine (Brahma/creation, Vishnu/preservation, Shiva/dissolution).

The physical and physiological effects of chanting Om have been extensively documented. The sustained vowel sounds resonate through the chest, throat, and skull, inducing a relaxation response in the autonomic nervous system. Research at the National Brain Research Centre in India found that Om chanting produces measurable changes in brain wave activity, particularly in the theta range associated with deep meditative states. The concluding nasal hum (the 'm') produces vibration in the frontal sinuses and palate associated with vagal nerve stimulation.

As a practice, Om is used both as a standalone meditation and as the opening and closing of other mantras and prayers. Simply sitting and sustaining the sound of Om — feeling the A in the belly, the U in the chest and throat, the M in the skull — for several minutes constitutes a complete and profound meditation. The silence at the end of each Om is as important as the sound itself; teachers say that the silence is where Om's deepest gift lives.

In contemporary yoga and meditation practice worldwide, Om has become possibly the most recognized spiritual sound on earth. Its universality speaks to something fundamental in the human nervous system's relationship to sustained vocal resonance. Countless practitioners from widely different spiritual backgrounds describe the same experience: a few rounds of Om, chanted slowly and mindfully, produce an immediate, unmistakable shift in consciousness toward stillness and openness.

translations.en: The primordial sound of the universe
translations.zh: 宇宙的原初之声
translations.es: El sonido primordial del universo
translations.ar: ॐ. يُمثّل الحقيقة المطلقة، أي "برهمان"، والاهتزاز الكوني. إنه الصوت الأولي الذي انبثقت منه كل الخليقة.
translations.bn: ॐ। এটি পরম বাস্তবতা, ব্রহ্ম এবং মহাবিশ্বের কম্পন का प्रतिनिधित्व করে। এটি আদিম শব্দ যা থেকে সকল সৃষ্টি उत्पन्न हुआ।
translations.de: Om. Es repräsentiert die ultimative Realität, Brahman, und die Vibration des Universums. Es ist der ursprüngliche Klang, aus dem alles entstanden ist.
translations.en-us: "Om" symbolizes the ultimate reality, Brahman, and the vibrational essence of the universe. It is the primordial sound from which all creation originated.
translations.en-gb: "Om. It embodies the ultimate reality, Brahman, and represents the vibrational essence of the universe. It is the primordial sound from which all creation originated."
translations.fr-fr: Om. Il représente la réalité ultime, Brahman, et la vibration de l'univers. C'est le son primordial d'où toute la création a émané.
translations.he: אום. הוא מייצג את המציאות האולטימטיבית, את ברהמן, ואת הרטט של היקום. הוא הצליל הקדמון שממנו בקע כל הבריאה.
translations.hi: ॐ। यह परम वास्तविकता, ब्रह्म, और ब्रह्मांड की कंपन का प्रतिनिधित्व करता है। यह आदिम ध्वनि है जिससे सभी सृजन उत्पन्न हुए।
translations.id: Om. Ia mewakili realitas tertinggi, Brahman, dan getaran semesta. Ia adalah suara primordial yang menjadi asal mula segala ciptaan.
translations.it: Om. Esso rappresenta la realtà ultima, il Brahman, e la vibrazione dell'universo. È il suono primordiale da cui è emersa tutta la creazione.
translations.ja: オームは、究極の現実であるブラフマン、そして宇宙の振動を表します。それは、すべての創造が発源した最初の音です。
translations.ko: 옴(Om). 이것은 궁극적인 실재, 즉 브라흐만(Brahman)과 우주의 진동을 상징합니다. 모든 창조가 비롯된 원시적인 소리입니다.
translations.nl: "Om. Het symboliseert de ultieme realiteit, Brahman, en de vibratie van het universum. Het is het oerklank waaruit alle schepping voortkwam."
translations.pl: Om. Symbolizuje ostateczną rzeczywistość, Brahman, oraz wibrację wszechświata. Jest to pierwotny dźwięk, z którego wywodzi się całe stworzenie.
translations.pt-br: Om. Ele representa a realidade última, Brahman, e a vibração do universo. É o som primordial do qual toda a criação emanou.
translations.ru: Ом. Он представляет собой абсолютную реальность, Брахман, и вибрацию Вселенной. Это изначальный звук, из которого возникло всё творение.
translations.sv: Om. Denna fras representerar den ultimata verkligheten, Brahman, och universums vibration. Det är det primära ljudet från vilket all skapelse utgick.
translations.th: โอม (Om). มันเป็นตัวแทนของสัจจะสูงสุด, บราหมัน (Brahman), และการสั่นสะเทือนของจักรวาล. มันคือเสียงเริ่มต้นที่ซึ่งทุกสรรพสิ่งถือกำเนิดขึ้น.
translations.tr: Om. Bu, mutlak gerçekliği, Brahman'ı ve evrenin titreşimini temsil eder. Bütün yaratılışın kaynaklandığı ilkel sestir.
translations.uk: Ом. Він представляє собою абсолютну реальність, Брахман, і вібрацію Всесвіту. Це первинний звук, з якого виникло все творіння.
translations.vi: "Om. Âm thanh này biểu thị thực tại tối thượng, Brahman, và sự rung động của vũ trụ. Nó là âm thanh nguyên thủy từ đó mọi sự sáng tạo bắt nguồn."
translations.zh-cn: 唵 (Ǎn). 它代表着终极实在，梵 (Fàn)，以及宇宙的震动。它是最初的音，万物由此而生。

mantras.background:
From ancient Vedic Sanskrit, Om (ॐ) is Hinduism's primordial sound—the vibration from which all creation arises, encompassing A (waking), U (dreaming), M (deep sleep).
  Grounding: Sources confirm Om originates in Vedic tradition (3000+ years), equated with Brahman, and its three letters represent three states of consciousness per the Mandukya Upanishad.
mantras.benefits:
Om—the universe's first sound—unites Atman with Brahman, dissolving ego boundaries. Chanting its A-U-M resonates through body and skull, inducing theta brainwaves and vagal calm, bridging ancient Vedic wisdom with measurable inner stillness.
  Grounding: Sources cite Mandukya Upanishad's A-U-M states, National Brain Research Centre theta findings, and vagal nerve stimulation from the nasal hum.

mantras.name: Om Aiṃ Sarasvatyai Namaḥ.
mantras.english: Om Aiṃ Sarasvatyai Namaḥ.
mantras.transliteration: ōṃ aiṃ sarasvatyai namaḥ.
mantras.background: From Hindu tradition, "Om Aiṃ Sarasvatyai Namaḥ" is a bīja (seed) mantra honoring Saraswati, goddess of knowledge. Aiṃ embodies the power of wisdom; Namaḥ means "I bow."
mantras.benefits: "Aiṃ" (ঐং) is Saraswati's bija — seed-sound of knowledge. Regular japa is traditionally said to sharpen memory, focus, and creativity, making it beloved by students and artists seeking clarity of mind.
mantras.tags: hindu, saraswati, devotion, knowledge, learning, focus, memory, concentration, creativity, wisdom, confidence

translations.es: Om, a Sarasvatí, reverencia.
translations.ar: "أوم، يا ساراسواتي، سلام عليكِ."
translations.bn: ওং, আমি सरस्वতীর উদ্দেশ্যে নমস্কার জানাই।
translations.de: Om, dem heiligen Laut, Sarasvatī, der Göttin der Weisheit und Künste, sei mein Lob geboten.
translations.en-us: Obeisance to Sarasvati.
translations.en-gb: Salutations to Sarasvati, the goddess of knowledge and wisdom.
translations.fr-fr: Ôm, je rends hommage à Sarasvatī.
translations.he: אום, אני משתחווה לסראסווטי.
translations.hi: ॐ ऐं सरस्वती को नमस्कार।
translations.id: Om, saya menyembah kepada Dewi Saraswati.
translations.it: Om, a Sarasvatī, io rendo omaggio.
translations.ja: オーム、アイム、サラスヴァティーへ、我は礼します。
translations.ko: 옴 아이  Sarasvatyai 나마. (Om Aiṃ Sarasvatyai namah.)
translations.nl: Ik eer Sarasvati, de godin van wijsheid en kennis.
translations.pl: Om. Składam hołd Saraswati.
translations.pt-br: Óm, eu me prostro a Sarasvatī.
translations.ru: Ом. Ей, Сарасвати, я возношу хвалу.
translations.sv: Om, jag böjer mig för Sarasvati.
translations.th: ข้าขอบูชาแด่พระศารทวี
translations.tr: Om, Sarasvati'ya (Sarasvati'ya) hitaben (hitaben), övgüler olsun (övgüler olsun).
translations.uk: Ом. Моєму поклоненню Сарасваті.
translations.vi: Ôm. Con kính cẩn gọi xin vâng lời, Nữ thần Sarasvati.
translations.zh-cn: ōm, 咏唱献给湿婆女神的赞美。

mantras.name: Om Gam Ganapataye Namaha
mantras.english: Om, salutations to Ganesha, the remover of obstacles
mantras.transliteration: Om Gam Ganapataye Namaha
mantras.abstract:
Om Gam Ganapataye Namaha is the primary mantra dedicated to Ganesha, the elephant-headed deity who is the lord of beginnings, the remover of obstacles (Vighnaharta), and the patron of arts, science, and wisdom. The mantra is drawn from the Ganapati Atharvasirsha, a late Upanishadic text of the Atharva Veda that presents Ganesha as the supreme reality underlying all existence. Ganesha is invariably invoked first in any Hindu ritual or undertaking, before all other deities.

The seed syllable Gam is the bija mantra of Ganesha — a single syllable encoding the entire energy and essence of the deity in its most concentrated form. In Tantric understanding, bija mantras are not symbolic references to a deity but actual sonic embodiments of their power. The syllable Gam is said to resonate at the frequency of the Muladhara chakra at the base of the spine, the energy center associated with earth, stability, grounding, and the removal of fear.

Ganapataye is a Sanskrit dative form meaning for Ganapati or to Ganapati. Ganapati is one of the principal names of Ganesha: Gana means a troop or assembly, and Pati means lord or master. Ganesha is thus the master of the ganas, and by extension the lord of all categories, groups, and principles of organization.

Ganesha worship has ancient Vedic roots and the festival of Ganesh Chaturthi, celebrated across India and particularly in Maharashtra, centers on this mantra. The mantra is chanted at the opening of business ventures, marriages, construction projects, and any new creative or spiritual endeavour.

translations.en: Om. I invoke the seed energy of Ganesha. Salutations to the lord of all beginnings and remover of obstacles, who grants wisdom, success, and auspicious starts.
translations.zh: 嗡。我召唤象神伽内什的种子能量。向一切开始的主宰和障碍的移除者致敬，他赐予智慧、成功和吉祥的开端。
translations.es: Om. Invoco la energía semilla de Ganesha. Salutaciones al señor de todos los comienzos y removedor de obstáculos, que otorga sabiduría, éxito y comienzos auspiciosos.

mantras.name: Om Hreem Shreem Kleem Namaha
mantras.english: Invocation of divine manifestation, abundance, and desire through the seed syllables
mantras.transliteration: Om Hreem Shreem Kleem Namaha
mantras.abstract:
Om Hreem Shreem Kleem Namaha is a powerful Tantric mantra combining three of the most important bija (seed) syllables in the Shakta tradition. Each bija syllable is considered a concentrated vibrational encoding of a specific divine energy: Om is the universal sound of Brahman; Hreem is the bija of Maya and divine manifestation, associated with Bhuvanesvari and the creative power of the cosmic mother; Shreem is the bija of Lakshmi, the goddess of prosperity, abundance, and auspiciousness; and Kleem is the bija of Kama and attraction, associated with Krishna and the magnetic power of love and desire.

In Tantric theory, bija mantras are the vibrational seeds from which the full energies of deities grow. They are not descriptions of divine qualities but direct encodings of those qualities in sound. Chanting Hreem is, in this understanding, not merely calling on the divine mother but actually resonating with the specific frequency of her creative power. The combination of Hreem, Shreem, and Kleem therefore invokes three complementary dimensions of the divine feminine: the power to create and manifest (Hreem), the power to attract abundance and beauty (Shreem), and the power of love and magnetic attraction (Kleem).

The mantra is used in multiple Tantric traditions for different purposes. As a prosperity practice, the emphasis is on Shreem — the Lakshmi energy that attracts material and spiritual abundance. As a manifestation practice, the emphasis is on Hreem — the creative power that brings intentions into form. As a devotional or relationship practice, the emphasis is on Kleem — the magnetic power that draws divine grace and human connection. The combined mantra addresses all three simultaneously.

Traditional teachers prescribe initiatory guidance for working with Tantric bija mantras, as their energies are considered powerful and specific. Nevertheless, the mantra has been widely transmitted in accessible yoga and meditation contexts and is used by practitioners worldwide. Many report that regular practice produces increased synchronicity — a sense of life becoming more responsive to intention — alongside a more tangible experience of abundance consciousness.

For meditation, Om Hreem Shreem Kleem Namaha is typically recited 108 times with a mala, with each repetition accompanied by a specific intention held lightly in awareness. The practice is often combined with visualization of the golden light of Lakshmi or the brilliant white light of the cosmic mother. Teachers emphasize that the mantra's power depends not on the visualization but on the quality of attention — sincere, present, and free from desperate craving.

translations.en: Om, divine manifestation, abundance, desire — I bow
translations.zh: 嗡，神圣显化，丰盛，愿望——礼敬
translations.es: Om, manifestación divina, abundancia, deseo — me inclino

mantras.name: Om Mayura, a vehicle of transformation, awakening.
mantras.english: Om Mayura, a vehicle of transformation, awakening.
mantras.original: おん まゆら きしゃ ていえい そわか
mantras.transliteration: Ōm Mayūra, vehikuru no henkaku, kakusei.
mantras.background: From ancient Vedic tradition, Om (ॐ) is the primordial sound of all creation — chanted before every mantra, prayer, and sacred act since time immemorial.
mantras.benefits: Chanting Om shifts brainwaves into deep meditative theta states, stimulates the vagus nerve, and quiets the mind — one syllable bridging waking life and pure consciousness, a complete universe in a single breath.
mantras.tags: hindu, vedic, yoga, pranava, brahman, chanting, sound, consciousness, transformation, awakening, upanishad

translations.es: Om, Mayura, vehículo de transformación y despertar.
translations.ar: يا "اوم" مايورا، يا وسيلة التحول واليقظة.
translations.bn: ওম, হে ময়ুরা, তুমি परिवर्तনের বাহন, তুমি জাগরণ।
translations.de: Om Mayūra, ein Vehikel der Transformation, das Erwachen.
translations.en-us: Om, White Buffalo, a vehicle of transformation, awakening.
translations.en-gb: Om Mayura, a vehicle of transformation, awakening.
translations.fr-fr: Om, Mayūra, véhicule de transformation, éveil.
translations.he: אוֹם, מַיּוּרָה, כלי של טרנספורמציה, התעוררות.
translations.hi: ॐ, हे सर्वशक्तिमान 'मयूर', जो परिवर्तन और जागृति का प्रतीक हैं।
translations.id: Om Mayura, sebuah wahana transformasi, pembangkitan.
translations.it: Om Mayūra, veicolo di trasformazione, risveglio.
translations.ja: オーム、変化の乗り物、覚醒の象。
translations.ko: 옴 마유라, 변화의 수단, 깨달음. (Om Mayura, byeonhwa-ui sudan, kkaedalum.)
translations.nl: Om Mayura, een voertuig van transformatie, ontwaking.
translations.pl: Om, Mayura, pojazd transformacji, przebudzenia.
translations.pt-br: Om, Mãe Iara, veículo de transformação, despertar.
translations.ru: Ом Майура, средство трансформации, пробуждение.
translations.sv: Om Mayura, ett fordon för förvandling, en väckare.
translations.th: อม มยุรา, พาหนะแห่งการเปลี่ยนแปลง, การตื่นรู้
translations.tr: Om Mayura, dönüşümün bir aracı, uyanış.
translations.uk: Ом Майура, транспорт перетворення, пробудження.
translations.vi: Om Mayura, phương tiện của sự chuyển đổi, sự thức tỉnh.
translations.zh-cn: 嗡 妙音，变革的载体，觉醒的象征。

mantras.name: Om Namah Shivaya
mantras.english: I bow to Shiva, the auspicious one who is the true self
mantras.transliteration: Om Namah Shivaya
mantras.abstract:
Om Namah Shivaya is among the most widely chanted mantras in the Shaiva tradition of Hinduism, appearing first in the Krishna Yajurveda's Shri Rudram. It is revered as the Panchakshara or five-syllable mantra, referring to the five sacred syllables Na-Ma-Shi-Va-Ya, each of which corresponds to one of the five elements that compose the universe and the body.

The five syllables map directly onto the Panchamahabhutas: Na corresponds to earth (Prithvi), Ma to water (Jala), Shi to fire (Agni), Va to air (Vayu), and Ya to ether or space (Akasha). By chanting this mantra, the practitioner is understood to purify and balance these five elements within themselves, restoring harmony between the individual body and the cosmic order.

The word Shiva itself means auspiciousness, and in the non-dual Shaiva tradition — particularly Kashmir Shaivism — Shiva is not merely a god but the universal consciousness that underlies all existence. The mantra is therefore not only an act of devotion to a deity but an affirmation of the practitioner's own deepest nature. Namah means both salutation and surrender; it implies the dissolution of ego as one bows to the truth of one's own being.

The mantra was popularized in the modern era by the Shaiva saint Thirugnana Sambandhar and is prominently featured in the Tamil Shaiva canon, the Thevaram. The sage Vasishtha declared it the greatest of all mantras in the Shiva Purana. Its six syllables including Om resonate across all sects of Hinduism, and it remains a living practice chanted during Shivaratri, daily puja, and meditative retreats worldwide.

translations.en: Om. I bow in reverence to the auspicious Lord Shiva, the supreme consciousness who dwells within as the true self.
translations.zh: 嗡。我向吉祥的湿婆神致敬，那存在于内心深处的至高意识，即真正的自我。
translations.es: Om. Me inclino con reverencia ante el auspicioso Señor Shiva, la conciencia suprema que mora en el interior como el verdadero ser.

mantras.name: Om Namah Shivaya Gurave
mantras.english: Om, I bow to Shiva the divine teacher who is sat-chit-ananda
mantras.original: ॐ नमः शिवाय गुरवे सच्चिदानन्दमूर्तये। निष्प्रपञ्चाय शान्ताय श्रीनिधये नमो नमः।
mantras.transliteration: Om Namah Shivaya Gurave Sacchidananda Murtaye, Nishprapanchaya Shantaya Shrinidhaye Namo Namah
mantras.abstract:
Om Namah Shivaya Gurave is the invocation mantra of the Anusara Yoga tradition and a deeply revered hymn in the broader Shaiva Tantra lineage. It presents the divine teacher (Guru) as an embodiment of the three qualities of Brahman — Sat (being), Chit (consciousness), and Ananda (bliss) — which together describe the essential nature of ultimate reality according to Advaita and Shaiva philosophy.

The word Gurave (to the Guru or teacher) is key to understanding this mantra's distinctive contribution to the Shaiva devotional canon. In the Guru-disciple tradition that runs through all schools of Indian philosophy, the Guru is not merely a human instructor but a living transmission of the divine. The Kularnava Tantra, the Guru Gita, and the Shiva Sutras all affirm that the Guru is Shiva in human form.

Nishprapanchaya means beyond the phenomenal world or transcendent of all manifestation. Shantaya means the peaceful, the tranquil, pointing to Shiva's quality of supreme equanimity. Shrinidhaye means the abode or treasure house of Shri — the divine beauty, grace, and auspiciousness that radiates from the awakened being. The concluding Namo Namah is a double salutation expressing profound reverence and complete self-surrender.

The mantra is traditionally chanted three times at the beginning of a Shaiva or Anusara yoga practice as an act of orientation and dedication. It serves to remind the practitioner that the ultimate purpose of all practice is the recognition of the divine grace that is already present within.

translations.en: Om. I bow to Shiva who appears as the Guru, whose form is being-consciousness-bliss, who is beyond the phenomenal world, who is utterly peaceful, and who is the abode of divine beauty and grace.
translations.zh: 嗡。我向以上师形象显现的湿婆神致敬，他的形态是存在-意识-喜乐，他超越现象世界，他完全安宁，他是神圣美丽与恩典的住所。
translations.es: Om. Me inclino ante Shiva que aparece como el Guru, cuya forma es ser-conciencia-bienaventuranza, que está más allá del mundo fenoménico, que es completamente pacífico, y que es la morada de la belleza y la gracia divinas.

mantras.name: Om Namo Bhagavate Vasudevaya
mantras.english: Om, I bow to Lord Vasudeva (Krishna), the all-pervading divine being
mantras.transliteration: Om Namo Bhagavate Vasudevaya
mantras.abstract:
Om Namo Bhagavate Vasudevaya is the principal mantra of the Vaishnavite tradition and is classified as a Dvadasakshara or twelve-syllable mantra. It appears prominently in the Vishnu Purana, the Bhagavata Purana, and is associated with the Naradiya school of devotional practice. The mantra is a salutation to Vasudeva, a name for Krishna that means son of Vasudeva and also, etymologically, the one who dwells in all beings and in whom all beings dwell.

Bhagavan is a comprehensive term denoting the divine being who possesses all six supreme qualities in fullness: infinite knowledge, lordship, power, strength, virility, and splendour. The mantra is therefore not just a personal devotional address to Krishna but a recognition of the universal lordship of the divine in its most complete and perfect expression.

In the Bhagavata Purana, the sage Narada teaches this mantra to Dhruva, a young prince who seeks divine vision and ultimately attains liberation through unwavering devotion. The story of Dhruva is one of the most celebrated accounts of the mantra's efficacy: through its repetition in deep forest meditation, he achieves the pole-star station, a symbol of eternal, fixed consciousness.

The mantra is chanted in Vaishnava temples as part of the Pancharatra ritual system, during the recitation of the Vishnu Sahasranama, and in domestic puja. It forms the foundational practice of the Bhakti Yoga path as articulated in the Bhagavata Purana, where pure devotion without any ulterior motive is presented as the highest path to liberation.

translations.en: Om. I bow to the all-pervading Lord Vasudeva-Krishna, the supreme divine being who is the source and ground of all existence.
translations.zh: 嗡。我向无处不在的瓦苏德瓦-克里希纳神致敬，他是至高神圣的存在，是一切存在的源头和基础。
translations.es: Om. Me inclino ante el omnipresente Señor Vasudeva-Krishna, el ser divino supremo que es la fuente y el fundamento de toda existencia.

mantras.name: Om OM OM, Earth, Atmosphere, Heaven, We meditate on the glorious, divine light of Savitur, May that enlightenment inspire our intellect.
mantras.english: Om OM OM, Earth, Atmosphere, Heaven, We meditate on the glorious, divine light of Savitur, May that enlightenment inspire our intellect.
mantras.original: OM OM OM, Bhud Bhuvah Svah, Tat Savituh Varenyam, Bharag Devasya Dhimahi, Dhiyah Yah Nah Drachodayat
mantras.transliteration: ॐ ॐ ॐ, पृथिवि, वायु, द्याव, सवितुः देवेभ्यः धीमहि, धियो यो नः प्रचोदयात्।
mantras.background:
The Gayatri Mantra, from Vedic Hindu tradition, invokes Savitur's divine light: "Om, Earth, Atmosphere, Heaven — may that radiance inspire our intellect." A timeless prayer for enlightenment.

  Grounding: Greek source names it "Μάντρα Gayatri," gives its Sanskrit text and translation invoking divine light/intellect. Existing entry confirms Om as Vedic primordial sound foundation.
mantras.benefits: The Gayatri Mantra invokes divine light to awaken intellect and inner wisdom. Chanting it is said to inspire higher thought, balance mental energy, and connect the practitioner to cosmic creative forces — a luminous path to transformation.
mantras.tags: hindu, vedic, gayatri, savitur, prayer, devotional, wisdom, illumination, enlightenment

translations.es: Om, Om, Om. Tierra, atmósfera, cielo. Meditamos en la gloriosa luz divina de Savitur. Que esa iluminación inspire nuestra inteligencia.
translations.ar: "أوم، أوم، أوم، أيها الأرض، وأيها الغلاف الجوي، وأيها السماء، نتأمل في النور المجيد والإلهي لسافيتار، فليُلهم هذا التنوير عقولنا."
translations.bn: ওম ওম ওম, পৃথিবী, বায়ুমণ্ডল, আকাশ, আমরা সেই glorious, ঐশ্বরিক আলো যা সাভিতুর (Savitur) নামে পরিচিত, তার উপর ধ্যান করি। সেই আলোক আমাদের বুদ্ধিকে আলোকিত করুক।
translations.de: Om, Om, Om. Erde, Atmosphäre, Himmel. Wir meditieren auf das glorreiche, göttliche Licht des Savitur. Möge diese Erleuchtung unseren Verstand erleuchten.
translations.en-us: "Om, Om, Om. We meditate on the glorious, divine light of Savitur, the sustainer of the Earth, Atmosphere, and Heaven. May that enlightenment inspire our intellect."
translations.en-gb: "Om, Om, Om. We contemplate the Earth, the Atmosphere, and the Heavens; we meditate on the glorious, divine light of Savitur, and may that enlightenment inspire our intellect."
translations.fr-fr: Om, Om, Om. Terre, atmosphère, ciel, nous méditons sur la glorieuse lumière divine de Savitur. Que cette illumination inspire notre intellect.
translations.he: אום אום אום, ארץ, אטמוספירה, שמיים, אנו מתמלאים בהרהורים על האור המפואר והאלוהי של סוויטור, ייתן אור זה השראה לחוכמה שלנו.
translations.hi: ॐ ॐ ॐ, पृथ्वी, वायु, आकाश, हम उस प्रकाशमय, दिव्य सत्यदेव की महिमा का ध्यान करते हैं, हे सत्यदेव, आपकी वह प्रेरणाशक्ति हमारे मन को प्रकाशित कर।
translations.id: Om, Om, Om, Bumi, Atmosfer, Langit, Kami merenungkan cahaya kemuliaan dan keilahian Savitur. Semoga pencerahan itu menginspirasi akal budi kami.
translations.it: Om, Om, Om. Terra, atmosfera, cielo. Meditiamo sulla gloriosa luce divina di Savitur. Possa quella illuminazione ispirare la nostra intelletto.
translations.ja: オーム オーム オーム。我々は、大地の、大気の、天の、栄光に満ちた、神聖なサヴィトル（Savitur）の光を瞑想します。その啓蒙が、我々の知性を照らしますように。
translations.ko: 옴 옴 옴, 우리는 지구, 대기, 천계를 명상하며, 찬란하고 신성한 사비투르의 빛을 묵상합니다. 그 깨달음이 우리의 지성을 밝히기를 바랍니다.
translations.nl: Om, Om, Om, Aarde, Atmosfeer, Hemel, Wij mediteren op het glorieuze, goddelijke licht van Savitur. Moge die verlichting onze intelligentie inspireren.
translations.pl: Om, Om, Om. O Ziemio, O Atmosfero, O Niebo, Medytujemy nad chwalebnym, boskim światłem Savitara. Niech to oświecenie zainspiruje nasz intelekt.
translations.pt-br: Om, Om, Om. Terra, atmosfera, céu. Meditamos na gloriosa luz divina de Savitur. Que essa iluminação inspire nosso intelecto.
translations.ru: Ом Ом Ом, Земля, Атмосфера, Небо, Мы созерцаем славный, божественный свет Савитра, да пребудет это просветление источником вдохновения для нашего интеллекта.
translations.sv: Om, Om, Om. Vi mediterar över det strålande, gudomliga ljuset från Savitur. Må denna upplysning inspirera vårt intellekt. Jord, atmosfär, himmel.
translations.th: โอ้ ม โอ้ ม, โลก, ชั้นบรรยากาศ, สวรรค์, เราใคร่ครวญถึงแสงสว่างอันรุ่งโรจน์และศักดิ์สิทธิ์ของพระอาทิตย์ (Savitur), ขอให้แสงสว่างนั้นจุดประกายปัญญาของเรา
translations.tr: Om, Om, Om. Ey Dünya, Ey Atmosfer, Ey Gökyüzü, Savitur'un yüce, ilahi ışığının ihtişamına medet ararız. Bu aydınlanma, zihnimizi yönlendirsin.
translations.uk: Ом Ом Ом, Земля, Атмосфера, Небо, Ми розмірковуємо про славетне, божественне світло Савітра, нехай це просвітлення надихне наш розум.
translations.vi: Om, Om, Om, Chúng tôi chiêm nghiệm ánh sáng huy hoàng, thần thánh của Savitur, xin ánh sáng đó soi sáng trí tuệ của chúng ta. Đất, không khí, thiên đàng.
translations.zh-cn: 唵 唵 唵，我们冥想宇宙的辉煌、神圣之光，那是太阳神（Savitur）的启迪，愿其智慧照耀我们的心智。

mantras.name: Om Purnamadah Purnamidam
mantras.english: That is whole, this is whole; from wholeness comes wholeness, and wholeness remains
mantras.original: ॐ पूर्णमदः पूर्णमिदम् पूर्णात्पूर्णमुदच्यते। पूर्णस्य पूर्णमादाय पूर्णमेवावशिष्यते।
mantras.transliteration: Om Purnamadah Purnamidam Purnat Purnamudachyate, Purnasya Purnamadaya Purnamevavashishyate
mantras.abstract:
The Purnamadah Mantra is the Shanti Patha of both the Brihadaranyaka Upanishad and the Isha Upanishad, and it stands as one of the most mathematically and spiritually elegant statements in all of Vedic literature. The mantra encapsulates the Upanishadic teaching on the infinite nature of Brahman by asserting that infinity subtracted from infinity still leaves infinity.

The word Purna is derived from the root Prna meaning to fill completely, and it is often translated as whole, full, or complete. In the Upanishadic context, however, Purna is not merely a relative completeness — it is the absolute fullness of Brahman that cannot be diminished, increased, or affected in any way. Adah (That) is Purna. Idam (This, referring to the manifest universe) is also Purna.

The second line explains the logical consequence: from Purna (the whole, the absolute), Purna (the manifest world) emerges. The universe is not a diminishment or a fragment of Brahman; it is the whole expressing itself as the whole. This is the teaching of Vivartavada — like the ocean and its waves, where the ocean does not become less oceanic by producing waves.

The third and final line is the most paradoxical: if you take Purna from Purna, what remains is still Purna. The mantra is used as a morning invocation, a pre-study blessing, and a meditation on the nature of abundance and wholeness. Contemplating it helps dissolve the sense of lack, incompleteness, or fragmentation that underlies much human suffering.

translations.en: Om. That infinite reality is whole. This manifest universe is whole. From that wholeness, this wholeness comes forth. Even when wholeness is taken from wholeness, wholeness alone remains.
translations.zh: 嗡。那无限的实在是完整的。这个显现的宇宙也是完整的。从那完整中，涌现出这个完整。即使从完整中取走完整，留下的仍然是完整。
translations.es: Om. Esa realidad infinita es completa. Este universo manifiesto es completo. De esa plenitud surge esta plenitud. Incluso cuando se toma la plenitud de la plenitud, solo queda la plenitud.

mantras.name: Om Saha Naavavatu
mantras.english: May we be protected together; may we be nourished together; may we work with great vigor together
mantras.original: ॐ सह नाववतु । सह नौ भुनक्तु । सह वीर्यं करवावहै । तेजस्वि नावधीतमस्तु मा विद्विषावहै । ॐ शान्तिः शान्तिः शान्तिः ॥
mantras.transliteration: Om Saha Naavavatu, Saha Nau Bhunaktu, Saha Veeryam Karavaavahai, Tejasvi Naavadheetamastu Maa Vidvishaavahai, Om Shantih Shantih Shantih
mantras.abstract:
Om Saha Naavavatu is the peace invocation (shanti patha) of the Taittiriya Upanishad and the Katha Upanishad, traditionally recited before a period of Vedic study to consecrate the relationship between teacher and student. Unlike the simple three-fold peace chant, this prayer is explicitly relational — it prays for the protection, nourishment, and vigorous effort of both teacher and student together, acknowledging that genuine learning is a shared endeavor that requires divine blessing on both parties.

The word 'saha' (together/jointly) appears three times in the opening three lines, emphasizing the communal nature of Vedic transmission. Knowledge in the Vedic tradition is not merely information transferred from a knower to a student but a living current that flows between two people whose relationship has been properly consecrated. The shanti patha creates this consecration — it invokes divine protection around the space of learning and around the relationship itself.

The petition for 'tejasvi' — brilliance or radiance — in the study ('may our study be luminous') reflects the Vedic understanding that sacred learning has a quality distinct from intellectual acquisition. When both teacher and student are properly prepared and the learning space is properly consecrated, the texts come alive in a way they cannot when approached merely as information. The luminosity invoked is the quality of realization rather than mere comprehension.

The final prayer 'Ma vidvishaavahai' — may we not dispute or quarrel with one another — is a built-in protection against the ego dynamics that often arise in teacher-student relationships: the student's resistance to being challenged, the teacher's defensiveness when questioned, the misunderstanding born of different levels of comprehension. By beginning with this prayer, both parties acknowledge the human tendency toward conflict and ask for divine help in transcending it.

The three-fold repetition of Shantih at the close invokes peace on three levels: the physical (Adhibhautika), the divine/supernatural (Adhidaivika), and the internal/mental (Adhyatmika). Any significant undertaking faces potential obstacles from all three levels, and the triple peace invocation addresses all three simultaneously, establishing the study session within a field of comprehensive divine protection.

translations.en: May we be protected and nourished together; may our study be luminous; may we not dispute with one another; Om Peace, Peace, Peace
translations.zh: 愿我们共同得到保护和滋养；愿我们的学习光辉灿烂；愿我们互不争论；嗡，平静，平静，平静
translations.es: Que seamos protegidos y nutridos juntos; que nuestro estudio sea luminoso; que no disputemos entre nosotros; Om Paz, Paz, Paz

mantras.name: Om Sarveshaam Svastir Bhavatu
mantras.english: May there be prosperity, peace, fullness, and auspiciousness in all things
mantras.original: ॐ सर्वेशां स्वस्तिर्भवतु । सर्वेशां शान्तिर्भवतु । सर्वेशां पूर्णम्भवतु । सर्वेशां मङ्गलम्भवतु ।
mantras.transliteration: Om Sarveshaam Svastir Bhavatu, Sarveshaam Shaantir Bhavatu, Sarveshaam Purnam Bhavatu, Sarveshaam Mangalam Bhavatu, Om Shaanti Shaanti Shaantih
mantras.abstract:
Om Sarveshaam Svastir Bhavatu is a Vedic peace prayer invoking welfare (svasti), peace (shanti), fullness (purnam), and auspiciousness (mangalam) for all beings — 'sarveshaam' literally means 'of all' or 'for all.' Like the Lokah Samastah prayer, this mantra expands the circle of blessing beyond the personal to encompass all of existence, but does so through four specific qualities that together constitute a comprehensive vision of flourishing life.

Each of the four qualities deserves contemplation: 'Svasti' (well-being/prosperity) comes from 'su' (good/well) and 'asti' (it is) — literally 'may it be well.' It is the same root as the ancient swastika symbol, before its misappropriation, which represented the auspicious turning of the cosmos. 'Shanti' (peace) is the deep, abiding stillness that underlies all activity. 'Purnam' (fullness/completeness) invokes the state described in the Isha Upanishad's great declaration: that completeness taken from completeness leaves completeness unchanged. 'Mangalam' (auspiciousness/blessing) is the quality that makes beginnings promising and transitions smooth.

The prayer is structured as four parallel petitions, each beginning with 'Sarveshaam' (for all), creating a rhythmic wave of blessing. This parallel structure is characteristic of Vedic prayer composition, where repetition with variation is used to deepen both the meaning and the vibratory impact of the words. The fourfold request also mirrors the Vedic understanding that complete welfare requires all four of these qualities — prosperity without peace is exhausting, peace without fullness is resignation, fullness without auspiciousness lacks joy.

In temple worship and family ceremonies, Om Sarveshaam Svastir Bhavatu is recited as a concluding blessing that extends the merit of any ritual outward to encompass all beings. This act of expansion prevents ritual from becoming self-serving and maintains its connection to the Vedic ideal of yajna — sacred action performed for the welfare of all. When individual practice ends with this prayer, the practitioner participates in the ancient tradition of offering their spiritual work as a gift to the world.

As a personal daily practice, reciting Om Sarveshaam Svastir Bhavatu first thing in the morning sets a tone of abundance-consciousness and compassion for the day. The prayer's four qualities — welfare, peace, fullness, and auspiciousness — become intentions that subtly shape how the practitioner moves through their day: with a wish for these qualities in every interaction, the quality of attention itself shifts from self-protection to blessing.

translations.en: May there be prosperity, peace, fullness, and auspiciousness for all beings
translations.zh: 愿一切众生皆有福祉、平静、圆满与吉祥
translations.es: Que haya prosperidad, paz, plenitud y buena fortuna para todos los seres

mantras.name: Om Shanti Shanti Shanti
mantras.english: Om, peace in body, peace in mind, peace in spirit
mantras.transliteration: Om Shanti Shanti Shanti
mantras.abstract:
The Shanti mantra, culminating in the threefold repetition of Shanti (peace), is one of the most universally recognized invocations in the Vedic and Upanishadic literature. The triple invocation of peace appears at the conclusion of major Upanishadic texts including the Brihadaranyaka, Chandogya, Taittiriya, and Mandukya Upanishads, where it serves as both an opening and closing benediction for study and practice.

The threefold repetition of Shanti is deeply intentional. In Vedic cosmology there are three types of obstacles or afflictions known as the Trividha Tapas: Adhibhautika (disturbances caused by other beings and the external world), Adhidaivika (disturbances caused by natural forces and fate), and Adhyatmika (disturbances arising from within the body and mind). The three invocations of Shanti petition for peace on all three of these levels simultaneously.

In the context of Vedic study, the Shanti Patha functions to create a sacred field of receptivity and protection around the act of learning. By invoking peace at the outset, the teacher and student acknowledge that true wisdom cannot be received in a disturbed or agitated state of mind. The mantra aligns the practitioner with the underlying peace that the Upanishads declare to be the very nature of Brahman.

In contemporary practice, Om Shanti Shanti Shanti is used as a closing invocation in yoga classes, meditation sessions, and prayer ceremonies. It is chanted at the beginning and end of pujas and is offered as a blessing at the conclusion of life-cycle rituals such as weddings and funerals. The mantra has transcended its Vedic origins to become a universal symbol of the aspiration for inner and outer peace.

translations.en: Om. Peace in the physical world, peace in the subtle and astral realms, peace in the spiritual sphere. May all afflictions cease and harmony prevail on every level of existence.
translations.zh: 嗡。物质世界中的平静，细微和星光界的平静，精神领域的平静。愿一切苦难消除，愿和谐在存在的每个层面上盛行。
translations.es: Om. Paz en el mundo físico, paz en los reinos sutiles y astrales, paz en la esfera espiritual. Que todas las aflicciones cesen y la armonía prevalezca en todos los niveles de la existencia.

mantras.name: Om Shree Dhanvantre Namaha
mantras.english: Salutation to Lord Dhanvantari, divine physician of the gods
mantras.transliteration: Om Shree Dhanvantre Namaha
mantras.abstract:
Om Shree Dhanvantre Namaha is the primary mantra of Lord Dhanvantari, the divine physician who emerged from the cosmic ocean during the churning of the primordial ocean (Samudra Manthan) bearing the pot of amrita (nectar of immortality) and the Ayurvedic wisdom for healing all disease. As the deity of Ayurveda, Dhanvantari is considered the ultimate source of all healing knowledge and the presiding deity of the medical arts in Hindu tradition.

Dhanvantari is depicted as holding a pot of amrita in one hand and Ayurvedic herbs in another, with a conch shell and the discus of Vishnu (he is considered an avatar of Vishnu). His emergence from the ocean of milk represents the discovery that healing knowledge — like amrita itself — is a treasure hidden within the depths of existence, available to those who churn deeply enough. His mantra is an invocation of that healing intelligence, asking it to arise and work through the practitioner.

In traditional Ayurvedic practice, this mantra is recited before the preparation and administration of medicines, before medical consultations, and at the beginning of any healing endeavor. It transforms the act of healing from a purely technical intervention into a sacred act — aligning the healer's intention with the cosmic healing intelligence embodied by Dhanvantari. The mantra acknowledges that the healer is a vessel for a wisdom that transcends personal training.

The festival of Dhanteras — two days before Diwali — is specifically dedicated to Lord Dhanvantari. On this day, practitioners clean and light their homes as an invocation of health and prosperity, recite the Dhanvantari mantra, and make offerings to request health and vitality for the coming year. The mantra is considered particularly potent on this day, when Dhanvantari's energy is said to be most accessible.

For personal use, Om Shree Dhanvantre Namaha is recited during illness, before taking medicines, before medical procedures, or as a daily health protection practice. Teachers recommend reciting it 108 times while visualizing golden, healing light filling the body. Whether or not one accepts the literal existence of a healing deity, the practice of dedicating one's health to a larger intelligence — and approaching one's body with reverence rather than manipulation — is itself a healing act that contemporary mind-body medicine increasingly validates.

translations.en: Salutation to Lord Dhanvantari, the divine healer
translations.zh: 礼敬檀梵陀利主，神圣的疗愈者
translations.es: Salutación al Señor Dhanvantari, el sanador divino

mantras.name: Om Shri Ram, All Hail Ram, All Hail Ram, All Hail Ram.
mantras.english: Om Shri Ram, All Hail Ram, All Hail Ram, All Hail Ram.
mantras.original: ૐ શ્રી રામ જય રામ જય જય રામ
mantras.transliteration: ॐ श्री राम, जय राम, जय राम, जय राम।
mantras.background: Rooted in Vedic tradition, "Om Shri Ram" unites Om — the primordial sound of all existence — with Ram's sacred name, inviting liberation through devoted repetition.
mantras.benefits:
Chanting Ram awakens devotion and inner strength. In Vedic tradition, mantra japa channels sound vibrations to focus the mind, dissolve ego, and stir spiritual consciousness — drawing the sincere seeker toward liberation.
  Grounding: Sources confirm mantra japa cultivates concentration on chosen deity, sound vibrations awaken kundalini, and repetition (108x) leads toward moksha/liberation.
mantras.tags: hindu, rama, vedic, japa, devotional, prayer, praise, bhakti

translations.es: "Om Shri Ram, Saludos a Ram, Saludos a Ram, Saludos a Ram."
translations.ar: "أوم، يا رام، تحية لك يا رام، تحية لك يا رام، تحية لك يا رام."
translations.bn: "ओम श्री राम, जय राम, जय राम, जय राम।"
translations.de: Om Shri Ram, Lobpreisung für Ram, Lobpreisung für Ram, Lobpreisung für Ram.
translations.en-us: "Om Shri Ram, Hail Ram, Hail Ram, Hail Ram."
translations.en-gb: "Om Shri Ram. Hail Ram. Hail Ram. Hail Ram."
translations.fr-fr: Om Shri Ram, Louange à Ram, Louange à Ram, Louange à Ram.
translations.he: "אוֹם, שבח לראם, שבח לראם, שבח לראם."
translations.hi: "ओम श्री राम, जय राम, जय राम, जय राम।"
translations.id: Om Shri Ram, Hormat Setinggi-Tingginya untuk Rama, Hormat Setinggi-Tingginya untuk Rama, Hormat Setinggi-Tingginya untuk Rama.
translations.it: Om Shri Ram, Gloria a Rama, Gloria a Rama, Gloria a Rama.
translations.ja: オーム・シリー・ラム、ラーむ神に敬意を表します、ラーむ神に敬意を表します、ラーむ神に敬意を表します。
translations.ko: 옴 시리 라무, 모든 분께 경의를 표합니다, 모든 분께 경의를 표합니다, 모든 분께 경의를 표합니다. (옴 시리 라무, 무엔 분께 경의를 표합니다, 무엔 분께 경의를 표합니다, 무엔 분께 경의를 표합니다.)
translations.nl: "Om Shri Ram, Geef eer aan Rama, Geef eer aan Rama, Geef eer aan Rama."
translations.pl: Om Śri Ram, Niech będzie chwała Ram, Niech będzie chwała Ram, Niech będzie chwała Ram.
translations.pt-br: "Om Shri Ram. Louvado seja Ram, Louvado seja Ram, Louvado seja Ram."
translations.ru: Ом Шри Рам, Слава Раму, Слава Раму, Слава Раму.
translations.sv:
Om Shri Ram, All Hail Ram, All Hail Ram, All Hail Ram.

"Om Shri Ram, Hälsa Ram, Hälsa Ram, Hälsa Ram."
translations.th: ข้าขอไหว้พระศรีราม, ขอคารวะพระราม, ขอคารวะพระราม, ขอคารวะพระราม
translations.tr: "Om Shri Ram, Tüm Hail Ram, Tüm Hail Ram, Tüm Hail Ram" çevirisi: "Om Shri Ram, Tüm Selamlar Rama, Tüm Selamlar Rama, Tüm Selamlar Rama."
translations.uk: Ом Шрі Рам, Хвала Раму, Хвала Раму, Хвала Раму.
translations.vi: "Ôm, Lạy Shri Ram, Kính chào Rama, Kính chào Rama, Kính chào Rama."
translations.zh-cn: ॐ 湿利·拉姆，万岁拉姆，万岁拉姆，万岁拉姆。

mantras.name: Om hrīṃ śrīṃ śukrāya namaḥ. This mantra is an invocation and salutation to the planet Venus (Śukra). It is a prayer directed towards Śukra, seeking his blessings. "Oṃ" is the primordial sound, a sacred syllable representing the ultimate reality. "hrīṃ" and "śrīṃ" are bija mantras (seed syllables) associated with wealth, beauty, and auspiciousness. "śukrāya" is the dative case, meaning "to Śukra." "namaḥ" is a salutation, meaning "I bow to" or "prostrations to.
mantras.english: Om hrīṃ śrīṃ śukrāya namaḥ. This mantra is an invocation and salutation to the planet Venus (Śukra). It is a prayer directed towards Śukra, seeking his blessings. "Oṃ" is the primordial sound, a sacred syllable representing the ultimate reality. "hrīṃ" and "śrīṃ" are bija mantras (seed syllables) associated with wealth, beauty, and auspiciousness. "śukrāya" is the dative case, meaning "to Śukra." "namaḥ" is a salutation, meaning "I bow to" or "prostrations to.
mantras.transliteration: ōṃ hrīṃ śrīṃ śukrāya namaḥ.
mantras.background: A Vedic salutation to Venus (Śukra), blending Oṃ—the primordial sound—with bīja seeds hrīṃ & śrīṃ for beauty and wealth. Bow to the cosmos's radiant grace!
mantras.benefits: Invoking Venus through seed syllables hrīṃ and śrīṃ alongside the primordial Om, this mantra channels forces of beauty, auspiciousness, and wealth — drawing the practitioner into a devotional relationship with cosmic abundance.
mantras.tags: hindu, vedic, sukra, devotional, wealth, beauty, prosperity, salutation, bija, planet

translations.es: Om, a Hrim, a Srim, a Shukraya namaḥ. Este mantra es una invocación y saludo al planeta Venus (Shukra). Es una oración dirigida a Shukra, buscando sus bendiciones. "Om" es el sonido primordial, una sílaba sagrada que representa la realidad última. "Hrim" y "Srim" son mantras semilla asociados con la riqueza, la belleza y la prosperidad. "Shukraya" está en caso dativo, lo que significa "a Shukra". "Namah" es una salutación, que significa "me inclino ante" o "saludos a".
translations.ar: يا أوم، يا هريم، يا شريم، سلامٌ عليك يا شُكْرا. (Yā ʾum, yā hrīm, yā shrīm, salām ʿalayka yā Shukra.)
translations.bn: ওঁHRIM্ শ্রীম্ শুক্রায় নামঃ এই মন্ত্রটি শুক্র গ্রহের উদ্দেশ্যে উৎসর্গিত একটি স্তোত্র। এটি শুক্রকে সম্বোধন করে তাঁর আশীর্বাদ লাভের জন্য নিবেদিত একটি প্রার্থনা। "ওঁ" হলো আদিম ধ্বনি, একটি পবিত্র অক্ষর যা পরম সত্তাকে প্রতিনিধিত্ব করে। "HRIM্" এবং "শ্রীম্" হলো বীজ মন্ত্র, যা সম্পদ, সৌন্দর্য এবং শুভভাবের সাথে সম্পর্কিত। "শুক্রায়" হলো শুক্র গ্রহের প্রতি দative case, যার অর্থ "শুক্রের প্রতি"। "নামঃ" হলো একটি অভিবাদন, যার অর্থ "আমি নত করি" অথবা "প্রণতি জানাই"।
translations.de: Om, Hrim, Srim, Shukraya Namah. Dieser Mantra ist eine Anrufung und eine Huldigung an den Planeten Venus (Shukra). Es ist ein Gebet, das an Shukra gerichtet ist, um seinen Segen zu erbitten. "Om" ist der ursprüngliche Klang, eine heilige Silbe, die die ultimative Realität repräsentiert. "Hrim" und "Srim" sind Bijamantras (Keimsilben), die mit Reichtum, Schönheit und Glück verbunden sind. "Shukraya" ist der Dativ, was "für Shukra" bedeutet. "Namah" ist eine Huldigung, was "Ich verneige mich vor" oder "Anbetung für" bedeutet.
translations.en-us: I bow to Shukra, invoking the blessings of wealth, beauty, and auspiciousness.
translations.en-gb: Salutations to Venus, invoking his blessings. May the grace of *hrīṃ* and *śrīṃ* be with him.
translations.fr-fr: Om, à Shukra, je m'incline. Que la grâce de Hrim et de Srim vous accompagne, ô Shukra.
translations.he: אום, אני משתחווה ליופי, לשגשוג ולשרי (שוקרה), אשר הוא נוגה.
translations.hi: ॐ ह्रीं श्रीं शुक्रāya नमः का हिंदी अनुवाद इस प्रकार है: "ओम् ह्रीं श्रीं, मैं शुक्र ग्रह को प्रणाम करता हूँ।" या "ओम् ह्रीं श्रीं, मैं शुक्र को समर्पित हूँ।"
translations.id: Om, persembahan kepada Shukra. Mantra ini adalah seruan dan penghormatan kepada planet Venus (Shukra). Ini adalah doa yang ditujukan kepada Shukra, memohon berkat-Nya. "Om" adalah suara primordial, sebuah suku kata sakral yang mewakili realitas tertinggi. "Hrim" dan "Shrim" adalah mantra bija (suku kata biji) yang terkait dengan kekayaan, keindahan, dan kebaikan. "Shukraya" adalah kasus datif, yang berarti "kepada Shukra". "Namah" adalah salam, yang berarti "saya membungkuk kepada" atau "penghormatan kepada".
translations.it: Om, inchino a Venere (Śukra), invocando la sua grazia. "Om" è il suono primordiale, una sillaba sacra che rappresenta l'ultima realtà. "hrīṃ" e "śrīṃ" sono mantra-seme associati a ricchezza, bellezza e prosperità. "śukrāya" indica il destinatario, "a Venere". "namaḥ" è un atto di riverenza, che significa "mi prostro" o "omaggio a".
translations.ja: オーム、シュクリャーヤ、プリーム、シュリーーム、ナマ。 (Ōmu, Shukrāya, Purīmu, Shirīmu, Nama.)
translations.ko: 옴, 존경과 숭배의 뜻을 담아 금성을 향합니다. "옴"은 근원적인 소리, 궁극적인 실재를 상징하는 신성한 음절입니다. "흐림"과 "쉬림"은 부, 아름다움, 길조와 관련된 비자 만트라(씨앗 음절)입니다. "쉬크라야"는 "쉬크라에게"라는 뜻의 여격입니다. "나마"는 존경의 인사로, "저는 (당신께) 경배합니다" 또는 "경배를 드립니다"라는 의미입니다.
translations.nl: Om, ik boog neder voor Shukra, de god van Venus. "Om" is het oerklank, een heilig syllabe dat de ultieme realiteit vertegenwoordigt. "Hrim" en "Shrim" zijn bijmantra's (zaad-syllaben) die geassocieerd worden met rijkdom, schoonheid en voorspoed. "Shukraya" is in de datieve vorm, wat betekent "aan Shukra". "Namah" is een groet, wat betekent "ik buig voor" of "eerbetoon aan".
translations.pl: Om, hymn Hrim, hymn Śrim, ofdany Shukrze, pokłon.
translations.pt-br: Om, Hrim, Srim, ofereço minhas homenagens a Vênus (Śukra).
translations.ru: Ом хри́м шри́м Шу́кре, приветствую (или: Шу́кре, я склоняюсь пред Тобой).
translations.sv: Om, jag böjer mig inför Shukra, Venus. Hrim och Srim är mantran som förknippas med rikedom, skönhet och lycka.
translations.th: ขอความกรุณาต่อดาวศุกร์ (ศุขระ) ด้วยบทสวดนี้ ซึ่งเป็นการอัญเชิญและถวายความเคารพแด่ดาวศุกร์ เป็นคำอธิษฐานที่ส่งไปยังศุขระ เพื่อขอพร "โอม" คือเสียงกำเนิด เป็นพยางค์ศักดิ์สิทธิ์ที่แสดงถึงความเป็นจริงสูงสุด "หรีม" และ "ศรีม" คือ มนต์บีช (พยางค์ต้น) ที่เกี่ยวข้องกับความมั่งคั่ง ความงาม และความเป็นสิริมงคล "ศุขระ" อยู่ในรูปกรรมกาล หมายถึง "แด่ศุขระ" "นะมะ" คือการถวายความเคารพ หมายถึง "ข้าพเจ้าขอคารวะ" หรือ "ขอแสดงความเคารพแด่"
translations.tr: Om, Venüs gezegeni olan Şukra'ya bir ilahi ve selamlama. Bu mantra, Şukra'ya yöneltilen bir duadır ve onun bereketini dilemektedir. "Om", evrensel ses, mutlak gerçekliği temsil eden kutsal bir hecedir. "Hrim" ve "Shrim", zenginlik, güzellik ve uğurlulukla ilişkili olan *bija* mantralarıdır (tohum heceleri). "Şukrāya", Şukra'ya yönelik bir yönelimi ifade eden bir zamir ekidir, yani "Şukra'ya doğru". "Namaḥ", bir selamlama olup, "size saygı" veya "size derin saygılar" anlamına gelir.
translations.uk: Ом хри́м шри́м, моли́тва Шукра́ю.
translations.vi: Om, tôi kính cúng dâng lên hành tinh Kim tinh (Śukra). Đây là một lời cầu nguyện, một lời tôn kính hướng đến Kim tinh, xin Ngài ban phước lành. "Om" là âm thanh nguyên thủy, một âm tiết thiêng liêng đại diện cho sự thật tối thượng. "hrīṃ" và "śrīṃ" là các âm tiết khởi đầu (bija mantras) liên quan đến sự giàu có, vẻ đẹp và điềm lành. "śukrāya" là cách dative, có nghĩa là "cho Kim tinh". "namaḥ" là một lời chào, có nghĩa là "Tôi cúi chào" hoặc "Lời tôn kính".
translations.zh-cn: 唵 赫林 斯林，献给金星（宿落）。这是对金星的祈祷，祈求他的祝福。“唵”是原始之音，是代表终极现实的神圣音节。“赫林”和“斯林”是种子咒语，与财富、美丽和吉祥相关。“宿落”是与格，意思是“献给宿落”。“纳摩”是一种敬礼，意思是“我向…鞠躬”或“向…致敬”。

mantras.name: Om is a primordial sound, the vibration from which the universe emanated. It represents the ultimate reality, Brahman, and encompasses all aspects of existence – past, present, and future. It is often chanted at the beginning and end of mantras and prayers as a sacred and auspicious sound.
mantras.english: Om is a primordial sound, the vibration from which the universe emanated. It represents the ultimate reality, Brahman, and encompasses all aspects of existence – past, present, and future. It is often chanted at the beginning and end of mantras and prayers as a sacred and auspicious sound.
mantras.transliteration: ॐ ऊँ एक आदिम ध्वनि है, जिससे ब्रह्मांड उत्पन्न हुआ। यह परम वास्तविकता, ब्रह्म का प्रतिनिधित्व करता है, और इसमें अस्तित्व के सभी पहलू शामिल हैं - अतीत, वर्तमान और भविष्य। यह अक्सर मंत्रों और प्रार्थनाओं की शुरुआत और अंत में एक पवित्र और शुभ ध्वनि के रूप में उच्चारण किया जाता है।
mantras.background: Om (ॐ), the "pranava mantra" of Hinduism, is the primordial sound from which all creation arises — a single syllable embodying ultimate reality itself.
mantras.benefits: Om unites waking, dream & deep sleep states into transcendent stillness. Chanting it calms the nervous system, stimulates the vagus nerve, and shifts brainwaves toward deep meditation — a complete practice in one sacred syllable.
mantras.tags: hindu, vedic, pranava, brahman, primordial, creation, chanting, liberation, stillness, healing

translations.es: "Om es un sonido primordial, la vibración de la cual emanó el universo. Representa la realidad última, Brahman, y abarca todos los aspectos de la existencia: pasado, presente y futuro. Se canta a menudo al principio y al final de mantras y oraciones como un sonido sagrado y auspicioso."
translations.ar: "أوم هو الصوت الأولي، الاهتزاز الذي انبثقت منه الكون. إنه يمثل الواقع المطلق، وهو "برهمان"، ويشمل جميع جوانب الوجود - الماضي والحاضر والمستقبل. غالبًا ما يتم تلاوته في بداية ونهاية الترانيم والصلوات باعتباره صوتًا مقدسًا وميمونًا."
translations.bn: ॐ একটি আদিম ধ্বনি, যে कंपन থেকে এই মহাবিশ্ব উদ্ভূত হয়েছে। এটি পরম সত্তা, ব্রহ্মের প্রতিনিধিত্ব করে, এবং এটি অস্তিত্বের সমস্ত দিককে ধারণ করে - অতীত, বর্তমান এবং ভবিষ্যৎ। এটি প্রায়শই মন্ত্র এবং প্রার্থনার শুরুতে এবং শেষে একটি পবিত্র এবং শুভ ধ্বনি হিসেবে উচ্চারণ করা হয়।
translations.de: Om ist ein ursprünglicher Klang, die Vibration, aus der das Universum hervorging. Er repräsentiert die ultimative Realität, Brahman, und umfasst alle Aspekte der Existenz – Vergangenheit, Gegenwart und Zukunft. Er wird oft am Anfang und Ende von Mantras und Gebeten als ein heiliger und glückbringender Klang rezitiert.
translations.en-us: "Om" is a primordial sound, the vibration from which the universe originated. It represents the ultimate reality, Brahman, and encompasses all aspects of existence – the past, the present, and the future. It is frequently chanted at the beginning and end of mantras and prayers, serving as a sacred and auspicious sound.
translations.en-gb: "Om" is considered a primal sound, the vibration from which the universe originated. It embodies the ultimate reality, Brahman, and encompasses all facets of existence – the past, the present, and the future. It is frequently chanted at the commencement and conclusion of mantras and prayers, serving as a sacred and propitious sound.
translations.fr-fr: "Om est un son primordial, une vibration d'où l'univers a émané. Il représente la réalité ultime, Brahman, et englobe tous les aspects de l'existence – passé, présent et futur. Il est souvent chanté au début et à la fin des mantras et des prières en tant que son sacré et propice."
translations.he: אוֹם הוא צליל קדום, הרטט שממנו נוצר היקום. הוא מייצג את המציאות האולטימטיבית, ברהמן, וכולל את כל היבטי הקיום - עבר, הווה ועתיד. הוא נהגה לעתים קרובות בתחילת ובסוף מנטרות ותפילות כצליל קדוש ומביא מזל.
translations.hi: ॐ एक आदिम ध्वनि है, वह कंपन जिससे ब्रह्मांड उत्पन्न हुआ। यह परम वास्तविकता, ब्रह्म का प्रतिनिधित्व करता है, और इसमें अस्तित्व के सभी पहलू शामिल हैं – अतीत, वर्तमान और भविष्य। यह अक्सर मंत्रों और प्रार्थनाओं की शुरुआत और अंत में एक पवित्र और शुभ ध्वनि के रूप में जपा जाता है।
translations.id: "Om adalah suara primordial, getaran dari mana alam semesta bermula. Ia mewakili realitas tertinggi, Brahman, dan mencakup semua aspek keberadaan – masa lalu, sekarang, dan masa depan. Ia sering dilantunkan di awal dan akhir mantra serta doa sebagai suara yang suci dan membawa keberuntungan."
translations.it: "Om è un suono primordiale, la vibrazione da cui è emano l'universo. Esso rappresenta la realtà ultima, Brahman, e comprende tutti gli aspetti dell'esistenza: passato, presente e futuro. Viene spesso cantato all'inizio e alla fine dei mantra e delle preghiere come un suono sacro e propizio."
translations.ja: オームは、宇宙が発現した根源的な音であり、その振動は究極の実体、ブラフマンを象徴し、過去、現在、未来におけるあらゆる存在の側面を包含します。それは、神聖で吉兆的な音として、しばしばマントラや祈りの冒頭および終わりに唱えられます。
translations.ko: 옴(Om)은 우주의 기원인 소리이며, 이 진동으로부터 우주가 발생했다고 여겨집니다. 이는 궁극적인 실재, 즉 브라흐만(Brahman)을 상징하며, 과거, 현재, 미래를 포함한 존재의 모든 측면을 포괄합니다. 옴은 종종 만트라(mantra)와 기도(기도)의 시작과 끝에 신성하고 길조로운 소리로 읊혀집니다.
translations.nl: "Om is een oerklank, de vibratie waaruit het universum is ontstaan. Het vertegenwoordigt de ultieme realiteit, Brahman, en omvat alle aspecten van het bestaan – verleden, heden en toekomst. Het wordt vaak gezongen aan het begin en aan het einde van mantra's en gebeden als een heilig en voorspoedig geluid."
translations.pl: Om jest dźwiękiem pierwotnym, wibracją, z której wyłonił się wszechświat. Reprezentuje ostateczną rzeczywistość, Brahman, i obejmuje wszystkie aspekty istnienia – przeszłość, teraźniejszość i przyszłość. Często jest wymawiany na początku i na końcu mantr i modlitw jako dźwięk święty i przynoszący szczęście.
translations.pt-br: "Om é um som primordial, a vibração da qual o universo emana. Ele representa a realidade última, Brahman, e abrange todos os aspectos da existência – passado, presente e futuro. É frequentemente entoado no início e no fim de mantras e orações, sendo considerado um som sagrado e auspicioso."
translations.ru: Ом – это прародительский звук, вибрация, из которой возникла Вселенная. Он представляет собой абсолютную реальность, Брахман, и охватывает все аспекты бытия – прошлое, настоящее и будущее. Его часто произносят в начале и в конце мантр и молитв как священный и благоприятный звук.
translations.sv: "Om är ett uråldrigt ljud, en vibration från vilken universum uppstod. Det representerar den ultimata verkligheten, Brahman, och omfattar alla aspekter av existensen – det förflutna, nuet och framtiden. Det sägs ofta framföras i början och slutet av mantran och böner som ett heligt och lyckobringande ljud."
translations.th: โอมเป็นเสียงธำรงค์เดิม, การสั่นสะเทือนที่จักรวาลกำเนิดขึ้น เสียงนี้เป็นตัวแทนของความจริงสูงสุด, บราหมัน, และครอบคลุมทุกแง่มุมของการดำรงอยู่ – อดีต, ปัจจุบัน, และอนาคต มักจะเปล่งเสียงโอมในช่วงเริ่มต้นและสิ้นสุดของคาถาและคำอธิษฐาน เนื่องจากเป็นเสียงศักดิ์สิทธิ์และเป็นมงคล
translations.tr: Om, evrenin titreşiminden doğduğu düşünülen ilkel bir sestir. Bu ses, Brahman olarak bilinen mutlak gerçekliği temsil eder ve geçmiş, şimdiki ve gelecek olmak üzere tüm varoluşun yönlerini kapsar. Genellikle, kutsal ve uğurlu bir ses olarak, mantraların ve duaların başlangıcında ve sonunda tekrarlanır.
translations.uk: Ом є первісним звуком, вібрацією, з якої виник Всесвіт. Він представляє собою абсолютну реальність, Брахман, і охоплює всі аспекти існування – минуле, сьогодення та майбутнє. Його часто проспівують на початку та в кінці мантр і молитов як священний та благодатний звук.
translations.vi: "Om là âm thanh nguyên thủy, rung động từ đó vũ trụ phát sinh. Nó đại diện cho thực tại tối thượng, Brahman, và bao gồm tất cả các khía cạnh của sự tồn tại – quá khứ, hiện tại và tương lai. Nó thường được tụng ở đầu và cuối các mantra và lời cầu nguyện như một âm thanh thiêng liêng và mang lại điềm tốt."
translations.zh-cn: 唵 (Ōm) 是一个原始的声音，是宇宙起源的震动。它代表着终极的实在，即梵 (Bàn)，并涵盖了存在的各个方面——过去、现在和未来。它通常在咒语和祈祷的开始和结束时被吟唱，作为一种神圣和吉祥的声音。

mantras.name: Om represents the sound of the universe, encompassing all of existence. It's a sacred syllable in Hinduism, Buddhism, Jainism, and Sikhism, symbolizing the ultimate reality, the divine, or the absolute. It's often chanted at the beginning and end of prayers and mantras.
mantras.english: Om represents the sound of the universe, encompassing all of existence. It's a sacred syllable in Hinduism, Buddhism, Jainism, and Sikhism, symbolizing the ultimate reality, the divine, or the absolute. It's often chanted at the beginning and end of prayers and mantras.
mantras.transliteration: ॐ represents the sound of the universe, encompassing all of existence. It's a sacred syllable in Hinduism, Buddhism, Jainism, and Sikhism, symbolizing the ultimate reality, the divine, or the absolute. It's often chanted at the beginning and end of prayers and mantras.
mantras.background: Om (ॐ) — a sacred syllable from the Vedic Hindu tradition, meaning "the primordial sound of the universe," chanted across Hinduism, Buddhism, Jainism & Sikhism.
mantras.benefits: Om connects the practitioner to the universe's primordial vibration. Chanting its A-U-M sounds shifts brainwaves toward deep meditative theta states, calms the nervous system, and opens awareness to turiya — the transcendent silence beneath all existence.
mantras.tags: hindu, vedic, buddhist, jain, pranava, primordial, chanting, sound, vibration, healing, peace, focus

translations.es: "Om" representa el sonido del universo, abarcando toda la existencia. Es una sílaba sagrada en el hinduismo, el budismo, el jainismo y el sijismo, que simboliza la realidad última, lo divino o lo absoluto. Se suele entonar al principio y al final de las oraciones y los mantras.
translations.ar: يُمثل "أوم" صوت الكون، الذي يشمل كل الوجود. إنه مقطع لفظي مقدس في الهندوسية والبوذية والجاينية والسيخية، ويرمز إلى الواقع المطلق، والإله، أو المطلق. غالبًا ما يتم ترديده في بداية ونهاية الصلوات والترانيم.
translations.bn:
ओम ब्रह्मांड की ध्वनि का प्रतिनिधित्व करता है, जो समस्त अस्तित्व को समाहित करता है। यह हिंदू धर्म, बौद्ध धर्म, जैन धर्म और सिख धर्म में एक पवित्र अक्षर है, जो परम वास्तविकता, दिव्य या पूर्ण का प्रतीक है। इसका अक्सर प्रार्थनाओं और मंत्रों की शुरुआत और अंत में जाप किया जाता है।

बांग्ला अनुवाद: ওম মহাবিশ্বের ধ্বনি, যা সমগ্র অস্তিত্বকে ধারণ করে। এটি হিন্দুধর্ম, বৌদ্ধধর্ম, জৈনধর্ম এবং শিখ ধর্মে একটি পবিত্র শব্দ, যা পরম বাস্তবতা, दिव্য বা পরম সত্তার প্রতীক। এটি প্রায়শই প্রার্থনা এবং মন্ত্রের শুরুতে এবং শেষে জপ করা হয়।
translations.de: "Om repräsentiert den Klang des Universums und umfasst die gesamte Existenz. Es ist eine heilige Silbe im Hinduismus, Buddhismus, Jainismus und Sikhismus und symbolisiert die ultimative Realität, das Göttliche oder das Absolute. Sie wird oft am Anfang und Ende von Gebeten und Mantras rezitiert."
translations.en-us: "Om" represents the sound of the universe, encompassing all of existence. It is a sacred syllable found in Hinduism, Buddhism, Jainism, and Sikhism, symbolizing the ultimate reality, the divine, or the absolute. It is frequently chanted at the beginning and end of prayers and mantras.
translations.en-gb: "Om" represents the sound of the universe, encompassing all of existence. It is a sacred syllable found in Hinduism, Buddhism, Jainism, and Sikhism, symbolising ultimate reality, the divine, or the absolute. It is frequently chanted at the beginning and end of prayers and mantras.
