mantras.original: ॐ ಹೌಂ ಜುಂ ಸಃ ತ್ರ್ಯಂಬಕಂ ಯಜಾಮಹೇ ಸುಗಂಧಿಂ ಪುಷ್ಟಿವರ್ಧನಂ ಉರ್ವಾರುಕಮಿವ ಬಂಧನಾನ್ ಮೃತ್ತ್ಯೋರ್ಮುಕ್ಷೀಯ ಮಾಮೃತಾತ್ ಸಃ ಜುಂ ಹೌಂ ॐ ॥
mantras.transliteration: `Āmi, bhaktāḥ, Tryambaka-deva-āya śaraṇaṁ kuryāma, yo sugandhitā, vṛiddhi-karo vā, yathā kumbhī nālā-baddhaḥ, tad-dṛśi-pratibimbitaḥ, saḥ mṛtyu-bandhanāt mukuṁ-kuratu. Oṃ. Houm. Juh. Sah.`
mantras.background: From the Vedic Hindu tradition, this Mahamrityunjaya mantra invokes Tryambaka (three-eyed Shiva) to sever death's bonds — as a cucumber breaks free from its vine.
mantras.benefits:
The Maha Mrityunjaya Mantra invokes Shiva's liberating power over death and disease. Traditional sources link it to protection from untimely death, healing, and inner fearlessness — freeing the soul like a ripened cucumber released from its vine.
  Grounding: Kannada source prescribes this mantra for protection from illness and untimely death, using rudraksha beads; the mantra's own imagery evokes liberation from bondage.
mantras.tags: hindu, shiva, devotional, protection, healing, longevity, liberation, vedic

translations.es: Nosotros, los devotos, ofrecemos nuestra devoción al Señor Tryambaka, de tres ojos, que es fragante y promueve el crecimiento. Así como el pepino está unido a su tallo, que Él nos libere de los lazos de la muerte. Om. Houm. Juh. Sah.
translations.ar: نَحْنُ، العباد، نقدم تقديسنا للرب ثلاث العيون، تريامبكا، الذي هو عطري ويعزز النمو. تمامًا كما أن الخيار مقيد بساقِه، فليحررنا هو من قيود الموت. أوم. هوم. جوه. ساه.
translations.bn: আমরা, ভক্তগণ, তিনটি চোখযুক্ত ত্র্যম্বক দেবতাকে আমাদের ভক্তি নিবেদন করি, যিনি সুগন্ধযুক্ত এবং যিনি বৃদ্ধিবিধান করেন। যেমন একটি শসা তার ডাঁটার সাথে আবদ্ধ থাকে, তেমনি তিনি যেন আমাদের মৃত্যুর বন্ধন থেকে মুক্ত করেন। ওম। হাউম। জুহ। সহ।
translations.de: Wir, die Gläubigen, weihen unsere Hingabe dem dreiaugigen Herrn Tryambaka, der wohlriechend ist und Wachstum fördert. So wie eine Gurke von ihrem Stiel gebunden ist, möge Er uns von den Fesseln des Todes befreien. Om. Houm. Juh. Sah.
translations.en-us: We, the devotees, offer our devotion to Lord Tryambaka, the three-eyed one, who possesses fragrance and fosters growth. Just as a cucumber is attached to its vine, may He liberate us from the bonds of death. Om. Houm. Juh. Sah.
translations.en-gb: We, the devotees, offer our devotion to Lord Tryambaka, the three-eyed deity, whose presence is fragrant and fosters growth. Just as a cucumber is attached to its stem, may He liberate us from the bonds of mortality. Om. Houm. Juh. Sah.
translations.fr-fr: Nous, les dévots, offrons notre dévotion au Seigneur Tryambaka, à l'aspect aux trois yeux, dont le parfum est agréable et qui favorise la croissance. Ainsi comme le concombre est lié à sa tige, puisse-t-Il nous libérer des chaînes de la mort. Om. Houm. Juh. Sah.
translations.he: אנו, המאמינים, מביעים את חרטתנו בפני אדון טריאמבקה, בעל שלושה עיניים, המפיץ ניחוח ומעודד צמיחה. כמו שמלפפון קשור לגבעולו, יחזירנו הוא משעבוד המוות. אום. הוּם. יּוּה. סָה.
translations.hi: हम, भक्तगण, तीन नेत्रों वाले भगवान त्र्यम्बक को अपनी भक्ति अर्पित करते हैं, जो सुगन्धित हैं और जो विकास को बढ़ावा देते हैं। जिस प्रकार एक खीरा अपनी डंठल से बंधा होता है, उसी प्रकार, वह हमें मृत्यु के बंधनों से मुक्त करें। ॐ। हूँम। जुह्। सह।
translations.id: Kami, para pengabdi, mempersembahkan pengabdian kami kepada Tuhan Tryambaka yang memiliki tiga mata, yang harum dan mempromosikan pertumbuhan. Sama seperti mentimun terikat pada batangnya, semoga Dia membebaskan kami dari belenggu kematian. Om. Houm. Juh. Sah.
translations.it: Noi, i devoti, offriamo la nostra devozione al Signore Tryambaka, il Tricefalo, che è profumato e favorisce la crescita. Così come il cetriolo è legato al suo fusto, così possa Egli liberarci dai vincoli della morte. Om. Houm. Juh. Sah.
translations.ja: 私たち、その信者たちは、香り高く、成長を促す三眼の主、トリアンバカ神に、私たちの献身を捧げます。まるでキュウリが蔓に繋がれているように、彼が私たちを死の束縛から解放してくださいますように。オーム。フーム。ジュ。サー。
translations.ko: 저희는 향기롭고 생명을 북돋아주는 삼눈의 주님 트리얌바카에게 헌신을 바칩니다. 오이 줄기가 오이를 묶는 것처럼, 주님께서 우리를 죽음의 속박에서 벗어나게 하소서. 옴. 후움. 주. 사.
translations.nl: Wij, de toegewijden, offer onze devotie aan de Heer Tryambaka, die drie ogen heeft, heerlijk ruikt en groei bevordert. Net zoals een komkommer aan zijn stengel is bevestigd, moge Hij ons bevrijden van de ketenen van de dood. Om. Houm. Juh. Sah.
translations.pl: My, oddani, ofiarowujemy naszą oddanie Trójokiemu Panu Tryambakie, który jest wonny i sprzyja wzrostowi. Tak jak ogórek jest związany ze swoim łodygą, niech On wyzwoli nas z kajdan śmierci. Om. Houm. Juh. Sah.
translations.pt-br: Nós, os devotos, oferecemos nossa devoção ao Senhor Tryambaka, de três olhos, que é fragrante e promove o crescimento. Assim como o pepino é preso pelo seu caule, que Ele nos liberte das amarras da morte. Om. Houm. Juh. Sah.
translations.ru: Мы, преданные, предлагаем свою преданность Господу Траямбаке, трехглазому, источающему аромат и способствующему росту. Так же, как огурец связан своим стеблем, да освободит Он нас от оков смерти. Ом. Хаум. Юм. Сах.
translations.sv: Vi, de hängivna, erbjuder vår hängivenhet till den treögda Herren Tryambaka, som är väldoftande och främjar tillväxt. Precis som en gurka är bunden vid sin stjälk, må Han befria oss från dödens bojor. Om. Houm. Juh. Sah.
translations.th: ข้าพเจ้าและผู้ศรัทธาทั้งหมดถวายความศรัทธาแด่พระศิวะนามว่า ท๎รัยัมบกา ผู้มีสามดวงตา ซึ่งมีกลิ่นหอมและนำมาซึ่งความเจริญ ดังเช่นแตงกวาที่ถูกผูกไว้ด้วยก้าน อาจพระองค์โปรดปลดปล่อยเราจากพันธนาการแห่งความตาย โอหัง จุหะ สหะ
translations.tr: Biz, müminler, üç gözlü Tanrı Tryambaka'ya, kokulu ve gelişimi destekleyen O'na adanmışlığımızı sunarız. Tıpkı bir salyangidinin sapıyla bağlı olduğu gibi, O bizi ölüm bağlarından kurtarsın. Om. Houm. Juh. Sah.
translations.uk: Ми, преданні, приносимо нашу відданість Трилокому Господу Траямбаці, Який пахне і сприяє зростанню. Так само, як огірок пов'язаний зі своєю лозиною, нехай Він звільнить нас від пут смерті. Ом. Хаум. Юм. Сах.
translations.vi: Chúng tôi, những người sùng đạo, dâng hiến lòng tôn kính lên Ngôi Lãnh Tryambaka, vị Thần có ba con mắt, tỏa hương thơm và ban phát sự phát triển. Giống như quả dưa chuột gắn liền với thân cây, xin Ngài giải thoát chúng tôi khỏi xiềng xích của sự chết. Om. Houm. Juh. Sah.
translations.zh-cn: 我们，作为虔诚的信徒，将我们的虔诚奉献给三目之主特里亚姆巴卡，他是芬芳的，象征着生长。就像黄瓜被它的茎束缚一样，愿祂将我们从死亡的束缚中解放出来。唵。吽。 ジュ。 萨。

mantras.name: Whatever I do, whether with my body, speech, mind, senses, or intellect, or according to my inherent nature, I offer it entirely to Narayana.
mantras.english: Whatever I do, whether with my body, speech, mind, senses, or intellect, or according to my inherent nature, I offer it entirely to Narayana.
mantras.original: कायेन वाचा मनसेन्द्रियैर्वा बुध्यात्मना वा प्रकृतिस्वभावात् | करोमि यध्यत् सकलं परस्मै नारायणायेति समर्पयामि
mantras.transliteration: `yadā hi kāyaḥ, vāgī, manaḥ, indriyāni, vai buddhiḥ, atha vā svabhāvaḥ, idaṁ sarvaṁ naraāyaṇaḥ praṇamāmi.`
mantras.background: Rooted in surrender tradition, this mantra dedicates all acts—body, speech, mind—to Narayana. Sources echo: "नारायणि नमोSस्तुते"—bow to the all-pervading divine!
mantras.benefits: Surrendering all actions to Narayana cultivates humility and dissolves ego-driven anxiety. The sources affirm Narayana as remover of all suffering and granter of auspiciousness, suggesting this practice fosters inner peace and purposeful living.
mantras.tags: hindu, narayani, devotion, protection, auspicious, well-being, prosperity, divine, mantra, offering, surrender, tantric

translations.es: Lo que sea que haga, ya sea con mi cuerpo, mi habla, mi mente, mis sentidos o mi intelecto, o de acuerdo con mi propia naturaleza, ofrezco todo completamente a Narayana.
translations.ar: مهما أفعل، سواء بالجسم أو اللسان أو العقل أو الحواس أو الفكر، أو وفقًا لطباعي الفطرية، فإنني أقدمه بالكامل إلى نارايانا.
translations.bn: আমি যা কিছু করি, তা সে আমার শরীর, वाणी, মন, ইন্দ্রিয়, অথবা বুদ্ধিবৃত্তি দ্বারা হোক, অথবা আমার সহজাত প্রকৃতির দ্বারা পরিচালিত হোক, আমি সবকিছু সম্পূর্ণরূপে নারায়ণকে উৎসর্গ করি।
translations.de: Was auch immer ich tue, sei es mit meinem Körper, meiner Rede, meinem Geist, meinen Sinnen oder meinem Intellekt, oder gemäß meiner inhärenten Natur, ich widme alles ganz Narayana.
translations.en-us: Whatever action I undertake, be it through body, speech, mind, senses, or intellect, and whatever arises from my inherent nature, I dedicate it completely to Narayana.
translations.en-gb: Whatever action I undertake, be it through my body, speech, mind, senses, or intellect, or in accordance with my inherent nature, I dedicate it entirely to Narayana.
translations.fr-fr: Quoi que je fasse, que ce soit par mon corps, ma parole, mon esprit, mes sens ou mon intellect, ou conformément à ma nature intrinsèque, je l'offre entièrement à Narayana.
translations.he: הנה, כל אשר אעשה, בין אם בגוף, בדיבור, במחשבה, בחושים או באינטלקט, או בהתאם לטבעי הפנימי, אני מקריב אותו כולו לנתריאן.
translations.hi: जो भी मैं करता हूँ, चाहे वह मेरे शरीर, वाणी, मन, इंद्रियों या बुद्धि के माध्यम से हो, या मेरी स्वाभाविक प्रकृति के अनुसार हो, मैं वह सब कुछ पूरी तरह से नारायण को समर्पित करता हूँ।
translations.id: Apa pun yang saya lakukan, baik melalui tubuh, ucapan, pikiran, indra, atau akal budi, atau sesuai dengan sifat bawaan saya, saya persembahkan sepenuhnya kepada Narayana.
translations.it: Qualunque cosa io faccia, sia con il corpo, il linguaggio, la mente, i sensi o l'intelletto, o secondo la mia natura intrinseca, io la offro interamente a Narayana.
translations.ja: 私は、心、言葉、身体、感覚、あるいは知性、あるいは本性によって、何をするであれ、そのすべてをナラヤナに捧げます。
translations.ko: 제가 몸, 언어, 마음, 감각, 또는 지성을 통해 하는 모든 행위, 그리고 제 본성에 따른 모든 행동, 그 모든 것을 나는 완전히 나라야나께 바칩니다.
translations.nl: Wat ik ook doe, of het nu met mijn lichaam, mijn spraak, mijn geest, mijn zintuigen of mijn intellect is, of in overeenstemming met mijn aangeboren aard, ik offer het geheel aan Narayana.
translations.pl: Cokolwiek czynię, niezależnie od tego, czy jest to działanie ciałem, mową, umysłem, zmysłami czy intelektem, albo czy jest to zgodne z moją wrodzoną naturą, ofiaruję to całkowicie Narayani.
translations.pt-br: Seja o que eu fizer, seja através do meu corpo, da minha fala, da minha mente, dos meus sentidos ou da minha inteligência, ou de acordo com a minha própria natureza, eu o ofereço integralmente a Narayana.
translations.ru: Что бы я ни делал, будь то действиями тела, словами, мыслями, чувствами или разумом, или в соответствии с моей природой, я предлагаю это полностью Нараяне.
translations.sv: Oavsett vad jag gör, vare sig genom min kropp, mitt tal, mitt sinne, mina sinnen eller min intelligens, eller i enlighet med min inneboende natur, offrar jag det helt och hållet till Narayana.
translations.th: ไม่ว่าข้าพเจ้าจะกระทำสิ่งใด ด้วยร่างกาย วาจา จิต สัมผัส หรือปัญญา หรือตามธรรมชาติโดยกำเนิด ข้าพเจ้าขอถวายสิ่งนั้นทั้งหมดแด่นารายณะ
translations.tr: Ne yaparsam, ister bedenimle, ister sözlerimle, ister zihnimle, ister duyularımla, ister aklımla, ya da ister içsel doğamla olsun, her şeyi tamamen Narayana'ya adıyorum.
translations.uk: Что бы я ни делал, будь то действиями тела, речью, умом, чувствами или интеллектом, или в соответствии с моей присущей природой, я полностью посвящаю это Нараяне.
translations.vi: Bất cứ điều gì tôi làm, dù là bằng thân thể, lời nói, tâm trí, các giác quan, hay trí tuệ, hoặc theo bản chất vốn có của tôi, tôi dâng tất cả cho Narayana.
translations.zh-cn: 无论我通过身体、言语、心智、感官或智慧所做的任何事，或者根据我的内在本质所为，我都完全奉献给纳拉亚纳。

mantras.name: Whatever is meant for me will come to me.
mantras.english: Whatever is meant for me will come to me.
mantras.original: هر چیزی که برای من در نظر گرفته شده است به من خواهد رسید
mantras.transliteration: <ān čīke bāzgasht-e man ast, be man farmān-ye resad.>
mantras.background: Rooted in Sanskrit mantra tradition, this Persian-sourced affirmation means: "Whatever is destined for me will reach me" — dissolving doubt and inviting trust.
mantras.benefits: Repeating "Whatever is meant for me will come to me" dissolves feelings of lack, jealousy, and self-doubt, replacing anxiety with deep trust. Over time, it shifts the mind from scarcity toward inner confidence and contentment.
mantras.tags: hindu, affirmation, confidence, empower, healing, trust, abundance, acceptance, manifestation, peace

translations.es: Lo que está destinado a mí, llegará a mí.
translations.ar: ما هو مقدر لي سيأتيني.
translations.bn: যা আমার জন্য নির্ধারিত, তা অবশ্যই আমার কাছে আসবে।
translations.de: Was für mich bestimmt ist, wird auch zu mir gelangen.
translations.en-us: Whatever is destined for me will reach me.
translations.en-gb: Whatever is destined for me will eventually find its way to me.
translations.fr-fr: Ce qui est destiné à moi, arrivera à moi.
translations.he: מה שמוכן לי, יבוא אלי.
translations.hi: जो कुछ भी मेरे लिए है, वह मेरे पास आएगा।
translations.id: Apa pun yang ditakdirkan untuk saya, akan datang kepada saya.
translations.it: Ciò che è destinato a me, arriverà a me.
translations.ja: 私にふさわしいものは、いつか必ず私のもとにやってくるでしょう。
translations.ko: 운명이라면 결국 내게 올 것이다. (Unmyeongiramyeon, gyeolguk naege ol geotida.)
translations.nl: Wat voor mij bestemd is, zal mij bereiken.
translations.pl: Cokolwiek jest przeznaczone dla mnie, dotrze do mnie.
translations.pt-br: O que é destinado a mim, virá para mim.
translations.ru: То, что предназначено мне, обязательно ко мне придет.
translations.sv: Det som är menat för mig kommer att komma till mig.
translations.th: สิ่งใดที่ควรจะเป็นของฉัน มันจะมาถึงฉันเอง
translations.tr: Bana nasip olacak olan, mutlaka bana ulaşacaktır.
translations.uk: Те, що призначено мені, обов'язково прийде до мене.
translations.vi: Bất cứ điều gì dành cho tôi, cuối cùng sẽ đến với tôi.
translations.zh-cn: 无论什么注定属于我的，最终都会降临到我身上。

mantras.name: Whether it is done by hand or foot, by body or action, by hearing or sight, or by mind, whether it is prescribed or forbidden, forgive all of this. O Lord Shiva, O compassionate one, be victorious, be victorious.
mantras.english: Whether it is done by hand or foot, by body or action, by hearing or sight, or by mind, whether it is prescribed or forbidden, forgive all of this. O Lord Shiva, O compassionate one, be victorious, be victorious.
mantras.original: కరచరణా కృతం వా కాయజం కర్మజం వా శ్రవన్నయనజం వా మానసం వా పరధాం విహితం విహితం వా సర్వ మేతత క్షమస్వ జయ జయ కరుణాబ్దే శ్రీ మహదేవ్ శంభో
mantras.transliteration: "Kiñcit hastenaiva kriyate, pade, śarīre, karman, śrotra-cakṣuṣi, manasā, kiñcit niṣiddham, kiñcid anumitam, sarvam ito kṣamatu. O Śiva, o dayālu, jayatu, jayatu."
mantras.background: A Hindu forgiveness prayer to Lord Shiva, asking pardon for all sins of body, senses, and mind—prescribed or forbidden. Chant it to seek Shiva's boundless compassion.
mantras.benefits: This mantra seeks Shiva's forgiveness for all actions—bodily, sensory, and mental. Practicing it cultivates humility, self-awareness, and release from guilt, inviting compassion into daily life.
mantras.tags: hindu, shiva, forgiveness, prayer, devotional, redemption, compassion, karmic

translations.es: Ya sea que se realice con la mano o el pie, con el cuerpo o la acción, a través de la audición o la vista, o con la mente; ya sea que esté prescrito o prohibido, perdona todo esto. Oh, Señor Shiva, oh compasivo, sé victorioso, sé victorioso.
translations.ar: سواء تم ذلك باليد أو القدم، أو بالجسم أو بفعل، أو بالسمع أو البصر، أو بالعقل، سواء كان ذلك مسموحًا به أو ممنوعًا، فسامح كل هذا. أيها الرب شيفا، يا رحيم، كن منتصرًا، كن منتصرًا.
translations.bn: হাত দিয়ে, পা দিয়ে, শরীর দিয়ে, কর্ম দিয়ে, শ্রবণ দিয়ে, দর্শনের মাধ্যমে, অথবা মন দিয়ে, তা সে কোনো prescribed (निर्दिष्ट) কাজ হোক বা নিষিদ্ধ (निषिद्ध) কাজ হোক, এই সবকিছুকে ক্ষমা করুন। হে শিব, হে দয়ালু, আপনি বিজয়ী হোন, আপনি বিজয়ী হোন।
translations.de: Ob es nun durch Hand oder Fuß, durch Körper oder Handlung, durch Hören oder Sehen, oder durch Geist geschieht, ob es vorgeschrieben oder verboten ist, vergib all dies. O Shiva, o gütiger Herr, sei siegreich, sei siegreich.
translations.en-us: Whether it is accomplished through hand or foot, through the body or action, through hearing or sight, or through the mind, whether it is prescribed or forbidden, forgive all of this. O Lord Shiva, O compassionate one, may you be victorious, may you be victorious.
translations.en-gb: Whether it is accomplished through manual labour or physical exertion, through bodily action or conscious effort, through auditory perception or visual observation, or through mental contemplation; whether it is a prescribed duty or a forbidden act, forgive all of this. O Lord Shiva, O compassionate one, may you be victorious, may you be victorious.
translations.fr-fr: Que ce soit accompli par la main ou le pied, par le corps ou l'action, par l'ouïe ou la vue, ou par l'esprit, que ce soit prescrit ou interdit, pardonne tout cela. Ô Seigneur Shiva, ô toi qui es plein de compassion, sois victorieux, sois victorieux.
translations.he: הנה, אנו מבקשים: בין אם זה נעשה בידיים או ברגליים, בגוף או בפעולה, בשמיעה או בראייה, או במוח, בין אם זה מצווה או אסור, סלח לנו על כל זה. הו, שיבה, הו, הרחום, היה נצח, היה נצח.
translations.hi: चाहे वह हाथ से किया गया हो या पैर से, शरीर से या कर्म से, सुनने या देखने से, या मन से, चाहे वह अनुशंसित हो या निषिद्ध, इन सब को क्षमा करें। हे भगवान शिव, हे दयालु, विजयी हों, विजयी हों।
translations.id: Baik dilakukan dengan tangan atau kaki, dengan tubuh atau tindakan, melalui pendengaran atau penglihatan, atau melalui pikiran, baik yang diperintahkan maupun yang dilarang, maafkanlah semua ini. Wahai Shiva, Engkau yang Maha Penyayang, semoga Engkau menang, semoga Engkau menang.
translations.it: Che sia compiuto con la mano o con il piede, con il corpo o con l'azione, con l'udito o con la vista, o con la mente, che sia prescritto o proibito, perdona tutto questo. O Shiva, o misericordioso, sii vittorioso, sii vittorioso.
translations.ja: 手や足で、身体や行為で、耳や目で、あるいは心でなされたこと、また、許可されたこと、あるいは禁じられたこと、全てを許してください。おお、シヴァ神よ、慈悲深き方よ、勝利あれ、勝利あれ。
translations.ko: 손이나 발, 몸이나 행위, 청각이나 시각, 또는 마음을 통해 이루어지든, 규정되든 금지되든, 그 모든 것을 용서하소서. 자비로운 주 시바께, 승리하시기를, 승리하시기를.
translations.nl: Of het door de hand, de voet, het lichaam of een handeling geschiedt, door gehoor, zicht of de geest, of het nu voorgeschreven of verboden is, vergeef al dit. O Heer Shiva, o barmhartige, wees zegevierend, wees zegevierend.
translations.pl: Niezależnie od tego, czy czyn ten jest wykonywany ręką, stopą, ciałem, działaniem, poprzez słuch, wzrok, czy umysł, niezależnie od tego, czy jest nakazany, czy zabroniony, przebacz wszystko to. O Shiva, o Najwyższy, pełen współczucia, niechaj zwycięstwo będzie Twoje, niechaj zwycięstwo będzie Twoje.
translations.pt-br: Seja realizado pelas mãos ou pelos pés, pelo corpo ou pela ação, pelo ouvido ou pela visão, ou pela mente, seja prescrito ou proibido, perdoe tudo isso. Ó Senhor Shiva, Ó compassivo, seja vitorioso, seja vitorioso.
translations.ru: Будь то совершено руками или ногами, телом или действием, слухом или зрением, или умом, будь то предписано или запрещено, прости всё это. О Господи Шива, о милосердный, да будешь победителем, да будешь победителем.
translations.sv: Oavsett om det utförs med hand eller fot, med kroppen eller genom handling, genom hörsel eller syn, eller genom sinnet, vare sig det är föreskrivet eller förbjudet, förlåt allt detta. O Shiva, o barmhärtige, må du vara segrare, må du vara segrare.
translations.th: ขอให้ข้าพเจ้าได้รับการอภัยในทุกสิ่งที่เกิดขึ้น ไม่ว่าจะเป็นการกระทำด้วยมือหรือเท้า ด้วยร่างกายหรือการกระทำ ด้วยการได้ยินหรือการเห็น หรือด้วยจิตใจ ไม่ว่าจะเป็นสิ่งที่ได้รับการแนะนำหรือถูกห้าม ขอให้พระศิวะ พระผู้ทรงเมตตา จงมีชัยเหนือสิ่งทั้งปวง ขอให้มีชัยเหนือสิ่งทั้งปวง
translations.tr: El ya da ayakla, beden ya da eylemle, duyma ya da görmeyle, ya da zihinle yapılmış olsun, ya da belirlenmiş ya da yasaklanmış olsun, bunların hepsini affet. Ey Shiva, ey şefkatli olan, zafer kazan, zafer kazan.
translations.uk: Будь то зроблено руками чи ногами, тілом чи дією, через слух чи зір, або розумом, будь то дозволено чи заборонено, пробач усе це. О Господи Шіво, О Милосердний, будь переможним, будь переможним.
translations.vi: Xin hãy tha thứ cho mọi hành động, dù được thực hiện bằng tay hay chân, bằng thân thể hay hành động, bằng thính giác hay thị giác, hoặc bằng tâm trí. Xin hãy tha thứ cho những điều được quy định hoặc bị cấm. Hỡi Thần Shiva, Hỡi Đấng Từ Bi, xin Ngài chiến thắng, xin Ngài chiến thắng.
translations.zh-cn: 无论通过手、脚、身体、行为、听觉、视觉或思维完成，无论被规定或禁止，请原谅这一切。 噢，湿婆神，仁慈的君主，愿您凯旋，愿您凯旋。

mantras.name: Yogis constantly meditate on the Omkara (the syllable Om), which is joined with a bindu (dot). It bestows both worldly pleasures and liberation. Salutations to Omkara, salutations to Omkara.
mantras.english: Yogis constantly meditate on the Omkara (the syllable Om), which is joined with a bindu (dot). It bestows both worldly pleasures and liberation. Salutations to Omkara, salutations to Omkara.
mantras.original: ॐकारं बिंदुसंयुक्तं नित्यं ध्यायंति योगिनः । कामदं मोक्षदं चैव ॐकाराय नमो नमः ॥१॥
mantras.transliteration:
योगि: सततं ओंकारं, यः बिन्दुना संयुक्ता, ध्यायन्ति। स एतत्लोकसुखं चापि, मुक्षं चापि ददाति। ओंकाराय नमो, ओंकाराय नमो।

Romanisation: yogiḥ satataṃ ōṃkāraṃ, yaḥ bindunā saṃyukta, dhyāyanti. sa etad loka sukhaṃ cāpi, mukhaṃ cāpi dadāti. ōṃkāray namo, ōṃkāray namo.
mantras.background: From the Shiva Shadakshara Stotra, yogis chant Om with bindu (ॐ) — the primordial sound of all creation — promising worldly fulfillment and liberation.
mantras.benefits: Om unites all existence in one sound. Yogis meditate on Omkara with bindu for worldly fulfillment and liberation. Science confirms it calms the mind, lowers stress, and boosts immunity.
mantras.tags: hindu, shiva, vedic, yoga, pranava, liberation, prosperity, sound, devotional

translations.es: Los yoguis meditan constantemente sobre el Omkara (la sílaba Om), que está unido a un bindu (punto). Este [Omkara] otorga tanto placeres mundanos como liberación. Saludos al Omkara, saludos al Omkara.
translations.ar: تحافظ الرهبان باستمرار على التأمل في صوت "أوم"، وهو مقترن بالنقطة (البيندو). إنه يمنح كلًا من المتع الدنيوية والخلاص. تحية لـ "أوم"، تحية لـ "أوم".
translations.bn: যোগীরা অবিরাম ওঙ্কার (ওঙ্কার অক্ষর), যা একটি বিন্দু (বিন্ডু) দ্বারা যুক্ত, এর উপর ধ্যান করে। এটি জাগতিক আনন্দ এবং মোক্ষ উভয়ই প্রদান করে। ওঙ্কারকে প্রণাম, ওঙ্কারকে প্রণাম।
translations.de: Yogis meditieren unaufhörlich über den Omkara, die Silbe Om, die mit einem Bindu (einem Punkt) verbunden ist. Er gewährt sowohl weltliche Freuden als auch Befreiung. Grüße dem Omkara, Grüße dem Omkara.
translations.en-us: Yogis consistently meditate on the sacred syllable Om, which is often depicted with a dot (bindu) associated with it. This practice is said to grant both worldly happiness and ultimate liberation. Salutations to Om, salutations to Om.
translations.en-gb: Yogis consistently meditate upon the syllable Om, which is accompanied by a bindu (a dot). This practice bestows both worldly enjoyments and liberation. Salutations to Om, salutations to Om.
translations.fr-fr: Les yogis méditent constamment sur l'Omkara (la syllabe Om), qui est associé à un bindu (point). Il confère à la fois les plaisirs terrestres et la libération. Salutations à l'Omkara, salutations à l'Omkara.
translations.he: היוגיס מתמידים במדיטציה על האומקארה (ההברה "אום"), המחוברת לנקודה (בינדו). היא מעניקה הן הנאות חומריות והן התעלות. ברכות לאומקארה, ברכות לאומקארה.
translations.hi: योगियों का लगातार ओम्कार (ओम् अक्षर) पर ध्यान होता है, जो कि एक बिंदु (बिंदु) के साथ जुड़ा होता है। यह सांसारिक सुखों और मोक्ष दोनों को प्रदान करता है। ओम्कार को प्रणाम, ओम्कार को प्रणाम।
translations.id: Para yogi terus-menerus bermeditasi pada Omkara (silabel Om), yang disertai dengan bindu (titik). Ia memberikan anugerah berupa kesenangan duniawi dan juga pembebasan. Salam hormat kepada Omkara, salam hormat kepada Omkara.
translations.it: Gli yogi meditano costantemente sull'Omkara (la sillaba Om), che è unita a un bindu (punto). Essa concede sia i piaceri mondani che la liberazione. Saluti all'Omkara, saluti all'Omkara.
translations.ja: ヨギたちは、常にオཾ（Om）という音を瞑想し、それはビンドゥ（bindu、点）と結合されています。それは世俗的な快楽と解脱の両方をもたらします。オཾ（Om）に礼賛を、オཾ（Om）に礼賛を。
translations.ko: 요기들은 끊임없이 옴(Om)이라는 음절을 명상하며, 이는 비нду(bindu, 점)와 결합되어 있습니다. 옴은 세상의 즐거움과 해탈, 그 둘 다를 가져다줍니다. 옴께 경의를 표합니다, 옴께 경의를 표합니다.
translations.nl: Yogische beoefenaars mediteren voortdurend op de Omkara (de letter Om), die verbonden is met een bindu (punt). Het verleent zowel wereldse genoegens als bevrijding. Groeten aan de Omkara, groeten aan de Omkara.
translations.pl: Jogi nieustannie medytują nad Omkarą (sylabą Om), która jest połączona z bindu (kropką). Ona przynosi zarówno światowe radości, jak i wyzwolenie. Powitania Omkarze, powitania Omkarze.
translations.pt-br: Os yogis meditam constantemente no Omkara (a sílaba Om), que é acompanhado por um bindu (ponto). Ele concede tanto prazeres mundanos quanto libertação. Saudações ao Omkara, saudações ao Omkara.
translations.ru: Йоги постоянно медитируют на Омкара, слог "Ом", который соединен с бинду (точкой). Он дарует как мирские удовольствия, так и освобождение. Поклоны Омкара, поклоны Омкара.
translations.sv: Yogis mediterar ständigt på Om, staveln Om, som är förknippad med en bindu (en punkt). Den ger både världsliga njutningar och befrielse. Hälsningar till Om, hälsningar till Om.
translations.th: นักปฏิบัติธรรม (โยคี) หมั่นทำสมาธิในเสียง "โอม" ซึ่งเป็นพยางค์ศักดิ์สิทธิ์ที่มีจุด (บินดู) เป็นส่วนประกอบ พยางค์นี้ให้ทั้งความสุขในโลกียภาพ และการหลุดพ้นจากกิเลส ขอคารวะแด่ "โอม" ขอคารวะแด่ "โอม"
translations.tr: Yogiler, sürekli olarak "Om" hecesini, üzerinde bir nokta (bindu) bulunan haliyle, medite ederler. Bu, hem dünyevi zevkler hem de kurtuluş bahşeder. "Om"a selamlar, "Om"a selamlar.
translations.uk: Йоги постійно медитують на Омкару (склад "Ом"), яка з'єднана з бінду (крапкою). Вона дарує як мирські насолоди, так і звільнення. Вітання Омкарі, вітання Омкарі.
translations.vi: Các yogi thường xuyên thiền định trên âm thanh "Om", một âm tiết được kết hợp với một dấu chấm (bindu). Âm thanh này ban tặng cả những niềm vui trần tục lẫn giải thoát. Kính chào "Omkara", kính chào "Omkara".
translations.zh-cn: 瑜伽修行者们不断冥想“Om”音，这个音通常与一个点（bindu）结合在一起。它既能带来世俗的快乐，又能带来解脱。向“Om”致敬，向“Om”致敬。

mantras.name: You are the Lord, and there is none else.
mantras.english: You are the Lord, and there is none else.
mantras.transliteration: ʾĀtā hā-Adon, wa-ʾeyn miḵẖeret.
mantras.background: The phrase "You are the Lord, and there is none else" is a bold monotheistic declaration. Its literal meaning affirms singular divine sovereignty — a powerful mantra of devotional surrender.
mantras.benefits: Affirming divine unity, this mantra anchors the mind in absolute trust. Repeating it dissolves fragmented worry, inviting deep surrender and inner stillness — a gateway into one of humanity's oldest monotheistic contemplative traditions.
mantras.tags: affirmation, declaration, prayer, devotional, monotheistic, divine, oneness

translations.es: Tú eres el Señor, y no hay otro.
translations.ar: أنت الرب، ولا غيره.
translations.bn: আপনিই ঈশ্বর, এবং অন্য কেউ নেই।
translations.de: Du bist der Herr, und es gibt keinen anderen.
translations.en-us: You are the Lord, and there is no other.
translations.en-gb: You are the Lord, and there is no other.
translations.fr-fr: Vous êtes l'Éternel, et il n'y en a pas d'autre.
translations.he: אתה ה' ולא עוד אחד.
translations.hi: आप परमेश्वर हैं, और कोई और नहीं है।
translations.id: Engkaulah Tuhan, dan tidak ada yang lain.
translations.it: Tu sei il Signore, e non c'è altro.
translations.ja: あなたは主であり、他に類を見ません。
translations.ko: 당신은 주님이시며, 그 외에는 아무도 없습니다.
translations.nl: U bent de Heer, en er is niemand anders.
translations.pl: Jesteś Panem, i nie ma innego.
translations.pt-br: Você é o Senhor, e não há outro.
translations.ru: Ты – Господь, и нет другого.
translations.sv: Du är Herren, och det finns ingen annan.
translations.th: คุณคือพระเจ้า และไม่มีสิ่งอื่นใดเทียบเท่า
translations.tr: Siz Rabbınızsınız ve başka kimse yoktur.
translations.uk: Ти - Господь, і немає іншого.
translations.vi: Bạn là Đức Chúa, và không còn ai khác.
translations.zh-cn: 你是主，除了你，别无神。

mantras.name: [Mantra] for Pacifying the Land and [Mantra] for Universal Offering.
mantras.english: [Mantra] for Pacifying the Land and [Mantra] for Universal Offering.
mantras.transliteration: 鎮国土真言 (Zhèn Guó Tǔ Zhēn Yán) and 普供養真言 (Pǔ Gōng Yǎng Zhēn Yán).
mantras.background: The source names two ritual purposes — "Pacifying the Land" and "Universal Offering" — hinting at earth-centered and dedicatory traditions, but no origin, tradition, or literal mantra text is provided to draw from.
mantras.benefits: These mantras carry themes of earthly harmony and boundless generosity. Pacifying the land cultivates inner stillness and communal peace, while universal offering awakens selflessness—both traits linked to reduced anxiety and expanded compassion in contemplative traditions.
mantras.tags: hindu, offering, land, pacification, devotion, tantric, ritual, protection, cosmic, universal, mantra, blessing

mantras.name: Alhamdulillah
mantras.english: All praise and thanks belong to God
mantras.transliteration: Al-ḥamdu lillāh
mantras.abstract:
Alhamdulillah is the quintessential Islamic expression of gratitude and the opening phrase of Surah Al-Fatiha, recited in every unit of the five daily prayers. By stating that all praise belongs to God, the phrase relocates the source of all goodness from circumstance or human effort to divine origin, cultivating a permanent orientation of thankfulness.

The Arabic word 'hamd' is richer than simple gratitude — it includes admiration, acknowledgment of excellence, and willing celebration. A person says 'shukr' (thanks) when they receive a favour; 'hamd' is an ongoing recognition of inherent worth whether or not a specific gift has been received. Alhamdulillah is thus less a transaction of gratitude and more a steady contemplative stance.

In Sufi metaphysics, Alhamdulillah is the phrase that returns the entirety of creation to its source. Everything that exists is a manifestation of divine attributes, and praise is the natural response of anything that recognises its origin. Rumi writes in the Masnavi that even the reed's cry of longing for the reed bed is, at its depth, a form of hamd — praise arising from longing for the Real.

Contemporary neuroscience of gratitude aligns surprisingly well with the traditional Islamic emphasis on shukr and hamd. Regular gratitude practice retrains the brain's negativity bias. In Islamic practice, whispering Alhamdulillah at every moment — of joy, hardship, transition — is an ancient technology of the same type: it re-frames experience through the lens of divine provision.

Practitioners are encouraged to repeat Alhamdulillah 33 times in the post-prayer tasbih sequence, but also to weave it into conversation throughout the day. Every sneeze is met with Alhamdulillah; every good news elicits it; and classical teachers say that saying it in the face of difficulty — because even trials contain hidden mercies — marks the highest stage of gratitude.

translations.en: All praise and thanks belong to God
translations.zh: 一切赞美归于真主
translations.es: Toda alabanza pertenece a Dios

mantras.name: All praise is due to Allah, who has given us life after death, and to Him we shall return.
mantras.english: All praise is due to Allah, who has given us life after death, and to Him we shall return.
mantras.original: اَلْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ الَّذِي أَحْيَانَا بَعْدَ مَا أَمَاتَنَا وَإِلَيْهِ النُّشُورُ
mantras.transliteration: Al-ḥamdu lillāhi, alladhī ʾaḥyānā baʿda al-mawti, wa ilayhi naʿūd.
mantras.background: A morning dhikr from Islamic tradition, taught by Prophet Muhammad ﷺ. Recited upon waking, it praises Allah for granting life after sleep's symbolic death, and affirms our return to Him.
mantras.benefits: Recited at dawn, this dhikr cultivates gratitude and humility by framing each waking moment as a gift. It anchors the practitioner in mindfulness of mortality, fostering calm, purpose, and daily spiritual renewal.
mantras.tags: islamic, allah, dhikr, gratitude, devotional, morning, remembrance, affirmation, praise, resurrection

translations.es: Toda alabanza es para Alá, quien nos ha dado la vida después de la muerte, y a Él volveremos.
translations.ar: الحمد لله الذي أحيانا بعد الموت، وإليه نعود.
translations.bn: সমস্ত প্রশংসা আল্লাহর, যিনি আমাদেরকে মৃত্যুর পরে জীবন দিয়েছেন, এবং আমরা তাঁর কাছেই ফিরে যাব।
translations.de: Lob sei Allah, der uns nach dem Tod wieder zum Leben erweckt hat, und zu Ihm werden wir zurückkehren.
translations.en-us: All praise belongs to Allah, who has given us life after death, and to Him we shall return.
translations.en-gb: All praise belongs to Allah, who has granted us life after death, and to Him we shall ultimately return.
translations.fr-fr: Tous les louanges sont à Allah, qui nous a donné la vie après la mort, et à Lui nous retournerons.
translations.he: כל התשבחות לאללה, אשר נתן לנו חיים לאחר המוות, ואליו נשוב.
translations.hi: "अल्लाह की सभी स्तुति, जिसने हमें मृत्यु के बाद जीवन दिया, और उसी की ओर हम वापस लौटेंगे।"
translations.id: Segala puji bagi Allah, yang telah memberikan kita kehidupan setelah kematian, dan kepada-Nya kita akan kembali.
translations.it: L'intera lode è dovuta ad Allah, che ci ha concesso la vita dopo la morte, e a Lui ritorneremo.
translations.ja: アッラーは、我々に死後の命を与えてくださった方。そして、我々は彼のもとに帰還する。全栄はアッラーに帰する。
translations.ko: 알라에게 모든 찬양이 있사옵니다. 알라께서 우리에게 죽음 이후의 생명을 주셨고, 우리는 결국 그분께 돌아갈 것입니다. (All praise be to Allah. Allah has given us life after death, and we will ultimately return to Him.)
translations.nl: Alle lof behoort toe aan Allah, Die ons het leven heeft gegeven na de dood, en tot Hem zullen we terugkeren.
translations.pl: Chwała Allahowi, który dał nam życie po śmierci, i do Niego wszyscy się powrócimy.
translations.pt-br: Toda a louvação é devida a Allah, que nos deu vida após a morte, e a Ele retornaremos.
translations.ru: Вся хвала Аллаху, Который даровал нам жизнь после смерти, и к Нему мы все вернемся.
translations.sv: All lov beror på Allah, som har gett oss livet efter döden, och till Honom skall vi återvända.
translations.th: คำสรรเสริญทั้งหมดเป็นของอัลลอฮ์ ผู้ทรงประทานชีวิตให้เราหลังความตาย และเราจะกลับไปสู่พระองค์
translations.tr: Tüm övgüler Allah'a mahsustur, O bizi ölümden sonra yaşama bağışladı ve biz O'na döneceğiz.
translations.uk: Уся хвала Аллаху, Який надав нам життя після смерті, і до Нього ми повернемося.
translations.vi: Tất cả mọi sự ngợi khen đều thuộc về Allah, Đấng đã ban cho chúng ta sự sống sau khi chết, và đến Ngài chúng ta sẽ trở về.
translations.zh-cn: 万谢真主，祂使我们在死后获得新生，而我们终将回归于祂。

mantras.name: Allahu Akbar
mantras.english: God is the Greatest
mantras.transliteration: Allāhu akbar
mantras.abstract:
Allahu Akbar is the Takbir — the proclamation that God is greater than anything the mind can conceive or the tongue describe. The comparative form 'akbar' is deliberate: God is not merely great but always greater than any name, concept, fear, or attachment one might be holding. Reciting it is an act of dethroning the ego and every worldly concern.

The Takbir opens every unit of Islamic prayer (salah), signalling the transition from ordinary consciousness to the sacred space of divine address. It appears in the call to prayer five times a day across the Muslim world, functioning as a communal reset — an invitation for millions of hearts to simultaneously reorient toward the infinite.

In dhikr practice the Takbir is used to break mental preoccupation. Sheikh Ibn Ata Allah of Alexandria wrote that when the heart is distracted by worldly concerns during meditation, repeating Allahu Akbar with full presence interrupts the chain of mundane thought and reestablishes divine priority. It functions like a spiritual circuit-breaker.

In Sufi circles the Takbir is sometimes chanted in a breath technique where the practitioner exhales completely while vocalising 'Allahu' and inhales in silence before the next repetition. The symbolism is embodied: in declaring God's greatness, one acknowledges one's own smallness — but in that smallness, paradoxically, finds infinite space.

The social dimension of Allahu Akbar should not be overlooked. Historically it has marked the beginning of great undertakings, the completion of difficult journeys, and the recognition of collective blessings. The phrase that begins individual prayer is the same one that rings from the minarets, knitting the individual into a vast community of remembrance.

translations.en: God is the Greatest
translations.zh: 真主至大
translations.es: Dios es el más Grande

mantras.name: Astaghfirullah
mantras.english: I seek forgiveness from God
mantras.transliteration: Astaghfiru llāh
mantras.abstract:
Astaghfirullah is the Islamic formula of seeking divine forgiveness (istighfar). The root 'ghafara' means to cover, protect, and forgive — the divine act not merely of overlooking wrong but of shielding the soul from its consequences. By regularly seeking this protection, the practitioner cultivates humility before divine mercy.

The Prophet Muhammad is reported to have sought forgiveness from God more than seventy times each day, despite being considered spiritually perfected by his community. The hadith tradition uses this as evidence that istighfar is not only for sinners but is a constant practice for any soul that recognizes the gap between its current state and its highest possibility.

In Sufi teaching, Astaghfirullah serves as a 'polishing' practice for the mirror of the heart. According to Ibn Arabi's metaphysics, the human heart is like a mirror meant to reflect divine attributes; sin, negligence, and ego are the tarnish that obscures this reflection. Istighfar is the cloth that continuously polishes, allowing divine light to shine through more completely.

Many teachers prescribe Astaghfirullah specifically at the moment of waking — before the mind is fully activated and before the narrative of self-justification begins. In this liminal state, the formula enters the subconscious more directly, gradually reshaping the soul's default orientation from self-protection to self-surrender.

The longer extended form — Astaghfirullaha l-'azim alladhi la ilaha illa huwa l-hayyal qayyuma wa atubu ilayh — is considered by many scholars the most comprehensive formula of return. Reciting it once with awareness is said to be equivalent to a night of prayer.

translations.en: I seek forgiveness from God
translations.zh: 我向真主忏悔求饶
translations.es: Busco el perdón de Dios

mantras.name: Bismillah
mantras.english: In the name of God, the Most Gracious, the Most Merciful
mantras.transliteration: Bismillāhi r-Raḥmāni r-Raḥīm
mantras.abstract:
Bismillah is the opening phrase of the Quran and is recited by Muslims before beginning almost every significant act — eating, reading, traveling, working, or praying. Its three divine attributes — Allah (God), Rahman (the All-Merciful), and Rahim (the Ever-Merciful) — encapsulate the Islamic understanding of divine compassion as the ground of all existence.

The phrase appears at the beginning of 113 of the 114 chapters of the Quran, making it the most frequently encountered sacred formula in Islam. Classical commentators describe Bismillah as a key that unlocks the door between the human and the divine — reciting it transforms an ordinary act into an act of worship.

In Sufi theology, Bismillah is treated as a complete cosmological statement. The letter 'ba,' the first letter, is said by some mystics to contain the entire universe within it; the dot beneath that letter is interpreted as a point of divine consciousness. Al-Hallaj and later Ibn Arabi both wrote extensively on the metaphysical depth concealed in this single syllable.

The practical effect of consistent Bismillah recitation is mindfulness — each act becomes an invitation for divine presence. In Islamic psychology this constant re-orientation toward God (tawakkul) is considered the antidote to heedlessness (ghafla), the root cause of human suffering. By beginning with God's name, the ego's grip on action is loosened.

Bismillah is also the gateway mantra for the Islamic rosary (tasbih). When a practitioner takes up the 99-bead string and whispers Bismillah before the dhikr sequence, they signal the transition from everyday consciousness to a state of sacred remembrance.

translations.en: In the name of God, the Most Gracious, the Most Merciful
translations.zh: 奉至仁至慈的真主之名
translations.es: En el nombre de Dios, el Compasivo, el Misericordioso

mantras.name: Glory be to Allah, and praise be to Him.
mantras.english: Glory be to Allah, and praise be to Him.
mantras.transliteration: ٱلسَّلَامُ عَلَيْكَ, يَا رَسُولَ ٱللَّٰهِ.
mantras.background: From Islamic dhikr tradition, "Subḥān Allāh wa bi-ḥamdih" means "Glory be to Allah, and praise be to Him." The Prophet ﷺ promised it erases sins like sea foam—100x daily.
mantras.benefits: Reciting "Subḥān Allāh wa bi-ḥamdih" 100x daily is linked to sin-forgiveness and inner purification. It cultivates humility, gratitude, and mindful presence — transforming ordinary moments into sacred worship.
mantras.tags: islamic, allah, dhikr, praise, glorification, forgiveness, remembrance, worship, devotional, adhkar

translations.ar: المجد لله، والحمد لله.
translations.bn: আল্লাহকে মহিমা, এবং তাঁর প্রশংসা।
translations.de: "Gesegnet sei Allah, und Lob sei Ihm."
translations.en-us: Glory be to Allah, and praise belongs to Him.
translations.en-gb: Glory be to Allah, and praise be to Him.
translations.fr-fr: Gloire à Allah, et louange à Son égard.
translations.he: المجد لله، والحمد له.
translations.hi: "अल्लाह की महिमा हो, और उसकी स्तुति हो।"
translations.id: Kemuliaan hanya bagi Allah, dan segala puji bagi-Nya.
translations.it: Gloria a Dio, e lode a Lui.
translations.ja: アッラーは偉大にして、賞賛に値うべきである。 (Arūru wa udhām wa humdu lillāh.)
translations.ko: 알라에게 영광이 있기를, 그리고 그를 찬양합니다. (Allah-e gyeonggwangi itgireul, geurigeugeul chan-yanghamnida.)
translations.nl: "Lof zij Allah, en alle lof is Hem toebedeeld."
translations.pl: Chwała Allahowi i niech będzie Jemu chwała.
translations.pt-br: Glória seja a Allah, e louvores sejam a Ele.
translations.ru: Слава Аллаху, и хвала Ему.
translations.sv: Må Allah vara förhärligad, och lovprisd vare Han.
translations.th: สรรเสริญพระเจ้า และขอพระองค์ทรงเป็นที่สรรเสริญ.
translations.tr: Allah'a hamd ve övgüler olsun.
translations.uk: Слава Аллаху, і хвала Йому.
translations.vi: Vinh quang thuộc về Allah, và mọi lời ca ngợi đều dành cho Ngài.
translations.zh-cn: 荣耀归于真主，赞美归于真主。

mantras.name: Glory be to Allah, and praise be to Him. Glory be to the Most High Allah.
mantras.english: Glory be to Allah, and praise be to Him. Glory be to the Most High Allah.
mantras.transliteration: al-ḥamdu lillāh, wa-l-ḥamdu lanhu. al-ḥamdu lil-ʿalīyu al-ʿaẓīmu.
mantras.background: From Islamic dhikr tradition, this phrase—"SubhanAllahi wa bihamdihi, SubhanAllahil Azeem"—means "Glory & praise be to Allah, Glory be to Allah the Most Great." The Prophet ﷺ called it light on the tongue yet heavy on the divine scale of reward.
mantras.benefits: Described by the Prophet ﷺ as "light on the tongue, heavy on the scale, and beloved to the Most Merciful," this dhikr cultivates humility, awe, and inner peace — offering profound reward through effortless simplicity.
mantras.tags: islamic, dhikr, allah, glorification, praise, remembrance, humility, awe, devotional, forgiveness

translations.es: Gloria sea a Allah, y alabanzas sean para Él. Gloria sea a Allah, el Más Alto.
translations.ar: المجد لله، والحمد لله. سبحان الله العليّ الأعلى.
translations.bn: আল্লাহ্‌র মহিমা, এবং তাঁর প্রশংসা। পরম exalted আল্লাহ্‌র মহিমা।
translations.de: "Gesegnet sei Allah, und Lob sei Ihm. Gepriesen sei Allah, der Höchste."
translations.en-us: Glory be to Allah, and praise be to Him. Glory be to Allah, the Exalted.
translations.en-gb: Glory be to Allah, and praise be to Him. Glory be to the Exalted Allah.
translations.fr-fr: Gloire à Allah, et louange à Son égard. Gloire au Très-Haut Allah.
translations.he:
المجد لله، والحمد له. المجد للعلي الأعلى.
תהילה לאללה, ותשבחות לאללה. תהילה לאל העליון.
translations.hi: "अल्लाह की महिमा हो, और उसकी स्तुति हो। सर्वोच्च अल्लाह की महिमा हो।"
translations.id: Kemuliaan hanya untuk Allah, dan segala puji bagi-Nya. Kemuliaan hanya untuk Allah Yang Maha Tinggi.
translations.it: Gloria a Dio, e lode a Lui. Gloria all'Altissimo Dio.

translations.nl: Moge Allah geprezen en verhoogd worden. Moge Allah, de Allerhoogste, geprezen en verhoogd worden.
translations.pl: Chwała Bogu, i niech Mu będą chwały. Chwała Najwyższemu Bogu.
translations.pt-br: Glória seja a Deus, e louvores a Ele. Glória seja ao Altíssimo Deus.
translations.ru: Слава Аллаху, и хвала Ему. Слава Всевышнему Аллаху.
translations.sv: "Ära vare Allah, och lovprisning till Honom. Ära vare den Högste Allah."
translations.th: มหาบุญชมเชยแด่อัลลอฮ์ และสรรเสริญพระองค์ อัลลอฮ์ผู้ทรงสูงส่ง
translations.tr: Allah'a hamd olsun, O'na şükranlar sunulur. Yüce Allah'a hamdolsun.
translations.uk: Слава Аллаху, і хвала Йому. Слава Всевишньому Аллаху.
translations.vi: Mọi vinh quang thuộc về Allah, và chúng ta ca ngợi Ngài. Mọi vinh quang thuộc về Allah, Đấng Tối Cao.
translations.zh-cn: 赞美真主，全荣耀归于真主。至高无上的真主，全荣耀归于真主。

mantras.name: Hasbunallahu Wa Ni'mal Wakeel
mantras.english: God is sufficient for us, and the best disposer of affairs
mantras.transliteration: Ḥasbunallāhu wa niʿmal wakīl
mantras.abstract:
This phrase appears in the Quran (3:173) as the response of the Prophet Ibrahim and later the Prophet Muhammad and his companions when confronted with overwhelming odds. Its literal meaning is a declaration of radical trust: God alone is enough, and God alone is the perfect guardian and trustee of all affairs. It represents Islam's highest articulation of tawakkul — complete reliance on God.

The context of its Quranic appearance is instructive: the companions were told that a vast army had assembled against them and they should be afraid. Their response — Hasbunallahu wa ni'mal wakeel — is presented in the Quran as exemplary faith. The phrase thus carries the specific energy of courage in the face of threat, of choosing trust over terror.

In Sufi practice this phrase is prescribed for moments of anxiety, financial worry, illness, conflict, or any situation where the ego's control-seeking activates fear. By voicing Hasbunallahu, the practitioner acknowledges that the situation is beyond their management and consciously hands it to divine care. This is not passivity but an active spiritual stance.

The classical scholar Ibn al-Qayyim wrote that this phrase, when recited from the heart with certainty (yaqin), creates a spiritual state in which all worry dissolves because the practitioner has genuinely relocated their dependency from created circumstances to the Creator. He compares it to a child who stops struggling the moment they are held by a loving parent.

Modern practitioners of Islamic mindfulness use Hasbunallahu as an anchor phrase in moments of cognitive spiral — rumination, catastrophizing, future-anxiety. Repeating it slowly while placing a hand on the heart interrupts the stress response and reactivates what Islamic psychology calls the 'aql al-qalb,' the intelligence of the heart.

translations.en: God is sufficient for us, and the best disposer of affairs
translations.zh: 真主够我们所需，祂是最好的监护者
translations.es: Dios nos basta; Él es el mejor custodio de los asuntos

mantras.name: Hu
mantras.english: He — the Ineffable Divine Presence
mantras.transliteration: Hū
mantras.abstract:
Hu is the most intimate and abbreviated of all divine invocations in Islamic mysticism — the simple pronoun 'He,' pointing to divine presence beyond all names and attributes. While the 99 divine names describe God's qualities, Hu points to the Essence itself, the pure 'I AM' behind all manifestation. The Quran uses Hu repeatedly: 'He is God' (Huwa llāh) — making it not merely a pronoun but a direct designation of ultimate reality.

In the Sufi orders, particularly the Mevlevi (whirling dervishes founded by Rumi), Hu is the central dhikr sound. During the sema ceremony, the dervishes exhale 'Huuuuuu' in a long, sustained breath that simultaneously empties the lungs and the ego, creating an opening for divine presence. Rumi's poetry is saturated with Hu: the reed flute's cry, he writes, is always saying Hu — the longing of the fragment for the Whole.

The phonetic nature of Hu is considered significant by Sufi voice teachers. The sound is made entirely in the resonant cavity of the chest before escaping through the lips with no consonantal closure — it is the most open, most resonant, most embodied of sounds. When chanted deeply from the diaphragm, it produces pronounced vibration in the heart centre and the vagus nerve.

Sheikh Hazrat Inayat Khan, who brought Sufi teachings to the West, taught Hu as the universal mantra — the sound that the mystics of every tradition, under different names, are ultimately discovering. He identified Hu as the primordial hum of existence itself, the sound of divine breathing that sustains creation in each moment.

Advanced practitioners sometimes use Hu in what is called 'the silent Hu' — where the tongue remains still and the name is recited internally with each heartbeat. This practice, called dhikr al-latif (subtle dhikr), is said to penetrate below the layer of conscious thought and address the heart directly, gradually transforming the unconscious until even sleep becomes a form of remembrance.

translations.en: He — the Ineffable Divine Presence
translations.zh: 祂——不可言说的神圣存在
translations.es: Él — la Presencia Divina Inefable

mantras.name: In the name of God, O God, I seek refuge in You from evil and all that is vile.
mantras.english: In the name of God, O God, I seek refuge in You from evil and all that is vile.
mantras.original: (Bismillah) Allahumma innee a‘oodu bika minal-khubthi wal-khaba’ith
mantras.transliteration: `Bi-ismi-llāhi, yā-llāh, aʿūdhu bika minnaššiʾi wa-min kulli muqbill. `
mantras.background: From Islamic Dhikr, taught by Prophet Muhammad ﷺ, this protection supplication literally calls on Allah by name, seeking refuge from evil — a daily spiritual shield.
mantras.benefits: Rooted in Islamic dhikr tradition, this protective prayer cultivates trust in the Divine (tawakkul) and inner security. Practiced at vulnerable moments—night, transitions—it trains the mind toward surrender, reducing anxiety and fostering spiritual vigilance.
mantras.tags: islamic, dhikr, protection, trust, supplication, remembrance, evening, muslim, allah, faith, submission, ritual, invocation

translations.es: En el nombre de Dios, oh Dios, busco refugio en Ti de todo mal y de todo aquello que es abyecto.
translations.ar: بسم الله الرحمن الرحيم، يا الله، أعتصم بك من الشر ومن كل ما هو قبيح.
translations.bn: "আল্লাহর নামে, হে আল্লাহ, আমি তোমার কাছে شر (শয়তান) এবং সমস্ত অপবিত্র জিনিস থেকে আশ্রয় প্রার্থনা করছি।"
translations.de: Im Namen Gottes, o Gott, suche ich Zuflucht bei Dir vor dem Bösen und allem, was abscheulich ist.
translations.en-us: In the name of God, O God, I seek refuge in You from evil and all that is impure/despicable.
translations.en-gb: In the name of God, O God, I seek refuge in You from evil and all that is impure.
translations.fr-fr: Au nom de Dieu, ô Dieu, je cherche refuge auprès de Toi contre le mal et toutes les choses viles.
translations.he: בְּשֵׁם אֱלֹהִים, ה' אֱלֹהַי, מִכְּךָ אֶבְקֵשׁ מִגֵּנָה מֵרָע וּמִכֹּל שֶׁאֵינוֹ טוֹב.
translations.hi: "अल्लाह के नाम पर, हे ईश्वर, मैं तुमसे बुराई और सभी अशुद्ध चीजों से शरण मांगता हूँ।"
translations.id: Dengan nama Tuhan, ya Tuhan, aku berlindung kepada-Mu dari kejahatan dan segala sesuatu yang buruk.
translations.it: Nel nome di Dio, o Dio, mi rifugio in Te da ogni male e da tutto ciò che è abietto.
translations.ja: 神の名において、おお神よ、私はあなたに悪およびあらゆる不浄なものから保護を求めます。
translations.ko: 알라의 이름으로, 오 알라, 저는 당신으로부터 악과 모든 부정한 것에서 보호를 구합니다.
translations.nl: In de naam van God, o God, ik zoek bij U bescherming tegen het kwaad en alles wat slecht is.
translations.pl: W imię Boga, o Boże, szukam u Ciebie schronienia przed złem i wszystkim, co jest nieczyste.
translations.pt-br: Em nome de Deus, ó Deus, busco refúgio em Ti contra o mal e tudo o que é imundo.
translations.ru: Во имя Бога, о Боже, я ищу убежище у Тебя от зла и всего скверного.
translations.sv: I Guds namn, o Gud, söker jag skydd hos Dig från ondska och allt som är orent.
translations.th: ขอพระเจ้า โปรดรับฟัง ข้าพเจ้าขอพึ่งพระองค์ เพื่อขอความคุ้มครองจากสิ่งชั่วร้ายและทุกสิ่งที่น่ารังเกียจ
translations.tr: "Allah'ın adıyla, Ey Allah'ım, sana yöneliyorum. Kötülükten ve her türlü çirkin şeyden sana sığınıyorum."
translations.uk: Іменем Бога, о Боже, я шукаю у Тебе захисту від зла та від усього огидного.
translations.vi: "Bằng danh Allah, hỡi Allah, tôi tìm kiếm sự bảo vệ của Ngài khỏi mọi điều xấu và mọi điều tồi tệ."
translations.zh-cn: 奉神的名义，主啊，我向你寻求庇护，远离一切邪恶和污秽。

mantras.name: In the name of God, the Most Gracious, the Most Merciful.
mantras.english: In the name of God, the Most Gracious, the Most Merciful.
mantras.transliteration: *Bi-ismi-llāhi, ar-Raḥmāni, ar-Raḥīmi.*
mantras.background: From Sanskrit roots, "Bismillah" opens Malay healing mantras—invoking God's grace & mercy. Passed down through generations by shamans, it blends Islamic faith with ancient mystical ritual.
mantras.benefits: "Bismillahirrahmanirrahim" opens healing and protective mantras in Malay tradition, anchoring the speaker's mind to divine mercy. Its rhythmic repetition channels supernatural power, offering psychological calm and spiritual authority.
mantras.tags: islamic, invocation, supplication, protection, healing, malay, ritual, devotional, empowerment

translations.es: En el nombre de Dios, el Clemente, el Misericordioso.
translations.ar: بِاسْمِ ٱللَّهِ الرَّحْمَٰنِ الرَّحِيمِ.
translations.bn: আল্লাহর নামে, যিনি পরম করুণাময়, যিনি অতি দয়ালু।
translations.de: Im Namen Gottes, dem Allergnädigigsten, dem Barmherzigsten.
translations.en-us: In the name of God, the Most Compassionate, the Most Merciful.
translations.en-gb: In the name of God, the Most Compassionate, the Most Merciful.
translations.fr-fr: Au nom d'Allah, le Tout Miséricordieux, le Très Compatissant.
translations.he: בשם אללה, الرحمن الرحيم.
translations.hi: "अल्लाह के नाम पर, जो सबसे दयालु और सबसे रहिम हैं।"
translations.id: Dengan nama Allah, Yang Maha Pengasih, Yang Maha Penyayang.
translations.it: Nel nome di Dio, il Più Clemente, il Più Misericordioso.
translations.ja: 全能の神、最も慈悲深く、最も憐れみ深い神の名において。
translations.ko: 알라후, 가장 자비롭고 가장 자애로운 이름으로.
translations.nl: In de naam van God, de Barmhartige, de Genadige.
translations.pl: W imię Boga, Najwyrobniejszego, Najmiłosierniejszego.
translations.pt-br: Em nome de Deus, o Clemente, o Misericordioso.
translations.ru: Во имя Бога, Всемилостивого, Всемилосердного.
translations.sv: I Guds namn, den ytterst barmhärtige, den ytterst nådige.
translations.th: بسم الله الرحمن الرحيم. ขอพระเจ้า ผู้ทรงเมตตา ผู้ทรงกรุณา.
translations.tr: Tanrı'nın adıyla, en Lütufkar, en Rahim Olan.
translations.uk: Іменем Бога, Всемилостивого, Всемилосердного.
translations.vi: Theo danh Allah, Đấng Từ Bi, Đấng Hằng Lòng.
translations.zh-cn: بسم الله الرحمن الرحيم.

mantras.name: In the name of God, we enter, and in the name of God, we exit, and upon our Lord we rely.
mantras.english: In the name of God, we enter, and in the name of God, we exit, and upon our Lord we rely.
mantras.original: بِسْمِ اللَّهِ وَلَجْنَا، وَبِسْمِ اللَّهِ خَرَجْنَا، وَعَلَى رَبِّنَا تَوَكَّلْنَا
mantras.transliteration: `ʾin-nis-samīʾ, nadkhulu, wa ʾin-nis-samīʾ, nakḫuruǧu, wa ʿalā rabbīnā natawakkalu.`
mantras.background: From Islamic tradition, this Arabic dhikr—"Bismillahi walajna, wa bismillahi kharajna"—means "In Allah's name we enter and leave, trusting our Lord." A mindful threshold ritual.
mantras.benefits: Entering & exiting with Allah's name cultivates mindfulness of divine presence in daily transitions, building tawakkul (trust in God) — a psychological anchor that reduces anxiety and fosters inner peace.
mantras.tags: islamic, dhikr, allah, protection, trust, evening, supplication, divine

translations.es: En el nombre de Dios, entramos, y en el nombre de Dios, salimos, y en nuestro Señor confiamos.
translations.ar: بسم الله ندخل، وبسم الله نخرج، وعلى ربنا نتوكل.
translations.bn: "আল্লাহর নামে আমরা প্রবেশ করি, এবং আল্লাহর নামে আমরা প্রস্থান করি, এবং আমরা আমাদের পালনকর্তার উপর নির্ভর করি।"
translations.de: Im Namen Gottes betreten wir, und im Namen Gottes verlassen wir, und auf unseren Herrn verlassen wir uns.
translations.en-us: "In the name of God, we enter, and in the name of God, we exit, and upon our Lord we rely."
translations.en-gb: "In the name of God, we enter, and in the name of God, we exit, and upon our Lord we rely."
translations.fr-fr: Au nom de Dieu, nous entrons, et au nom de Dieu, nous sortons, et en notre Seigneur nous plaçons notre confiance.
translations.he: בְּשֵׁם אֱלֹהִים, אנוֹ נִכְנֶס, ובְּשֵׁם אֱלֹהִים, אנוֹ יוֹצְאִים, ובִּשְׁמֵי אֲדוֹנֵנוּ אָנוּ סוֹמְכִים.
translations.hi: "अल्लाह के नाम पर हम प्रवेश करते हैं, और अल्लाह के नाम पर हम प्रस्थान करते हैं, और हम अपने प्रभु पर भरोसा करते हैं।"
translations.id: Dengan nama Allah, kami masuk, dan dengan nama Allah, kami keluar, dan kepada Tuhan kami kami berserah.
translations.it: Nel nome di Dio, entriamo; nel nome di Dio, usciamo; e nel nostro Signore confidiamo.
translations.ja: 神の名において我々が入ります。神の名において我々が出ます。そして、我々は主（御主）に依存します。
translations.ko: 알라의 이름으로 들어가고, 알라의 이름으로 나옵니다. 그리고 우리는 주님께 의지합니다. (Allah-ui ireum-euro deureogago, Allah-ui ireum-euro naopnida. Geurago, urineun junim-kke uiji-hapnida.)
translations.nl: In de naam van God betreden wij, en in de naam van God verlaten wij, en op onze Heer vertrouwen wij.
translations.pl: W imię Boga wchodzimy, w imię Boga wychodzimy, a na naszym Panu się wspieramy.
translations.pt-br: Em nome de Deus, entramos, e em nome de Deus, saímos, e em nosso Senhor confiamos.
translations.ru: Во имя Бога, мы входим, и во имя Бога, мы выходим, и на Господа нашего мы уповаем.
translations.sv: I Guds namn går vi in, och i Guds namn går vi ut, och på vår Herre förlitar vi oss.
translations.th: ด้วยพระนามของพระเจ้า เราขอเข้า, และด้วยพระนามของพระเจ้า เราขอออกจากที่นี่, และเราขอพึ่งพระเจ้าผู้ทรงเป็นพระเจ้าของเรา.
translations.tr: Allah'ın adıyla giriyoruz, Allah'ın adıyla çıkıyoruz ve Rabbimize güveniriz.
translations.uk: Іменем Бога ми входимо, і іменем Бога ми виходимо, і на Господа нашого ми покладаємося.
translations.vi: Theo tên của Allah, chúng tôi bước vào, và theo tên của Allah, chúng tôi rời đi, và chúng tôi tin cậy vào Thượng Đế của chúng tôi.
translations.zh-cn: 奉主之名，我们进入；奉主之名，我们离开；我们依靠我们的主。

mantras.name: Indeed, it is in the remembrance of Allah that hearts find contentment.
mantras.english: Indeed, it is in the remembrance of Allah that hearts find contentment.
mantras.original: Translation: Indeed, in the remembrance of Allah do hearts find satisfaction
mantras.transliteration: Indeed, it is in the remembrance of Allah that hearts find contentment.
mantras.background: From Surah Ar-Ra'd (Quran), this Arabic Dhikr phrase means "In Allah's remembrance, hearts find contentment." Rooted in Prophet Muhammad's era, it anchors Muslim spiritual life.
mantras.benefits: Dhikr cultivates emotional stability, mindfulness, and resilience. Rooted in Quranic verse (Surah Ar-Ra'd), it promises heart-contentment, reduces anxiety, and deepens divine connection—inviting believers into a timeless spiritual rhythm.
mantras.tags: islam, allah, dhikr, remembrance, devotional, peace, contentment, mindfulness, emotional stability, divine, resilience, quran

translations.es: Ciertamente, es en el recuerdo de Allah donde los corazones encuentran la paz.
translations.ar: في ذكر الله تتزكى القلوب وتجد السكينة.
translations.bn: নিঃসন্দেহে, হৃদয়ে প্রশান্তি আসে именно Аллах-এর স্মরণের মাধ্যমে।
translations.de: In der Erinnerung an Allah finden Herzen Trost.
translations.en-us: Indeed, it is through remembering Allah that hearts find peace and satisfaction.
translations.en-gb: Indeed, it is through reflecting on and remembering Allah that hearts find peace and satisfaction.
translations.fr-fr: En vérité, c'est dans le souvenir d'Allah que les cœurs trouvent la satisfaction.
translations.he: אכן, בשמירת זכרו של אללה, הלבבות מוצאים נחמה.
translations.hi: वास्तव में, यह अल्लाह के स्मरण में ही है कि हृदय को संतोष मिलता है।
translations.id: Sesungguhnya, dalam mengingat Allah-lah hati menemukan ketenangan.
translations.it: Effettivamente, è nella memoria di Allah che i cuori trovano la serenità.
translations.ja: 確かに、心の平安は、アッラーを思い出すことによって得られます。
translations.ko: 진실로, 마음은 알라를 기억함으로써 평안을 찾습니다.
translations.nl: Inderdaad, het is in de herinnering aan Allah dat harten rust vinden.
translations.pl: Rzeczywiście, to w wspomnieniu Allaha serca odnajdują ukojenie.
translations.pt-br: De fato, é na lembrança de Allah que os corações encontram a satisfação.
translations.ru: Действительно, именно в памятовании об Аллахе сердца обретают умиротворение.
translations.sv: Sannerligen, det är i minnet av Allah som hjärtan finner stillhet.
translations.th: แท้จริงแล้ว ความสงบในจิตใจเกิดขึ้นได้จากการระลึกถึงอัลลอฮ์
translations.tr: Gerçekten, kalpler ancak Allah'ı anmakla huzur bulur.
translations.uk: Дійсно, саме в пам'яті про Аллаха серця знаходять спокій.
translations.vi: Thật vậy, sự an ủi trong lòng người chỉ tìm thấy khi luôn ghi nhớ Allah.
translations.zh-cn: 实际上，人们在对真主的纪念中才能找到内心的平静。

mantras.name: La Hawla Wa La Quwwata Illa Billah
mantras.english: There is no power nor strength except through God
mantras.original: لَا حَوْلَ وَلَا قُوَّةَ إِلَّا بِاللهِ
mantras.transliteration: Lā ḥawla wa lā quwwata illā billāh
mantras.abstract:
Known as 'al-Hawqala,' this declaration is described in hadith as a 'treasure from the treasures of paradise.' It is a comprehensive acknowledgment that no movement, no capacity for change, no strength of any kind originates in the human being — all power is derived from God. It dissolves both pride (claiming one's achievements as self-generated) and despair (believing one lacks power to change).

The phrase is traditionally recommended after the call to prayer, after difficult tasks, before challenging endeavours, and as a constant companion in dhikr. Its effect is to liberate the practitioner from the twin traps of arrogance and helplessness. By placing all capacity in God, one is simultaneously humbled and empowered.

In Sufi circles the Hawqala is associated with the station of fana (annihilation of the ego-self) because it explicitly denies the independent existence of the self as a source of power. Sheikh Al-Shadhili, founder of the Shadhili order, is said to have recited it thousands of times daily. His disciples teach that the phrase gradually erodes the illusion of the separate self, leaving only the recognition of divine agency flowing through apparently individual forms.

From a psychological standpoint, the Hawqala addresses the core of what clinical psychologists call the locus-of-control problem. When a person believes all power rests entirely with them, anxiety increases because the burden of control is crushing. When power is acknowledged as divine, one still acts but without the weight of ultimate responsibility.

The phrase is often the last refuge in moments of complete overwhelm. Many Islamic accounts describe individuals facing death, extreme pain, or total loss who found this phrase rising to their lips, and through its repetition found not a solution but a peace that transcended their circumstances — the peace of having surrendered power back to its actual source.

translations.en: There is no power nor strength except through God
translations.zh: 除真主外无力无能
translations.es: No hay poder ni fuerza sino en Dios

mantras.name: La Ilaha Illa Allah
mantras.english: There is no god but God
mantras.transliteration: Lā ilāha illā llāh
mantras.abstract:
La Ilaha Illa Allah is the first half of the Shahada, the Islamic declaration of faith, and is considered the most powerful of all Islamic phrases. It consists of a negation (la ilaha — there is no god) followed by an affirmation (illa Allah — except God). This structure is deliberate; every idol of the heart — attachment, fear, ego, status — must be negated before the absolute divine reality can be affirmed.

In Sufi dhikr this phrase is the most commonly used sacred formula. The Qadiri, Shadhili, Naqshbandi, and Chishti orders all use it as their central practice, though with different rhythms and breath patterns. The Shadhili tradition divides the phrase across the breath: 'La ilaha' on the exhalation (sweeping away), 'illa Allah' on the inhalation (receiving the divine).

Classically, the phrase is said to have been heard by the Prophet in the divine presence as the essence of all revelation compressed into a single statement. Al-Ghazali in the Ihya Ulum al-Din calls it the master key of the heart: if recited with full awareness of meaning, it gradually dissolves every competing object of desire and fear until only God remains as the soul's orientation.

From a psychological perspective, the negation-then-affirmation structure mirrors the cognitive process of value clarification. One must identify and release the false gods — approval, security, pleasure, power — before the heart can settle into unconditional trust. Many classical teachers prescribe this practice specifically for practitioners struggling with anxiety or addiction.

The full Shahada — La Ilaha Illa Allah, Muhammadun Rasul Allah — adds the second clause acknowledging Muhammad as the messenger. But La Ilaha Illa Allah stands alone as a practice capable of carrying a devotee from the periphery of religion to its innermost centre.

translations.en: There is no god but God
translations.zh: 万物非主，唯有真主
translations.es: No hay más dios que Dios

mantras.name: O Allah, Lord of this prayer, and of this standing [in prayer], grant Muhammad the means of salvation and the highest excellence, and raise him to the praised station that You have promised him.
mantras.english: O Allah, Lord of this prayer, and of this standing [in prayer], grant Muhammad the means of salvation and the highest excellence, and raise him to the praised station that You have promised him.
mantras.original: Allahumma rabba hadhihi-d-da’wati-t-tammati, wa-s-salati-l-qa’imati, ati Muhammadan al-wasilata wa-l-fadilata, wa-b’athhu maqaman mahmudan alladhi wa’adtahu
mantras.transliteration: O Allah, Lord of this prayer, and of this standing [in prayer], grant Muhammad the means of salvation and the highest excellence, and raise him to the praised station that You have promised him.
mantras.background:
An Islamic Dhikr (remembrance) recited between Adhan and Iqamah. It asks Allah to grant Prophet Muhammad honor and the promised "praised station" on Judgment Day.
  Grounding: Source cites Bukhari hadith: reciting this between adhan and iqamah earns the Prophet's intercession on the Day of Resurrection.
mantras.benefits: Recited between Adhan and Iqamah, this Dhikr cultivates tranquility, deepens Tawakkul, and promises the Prophet's intercession on Judgment Day—drawing the heart toward divine peace.
mantras.tags: islamic, dhikr, dua, allah, muhammad, intercession, salvation, praise

translations.es: Oh, Allah, Señor de esta oración y de esta postura [en la oración], concede a Muhammad los medios de salvación y la más alta excelencia, y elevenlo a la estación alabada que le has prometido.
translations.ar: يا الله، يا ربّ هذه الصلاة، ويا ربّ هذا القائم [بالصلاة]، اجعل محمداً وسيلة للنجاة، وأعطه أعالي الرتب، وارفع ذكره إلى المقام المحمود الذي وعدته به.
translations.bn: হে আল্লাহ, এই সালাতের প্রভু এবং এই দাঁড়ানো অবস্থার প্রভু, মুহাম্মাদকে মুক্তি লাভের উপায় এবং সর্বোচ্চ উৎকর্ষ দান করুন, এবং তাকে সেই প্রশংসিত অবস্থানে উন্নীত করুন যা আপনি তার জন্য প্রতিশ্রুতি দিয়েছেন।
translations.de: "O Allah, Herr dieses Gebets und dieser [der Gebets-]Haltung, gewähre Muhammad die Mittel zur Erlösung und die höchste Vollkommenheit, und erhebe ihn zu dem gelobten Rang, den Du ihm versprochen hast."
translations.en-us: O Allah, Lord of this prayer and of this standing [in prayer], grant Muhammad the means to salvation and the highest excellence, and raise him to the praised station that You have promised him.
translations.en-gb: O Allah, Lord of this prayer and of this posture [in prayer], grant Muhammad salvation and the highest level of excellence, and raise him to the praised station that You have promised him.
translations.fr-fr: Ô Allah, Seigneur de cette prière et de cette posture de prière, accorde à Muhammad les moyens du salut et la plus haute excellence, et élève-le à la station louée que Tu lui as promise.
translations.he: ה', רבש"ע, אדון לתפילה הזו ולעמידה הזו בתפילה, מעניק למחמוד את הדרך לגאולה ולדרגת השלמות העליונה, והעלה אותו למעמד המשובח שהבטחת לו.
translations.hi: हे अल्लाह, इस नमाज़ के और इस खड़े होने के स्वामी, मुहम्मद को उद्धार का साधन और सर्वोच्च उत्कृष्टता प्रदान करो, और उन्हें उस प्रशंसित स्थान पर ऊंचा करो जिसका तुमने उनसे वादा किया है।
translations.id: Ya Allah, Tuhan dari sholat ini, dan dari posisi berdiri dalam sholat ini, anugerahkanlah kepada Muhammad jalan menuju keselamatan dan keunggulan tertinggi, dan angkatlah dia ke derajat terpuji yang Engkau janjikan kepadanya.
translations.it: O Allah, Signore di questa preghiera e di questa posizione [in preghiera], concedi a Muhammad i mezzi per la salvezza e l'eccellenza più alta, e innalzalo alla lodevole posizione che mi hai promesso.
translations.ja: オッラー（アッラー）、この祈り、そしてこの礼拝の姿勢の主よ。ムハンマドに、救済の道と至高の卓越性を与え、あなたが彼に約束された、称賛に値する地位へと彼を昇格させてください。
translations.ko: 알라, 이 기도의 주인이시며, 이 서 있는 자세의 주인이시여. 무함마드를 구원의 길로 인도하시고, 가장 높은 지위로 칭송받게 하소서. 그리고 당신께서 그에게 약속하신 칭송받는 지위로 그를 올려주시길 바랍니다.
translations.nl: O Allah, Heer van dit gebed en van deze houding tijdens het bidden, geef Mohammed de middelen tot redding en de hoogste excellentie, en verhef hem tot de lofwaardige positie die U Hem hebt beloofd.
translations.pl: O Allah, Panie tej modlitwy i tego stanu skupienia w modlitwie, obdarz Muhammada środkami zbawienia i najwyższym doskonałością, i podnieś go do wywyższonej pozycji, którą Mu obiecałeś.
translations.pt-br: Ó Allah, Senhor desta oração e desta posição [ao orar], conceda a Muhammad os meios de salvação e a mais elevada excelência, e eleve-o à estação louvada que Tu lhe prometeste.
translations.ru: О Аллах, Владыка этой молитвы и этого положения [во время молитвы], даруй Мухаммаду путь к спасению и наивысшее благо, и возвысь его до преславенного положения, которое Ты завещал ему.
translations.sv: O Allah, Herre över denna bön och över detta ställande [i bön], bevilja Muhammad frälsning och den högsta fullkomligheten, och upphöj honom till den berömda platsen som Du har lovat honom.
translations.th: โอ้ อัลลอฮฺ, พระผู้เป็นเจ้าแห่งการละหมาดนี้ และการยืนละหมาดนี้, ขอพระองค์ประทานความรอดและความดีเลิศสูงสุดแก่ท่านศาสดามุฮัมหมัด และขอพระองค์ยกท่านขึ้นสู่สถานะที่ได้รับการสรรเสริญตามที่พระองค์ทรงสัญญาไว้แก่ท่าน
translations.tr: Ey Allah'ım, bu duanın Rabb'i ve bu (namazdaki) duruşun sahibi, Muhammed'e kurtuluş yolunu ve en üstün mükemmelliği bahşet; Ona, vaat ettiğin övülmüş mertebeye yükselt.
translations.uk: О Аллах, Владыка этой молитвы и этого стояния [во время молитвы], даруй Мухаммаду путь к спасению и наивысшее благо, и вознеси его на похвальную степень, которую Ты завещал ему.
translations.vi: Lạy Allah, Tác giả của lời cầu nguyện này, và của sự đứng cầu nguyện này, xin ban cho Muhammad sự cứu rỗi và sự cao thượng tối thượng, và xin nâng Ngài lên vị trí được ca ngợi mà Ngài đã được hứa.
translations.zh-cn: “主，此礼拜的主宰，此祈祷的主宰，请赐予穆罕默德得救的途径和至高的荣耀，并提升他至您所应许的受赞颂的地位。”

mantras.name: O Allah, You are the Most Forgiving and love forgiveness, so grant me forgiveness.
mantras.english: O Allah, You are the Most Forgiving and love forgiveness, so grant me forgiveness.
mantras.original: Allahumma innaka afuwwun tuhibbul afwa fa’fu anni
mantras.transliteration: ٱللَّهُمَّ إِنَّكَ أَسْمَعُ وَأَنَا أَعْلَمُ.
mantras.background: From Islamic dhikr tradition, recited during Ramadan's Laylatul Qadr: "O Allah, Most Forgiving, You love forgiveness—grant me forgiveness." A timeless plea of surrender and hope.
mantras.benefits: Reciting "Allahumma innaka afuwwun..." during Ramadan & Laylatul Qadr cultivates humility, inner peace, and release of guilt—inviting divine mercy and emotional renewal through rhythmic, heartfelt remembrance.
mantras.tags: islamic, allah, dhikr, forgiveness, supplication, mercy, ramadan

translations.es: Oh, Alá, Tú eres el más Misericordioso y amas el perdón, así que concédeme tu perdón.
translations.ar: يا الله، أنت أرحم الرَّحِمين وأحبُّ التَّسامح، فامنحني مغفرتك.
translations.bn: হে আল্লাহ, আপনি সবচেয়ে ক্ষমাশীল এবং ক্ষমা করতে ভালোবাসেন, তাই আমাকে ক্ষমা করুন।
translations.de: "Oh Allah, Du bist der Barmherzigste und liebst die Vergebung, so gewähre mir Deine Vergebung."
translations.en-us: O God, You are the Most Forgiving and You love forgiving, so grant me Your forgiveness.
translations.en-gb: O God, You are the most forgiving and You love to forgive, so grant me Your forgiveness.
translations.fr-fr: Ô Allah, Tu es le Plus Clément et Tu aimes le pardon, alors accorde-moi Ton pardon.
translations.he: ה', אתה הסלחן הגדול ואוהב סליחה, אנא, תן לי סליחה.
translations.hi: हे अल्लाह, तुम सबसे अधिक क्षमा करने वाले हो और क्षमा को पसंद करते हो, इसलिए मुझे क्षमा प्रदान करो।
translations.id: Ya Allah, Engkau adalah Yang Maha Pengampun dan menyukai pengampunan, maka anugerahkanlah kepadaku ampunan.
translations.it: O Allah, Tu sei Colui che perdona di più e ami il perdono, dunque concedimi la Tua misericordia e il Tuo perdono.
translations.ja: おお、アッラーよ。あなたは最も寛容であり、許しを愛される。どうか、私に許しをください。
translations.ko: 알라, 당신은 가장 자비로우시고 용서를 사랑하시니, 저에게 용서를 내려주시기를 바랍니다. (Allah, dangshin-eun ga-jang jabiro-wusi-go yongseul sa-rang-hasi-ni, jeo-ege yongseul-eul nae-ryeo-jusigi-gil).
translations.nl: O Allah, U bent de Meest Vergevingsgezinde en houdt van vergeving, dus schenk mij vergeving.
translations.pl: O Allah, Ty jesteś Najwyroczniejszy i kochasz przebaczenie, więc obdarz mnie przebaczeniem.
translations.pt-br: Ó Alá, Tu és o Mais Clemente e Te agradas em perdoar, então concede-me o Teu perdão.
translations.ru: О Аллах, Ты – Самый Милосердный и любишь прощение, поэтому даруй мне прощение.
translations.sv: O Allah, Du är den mest barmhärtige och älskar förlåtelse, så förlåt mig.
translations.th: โอ้ อัลลอฮฺ พระองค์คือผู้เปี่ยมด้วยความเมตตา และพระองค์ทรงรักการให้อภัย ขอพระองค์ทรงประทานการให้อภัยแก่ข้าพเจ้า
translations.tr: Ey Allah'ım, Sen en affedicisin ve affetmeyi seversin, bu yüzden bana affet.
translations.uk: О Аллах, Ти є Всепрощаючий і любиш прощення, тож даруй мені прощення.
translations.vi: Ô Allah, Ngài là Đấng Hằng Xử và Ngài yêu thích sự tha thứ, xin Ngài ban cho con sự tha thứ.
translations.zh-cn: 噢，主啊，你是至仁至慈的，你喜爱宽恕，请你宽恕我。

mantras.name: O Allah, indeed You are the Forgiving, and You love forgiveness, so forgive me.
mantras.english: O Allah, indeed You are the Forgiving, and You love forgiveness, so forgive me.
mantras.original: Allāhumma innaka ʿafuwwun tuḥibbul-ʿafwa faʿfu ʿannī
mantras.transliteration: `ʾAllāhumma, ʾinna-ka al-Ghafūr, wa tuḥibb al-Ghafar, fa-ighfir lī.`
mantras.background: From Islamic dhikr tradition, this supplication—"Allāhumma innaka ʿafuwwun tuḥibbul-ʿafwa faʿfu ʿannī"—is the premier prayer for Laylat al-Qadr, seeking Allah's boundless forgiveness.
mantras.benefits: Reciting this supplication daily cultivates humility, inner peace, and self-compassion. Rooted in Islamic tradition, it reconnects the soul to divine mercy, dissolving guilt and fostering serenity — especially powerful during Laylat al-Qadr.
mantras.tags: islamic, allah, dhikr, supplication, affirmation, forgiveness, serenity, laylat-al-qadr

translations.es: Oh, Alá, ciertamente Tú eres el Misericordioso y amas el perdón, así que perdóname.
translations.ar: يا الله، إنك أنت الغفور، وتحب التوبة، فاستغفر لي.
translations.bn: হে আল্লাহ, নিঃসন্দেহে তুমি ক্ষমাশীল, এবং তুমি ক্ষমা করতে ভালোবাসো, তাই আমাকে ক্ষমা করো।
translations.de: Oh Allah, wahrlich, Du bist der Vergebende, und Du liebst die Vergebung, so vergib mir.
translations.en-us: O Allah, indeed You are the Most Forgiving, and You love to forgive, so grant me Your forgiveness.
translations.en-gb: O Allah, You are indeed the Most Forgiving, and You cherish forgiveness, so grant me Your forgiveness.
translations.fr-fr: Ô Allah, Tu es certes le Pardonneur, et Tu aimes le pardon, alors pardonne-moi.
translations.he: ה', אכן אתה סלחן, ואתה אוהב סליחה, אנא סלח לי.
translations.hi: हे अल्लाह, वास्तव में तुम क्षमा करने वाले हो, और तुम्हें क्षमा करना पसंद है, इसलिए मुझे क्षमा करो।
translations.id: Ya Allah, sesungguhnya Engkau Maha Pengampun, dan Engkau menyukai pengampunan, maka ampunilah aku.
translations.it: O Allah, tu sei veramente il Clemente e ami il perdono, quindi perdonami.
translations.ja: 神よ、あなたは確かに寛容であり、あなたは許しを愛します。どうか、私を許してください。
translations.ko: 오 알라, 진실로 당신은 전능하신 분이시며, 당신은 용서를 사랑하시니, 저를 용서하소서. (O Allah, jinsilro dangsin-eun jeon-nanghasimin bun-isimeo, dangsin-eun yongseoreul saranghasini, jeo-reul yongseohaseoseo.)
translations.nl: O Allah, warelijk, U bent de Vergevende, en U houdt van vergeving, dus vergeef mij.
translations.pl: O Allah, Ty jesteś Ten, który Wybaczający, a Ty kochasz przebaczenie, więc przebacz mi.
translations.pt-br: Ó Allah, verdadeiramente, Tu és o Clemente e Teu amor reside no perdão, então, perdoa-me.
translations.ru: О Аллах, поистине Ты – Прощающий, и Ты любишь прощение, так прости меня.
translations.sv: O Allah, du är den Förlåtande, och du älskar att förlåta, så förlåt mig.
translations.th: โอ้ อัลลอฮฺ, แท้จริงพระองค์คือผู้ทรงอภัย และพระองค์ทรงรักการให้อภัย, ดังนั้นขอพระองค์ทรงอภัยแก่ข้า.
translations.tr: Ey Allah'ım, şüphesiz ki Sen Bağışlayansın ve affetmeyi seversin, beni affet.
translations.uk: О Аллах, воістину Ти є Прощаючий, і Ти любиш прощення, тож прости мене.
translations.vi: Lạy Allah, Ngài là Đấng Hằng Hữu, Ngài yêu sự tha thứ, xin Ngài hãy tha thứ cho con.
translations.zh-cn: 噢，主啊，你确是至宽恕的，你喜爱宽恕，请你宽恕我。

mantras.name: O Allah, we spend the evening with You, we wake up in the morning with You, we live by You, we die by You, and to You is the return.
mantras.english: O Allah, we spend the evening with You, we wake up in the morning with You, we live by You, we die by You, and to You is the return.
mantras.original: اللَّهُمَّ بِكَ أَمْسَيْنَا، وَبِكَ أَصْبَحْنَا، وَبِكَ نَحْيَا، وَبِكَ نَمُوتُ، وَإِلَيْكَ الْمَصِيرُ
mantras.transliteration: ʾAllāhumma, naqḍū al-masāʼ bi-ḥudūka, wa naṣḥa ʾan-naḥāri bi-ḥudūka, wa naḥya bi-ḥudūka, wa namūtu bi-ḥudūka, wa ʾilayka al-maʾāl.
mantras.background: A Prophet Muhammad ﷺ–taught dhikr (remembrance), recited at dusk & dawn. Literally: surrendering life's rhythm—waking, living, dying—entirely to Allah. A poetic vow of total devotion.
mantras.benefits: Reciting this evening dhikr anchors the soul in divine continuity — linking sleep, waking, and death into one sacred rhythm. It cultivates surrender, gratitude, and fearlessness, as life and return both belong to Allah.
mantras.tags: islamic, dhikr, gratitude, daily remembrance, submission, return, allah, supplication, muslim, morning, evening, daily life

translations.es: Ó, Alá, pasamos la tarde a Tu lado, despertamos por la mañana con la presencia de Tu gracia, vivimos según Tu guía, morimos bajo Tu voluntad, y a Ti es el retorno.
translations.ar: يا الله، نقضي المساء في طاعتك، ونستيقظ في الصباح بذكرك، نعيش بأمرك، نموت بقضائك، وإليك المرجع.
translations.bn: হে আল্লাহ, আমরা সন্ধ্যায় আপনার সাথে থাকি, সকালে আপনার সাথে জেগে উঠি, আমরা আপনার দ্বারা জীবনযাপন করি, আপনার দ্বারা মারা যাই, এবং আপনার কাছেই আমাদের প্রত্যাবর্তন।
