mantras.transliteration: Barēkh ata Adonai Eloheinu Melekh ha-olam, אשר qidshatanu b'mitzvotecha u-tzivatanu al mitzvvat tzitzit.
mantras.background:
A Jewish blessing (berakhah) from the Hebrew liturgical tradition, recited before performing tzitzit (fringes). It opens with the sacred formula "Barukh ata Adonai..." — sanctifying divine commandment through spoken word.
  Grounding: Source lists Jewish blessings on mitzvot using the formula "Blessed are You, LORD our God...commanded us," confirming this as a liturgical berakhah tied to tzitzit observance.
mantras.benefits: Reciting tzitzit blessing cultivates mindfulness of divine commandments, grounding practitioners in sacred obligation. It fosters humility, intentionality, and a felt sense of covenant identity—transforming a physical act into spiritual awareness.
mantras.tags: jewish, blessing, prayer, mitzvah, tzitzit, devotion, sanctification, observance, adonai, law

translations.es: Bendito seas, Señor, nuestro Dios, Rey del universo, que nos has santificado con tus mandamientos y nos has ordenado con respecto a la mandamiento de los tzitzit.
translations.ar: مباركٌ أنتَ يا ربّنا إلهنا، يا ملكَ الكون، الذي قدّسنا بأوامرك وأمرنا بالوصية المتعلقة بالزُّرّ.
translations.bn:
আপনিBlessed are You, Lord our God, King of the universe, who has sanctified us with Your commandments and commanded us regarding the commandment of tzitzit.

আপনি আমাদের ঈশ্বর, মহাবিশ্বের রাজা, যিনি আপনার আদেশগুলির মাধ্যমে আমাদের পবিত্র করেছেন এবং যিনি "টিজ্বিত" (tzitzit) নামক বিধান সম্পর্কে আমাদের নির্দেশ দিয়েছেন।
translations.de: Gepriesen seist Du, Herr, unser Gott, König des Universums, der uns durch Deine Gebote geheiligt hat und uns bezüglich der Gebote der Zizit befohlen hat.
translations.en-us: Blessed are You, Lord our God, King of the universe, who has made us holy through Your commandments and commanded us concerning the commandment of tzitzit.
translations.en-gb: Blessed are You, Lord our God, King of the universe, who has made us holy through Your commandments, and who has commanded us with regard to the commandment of tzitzit.
translations.fr-fr:
Voici une traduction en français, cherchant à rendre le sens des mots tout en respectant la structure et le ton de la phrase hébraïque :

"Béni sois-Tu, Seigneur, notre Dieu, Roi de l'univers, qui nous as consacrés par tes commandements et qui nous a ordonné de porter le *tzitzit*."
translations.he: ברוך אתה ה', אלוהינו, מלך היקום, אשר קדשתנו במצוותיך וציויתנו על מצוַת הציצית.
translations.hi: हे भगवान, हमारे ईश्वर, ब्रह्मांड के राजा, आप जो हमें अपने आज्ञाओं के द्वारा पवित्र करते हैं और हमें 'त्ज़ित्ज़िट' (fringes) के विधान के बारे में आदेश देते हैं, आपको हमारा प्रणाम।
translations.id: Terpujilah Engkau, Tuhan kami Allah, Raja alam semesta, yang telah menguduskan kami dengan perintah-Mu dan memerintahkan kami mengenai perintah *tzitzit*.
translations.it: Benedetto sei, Signore, nostro Dio, Re dell'universo, che ci hai consacrati con i tuoi comandamenti e ci hai dato il precetto del *tzitzit*.
translations.ja: 主よ、我々の神よ、宇宙の王よ。あなたは、あなたの戒めによって私たちを聖別し、タリート（tzitzit）の掟について私たちに命じられました。
translations.ko: 주님, 우리 하나님이시여, 전 우주의 왕이시여, 찬미합니다. 당신의 계명으로 우리를 거룩하게 하시고, '츠츠트'라는 끈에 대한 명령을 주셨습니다.
translations.nl: Gezegend zij U, Heer onze God, Koning van het universum, die ons heeft geheiligd met Uw geboden en ons de wet van de tsitsit heeft opgedragen.
translations.pl: Błogosławiony jesteś, Panie, nasz Boże, Królu wszechświata, który poświęcił nas swoimi przykazaniami i który nakazał nam przestrzeganie przykazania dotyczącego talisów (tzitzit).
translations.pt-br: Bendito(a) és Tu, Senhor nosso Deus, Rei do universo, que nos santificaste com Teus mandamentos e nos ordenaste em relação ao mandamento do talit (ou da tzitzit).
translations.ru: Благословен Ты, Господь, Бог наш, Царь вселенной, Который освятил нас своими заповедями и повелел нам исполнять заповедь о цыцит.
translations.sv: Välsignad vare Du, Herre, vår Gud, Konung över universum, som har helgat oss genom Dina bud och befallt oss att följa budet om tzitzit.
translations.th: พระองค์ทรงเป็นที่สรรเสริญ พระผู้เป็นเจ้า พระองค์ คือพระราชาแห่งจักรวาล ผู้ได้ทรงทำให้เราบริสุทธิ์ด้วยพระบัญญัติของพระองค์ และทรงบัญชาเราเกี่ยวกับข้อบัญญัติของ "tzitzit"
translations.tr: Rabbiniz, Tanrımız, evrenin Hükümdarı olan, bizi emirlerinizle kutsayan ve bize "tzitzit" (tül) ile ilgili emri veren, Sizinle kutsanmış bulunuyoruz.
translations.uk: Благословенний Ти, Господи, наш Бог, Цар Всесвіту, Який освятив нас Своїми заповідями і заповідав нам щодо заповіти ціцїт.
translations.vi: Kính cẩn, Ngài là Đức Chúa Trời, Thượng Đế của chúng con, Vua của vũ trụ, Đấng đã thánh hóa chúng con bằng các điều răn của Ngài và ra lệnh cho chúng con về điều răn của "tzitzit".
translations.zh-cn: 赞美您，主，我们的上帝，宇宙之王，您以您的诫命使我们蒙圣，并以关于“塔利特（或丝线）”的诫命来教导我们。

mantras.name: Blessed are You, Lord our God, King of the universe, who has sanctified us with Your commandments and commanded us to dwell in the sukkah.
mantras.english: Blessed are You, Lord our God, King of the universe, who has sanctified us with Your commandments and commanded us to dwell in the sukkah.
mantras.original: Barukh ata Adonai Eloheinu, melekh ha'olam, asher kid'shanu b'mitzvotav v'tzivanu leishev basukah
mantras.transliteration: Barukh ata Adonai Eloheinu Melech ha-olam, asher kidshatanu b'mitzvotecha v'tzivanu l'such bi-sukah.
mantras.background: A Hebrew berakhah from the Jewish Siddur, recited during Sukkot. Its ancient formula "Blessed are You, LORD our God…" sanctifies the mitzvah of dwelling in the sukkah.
mantras.benefits: Reciting this blessing cultivates mindful gratitude and sacred awareness, linking a physical act—dwelling—to divine commandment. It invites practitioners to find holiness in everyday space, fostering presence and communal spiritual identity.
mantras.tags: jewish, blessing, sukkah, commandment, gratitude, dwelling, praise, observance

translations.es: Bendito seas, Señor, nuestro Dios, Rey del universo, que nos has santificado con tus mandamientos y nos has ordenado habitar en la sukká.
translations.ar: مباركٌ أنتَ يا ربُّنا وإلهنا، يا ملكَ الكون، الذي قدَّسنا بأوامرِكَ وأمرَنا أن نسكنَ في السُّكَّة.
translations.bn:
Blessed are You, Lord our God, King of the universe, who has sanctified us with Your commandments and commanded us to dwell in the sukkah.

আমরা তোমার কাছে কৃতজ্ঞ, হে ঈশ্বর, আমাদের সৃষ্টিকর্তা, মহাবিশ্বের রাজা, যিনি আপনার আদেশ দ্বারা আমাদের পবিত্র করেছেন এবং আমাদের সুক্কাহতে বসবাস করার নির্দেশ দিয়েছেন।
translations.de: Gepriesen seist Du, HERR, unser Gott, König des Universums, der uns durch Deine Gebote heilig gemacht hat und uns geboten hat, in der Sukka zu wohnen.
translations.en-us: Blessed are You, Lord our God, King of the universe, who has sanctified us with Your commandments and commanded us to dwell in the sukkah.
translations.en-gb: Blessed are You, Lord our God, King of the universe, who has made us holy through Your commandments and commanded us to dwell in the sukkah.
translations.fr-fr: Béni sois-Tu, Seigneur notre Dieu, Roi de l'univers, qui nous a consacrés par tes commandements et qui nous a ordonné de résider dans la soukkah.
translations.he: ברוך אתה ה', א-להינו, מלך היקום, אשר קדשתנו במצוותיך וציויתנו לשבת בסוכה.
translations.hi: हे भगवान, हमारे परमेश्वर, जगत के राजा, जो हमें अपने आदेशों से पवित्र करते हैं और हमें सुक्काह में निवास करने का आदेश देते हैं, आपको हमारा प्रणाम।
translations.id: Terpujilah Engkau, Tuhan kami Allah, Raja alam semesta, yang telah menguduskan kami melalui perintah-perintah-Mu dan memerintahkan kami untuk tinggal di dalam *sukah*.
translations.it: Benedetto sei, Signore nostro Dio, Re dell'universo, che ci hai santificati con i tuoi comandamenti e ci hai comandato di abitare nella *sukka*.
translations.ja: 主よ、我々の神、宇宙の王よ。あなたは、あなたの戒めによって私たちを聖別し、住居である「スカー」に住むように命じられました。我々はあなたに感謝します。
translations.ko: 주님, 우리 하나님, 전 우주의 왕이시여, 당신의 계명으로 우리를 거룩하게 하시어, 임시 거처인 수카에 머물도록 명령하셨으니, 당신은 복되시옵소서.
translations.nl: Gezegend zij U, Heer onze God, Koning van het universum, Die ons heilig heeft gemaakt met Uw geboden en ons bevolen heeft in de *sukkah* te wonen.
translations.pl: Błogosławiony jesteś, Panie, nasz Bóg, Królu wszechświata, który uświęcił nas swoimi przykazaniami i nakazał nam mieszkać w chatce Sukkot.
translations.pt-br: Bendito és Tu, Senhor nosso Deus, Rei do universo, que nos santificaste com teus mandamentos e nos ordenaste habitar na Sucá.
translations.ru: Благословен Ты, Господь, Бог наш, Царь вселенной, Который освятил нас своими заповедями и повелел нам жить в сукке.
translations.sv: Välsignad vare Du, Herre, vår Gud, Konung över universum, som har helgat oss genom Dina bud och befallt oss att bo i sukkah.
translations.th: ข้าขอสรรเสริญพระองค์ พระผู้เป็นเจ้า พระพระเจ้าของเรา พระมหากษัตริย์แห่งจักรวาล ผู้ทรงทำให้เราบริสุทธิ์ด้วยพระบัญญัติของพระองค์ และทรงบัญชาให้เราอาศัยอยู่ในกระท่อม (Sukkot).
translations.tr: Rabbe'm, Evrenin Efendisi, Tanrımız, bizi emirlerinizle kutsayan ve bizi sukka'da yaşamaya buyuran, Sizin için övgüler olsun.
translations.uk: Благословенний Ти, Господи, наш Бог, Царю Всесвіту, Який освятив нас Своїми заповідями і заповідав нам жити в сукці.
translations.vi: Xin Ngài ban phước cho chúng con, Lạy Chúa chúng con, Đấng Tối Cao, Vua của vũ trụ, Đấng đã thánh hóa chúng con qua các điều răn của Ngài và ra lệnh cho chúng con cư ngụ trong lều Sukkah.
translations.zh-cn: 赞美您，主，我们的上帝，宇宙之王，您以您的诫命使我们蒙圣，并命令我们居住在简易棚内。

mantras.name: Blessed are You, Lord our God, King of the universe, who has sanctified us with Your commandments and commanded us to kindle the Sabbath candles.
mantras.english: Blessed are You, Lord our God, King of the universe, who has sanctified us with Your commandments and commanded us to kindle the Sabbath candles.
mantras.original: Barukh ata Adonai Eloheinu, melekh ha'olam, asher kid'shanu b'mitzvotav v'tzivanu likboa m'zuza
mantras.transliteration: Barukh ata Adonai Eloheinu Melekh ha-olam, asher kidashtanu be-mitzvotecha ve-tzivanatani le-hadlik nerot ha-Shabbat.
mantras.background: A Jewish blessing (berakhah) from the Siddur, recited at Shabbat candle-lighting. Its Hebrew opens: "Barukh ata Adonai…" — inviting the sacred into ordinary time.
mantras.benefits: Reciting this Shabbat candle blessing marks a sacred threshold—transitioning from ordinary time into holy rest. It cultivates mindfulness, communal belonging, and divine connection through weekly rhythmic practice.
mantras.tags: jewish, shabbat, blessing, sanctification, candle-lighting, mitzvah, ritual, observance

translations.es: Bendito seas, Señor, nuestro Dios, Rey del universo, que nos has santificado con tus mandamientos y nos has ordenado encender las velas del Shabat.
translations.ar: مباركٌ أنتَ يا ربّنا وإلهنا، يا ملك الكون، الذي قدّسنا بأوامرِك وأمرنا بإشعال شموع السبت.
translations.bn:
Blessed are You, Lord our God, King of the universe, who has sanctified us with Your commandments and commanded us to kindle the Sabbath candles.

আমরা ঈশ্বরের প্রতি কৃতজ্ঞ, যিনি আমাদের সৃষ্টিকর্তা, মহাবিশ্বের রাজা। তিনি তাঁর আদেশ দ্বারা আমাদের পবিত্র করেছেন এবং আমাদের শabbat-এর মোমবাতি জ্বালানোর নির্দেশ দিয়েছেন।
translations.de: Gepriesen seist Du, Herr, unser Gott, König des Universums, der uns durch Deine Gebote geheiligt hat und uns geboten hat, die Schabbatkerzen anzuzünden.
translations.en-us: Blessed are You, Lord our God, King of the universe, who has made us holy with Your commandments, and who has commanded us to light the Sabbath candles.
translations.en-gb: Blessed are You, Lord our God, King of the universe, who has made us holy through your commandments and commanded us to light the Sabbath candles.
translations.fr-fr: Béni sois-Tu, Seigneur, notre Dieu, Roi de l'univers, qui nous as sanctifiés par Tes commandements et qui nous a ordonné d'allumer les bougies du Shabbat.
translations.he: ברוך אתה ה', א-לוקינו, מלך היקום, אשר קדשתנו במצוותיך וציויתנו להדליק את נרות השבת.
translations.hi: आप, हमारे ईश्वर, जगत के राजा, जो हमें अपने आदेशों से पवित्र करते हैं और हमें शनिवार की शाम को मोमबत्तियाँ जलाने का आदेश देते हैं, आपका धन्यवाद।
translations.id: Terpujilah Engkau, Tuhan Allah kami, Raja alam semesta, yang telah menguduskan kami dengan perintah-perintah-Mu dan memerintahkan kami untuk menyalakan lilin Sabat.
translations.it: Benedici tu, Signore nostro Dio, Re dell'universo, che ci hai santificati con i tuoi comandamenti e ci hai ordinato di accendere le candele dello Shabbat.
translations.ja: 主よ、我々の神よ、全宇宙の王よ。あなたは我々を御命令によって聖別し、安息日のろうそくを灯すように命じられました。我々はあなたに感謝します。
translations.ko: 주님, 우리 하나님, 전 세계의 왕이시여, 당신의 계명으로 우리를 거룩하게 하시고, 안식일 초를 켜라고 명령하셨으니, 당신은 복되시어라.
translations.nl: Gezegend zij U, Heer onze God, Koning van het universum, die ons heeft geheiligd met Uw geboden en ons heeft bevolen de Sabbath-kaarsen aan te steken.
translations.pl: Błogosławiony jesteś, Panie, nasz Boże, Królu wszechświata, który uświęcił nas Swoimi przykazaniami i nakazał nam zapalać świece szabatowe.
translations.pt-br: Bendito(a) és Tu, Senhor nosso Deus, Rei do universo, que nos santificaste com teus mandamentos e nos ordenaste acender as velas do Shabat.
translations.ru: Благословен Ты, Господь, Бог наш, Царь вселенной, Который освятил нас своими заповедями и повелел нам зажигать свечи шаббата.
translations.sv: Välsignad vare Du, Herre, vår Gud, Konung över universum, som har helgat oss genom Dina bud och befallt oss att tända sabbatsljusen.
translations.th: ขอพระเจ้าทรงอวยพร พระองค์ คือพระเจ้าของเรา พระราชาแห่งจักรวาล ผู้ทรงทำให้เราบริสุทธิ์ด้วยพระบัญญัติของพระองค์ และทรงบัญชาให้เราจุดเทียนวันสะบาโต
translations.tr: Rabbiniz, Tanrımız, evrenin hükümdarı olan, bizi emirlerinizle kutsayan ve bize Şabat mumlarını yakmayı emreden Rab'be hamdolsun.
translations.uk: Благословенний Ти, Господи, наш Бог, Цар Всесвіту, Який освятив нас Своїми заповідями і повелів нам запалювати свічки шабату.
translations.vi: Phúc cho Ngài, Lạy Chúa chúng tôi, Đấng cai trị vũ trụ, Đấng đã thánh hóa chúng con bằng các điều răn của Ngài và ra lệnh cho chúng con thắp nến Sabbath.
translations.zh-cn: 赞美您，我们的上帝，宇宙之王，您用您的诫命使我们蒙圣，并命令我们点燃安息日的蜡烛。

mantras.name: Blessed are You, Lord our God, King of the universe, who has sanctified us with Your commandments and commanded us to light the Sabbath candle.
mantras.english: Blessed are You, Lord our God, King of the universe, who has sanctified us with Your commandments and commanded us to light the Sabbath candle.
mantras.original: Barukh ata Adonai Eloheinu, Melekh ha'olam, asher kid'shanu b'mitzvotav v'tzivanu l'hadlik ner shel Shabbat
mantras.transliteration: Barukh ata Adonai Eloheinu Melech ha-olam, asher kidashtanu be-mitzvotecha ve-tzivanenu le-hadlik ner shel Shabbat.
mantras.background: A Jewish Shabbat blessing (berakhah) from the Siddur, recited while lighting candles: "Barukh ata Adonai Eloheinu, Melekh ha'olam..." — sanctifying sacred time through divine commandment.
mantras.benefits: Reciting this weekly blessing marks a sacred boundary between ordinary and holy time. The act of sanctification through commanded practice (mitzvot) cultivates mindfulness, devotion, and a felt sense of divine connection central to Jewish tradition.
mantras.tags: jewish, shabbat, blessing, mitzvah, liturgical, consecration, reverence, affirmation

translations.es: Bendito eres, Señor, nuestro Dios, Rey del universo, que nos has santificado con tus mandamientos y nos has ordenado encender la vela del Shabat.
translations.ar: مباركٌ أنتَ يا ربّنا إلهنا، يا ملك الكون، الذي قدّسنا بأوامرِك وأمرنا بإشعال شمعة السبت.
translations.bn:
আপনিBlessed are You, Lord our God, King of the universe, who has sanctified us with Your commandments and commanded us to light the Sabbath candle.

"আপনি, আমাদের ঈশ্বর, মহাবিশ্বের রাজা, যিনি আপনার আদেশ দ্বারা আমাদের পবিত্র করেছেন এবং আমাদের শabbat মোমবাতি জ্বালানোর নির্দেশ দিয়েছেন।"
translations.de: Gepriesen seist Du, Herr, unser Gott, König des Universums, der uns durch Deine Gebote geheiligt hat und uns geboten hat, das Sabbatlicht anzuzünden.
translations.en-us: Blessed are You, Lord our God, King of the universe, who has made us holy with Your commandments and commanded us to kindle the Sabbath candles.
translations.en-gb: Blessed are You, Lord our God, King of the universe, who has made us holy through Your commandments and commanded us to light the Sabbath candles.
translations.fr-fr: Béni sois-Tu, Seigneur notre Dieu, Roi de l'univers, qui nous as consacrés par tes commandements et qui nous a ordonné d'allumer la bougie du Shabbat.
translations.he: ברוך אתה ה', אלוהינו, מלך היקום, אשר קדשנו במצוותיך וציויתנו להדליק את נר השבת.
translations.hi: हम आपको धन्यवाद करते हैं, हे भगवान, हमारे ईश्वर, ब्रह्मांड के राजा, जिन्होंने हमें अपने आज्ञाओं से पवित्र किया है और हमें शबात के मोमबत्ती जलाने का आदेश दिया है।
translations.id: Terpujilah Engkau, Tuhan Allah kami, Raja alam semesta, yang telah menguduskan kami dengan perintah-Mu dan memerintahkan kami untuk menyalakan lilin Sabat.
translations.it: Benedici tu, Signore nostro Dio, Re dell'universo, che ci hai santificati con i tuoi comandamenti e ci hai comandato di accendere la candela del Sabbath.
translations.ja: 主よ、我々の神、全宇宙の王よ。あなたは、あなたの戒めによって私たちを聖別し、安息日のロウソクを灯すように命じられました。あなたは祝福に値します。
translations.ko: 주님, 우리 하나님의 왕이시여, 온 우주를 다스리시는 주님께 감사드립니다. 주님께서는 당신의 계명으로 우리를 거룩하게 하시고, 안식일 초를 켜도록 명령하셨습니다.
translations.nl: Gezegend zij U, Heer onze God, Koning van het heelal, die ons heeft geheiligd met Uw geboden en ons heeft bevolen de Sabbath-kaars aan te steken.
translations.pl: Błogosławiony jesteś, Panie nasz Bóg, Królu wszechświata, który uświęcił nas swoimi przykazaniami i nakazał nam zapalać świece szabatowe.
translations.pt-br: Bendito(a) és Tu, Senhor nosso Deus, Rei do universo, que nos santificaste com Teus mandamentos e nos ordenaste acender a vela do Shabat.
translations.ru: Благословен Ты, Господь, Бог наш, Царь вселенной, Который освятил нас своими заповедями и повелел нам зажечь свечу шаббата.
translations.sv: Välsignad vare Du, Herre vår Gud, Konung över universum, som har helgat oss genom Dina bud och befallt oss att tända sabbatsljuset.
translations.th: พระองค์ทรงเป็นที่สรรเสริญ พระผู้เป็นเจ้า พระบิดาของเรา พระราชาแห่งจักรวาล ผู้ทรงทำให้เราบริสุทธิ์ด้วยพระบัญญัติของพระองค์ และทรงบัญชาให้เราจุดเทียนวันสะบาโต
translations.tr: Rabbiniz, Tanrımız, evrenin hükümdarı olan, bizi emirlerinizle kutsayan ve bize Şabat kandilini yakmamızı emreden, Sizin için şükran duası.
translations.uk: Будь славлений, Господи, наш Бог, Царю всесвіту, Який освятив нас Своїми заповідями і заповідав нам запалювати свічку шабату.
translations.vi: Phước thay Ngài, Đức Chúa Trời chúng con, Đấng Tác Vương của vũ trụ, Đấng đã thánh hóa chúng con qua các điều răn của Ngài và đã ra lệnh cho chúng con thắp nến Sabbath.
translations.zh-cn: 赞美您，我们的上帝，宇宙之王，您以您的诫命使我们蒙圣，并命令我们点燃安息日的蜡烛。

mantras.name: Blessed are You, Lord our God, King of the universe, who has sanctified us with Your commandments and commanded us to listen to the sound of the shofar.
mantras.english: Blessed are You, Lord our God, King of the universe, who has sanctified us with Your commandments and commanded us to listen to the sound of the shofar.
mantras.original: Barukh ata Adonai Eloheinu, melekh ha'olam, asher kid'shanu b'mitzvotav v'tzivanu lishmoa kol shofar
mantras.transliteration: Barukh ata Adonai Eloheinu Melekh ha-olam, asher kidashtanu be-mitzvotav ve-tzivanu leshmo'a kol ha-shofar.
mantras.background:
A Jewish blessing (berakhah) from the Hebrew liturgical tradition, it praises God as King of the universe who commands the shofar's sound—a call to spiritual awakening.
  Grounding: Source lists Jewish blessings beginning "Blessed are You, LORD our God, King of the universe," tied to commandments (mitzvot), matching this shofar blessing's structure.
mantras.benefits: Reciting this blessing before the shofar's call awakens awe and communal renewal. Mindful listening, sanctified by commandment, stills the mind and opens the heart to divine presence and self-reflection.
mantras.tags: jewish, shofar, blessing, sound, liturgical, mitzvot, devotional, divine

translations.es: Bendito seas, Señor, nuestro Dios, Rey del universo, que nos has consagrado con tus mandamientos y nos has ordenado escuchar el sonido del shofar.
translations.ar: باركك يا ربّنا وإلهنا، يا ملك الكون، الذي قدّسنا بأوامرِك وأمرنا أن نصغي لصوت البوق.
translations.de: Gepriesen seist Du, Herr, unser Gott, König des Universums, der uns durch Deine Gebote geheiligt hat und uns geboten hat, dem Klang des Schofar zu lauschen.
translations.en-us: Blessed are You, Lord our God, King of the universe, who has consecrated us with Your commandments and commanded us to heed the sound of the shofar.
translations.en-gb: Blessed are You, Lord our God, King of the universe, who has made us holy through Your commandments and has commanded us to hear the sound of the shofar.
translations.fr-fr: Béni sois-Tu, Seigneur, notre Dieu, Roi de l'univers, qui nous as consacrés par Tes commandements et qui nous a ordonné d'écouter le son du *shofar*.
translations.he: ברוך אתה אדוני אלוהינו, מלך היקום, אשר קדשתנו מצוותיך וציויתנו לשמוע קול שופר.
translations.hi: आप धन्य हैं, हे प्रभु, हमारे परमेश्वर, ब्रह्मांड के राजा, जिन्होंने हमें अपने आदेशों से पवित्र किया है और हमें शोफ़र की ध्वनि सुनने का आदेश दिया है।
translations.id: Terpujilah Engkau, Tuhan kami Allah, Raja alam semesta, yang telah menguduskan kami dengan perintah-Mu dan memerintahkan kami untuk mendengarkan suara shofar.
translations.it: Benedetto tu sei, Signore nostro Dio, Re dell'universo, che ci hai consacrati con i tuoi comandamenti e ci hai ordinato di ascoltare il suono dello shofar.
translations.ja: 主よ、我々の神よ、宇宙の王よ。あなたは、あなたの戒めによって私たちを聖別し、そしてラマの角笛の音を聞くように命じられました。我々はあなたに感謝します。
translations.ko: 주님, 우리 하나님, 전 세계의 왕이신 당신께 감사합니다. 당신은 당신의 계명으로 우리를 거룩하게 하시고, 나팔 소리를 듣도록 명령하셨습니다.
translations.nl: Gezegend zij U, Heer onze God, Koning van het universum, die ons heilig heeft gemaakt met Uw geboden en ons heeft geboden om naar het geluid van de sjofar te luisteren.
translations.pl: Błogosławiony jesteś, Panie nasz Boże, Królu wszechświata, który uświęcił nas swoimi przykazaniami i nakazał nam słuchać dźwięku szofaru.
translations.pt-br: Bendito(a) és Tu, Senhor nosso Deus, Rei do universo, que nos santificaste com Teus mandamentos e nos ordenaste a ouvir o som do shofar.
translations.ru: Благословен Ты, Господь, Бог наш, Царь вселенной, Который освятил нас своими заповедями и повелел нам слушать звук шофара.
translations.sv: Välsignad vare Du, Herre, vår Gud, Konung över universum, som har helgat oss genom dina bud och befallt oss att lyssna till hornets ljud.
translations.th: ข้าขอสรรเสริญพระองค์ พระผู้เป็นเจ้า พระเจ้าของเรา พระมหากษัตริย์แห่งจักรวาล ผู้ได้ทำให้เราบริสุทธิ์ด้วยพระบัญญัติของพระองค์ และทรงบัญชาให้เราฟังเสียงของโชฟาร์
translations.tr: Rabbiniz, Tanrımız, evrenin hükümdarı olan, bizi emirlerinizle kutsayan ve bize şofarın sesini dinlemeyi emreden, Sizin adınızla kutsanmıştır.
translations.uk: Будь славлений, Господи, наш Боже, Царю Всесвіту, Який освятив нас Своїми заповідями та заповідав нам слухати звук шофара.
translations.vi: Kính vinh Ngài, Lạy Chúa chúng tôi, Đấng Tối Cao của vũ trụ, Đấng đã thánh hóa chúng con bằng các điều răn của Ngài và ra lệnh cho chúng con lắng nghe tiếng kèn shofar.
translations.zh-cn: 赞美您，主，我们的上帝，宇宙之王，您以您的诫命使我们分别为圣，并命令我们聆听号角的声音。

mantras.name: Blessed are You, Lord our God, King of the universe, who has sanctified us with Your commandments and commanded us to perform the mitzvah of wearing tefillin.
mantras.english: Blessed are You, Lord our God, King of the universe, who has sanctified us with Your commandments and commanded us to perform the mitzvah of wearing tefillin.
mantras.original: Barukh ata Adonai Eloheinu, melekh ha'olam, asher kid'shanu b'mitzvotav v'tzivanu l'hani'aḥ t'filin
mantras.transliteration: Barukh ata Adonai Eloheinu Melekh ha-olam, asher kidshatanu be-mitzvotei ve-tzivanu le-asot mitzvat tefillin.
mantras.background: A Jewish blessing (berakhah) recited while donning tefillin. It honors God as King who sanctifies Israel through commandments — linking the divine to daily, embodied ritual.
mantras.benefits: Reciting this blessing before wearing tefillin anchors the practitioner in mindful intention (kavanah), transforming a physical act into sacred devotion, fostering discipline, divine connection, and daily spiritual awakening.
mantras.tags: jewish, hebrew, berakhah, tefillin, commandment, liturgical, morning, devotional, gratitude

translations.es: Bendito seas, Señor, nuestro Dios, Rey del universo, que nos has santificado con tus mandamientos y nos has ordenado cumplir el precepto de poner el tefilín.
translations.ar: "تبارك أنت يا ربّنا وإلهنا، يا ملك الكون، الذي قدّسنا بأوامرك وأمرنا بأداء الميتزڤاه (الواجب الديني) ارتداء التّفيلين."
translations.bn:
আপনিBlessed are You, Lord our God, King of the universe, who has sanctified us with Your commandments and commanded us to perform the mitzvah of wearing tefillin.
 blessed are you, Lord, our God, King of the universe, who has sanctified us with Your commandments and commanded us to perform the mitzvah of wearing tefillin.

Answer: তুমি, পরমেশ্বর, আমাদের ঈশ্বর, মহাবিশ্বের রাজা, যিনি তোমার আদেশ দ্বারা আমাদের পবিত্র করেছেন এবং আমাদের "তেফিলিন" পরার নির্দেশ দিয়েছেন, তোমার প্রতি আমরা কৃতজ্ঞ।
translations.de: Gepriesen seist Du, HERR, unser Gott, König des Universums, der uns durch Deine Gebote heilig gemacht hat und uns geboten hat, die Mizwa des Tragens von Tefillin zu erfüllen.
translations.en-us: Blessed are You, Lord our God, King of the universe, who has made us holy with Your commandments and commanded us to perform the mitzvah (commandment) of wearing tefillin.
translations.en-gb: Blessed are You, Lord our God, King of the universe, who has made us holy through Your commandments and commanded us to perform the mitzvah of wearing phylacteries.
translations.fr-fr: Béni sois-Tu, Seigneur notre Dieu, Roi de l'univers, qui nous as consacrés par Tes commandements et qui nous a ordonné d'accomplir le *mitzva* de porter le *tefillin*.
translations.he: ברוך אתה ה', א-להינו, מלך היקום, אשר קדשנו מצוותיך וציויתנו לעשות מצוַת הַתְפִלִין.
translations.hi: हम आपको धन्यवाद करते हैं, हे प्रभु, हमारे परमेश्वर, ब्रह्मांड के राजा, जिन्होंने हमें अपने आदेशों से पवित्र किया है और हमें तेफिलिन पहनने के कर्तव्य का पालन करने का आदेश दिया है।
translations.id: Terpujilah Engkau, Tuhan Allah kami, Raja alam semesta, yang telah menguduskan kami dengan perintah-Mu dan memerintahkan kami untuk melaksanakan mitzvah mengenakan tefilin.
translations.it: Benedetto tu, Signore nostro Dio, Re dell'universo, che ci hai consacrati con i tuoi comandamenti e ci hai comandato di adempiere il precetto di indossare il tefillin.
translations.ja: 主よ、我々の神よ、宇宙の王よ。あなたは我々を御命令によって聖別し、テフィリンを身につけるという善行を命じられました。我々はあなたに感謝します。
translations.ko: 주님, 우리 하나님, 전 우주의 왕이시여, 찬양합니다. 당신의 계명으로 우리를 거룩하게 하시고, 테필린을 착용하는 의무를 주셔서 감사합니다.
translations.nl: Gezegend zij U, Heer onze God, Koning van het universum, Die ons heilig heeft gemaakt met Uw geboden en ons heeft bevolen de mitzvah van het dragen van tefillin te vervullen.
translations.pl: Błogosławiony jesteś, Panie, nasz Boże, Królu wszechświata, który poświęcił nas swoimi przykazaniami i nakazał nam wypełnić nakaz noszenia tefilin.
translations.pt-br: Bendito és Tu, Senhor nosso Deus, Rei do universo, que nos santificaste com teus mandamentos e nos ordenaste a cumprir o mitzvá de usar o talitín.
translations.ru: Благословен Ты, Господь, Бог наш, Царь вселенной, Который освятил нас своими заповедями и повелел нам исполнять заповедь надевания тефилин.
translations.sv: Välsignad vare Du, Herre, vår Gud, Konung över universum, som har helgat oss genom Dina bud och befallt oss att utföra budet att bära tefillin.
translations.th: ข้าขอสรรเสริญพระองค์ พระผู้เป็นเจ้าของเรา พระมหากษัตริย์แห่งจักรวาล ผู้ทรงทำให้เราบริสุทธิ์ด้วยพระบัญญัติของพระองค์ และทรงบัญชาให้เราปฏิบัติธรรมเนียมการพันเทฟิลิน
translations.tr: İlahi, Rabbimiz, evrenin hükümdarı, bizi emirlerinizle kutsayan ve bize tefillin takma gibi bir farzı yerine getirmeyi emreden, sana hamdolsun.
translations.uk: Благословенний Ти, Господи, наш Бог, Цар Всесвіту, Який освятив нас Своїми заповідями і заповідав нам виконувати заповіт про надягання тефілін.
translations.vi: Kính vạn phúc, Lạy Chúa, Đức Chúa Trời của chúng con, Đấng cai trị vũ trụ, Ngài đã thánh hóa chúng con bằng các điều răn của Ngài và ra lệnh cho chúng con thực hiện việc đeo phylacteries (tefillin).
translations.zh-cn: 赞美您，主，我们的上帝，宇宙之王，您以您的诫命使我们蒙圣，并命令我们遵行佩戴经文匣的诫命。

mantras.name: Blessed are You, Lord our God, King of the universe, who has sanctified us with Your commandments and commanded us to wrap ourselves in fringes (tzitzit).
mantras.english: Blessed are You, Lord our God, King of the universe, who has sanctified us with Your commandments and commanded us to wrap ourselves in fringes (tzitzit).
mantras.original: Barukh ata Adonai Eloheinu, melekh ha'olam, asher kid'shanu b'mitzvotav v'tzivanu l'hit'atef ba'tzitzit
mantras.transliteration: Barukh atah, Adonai Eloheinu, Melekh ha-olam, asher kidshatanu be-mitzvotav ve-tzivanu le-lavi'at begadiyl.
mantras.background: A Jewish blessing (berakhah) from the Hebrew Siddur, recited before wrapping in tzitzit (fringes). It begins with the universal formula "Barukh ata Adonai..." — sanctifying a mitzvah through divine command.
mantras.benefits: Reciting this blessing cultivates mindfulness and sacred embodiment — wrapping in tzitzit transforms a daily act into a conscious covenant, sanctifying ordinary moments and fostering spiritual identity, belonging, and divine connection.
mantras.tags: jewish, hebrew, blessing, liturgical, commandment, mitzvah, tzitzit, daily, sanctification, gratitude

translations.es: Bendito seas, Señor, nuestro Dios, Rey del universo, que nos has santificado con tus mandamientos y nos has ordenado cubrirnos con flecos (tzitzit).
translations.ar: باركك يا ربّنا وإلهنا، يا ملك الكون، الذي قدّسنا بأوامرِك وأمرنا أن نرتدي الأطراف (التّسّويت).
translations.bn:
আপনিBlessed are You, Lord our God, King of the universe, who has sanctified us with Your commandments and commanded us to wrap ourselves in fringes (tzitzit).

"আপনি, আমাদের ঈশ্বর, মহাবিশ্বের রাজা, যিনি আপনার আদেশ দ্বারা আমাদের পবিত্র করেছেন এবং আমাদের প্রান্তিক বস্ত্র (ৎzitৎ) পরিধান করতে আদেশ করেছেন।"
translations.de: Gepriesen seist Du, Herr, unser Gott, König des Universums, der uns durch Deine Gebote geheiligt hat und uns geboten hat, uns mit Fransen (tzitzit) zu bedecken.
translations.en-us: Blessed are You, Lord our God, King of the universe, who has made us holy through Your commandments and commanded us to don fringes (tzitzit).
translations.en-gb: Blessed are You, Lord our God, King of the universe, who has made us holy through Your commandments and commanded us to wear garments adorned with fringes (tzitzit).
translations.fr-fr: Voici une traduction française de la phrase : "Béni sois-Tu, Seigneur, notre Dieu, Roi de l'univers, qui nous a sanctifiés par tes commandements et qui nous a ordonné de nous couvrir de franges (tzitzit)."
translations.he: ברוך אתה ה', א-להינו, מלך היקום, אשר קדשנו במצוותיך וציויתנו להרכיב ציציות.
translations.hi: आप धन्य हैं, हे प्रभु, हमारे ईश्वर, जगत के राजा, जिन्होंने हमें अपने आज्ञाओं से पवित्र किया है और हमें किनारों (त्ज़ित्ज़िट) से खुद को ढकने का आदेश दिया है।
translations.id: Terpujilah Engkau, Tuhan Allah kami, Raja alam semesta, yang telah menguduskan kami dengan perintah-Mu dan memerintahkan kami untuk mengenakan jubah berumbai (tzitzit).
translations.it: Benedetto tu, Signore nostro Dio, Re dell'universo, che ci hai santificati con i tuoi comandamenti e ci hai comandato di avvolgerci nelle frange (tzitzit).
translations.ja: 主（しゅ）、私たちの神であり、全宇宙の王であるあなたは、あなたの戒めによって私たちを聖別し、私たちにフリンジ（ツィツィト）を身につけるように命じられました。我々はあなたを賛えます。
translations.ko: 주님, 우리 하나님, 전 세계의 왕이시여, 당신의 계명으로 우리를 거룩하게 하시고, 우리에게 수술(tzitzit)을 두르라고 명령하셨으니, 당신은 복되시옵니다.
translations.nl: Gezegend zij U, Heer onze God, Koning van het heelal, Die ons heeft geheiligd met Uw geboden en ons bevolen heeft om ons te omhullen met franje (tzitzit).
translations.pl: Błogosławiony jesteś, Panie, nasz Boże, Królu wszechświata, który uświęcił nas swoimi przykazaniami i nakazał nam nosić frędzle (tzit).
translations.pt-br: Bendito és Tu, Senhor nosso Deus, Rei do universo, que nos santificaste com Teus mandamentos e nos ordenaste a usar a orla (tzitzit).
translations.ru: Благословен Ты, Господь, Бог наш, Царь вселенной, Который освятил нас своими заповедями и повелел нам обвить себя бахромой (цицит).
translations.sv: Välsignad vare Du, Herre, vår Gud, Konung över universum, som har helgat oss genom dina bud och befallt oss att bära prydnadsremsor (tzitzit).
translations.th: ขอพระเจ้า ผู้เป็นพระเจ้าของเรา พระราชาแห่งจักรวาล จงอวยพร พวกเรา ผู้ซึ่งพระองค์ได้ทำให้บริสุทธิ์ด้วยบัญญัติของพระองค์ และทรงบัญชาให้เราสวมใส่เครื่องแต่งกายที่มีพู่ (tzitzit).
translations.tr: Rabbeŋiz, Tanrımız, evrenin Hükümdarı olan, bizi emirlerinizle kutsayan ve bizi 'tzitzit' adı verilen, kenarlı örtülerle örtünmemizi emreden size şükürler olsun.
translations.uk:
Будь ласка, ось переклад фрази на українську мову:
"Благословенний Ти, Господи, наш Бог, Цар всесвіту, Який освятив нас Своїми заповітами і повелів нам одягати таліс (цицит)."
translations.vi: Kính trọng Ngài, Thượng Đế của chúng con, Vua của vũ trụ, Đấng đã thánh hóa chúng con bằng các điều răn của Ngài và ra lệnh cho chúng con mặc y phục có sợi tua (tzitzit).
translations.zh-cn: 赞美您，主，我们的上帝，宇宙之王，您以您的诫命使我们蒙圣，并命令我们用带有边 fringe (tzitzit) 的衣服来遮蔽自己。

mantras.name: Blessed are You, Lord our God, King of the universe, who has sanctified us with Your commandments and desired us, and with love and willingness, we have been granted Your holy Sabbath.
mantras.english: Blessed are You, Lord our God, King of the universe, who has sanctified us with Your commandments and desired us, and with love and willingness, we have been granted Your holy Sabbath.
mantras.original: Barukh ata Adonai Eloheinu, melekh ha'olam, asher kideshanu be'mitzvotav ve'ratza banu, ve'shabbat kodsho b'ahava uv'ratzon hinchilanu
mantras.transliteration: Barukh ata Adonai Eloheinu Melekh ha-olam, asher kidshatanu be-mitzvotecha ve-shemucha, uvahava u-re'shonut, natan lanu Shabbat hakodesh.
mantras.background: From Jewish Shabbat liturgy, this Kiddush blessing sanctifies the holy day with love. Its Hebrew roots reveal a covenant of divine choosing, rest, and sacred time.
mantras.benefits: Reciting Kiddush weekly anchors identity through sacred time, fostering gratitude, communal belonging, and mindful rest. Its themes of sanctification and love invite a weekly psychological reset rooted in covenant and creation.
mantras.tags: jewish, kiddush, blessing, shabbat, adonai, sanctification, liturgical, ceremonial, gratitude, remembrance, ashkenazi

translations.es: Bendito seas, Señor, nuestro Dios, Rey del universo, que nos has santificado con tus mandamientos y nos has elegido, y con amor y voluntad, nos has concedido tu santo Sabbat.
translations.ar: مباركٌ أنتَ يا ربّنا وإلهنا، يا ملكَ الكون، الذي قدّسنا بأوامرِك، وقد منحنا "السبت" المقدس الخاص بك برغبةٍ وحبٍ.
translations.bn: আপনি আমাদের ঈশ্বর, মহাবিশ্বের রাজা, যিনি আপনার আদেশ দ্বারা আমাদের পবিত্র করেছেন, এবং আপনার ইচ্ছা অনুসারে, আমরা আপনার পবিত্র শabbat লাভ করেছি—এইভাবে আমরা আপনার প্রতি কৃতজ্ঞতা প্রকাশ করি।
translations.de: Gepriesen seist Du, Herr, unser Gott, König des Universums, der uns durch Deine Gebote geheiligt hat und uns erwählt hat, und in Liebe und Freude wurde uns Dein heiliger Sabbat gewährt.
translations.en-us: Blessed are You, Lord our God, King of the universe, who has sanctified us with Your commandments and chosen us, and with love and joy, we have been granted Your holy Sabbath.
translations.en-gb: Blessed are You, Lord our God, King of the universe, who has sanctified us with Your commandments and chosen us, and with love and willingness, we have been granted Your holy Sabbath.
translations.fr-fr:
Voici une traduction possible en français :

"Béni sois-Tu, Seigneur, notre Dieu, Roi de l'univers, qui nous a sanctifiés par tes commandements et qui nous a élus. Et avec amour et joie, nous avons reçu ton saint jour de repos, le Shabbat."
translations.he: ברוך אתה ה', א-להינו, מלך היקום, אשר קדשנו במצוותיך ורצית בנו, ובאהבה ובטוב לב נתנה לנו שבת קודשך.
translations.hi: "धन्यवाद, हे हमारे ईश्वर, ब्रह्मांड के राजा, जिन्होंने अपने आदेशों से हमें पवित्र किया और हमें प्रेम और इच्छा के साथ आपका पवित्र शबात प्रदान किया।"
translations.id: Terpujilah Engkau, Tuhan Allah kami, Raja alam semesta, yang telah menguduskan kami dengan perintah-Mu dan yang telah memilih kami, dan dengan cinta dan kerelaan, kami telah dianugerahi Sabat Kudus-Mu.
translations.it: Benedetto tu, Signore nostro Dio, Re dell'universo, che ci hai santificato con i tuoi comandamenti e ci hai scelto, e con amore e gioia ci hai concesso il tuo santo Sabbath.
translations.ja: 主よ、私たちの神、宇宙の王よ。あなたは私たちにあなたの戒めを与え、私たちを愛し、その愛と喜びをもって、私たちはあなたの聖なる安息日を授けられました。
translations.ko: 주님, 우리 하나님, 전 우주의 왕이시여, 당신의 계명으로 우리를 거룩하게 하시고, 우리를 향한 사랑으로 우리에게 당신의 거룩한 안식일을 주셔서 감사합니다.
translations.nl: Gezegend zij U, Heer onze God, Koning van het universum, die ons heeft geheiligd met Uw geboden en ons heeft begenadigd, en met liefde en vrije wil is ons de Heilige Sabbat geschonken.
translations.pl: Błogosławiony jesteś, Panie, nasz Boże, Królu wszechświata, który uświęcił nas swoimi przykazaniami i który, z miłością i dobrowolnością, obdarzył nas Twoją świętą Szabat.
translations.pt-br: Bendito(a) sejais, Senhor, nosso Deus, Rei do universo, que nos santificou com vossos mandamentos e que nos agraciou. E com amor e alegria, fomos agraciados com vosso santo Shabat.
translations.ru: Благословен Ты, Господь, Бог наш, Царь вселенной, Который освятил нас своими заповедями и пожелал нам, и с любовью и радостью мы удостоены Твоего святого Шаббата.
translations.sv: Välsignad vare Du, Herre, vår Gud, Konung över universum, som har helgat oss genom dina bud och som, med kärlek och villighet, har förlänat oss din heliga sabbat.
translations.th: ข้าขอสรรเสริญพระองค์ พระผู้เป็นเจ้าแห่งจักรวาล พระมหากษัตริย์ ผู้ทรงทำให้เราบริสุทธิ์ด้วยพระบัญญัติของพระองค์ และทรงรักเรา พร้อมทั้งด้วยความรักและความเต็มใจ เราได้รับการมอบวันสะบาโตอันศักดิ์สิทธิ์ของพระองค์
translations.tr: Rabbiniz, Tanrımız, evrenin hükümdarı olan, bizi emirlerinizle kutsayan ve bizi seven, ve sevgi ve gönüllülükle, kutsal Şabatınızı bize bahşeden, size şükranlarımızı sunuyoruz.
translations.uk: Благословен Ти, Господи, наш Бог, Цар всесвіту, Який освятив нас Своїми заповідями, і Який, з любов'ю та з радістю, подарував нам Твою святу Суботу.
translations.vi: Kính cẩn, Ngài là Chúa, Đức Chúa Trời chúng con, Đấng cai trị vũ trụ, Đấng đã thánh hóa chúng con bằng các điều răn của Ngài, và với tình yêu thương và sự sẵn lòng, chúng con đã được ban cho ngày Sabbath thiêng liêng của Ngài.
translations.zh-cn: 赞美您，主，我们的上帝，宇宙之王，您以您的诫命使我们分别为圣，并以爱和意愿，我们被赋予了您神圣的安息日。

mantras.name: Blessed are You, Lord our God, King of the universe, who performed miracles for our ancestors in those days, in this season.
mantras.english: Blessed are You, Lord our God, King of the universe, who performed miracles for our ancestors in those days, in this season.
mantras.original: Barukh ata Adonai Eloheinu, melekh ha'olam, she'asa nisim la'avoteinu ba'yamim ha'heim ba'z'man ha'ze
mantras.transliteration: Barukh ata Adonai Eloheinu Melech ha-olam, אשר asita nifla'ot la-avoteinu be-yamim ha-he'm, be-mo'ed zeh.
mantras.background: A Jewish blessing from the Siddur, recited at Chanukah. It literally thanks God for miracles performed for ancestors "in this season," connecting past to present.
mantras.benefits: Reciting this Chanukah blessing fosters gratitude and historical memory, connecting the practitioner to ancestral resilience. It cultivates humility before the divine and wonder at miraculous deliverance, renewing hope each season.
mantras.tags: jewish, hebrew, blessing, liturgical, gratitude, miracles, chanukah, purim, praise, divine

translations.es: Bendito seas, Señor, nuestro Dios, Rey del universo, que hiciste maravillas para nuestros antepasados en aquellos días, en esta época.
translations.ar: "تبارك أنت يا ربّنا وإلهنا، يا ملك الكون، الذي أجريتَ معجزات لآبائنا في تلك الأيام، وفي هذا الوقت."
translations.bn: ঈশ্বর, আমাদের ঈশ্বর, মহাবিশ্বের রাজা, যিনি আমাদের পূর্বপুরুষদের জন্য সেই দিনগুলোতে, এই সময়ে অলৌকিক কাজ করেছেন, আপনার প্রতি আমরা কৃতজ্ঞ।
translations.de: Gepriesen seist Du, HERR, unser Gott, König des Universums, der für unsere Vorfahren an jenen Tagen, in dieser Zeit, Wunder vollbracht hat.
translations.en-us: Blessed are You, Lord our God, Ruler of the universe, who has wrought wonders for our ancestors in those days, in this season.
translations.en-gb: Blessed are You, Lord our God, King of the universe, who wrought wonders for our ancestors in those days, at this time of year.
translations.fr-fr: Béni sois-Tu, Seigneur, notre Dieu, Roi de l'univers, qui as accompli des merveilles pour nos ancêtres en ces jours, en cette saison.
translations.he: ברוך אתה ה', אלוהינו, מלך היקום, אשר עשית ניסים לאבותינו בימים ההם ובזמן הזה.
translations.hi: "धन्य हैं आप, हमारे परमेश्वर, जगत के राजा, जिन्होंने हमारे पूर्वजों के लिए, उन दिनों में, इसी ऋतु में, चमत्कारी कार्य किए।"
translations.id: Terpujilah Engkau, Tuhan Allah kami, Raja alam semesta, yang telah melakukan mukjizat bagi nenek moyang kami pada masa itu, pada musim ini.
translations.it: Benedici tu, Signore, nostro Dio, Re dell'universo, che hai compiuto meraviglie per i nostri antenati in quei giorni, in questa stagione.
translations.ja: 主よ、私たちの神よ、全宇宙の王よ。あなたは、過去の世代のために、そしてこの時期に、奇跡を成し遂げた方。祝福あれ。
translations.ko: 주님, 우리 하나님, 전 우주의 왕이시여, 당신은 과거 우리 조상들에게, 그리고 오늘 이 계절에 놀라운 일을 행하셨습니다.
translations.nl: Gezegend zij U, Heer onze God, Koning van het universum, die wonderbaarlijke daden verrichtte voor onze voorouders in die dagen, in dit seizoen.
translations.pl: Błogosławiony jesteś, Panie, nasz Boże, Królu wszechświata, który uczyniłeś cuda dla naszych przodków w tamtych dniach, w tym czasie.
translations.pt-br: Bendito és Tu, Senhor nosso Deus, Rei do universo, que fizeste maravilhas para os nossos antepassados naqueles dias, nesta época.
translations.ru: Благословен Ты, Господь, Бог наш, Царь вселенной, Который совершил чудеса для наших предков в те дни, в это время года.
translations.sv: Välsignad vare Du, Herre, vår Gud, Konung över universum, som utförde under för våra förfäder i de dagar som var, under denna tid.
translations.th: ขอพระเจ้าทรงอวยพร พระองค์ ผู้เป็นพระเจ้าของเรา พระมหากษัตริย์แห่งจักรวาล ผู้ทรงกระทำปาฏิหาริย์ให้แก่บรรพบุรุษของเรา ในวันนั้น ในฤดูกาลนี้
translations.tr: Rabbiniz, Tanrımız, evrenin kralı olan, atalarımıza o günlerde, bu zamanda mucizeler bahşeden, Sizin için şükürler olsun.
translations.uk: Благословенний Ти, Господи, наш Бог, Цар Всесвіту, Який творив чудеса для наших предків у ті дні, у цю пору.
translations.vi: Xin Ngài ban phước, Lạy Chúa chúng con, Đấng Tối Cao của vũ trụ, Đấng đã thực hiện những điều kỳ diệu cho tổ tiên chúng con vào những ngày xưa, trong mùa này.
translations.zh-cn: 赞美您，主，我们的上帝，宇宙之王，您在过去的日子里，在这个季节里，为我们的祖先行了奇迹。

mantras.name: Echad
mantras.english: One
mantras.transliteration: Echad
mantras.abstract:
Echad — One — is the final and most weighted word of the Shema, prolonged in traditional prayer while the practitioner holds in awareness the unity of the divine and the unity of all existence. It is simultaneously the simplest and most profound word in the Jewish liturgical vocabulary, condensing the entirety of Jewish theology into a single syllable: the absolute non-duality of reality at its source.

The three letters of Echad — Aleph, Chet, Dalet — are given Kabbalistic significance. The Aleph (1) represents the divine unity; the Chet (8) represents the seven directions of space plus their transcendent source; and the Dalet (4) represents the four cardinal directions of the earthly realm. Together they encode the divine One permeating all dimensions of space-time.

In the Nusach Sefard tradition, the word Echad is held for the count of ten — representing the ten sephirot of Kabbalah — while the practitioner meditates on God's dominion extending through all levels of reality. This brief meditation within the Shema is considered by many masters the most concentrated moment of Jewish prayer, a point at which normal consciousness is temporarily suspended.

Rebbe Nachman of Breslov taught a meditation on Echad for practitioners struggling with confusion or fragmentation. By simply repeating this one word slowly, feeling its meaning rather than thinking about it, the mind begins to release its habitual division of experience into problems, conflicts, and distinctions. The single word acts as a solvent of multiplicity.

In contemporary Jewish mindfulness, Echad is used as a single-word mantra during sitting meditation. Unlike longer formulas that engage the thinking mind, the single word Echad is brief enough to be held in pure awareness. As it is repeated slowly, the gap between repetitions expands, and in those gaps, practitioners report touching the silence that is the experiential correlate of divine unity.

translations.en: One
translations.zh: 一
translations.es: Uno

mantras.name: Ein Sof
mantras.english: The Infinite — Without End
mantras.transliteration: Ein Sof
mantras.abstract:
Ein Sof — Without End, The Infinite — is the Kabbalistic term for the ultimate divine reality beyond all names, attributes, and descriptions. In the Zohar and subsequent Kabbalistic literature, Ein Sof is the absolute ground of being, so utterly beyond conception that not even the divine names of the Torah can be applied to it literally. It is from Ein Sof that all the divine emanations (sephirot) emerge, and toward Ein Sof that all existence ultimately tends.

The term first appears systematically in the writings of Rabbi Azriel of Gerona in the thirteenth century. Its emergence represents a turning point in Jewish theology: by insisting that God's ultimate nature is beyond all positive description — beyond wisdom, beyond love, beyond even being in any sense we understand — the Kabbalists preserved the genuine ineffability of the divine while still mapping the terrain of divine emanation.

Meditating on Ein Sof is an advanced practice in the Kabbalah. Unlike mantras that invoke specific divine qualities, contemplating Ein Sof requires the meditator to voluntarily dissolve all conceptual handles. One moves through the sephirot (divine attributes) as stepping stones, releasing each in turn, until one approaches the awareness of pure, boundless, attribute-free existence. This practice is analogous to apophatic theology in Christian mysticism and nirguna meditation in Advaita Vedanta.

Rabbi Moshe Cordovero in his Pardes Rimonim describes Ein Sof as the light that illuminates all the divine vessels from within. The sephirot are not separate from Ein Sof but are the way Ein Sof makes itself accessible to finite consciousness — like a spectrum of colors that is still, at its source, pure white light. To meditate on Ein Sof is to trace those colors back to their undivided source.

In contemporary Neo-Hasidic practice, Ein Sof is approached through the experience of natural awe — standing before the ocean, gazing at a starfield, or sitting in old-growth forest and feeling one's own smallness in the face of the vast. This somatic encounter with the unbounded is understood as a direct meeting with Ein Sof, as the boundary of individual identity temporarily becomes permeable to the infinite.

translations.en: The Infinite — Without End
translations.zh: 无限——无终无止
translations.es: Lo Infinito — Sin Fin

mantras.name: Elohim
mantras.english: God — the Power of Powers, the plural unity of divine forces
mantras.transliteration: Elohim
mantras.abstract:
Elohim is one of the primary Hebrew divine names appearing throughout the Hebrew Bible, most famously as the name used in the first chapter of Genesis: 'In the beginning Elohim created the heavens and the earth.' Grammatically plural but theologically singular, Elohim encodes within its form the mystery of divine unity expressing itself through infinite multiplicity — the One that contains and manifests as the many.

The numerical value (gematria) of Elohim is 86, identical to the value of HaTeva (Nature). This equivalence, noted by Kabbalists from the Baal Shem Tov onward, suggests that Nature and the divine name Elohim are the same reality perceived from different levels of awareness. Those who see only natural processes see HaTeva; those who perceive the divine intelligence within those same processes see Elohim.

In the sephirotic system, Elohim is the name associated with the sephira of Binah (Understanding) — the divine mother, the womb of creation, the formative intelligence that shapes the infinite flow of divine light into the specific forms of existence. Reciting or meditating on Elohim is an invocation of this formative, nurturing, structuring aspect of divine intelligence.

The plural form of Elohim has invited theological reflection throughout Jewish history. While mainstream Judaism reads it as a 'plural of majesty,' Kabbalists read the plurality as pointing to the divine attribute-forces (sephirot) that are multiple in their manifestation but one in their source. Every divine quality — wisdom, understanding, love, judgment, beauty — is an Eloah (singular), and Elohim is their unity.

Meditation on Elohim involves holding in awareness the paradox of one and many, unity and diversity. Practitioners are invited to notice the same dynamic in their own experience: there are many thoughts, emotions, sensations — and yet they all arise in a single awareness. That awareness is the experiential correlate of Elohim — the one field in which all the many arise.

translations.en: God — the Power of Powers, the plural unity of divine forces
translations.zh: 神——诸力之力，神圣力量的多元一体
translations.es: Dios — el Poder de Poderes, la unidad plural de fuerzas divinas

mantras.name: He was, He is, and He is to be.
mantras.english: He was, He is, and He is to be.
mantras.transliteration: היה, הוא, והוא יהיה.
mantras.background: From Jewish scripture, "He was, He is, and He is to be" captures God's eternal nature. Found in the Torah and Psalms, it invites deep contemplative alignment with the Divine.
mantras.benefits: Rooted in Torah and Psalms, this mantra's rhythmic repetition cultivates deep receptivity, humility, and awe. Like *ajapa japa*, it transcends thought, aligning practitioner with life's source and inviting the soul into sacred, transformative silence.
mantras.tags: jewish, shema, kabbalah, hinani, adonai, breathwork, meditation, revelation, submission, service, tetragrammaton, mystical

mantras.name: Hear, Israel, the Lord our God, the Lord is one.
mantras.english: Hear, Israel, the Lord our God, the Lord is one.
mantras.original: Shema Yisrael Adonai Eloheinu Adonai Echad
mantras.transliteration: Ṡəmʿu, Yisraʼel, ʾAdonay Eloheinu, ʾAdonay ʾechad.
mantras.background: Rooted in Judaism, the Shema—"Hear, O Israel, the Lord our God, the Lord is One"—is chanted daily at dawn and dusk, uniting all souls in divine oneness.
mantras.benefits: Daily Shema chanting cultivates deep listening, dissolves separation, and awakens unity consciousness. Through intentional repetition, it transforms prayer into embodied truth—opening the heart to divine oneness and interconnection.
mantras.tags: jewish, prayer, chanting, adonai, unity, listening, consciousness, healing, mantra, incantation, blessing

translations.es: Escucha, Israel, el Señor, nuestro Dios, el Señor es uno.
