mantras.name: Hineni
mantras.english: Here I Am — complete, present, ready
mantras.transliteration: Hineni
mantras.abstract:
Hineni — Here I Am — is the single most recurring response to divine call in the Hebrew Bible. Abraham says Hineni when God calls him to sacrifice Isaac. Moses says Hineni at the burning bush. Samuel says Hineni when he hears his name called in the night. Isaiah says Hineni when God asks 'Whom shall I send?' The word appears at every defining moment of divine-human encounter, representing the foundational human response to transcendence: full presence, availability, readiness.

The word is deceptively simple: it is the word 'here' (hen) plus the first-person suffix 'ni' (me). 'I am here.' Yet in its biblical contexts it carries enormous weight. To say Hineni is not merely to report one's physical location; it is to offer one's entire being — to stand undivided, without reservation, in the face of whatever the moment requires. It is the anti-distraction, the total mobilization of self in response to a call.

In Hasidic thought, Hineni is the stance that God asks of human beings in every moment, not only in dramatic divine encounters. Every instant presents a call — the need of another person, the opportunity for a kind act, the voice of conscience — and the spiritual life consists of being present enough to hear and respond with Hineni. The masters teach that most human suffering comes from not being present — from living in memory or anticipation rather than here, in this moment.

Contemplative use of Hineni involves sitting quietly and, on each breath, offering it as a Hineni — a gift of presence. The inhale is the call; the exhale is the response. Gradually the boundaries between the one who is present and the presence itself soften. Practitioners find that consistent Hineni practice develops what Jewish teachers call kavanah (directed intention), the quality of full engagement that distinguishes routine from sacred action.

Rabbi Abraham Joshua Heschel wrote that wonder is the beginning of spiritual life and that what we need is not more information but more presence. Hineni encodes this teaching: before wisdom, before knowledge, before any spiritual attainment, one must simply be here. The word is a practice of arriving — again and again, breath by breath — in the only place where God and life can actually be encountered: the present moment.

translations.en: Here I Am — complete, present, ready
translations.zh: 我在这里——完全、当下、准备好了
translations.es: Aquí estoy — completo, presente, listo

mantras.name: L'Chaim
mantras.english: To Life!
mantras.transliteration: L'chaim
mantras.abstract:
L'Chaim — To Life! — is the universal Jewish toast and an affirmation that has far exceeded its role as a drinking gesture to become a complete spiritual stance. To say L'Chaim is to align oneself with the force of life itself — to choose vitality, engagement, and the affirmation of existence over withdrawal, resignation, or despair. In a tradition where the preservation of life (pikuach nefesh) overrides virtually all other commandments, L'Chaim is both theology and daily practice.

Hasidic masters teach that the divine name Chayyim (Life) is one of the most direct names of God because God is not merely alive but is the very principle of life itself — 'the living God' (Elohim Chayyim). To say L'Chaim is therefore to invoke and affirm divine presence: every instance of life, in any creature, is a flame from the fire of divine vitality. The toast is an act of recognizing the sacred in the biological.

In times of grief and loss — which have been frequent in Jewish history — L'Chaim takes on deeper resonance. To say L'Chaim at a shiva (mourning house), as Jewish tradition encourages, is an act of radical affirmation in the face of death. It says: even in this darkness, we choose life. Even now, we assert that the force of existence is stronger than the fact of death. This is not denial but a fierce, deliberate orientation of the soul toward the source of being.

As a daily mantra, L'Chaim can be whispered as a greeting to each new moment — each breath, each sunrise, each encounter. It transforms the repetitive into the revelatory. Teachers in the Jewish Renewal movement, particularly Rabbi Zalman Schachter-Shalomi, used L'Chaim as a practice of 'enlivening' — bringing full presence to whatever one is doing.

The phrase appears in its full form across Jewish liturgy and folk tradition. Its endurance across centuries and vastly different cultural contexts — from the shtetls of Eastern Europe to modern Israel to Jewish communities worldwide — testifies to the deep human need for an affirmation of life that is simple, joyful, and unconditional.

translations.en: To Life!
translations.zh: 为了生命！
translations.es: ¡Por la vida!

mantras.name: Modeh Ani
mantras.english: I offer thanks before You, living and eternal King
mantras.original: מוֹדֶה אֲנִי לְפָנֶיךָ מֶלֶךְ חַי וְקַיָּם
mantras.transliteration: Modeh ani lefanecha melech chai ve'kayyam
mantras.abstract:
Modeh Ani is the first prayer of the Jewish day, recited immediately upon waking before even rising from bed — before washing hands, before the morning blessings, before any other act. Its form deliberately places 'Modeh' (I am grateful) before 'Ani' (I), because before asserting one's existence, one acknowledges the source of that existence. The day begins not with 'I' but with gratitude.

The prayer thanks God for returning the soul to the body after the sleep that Jewish tradition calls 'one-sixtieth of death.' Each morning is understood as a micro-resurrection — the soul is received by divine hands during sleep and returned at waking with renewed potential. Modeh Ani acknowledges this gift and orients the waking consciousness immediately toward gratitude as the primary stance toward existence.

Notably, Modeh Ani contains no divine name, making it the rare prayer that can be recited even in a state of ritual impurity (before morning washing). The rabbis designed it this way deliberately: the first act of consciousness every day should be gratitude, without any ritual prerequisite. Gratitude requires nothing but awareness and a willing heart.

Hasidic masters use Modeh Ani as an entry into 'the point of truth' — the moment before the habitual self reconstructs itself after sleep. For a brief window upon waking, the accumulated narratives of identity, grievance, and anxiety have not yet reassembled. Modeh Ani catches this window and fills it with gratitude and awe, establishing the day's foundational tone before the ego has a chance to impose its agenda.

Contemporary practitioners of Jewish mindfulness extend the Modeh Ani into a brief morning body scan and gratitude practice. After reciting the traditional lines, they spend a few minutes noticing what functions in the body — breath, heartbeat, senses — and mentally offering each as an occasion for gratitude. Research in positive psychology validates this ancient practice: daily gratitude exercises first thing in the morning demonstrably shift baseline mood and increase resilience.

translations.en: I offer thanks before You, living and eternal King
translations.zh: 我感谢祢，永活永存的君王
translations.es: Te doy gracias ante Ti, Rey eterno y viviente

mantras.name: Ribbono Shel Olam
mantras.english: Master of the Universe
mantras.transliteration: Ribbono shel Olam
mantras.abstract:
Ribbono shel Olam — Master of the Universe — is perhaps the most intimate divine address in Jewish prayer. While other divine names carry formal theological weight, this phrase is used in spontaneous, personal supplication, as if speaking directly to a sovereign who is also a friend. It appears throughout the Talmud in the mouths of the sages when making personal requests, and in Hasidic literature it has become the quintessential cry of the heart.

The Baal Shem Tov, founder of Hasidism, taught that the three words Ribbono shel Olam, uttered with simple sincerity, were sufficient to open the heavens. He emphasized that God does not require elaborate petition — the heart's plain, direct address is the most powerful prayer of all. His student Rebbe Nachman of Breslov built on this, developing the practice of hitbonenut — personal, informal, vernacular prayer — in which speaking to God as 'Ribbono shel Olam' in one's native language was actively encouraged.

Kabbalistically, the phrase encodes a complete cosmological statement. 'Ribbono' (Master) affirms divine sovereignty over all forces. 'Shel' (of) establishes relationship — God is not distant but has a specific connection to creation. 'Olam' (Universe) comes from the root 'alam' meaning 'hidden' — the universe is the dimension in which God's presence is concealed so that creation can have independent existence.

In Hasidic meditation, Ribbono shel Olam is used as an entry into deveikut — cleaving to God. The practice involves releasing all other thoughts and standing, as it were, in the direct awareness of divine presence, addressing God with these three words repeatedly until the separation between the self and its source diminishes.

Modern practitioners use Ribbono shel Olam as a reset phrase — a way of interrupting the momentum of habitual thought and reestablishing orientation. When life feels chaotic or meaningless, these three words place the practitioner back in relationship with the One who holds all of it. The simplicity is the point: in moments of crisis, elaborate language fails; the soul reaches for the most direct address it knows.

translations.en: Master of the Universe
translations.zh: 宇宙的主宰
translations.es: Señor del Universo

mantras.name: Shalom
mantras.english: Peace, Wholeness, and Complete Well-being
mantras.transliteration: Shalom
mantras.abstract:
Shalom is among the most widely known Hebrew words in the world — the universal Jewish greeting and farewell, a word that means simultaneously peace, wholeness, completeness, and well-being. Its root, shalem, connects to health, integrity, and the absence of deficiency. Shalom is therefore not merely the cessation of conflict but a positive state of harmony in which every part is in its proper relationship to the whole.

In Kabbalistic tradition, Shalom is one of the names of God — HaShalom, the One who is Peace. This identification is profound: God is not described as the giver of peace but as Peace itself. Just as God is the source of all light, life, and being, so too is God the source of all shalom. Human experience of peace is therefore a direct contact with the divine nature.

The Mishnah concludes with a teaching that the only vessel that holds blessing is peace. This is interpreted to mean that all other spiritual attainments — wisdom, piety, scholarship, charity — can only be received and retained in a vessel of inner peace. Without shalom, the capacity to contain blessing is absent, no matter how worthy the person.

In contemplative practice, the Hebrew greeting 'Shalom Aleichem' (Peace be upon you) is used as a mantra of blessing. Practitioners extend the phrase silently toward everyone they encounter throughout the day, including people they find difficult, people suffering, and even themselves. This practice trains compassionate awareness and gradually dissolves the habitual division between self and other.

The angels' song 'Shalom Aleichem' sung on Friday evening to welcome the Shabbat angels also functions as a mantra. Its repetitive structure, sung to traditional melodies, induces a gentle trance state associated with the transition into Shabbat consciousness — what Jewish tradition describes as a 'foretaste of the world to come,' where the quality of shalom prevails without interruption.

translations.en: Peace, Wholeness, and Complete Well-being
translations.zh: 平安、完整与全然的安康
translations.es: Paz, plenitud y bienestar completo

mantras.name: Shema Yisrael
mantras.english: Hear, O Israel: the Lord is our God, the Lord is One
mantras.original: שְׁמַע יִשְׂרָאֵל יְהוָה אֱלֹהֵינוּ יְהוָה אֶחָד
mantras.transliteration: Shema Yisrael Adonai Eloheinu Adonai Echad
mantras.abstract:
The Shema is the central declaration of Jewish faith, drawn from Deuteronomy 6:4, and is considered the most important prayer in Judaism. Its six Hebrew words proclaim the absolute unity of God — not merely that there is one God, but that the One is utterly without division or multiplicity. Recited twice daily, the Shema marks the Jewish understanding that existence is not fragmented but fundamentally whole at its source.

The Shema is traditionally recited with eyes closed and the right hand covering the eyes to prevent distraction, emphasizing that hearing — shema — means not merely acoustic reception but an inner listening with the full being. The word 'Echad' (One) is prolonged, allowing the practitioner to dwell in the space of that ultimate unity. Kabbalists say that in this prolongation, one briefly touches the world of Atzilut (Divine Emanation), the highest of the four worlds.

The Shema is also the dying declaration of Jewish martyrs throughout history — from Rabbi Akiva tortured by the Romans to those who perished in the Holocaust. Its use at death's threshold reflects the belief that the final act of consciousness should be an affirmation of divine unity. The Talmud relates that Rabbi Akiva expired on the word 'Echad,' teaching that a life of Torah reaches completion in this ultimate recognition.

In Kabbalistic practice, the Shema is understood as a unification ritual (yichud). Each word corresponds to a different dimension of divine reality: Yisrael points to the divine-human relationship, Adonai to the attribute of Judgment being unified with Elohim, and Echad to the integration of all worlds into their source. By reciting the Shema with this awareness, the practitioner participates in the cosmic work of tikkun — the repair and reunification of reality.

Contemporary Jewish meditation teachers have developed extended Shema practices where the six words are held in awareness for extended periods, with each word dissolving progressively into the next until only the recognition of pure unity remains. This approach treats the Shema not as a statement to be made but as a state to be entered — the state of perceiving all reality as expressions of one undivided being.

translations.en: Hear, O Israel: the Lord is our God, the Lord is One
translations.zh: 以色列啊，你要听：耶和华是我们的神，耶和华是唯一的
translations.es: Escucha, Israel: el Señor es nuestro Dios, el Señor es Uno

mantras.name: Tikkun Olam
mantras.english: Repair of the World
mantras.transliteration: Tikkun Olam
mantras.abstract:
Tikkun Olam — Repair of the World — originates in Kabbalistic cosmology as a technical term for the spiritual work of restoring divine sparks (nitzotzot) to their source. According to the Lurianic Kabbalah of Rabbi Isaac Luria, the vessels (kelim) meant to contain divine light shattered at creation (shevirat ha-kelim), scattering sparks of divine light throughout the material world. The purpose of human existence and spiritual practice is to gather these sparks through conscious action and return them to the divine source.

In Lurianic theology, every mitzvah (divine commandment), every act of kindness, every moment of authentic prayer, every ethical choice — these all 'raise the sparks' embedded in the materials and situations involved. Eating with awareness, conducting business with integrity, speaking truth, loving one's neighbor — all are forms of Tikkun. The entire social-ethical program of Judaism is framed as cosmic repair work, giving every human act ultimate metaphysical significance.

The phrase entered mainstream discourse through the Reform and Reconstructionist movements of the twentieth century, where it came to denote social justice work more broadly. Tikkun Olam in this usage means any effort to alleviate suffering, reduce inequality, protect the environment, or advance human dignity. The ethical imperative it expresses is genuinely continuous with the tradition's deepest values.

As a mantra, Tikkun Olam serves as an intention-setter. Before engaging in any activity — work, parenting, creative endeavor, social action — pausing to whisper Tikkun Olam frames that activity as part of the cosmic repair project. It transforms the mundane into the purposeful, connecting individual action to the largest possible frame of meaning. This reframing is a powerful antidote to nihilism and the sense that one's actions are too small to matter.

Contemporary practitioners of Jewish mindfulness use Tikkun Olam in combination with breath awareness: with each inhale, one draws in the brokenness of the world; with each exhale, one releases healing intention. This is a practice not of toxic positivity but of engaged compassion — the willingness to be present to what is broken in oneself and in the world, and to offer whatever capacity for healing one has, without demanding results.

translations.en: Repair of the World
translations.zh: 修复世界
translations.es: Reparación del Mundo

mantras.name: Aspire to great goals, dedicate yourself to diligent effort, and maintain a modest demeanor.
mantras.english: Aspire to great goals, dedicate yourself to diligent effort, and maintain a modest demeanor.
mantras.original: Dream big, work hard, stay humble
mantras.transliteration: Aspire to great goals, dedicate yourself to diligent effort, and maintain a modest demeanor.
mantras.background: A daily reminder of inner power: aspire boldly, work diligently, stay humble. Repeated practice shifts doubt to belief, guiding any journey with quiet strength.
mantras.benefits: Practicing this mantra cultivates ambition, discipline, and humility—a triad linked to sustained inner growth. Daily repetition shifts mindset from doubt to action, fostering resilience and grounded confidence rooted in purposeful living.
mantras.tags: motivation, self-belief, diligence, modesty, aspiration, mindset, inner strength, daily practice, personal growth, encouragement, positive affirmation, goal setting

translations.es: Aspira a alcanzar grandes metas, dedícate con esmero al trabajo diligente y mantén una actitud humilde.
translations.ar: طَمحْ إلى أهدافٍ عظيمة، وَوَجِّهْ نفسك لِبذلِ جُهْدٍ مُجِدّ، واحْفَظْ تواضعاً.
translations.bn: মহৎ লক্ষ্যের দিকে আকাঙ্ক্ষা পোষণ করুন, কঠোর পরিশ্রমে নিজেকে উৎসর্গ করুন, এবং নম্র আচরণ বজায় রাখুন।
translations.de: Streben Sie nach großen Zielen, widmen Sie sich fleißiger Arbeit und bewahren Sie eine bescheidene Haltung.
translations.en-us: Strive for ambitious objectives, commit yourself to dedicated work, and cultivate a humble attitude.
translations.en-gb: Strive for lofty ambitions, commit yourself to dedicated work, and cultivate a humble attitude.
translations.fr-fr: Aspirez à de grands objectifs, consacrez-vous à un travail assidu et cultivez une attitude humble.
translations.he: שאפו למטרות גדולות, הקדישו את עצמכם למאמץ מעמיק, ושמרו על צניעות.
translations.hi: महान लक्ष्यों को प्राप्त करने की आकांक्षा रखें, अथक प्रयास में खुद को समर्पित करें, और विनम्र स्वभाव बनाए रखें।
translations.id: Jadilah orang yang memiliki cita-cita tinggi, curahkan diri dalam kerja keras yang tekun, dan pertahankan sikap rendah hati.
translations.it: Ambite obiettivi elevati, dedicatevi a un impegno costante e mantenete un comportamento umile.
translations.ja: 高い目標を抱き、勤勉に努力を続け、謙虚な態度を保つこと。
translations.ko: 위대한 목표를 향해 노력하고, 부지런히 노력하며, 겸손한 태도를 유지하십시오.
translations.nl: Streef naar grote doelen, wijdt je toe aan ijverige inspanningen en behoud een bescheiden houding.
translations.pl: Dąż do ambitnych celów, poświęć się pracowitej pracy i zachowuj skromne usposobienie.
translations.pt-br: Aspire a grandes objetivos, dedique-se a um esforço diligente e mantenha uma postura humilde.
translations.ru: Стремитесь к великим целям, посвятите себя усердной работе и сохраняйте скромное поведение.
translations.sv: Sträva efter stora mål, ägna dig åt flitig ansträngning och upprätthåll ett ödmjukt uppträdande.
translations.th: จงมุ่งมั่นสู่เป้าหมายอันยิ่งใหญ่, อุทิศตนให้กับการทำงานอย่างขยันหมั่นเพียร, และรักษาความอ่อนน้อมถ่อมตนไว้.
translations.tr: Büyük hedeflere ulaşmayı amaçlayın, kendinizi özverili çalışmaya adamın ve mütevazı bir duruş sergileyin.
translations.uk: Прагніть до великих цілей, віддавайтеся наполегливій праці та зберігайте скромність.
translations.vi: Hãy khao khát những mục tiêu cao cả, tận tâm với sự nỗ lực cần mẫn, và giữ thái độ khiêm tốn.
translations.zh-cn: 努力追求崇高的目标，全身心投入到勤奋的努力中，并保持谦逊的姿态。

mantras.name: Become the embodiment of the transformation you desire to witness in the world.
mantras.english: Become the embodiment of the transformation you desire to witness in the world.
mantras.original: Be the change you wish to see in the world
mantras.transliteration: Become the embodiment of the transformation you desire to witness in the world.
mantras.background: An English affirmation mantra — repeated to rewire the mind via neuroplasticity. Its literal meaning: *be* the change, don't just wish for it.
mantras.benefits: Rooted in affirmation tradition, this mantra cultivates agency and inner alignment. Repetition rewires the brain via neuroplasticity, building confidence and purposeful action—transforming the self before transforming the world.
mantras.tags: affirmation, empower, transformation, modern, confidence, inspirational, embodiment, agency

translations.es: Conviértete en la encarnación de la transformación que deseas ver en el mundo.
translations.ar: "كُن تجسيدًا للتغيير الذي ترغب في رؤيته في العالم."
translations.bn: আপনি যে পরিবর্তনটি পৃথিবীতে দেখতে চান, সেই পরিবর্তনের প্রতিচ্ছবি হয়ে উঠুন।
translations.de: Werden Sie die Verkörperung der Veränderung, die Sie in der Welt sehen möchten.
translations.en-us: Strive to exemplify the change you wish to see in the world.
translations.en-gb: Strive to exemplify the change you wish to see enacted in the world.
translations.fr-fr: Devenez l'incarnation de la transformation que vous désirez voir dans le monde.
translations.he: הפכו לגילוי של השינוי שאתם רוצים לראות בעולם.
translations.hi: वह परिवर्तन जो आप दुनिया में देखना चाहते हैं, उसे स्वयं में साकार करें।
translations.id: Jadilah perwujudan dari perubahan yang ingin Anda lihat di dunia.
translations.it: Diventa l'incarnazione della trasformazione che desideri vedere nel mondo.
translations.ja: 世界で実現したい変革を、あなた自身が体現するように生きなさい。
translations.ko: 세상에서 보고자 하는 변화의 화신이 되십시오.
translations.nl: Word en belichaming van de transformatie die je graag wilt zien in de wereld.
translations.pl: Stań się uosobieniem przemiany, której pragniesz dostrzec w świecie.
translations.pt-br: Torne-se a personificação da transformação que você deseja ver no mundo.
translations.ru: Станьте воплощением той трансформации, которую вы хотите видеть в мире.
translations.sv: Bli inkarnationen av den förändring du önskar se i världen.
translations.th: จงเป็นตัวแทนของการเปลี่ยนแปลงที่คุณปรารถนาที่จะเห็นในโลกใบนี้
translations.tr: Dünyada görmek istediğiniz değişimin kişileştirilmiş hali olun.
translations.uk: Станьте втіленням тієї трансформації, яку ви прагнете побачити у світі.
translations.vi: Hãy trở thành hiện thân của sự chuyển đổi mà bạn khao khát được thấy trên thế giới.
translations.zh-cn: 成为你希望在世界中看到的变革的化身。

mantras.name: Breathe in the possibilities of what is to come, and release the experiences that are behind you.
mantras.english: Breathe in the possibilities of what is to come, and release the experiences that are behind you.
mantras.original: Inhale the future, exhale the past.
mantras.transliteration: Briz in ði ˌposəˈbilətiz əv wʌt ɪz tə kʌm, ænd rɪˈlis ði ɪkˈspɪriənsɪz ðæt ɑːr bɪˈhaɪnd juː.
mantras.background: A modern English affirmation mantra, echoing the timeless call to "inhale the future, exhale the past" — inviting mindful renewal with each breath.
mantras.benefits: This breath-anchored mantra trains the mind to release the past and embrace possibility—mirroring research showing mantra practice enhances emotional regulation, reduces cortisol, and sharpens present-moment awareness.
mantras.tags: affirmation, personal, breathing, modern, contemplative, manifestation, letting go, renewal, peace

translations.es: Inhala las posibilidades de lo que está por venir, y libera las experiencias que quedan atrás.
translations.ar: تنفسوا إمكانيات ما هو قادم، وتخلوا عن التجارب التي مضت.
translations.bn: শ্বাস নিন, ভবিষ্যতের সম্ভাবনার জন্য, এবং অতীতের অভিজ্ঞতাগুলোকে ছেড়ে দিন।
translations.de: Atmen Sie die Möglichkeiten ein, die vor Ihnen liegen, und lassen Sie die Erfahrungen los, die hinter Ihnen liegen.
translations.en-us: Embrace the potential of the future, and let go of the past.
translations.en-gb: Inhale the potential that lies ahead, and let go of the experiences that are now in the past.
translations.fr-fr: Inspirez les possibilités de ce qui est à venir, et laissez derrière vous les expériences passées.
translations.he: שאפו את האפשרויות הטמונות במה שעתיד לבוא, ושחררו את החוויות שמאחוריכם.
translations.hi: सांस लें उन संभावनाओं को जो भविष्य में हैं, और उन अनुभवों को जो आपके पीछे छूट गए हैं, उन्हें मुक्त कर दें।
translations.id: Tarik napas dalam-dalam untuk merasakan semua kemungkinan yang ada di depan, dan lepaskan semua pengalaman yang telah berlalu.
translations.it: Inspira le infinite possibilità di ciò che verrà, e lascia andare le esperienze che ti precedono.
translations.ja: 未来への可能性を吸い込み、過去の経験を手放しましょう。
translations.ko: 다가올 미래의 가능성을 마음껏 흡수하고, 뒤에 남겨둔 경험들을 놓아 보내세요.
translations.nl: Adem in de mogelijkheden van wat er nog komen gaat, en laat los de ervaringen die achter je liggen.
translations.pl: Wdychaj możliwości tego, co ma nadejść, i pozwól, by doświadczenia, które są za Tobą, odeszły.
translations.pt-br: Inspire-se nas possibilidades do que está por vir, e deixe para trás as experiências que ficaram no passado.
translations.ru: Вдохните возможности того, что ждёт вас впереди, и отпустите пережитый опыт, который остался позади.
translations.sv: Andas in möjligheterna som framtiden bär med sig, och släpp taget om de erfarenheter som ligger bakom dig.
translations.th: จงรับลมหายใจเข้าเพื่อสัมผัสความเป็นไปได้ของสิ่งที่กำลังจะมาถึง และปล่อยวางประสบการณ์ที่อยู่เบื้องหลังคุณ
translations.tr: Gelecek olanın sunduğu tüm olanakları içine çek, ve geride bıraktığın deneyimleri artık kendini bırak.
translations.uk: Вдихніть можливості, які приховує майбутнє, і відпустіть переживання, що залишилися позаду.
translations.vi: Hãy hít vào những khả năng của tương lai, và hãy buông bỏ những kinh nghiệm đã qua.
translations.zh-cn: 感受未来无限的可能性，并放下过去的一切。

mantras.name: By altering my thoughts, I alter my reality.
mantras.english: By altering my thoughts, I alter my reality.
mantras.original: I change my thoughts, I change my world.
mantras.transliteration: By altering my thoughts, I alter my reality.
mantras.background: A modern English affirmation rooted in the power of personal mantras, echoing Norman Vincent Peale's quote: "I change my thoughts, I change my world." Repeat it to rewire your reality!
mantras.benefits: Rooted in the belief that thoughts shape reality, this mantra trains the mind to consciously redirect neural pathways. Research shows mantra meditation boosts prefrontal cortex activity, sharpening self-awareness and emotional regulation—transforming inner dialogue into outer change.
mantras.tags: affirmation, personal, manifestation, empowerment, western, transformation, cognitive, modern, intention

translations.es: Al modificar mis pensamientos, modifico mi realidad.
translations.ar: من خلال تغيير أفكاري، أغيّر واقعي.
translations.bn: আমার চিন্তাভাবনা পরিবর্তন করে, আমি আমার বাস্তবতাকে পরিবর্তন করি।
translations.de: Durch die Veränderung meiner Gedanken verändere ich meine Realität.
translations.en-us: By changing the way I think, I change my experience of the world.
translations.en-gb: By changing the way I think, I change the world around me.
translations.fr-fr: En modifiant mes pensées, je modifie ma réalité.
translations.he: על ידי שינוי מחשבותיי, אני משנה את המציאות שלי.
translations.hi: अपने विचारों को बदलकर, मैं अपनी वास्तविकता को बदलता हूँ।
translations.id: Dengan mengubah pikiran saya, saya mengubah realitas saya.
translations.it: Modificando i miei pensieri, modifico la mia realtà.
translations.ja: 思考を変えることで、私は現実を変える。
translations.ko: 제 생각을 바꾸면, 제 현실도 바뀝니다.
translations.nl: Door mijn gedachten te veranderen, verander ik mijn realiteit.
translations.pl: Zmieniając moje myśli, zmieniam moją rzeczywistość.
translations.pt-br: Ao modificar meus pensamentos, eu modifico minha realidade.
translations.ru: Изменяя свои мысли, я изменяю свою реальность.
translations.sv: Genom att förändra mina tankar, förändrar jag min verklighet.
translations.th: การเปลี่ยนแปลงความคิดของฉัน นำไปสู่การเปลี่ยนแปลงความเป็นจริงของฉัน
translations.tr: Düşüncelerimi değiştirerek, gerçekliğimi de değiştiririm.
translations.uk: Змінюючи свої думки, я змінюю свою реальність.
translations.vi: Bằng cách thay đổi suy nghĩ của tôi, tôi thay đổi thực tại của mình.
translations.zh-cn: 通过改变我的想法，我从而改变我的现实。

mantras.name: Calm yourself, let go of tension, and find peace.
mantras.english: Calm yourself, let go of tension, and find peace.
mantras.transliteration: Calm yourself, let go of tension, and find peace.
mantras.background: A modern secular mantra rooted in mindfulness: "Calm yourself, let go of tension, and find peace." Used on and off the yoga mat to center the mind anytime.
mantras.benefits: Practicing "Calm yourself, let go of tension, and find peace" cultivates psychological stillness and emotional release. Sources link such mantras to daily centering, stress relief, and inner calm—inviting a transformative pause in any moment.
mantras.tags: affirmation, peace, calm, stress-relief, breathing, focus, centering, relaxation, letting-go, yoga

translations.es: Cálmate, libera la tensión y encuentra la paz.
translations.ar: هدأ من روعك، ودع عن التوتر، وابحث عن السلام.
translations.bn: নিজেকে শান্ত করুন, উত্তেজনা ত্যাগ করুন এবং শান্তি খুঁজে নিন।
translations.de: Beruhige dich, lass die Anspannung los und finde inneren Frieden.
translations.en-us: Compose yourself, release the stress, and discover tranquility.
translations.en-gb: Compose yourself, release the tension, and discover serenity.
translations.fr-fr: Calmez-vous, relâchez vos tensions et trouvez la paix.
translations.he: הרגע את עצמך, שחרר את המתח, ומצא שלווה.
translations.hi: शांत हो जाओ, तनाव को दूर करो, और शांति प्राप्त करो।
translations.id: Tenangkan diri Anda, lepaskan ketegangan, dan temukan kedamaian.
translations.it: Calmati, abbandona la tensione e trova la serenità.
translations.ja: 落ち着いて、緊張を手放し、心の平和を見つけてください。
translations.ko: 마음을 가라앉히고, 긴장을 풀고, 평화를 찾으세요.
translations.nl: Blijf kalm, laat de spanning los en vind innerlijke rust.
translations.pl: Uspokój się, odpuść napięcie i znajdź wewnętrzny spokój.
translations.pt-br: Acalme-se, liberte-se da tensão e encontre a paz.
translations.ru: Успокойтесь, отпустите напряжение и обретите внутренний покой.
translations.sv: Lugna dig själv, släpp spänningen och finn din inre frid.
translations.th: สงบจิตใจ, ปล่อยวางความตึงเครียด, และค้นพบความสงบ
translations.tr: Sakinleş, gerginliği bırak ve huzur bul.
translations.uk: Заспокойтеся, відпустіть напругу та знайдіть внутрішній спокій.
translations.vi: Hãy giữ bình tĩnh, buông bỏ căng thẳng và tìm thấy sự bình an.
translations.zh-cn: 平静你的内心，放松身心，找到内心的平静。

mantras.name: Entreat the wind that passes by.
mantras.english: Entreat the wind that passes by.
mantras.original: วอน วอนสายลมที่ผ่าน
mantras.transliteration: `ขอพรต่อลมที่ผ่านไป`
mantras.background: From Thai pop tradition, "Mantra" by Keng Harit entreats wind and moon as spiritual witnesses to love's plea — chanting "Om" as heartfelt prayer to keep two souls united.
mantras.benefits: This Thai love-mantra blends supplication to wind, moon, and sky with the "Om" invocation, channeling devotion into prayer. Practicing such heartfelt entreaty cultivates emotional surrender, vulnerability, and trust—psychological traits linked to deeper connection and resilience.
mantras.tags: Thai, devotional, longing, relationship, entreaty, prayer, seeking, love, yearning, protection, hope, commitment

translations.es: Suplico al viento que pasa.
translations.ar: تضرّع إلى الريح العابرة.
translations.bn: বাতাসের কাছে প্রার্থনা করা।
translations.de: Flehe den vorbeiziehenden Wind an.
translations.en-us: To implore the passing wind.
translations.en-gb: To implore the passing breeze.
translations.fr-fr: Implorer le vent qui passe.
translations.he: התחננו לרוח החולפת.
translations.hi: हवा से प्रार्थना करो जो गुजर रही है।
translations.id: Mohonlah pada angin yang berlalu.
translations.it: Invoca il vento che passa.
translations.ja: 風が吹くたびに、その風に語りかける。
translations.ko: 바람이 불어 지나가는 것을 간청하다.
translations.nl: De wind die voorbijtrekt, smeek ik.
translations.pl: Proszę wiatru, który przeminie.
translations.pt-br: Implorar ao vento que passa.
translations.ru: Умолять проходящий мимо ветер.
translations.sv: Bön till vinden som passerar.
translations.th: ขอพรต่อลมที่ผ่านไป
translations.tr: Geçen rüzgara yalvar.
translations.uk: Звернення до вітру, що минає.
translations.vi: Van nài làn gió thoảng qua.
translations.zh-cn: 恳求吹过的风。

mantras.name: Every day, in every way, I am getting better and better.
mantras.english: Every day, in every way, I am getting better and better.
mantras.original: Jeden Tag auf jede Art werde ich besser und besser
mantras.transliteration: Jede Tag, auf jede Art und Weise, werde ich immer besser.
mantras.background:
Coined by Émile Coué, this autosuggestion mantra—"Tous les jours, à tous points de vue, je vais de mieux en mieux"—uses repetition to reshape the subconscious mind toward growth.
  Grounding: French source names it "mantra d'autosuggestion" by Coué; German lists it as #47; both sources confirm repetition transforms inner mindset.
mantras.benefits:
This autosuggestion mantra, popularized by Émile Coué, rewires the subconscious through repetition. Sources link such practice to reduced stress, boosted self-confidence, and sharper focus—inviting real inner transformation.
  Grounding: French source names Coué's phrase as a mantra d'autosuggestion; German/French sources confirm stress reduction, confidence, and concentration benefits from mantra repetition.
mantras.tags: affirmation, autosuggestion, western, modern, self-improvement, empowerment, confidence, psychological, focus

translations.es: Cada día, en cada aspecto, me estoy volviendo mejor y mejor.
translations.ar: كل يوم، في كل طريقة، أنا أتحسن وأتحسن.
translations.bn: প্রতিদিন, প্রতিটি মুহূর্তে, আমি ক্রমাগত উন্নতি করছি।
translations.de: Jeden Tag, auf jede Art und Weise, werde ich immer besser.
translations.en-us: Every day, in every way, I am improving more and more.
translations.en-gb: Every day, in every respect, I am improving more and more.
translations.fr-fr: Chaque jour, à tous égards, je deviens de mieux en mieux.
translations.he: בכל יום, בכל דרך, אני משתפר ומשתפר.
translations.hi: हर दिन, हर तरह से, मैं लगातार बेहतर होता जा रहा हूँ।
translations.id: Setiap hari, dengan segala cara, aku menjadi lebih baik dan lebih baik.
translations.it: Ogni giorno, in ogni modo, mi miglioro sempre di più.
translations.ja: 毎日、あらゆる面で、私は日々向上しています。 (Mainichi, arayuu men de, watashi wa hibi kōjō shite imasu.)
translations.ko: 매일, 모든 면에서, 나는 점점 더 나아지고 있습니다. (Maeil, modeun myeon-eseo, naneun jeomjeom deo naajigo itseumnida.)
translations.nl: Jeder Tag, auf jede Art und Weise, werde ich immer besser.
translations.pl: Każdego dnia, na każdym kroku, staję się coraz lepsza i lepsza.
translations.pt-br: Todos os dias, de todas as maneiras, eu estou me tornando cada vez melhor.
translations.ru: Каждый день, во всех отношениях, я становлюсь всё лучше и лучше.
translations.sv: Varje dag, på alla sätt, blir jag bättre och bättre.
translations.th: ทุกวัน ทุกวิถีทาง ฉันกำลังพัฒนาและดีขึ้นเรื่อยๆ
translations.tr: Her gün, her durumda, her geçen gün daha da iyiye gidiyorum.
translations.uk: Щодоби, у кожному аспекті, я стаю кращою/кращим з кожним днем.
translations.vi: Mỗi ngày, theo mọi cách, tôi đang ngày càng tốt hơn.
translations.zh-cn: 每天，在方方面面，我都在变得越来越好。

mantras.name: Gratitude transforms mundane days into blessings.
mantras.english: Gratitude transforms mundane days into blessings.
mantras.original: Gratitude turns ordinary days into blessings
mantras.transliteration: ˈɡrætɪˌtjuːd trænsˈfɔːrmz ˈmʌndaɪn deɪz ˈɪntuː ˈblɛsɪŋz
mantras.tags: affirmation, gratitude, positive thinking, daily life, self-improvement, motivational, mindset, encouragement, personal growth, intention, optimism, manifestation

translations.es: La gratitud transforma los días ordinarios en bendiciones.
translations.ar: الشكر يحول الأيام العادية إلى نِعم.
translations.bn: কৃতজ্ঞতা সাধারণ দিনগুলোকে আশীর্বাদে রূপান্তরিত করে।
translations.de: Dankbarkeit verwandelt gewöhnliche Tage in Segen.
translations.en-us: Appreciation and thankfulness have the power to turn ordinary, unremarkable days into experiences that feel like gifts or fortunate events.
translations.en-gb: Gratitude has the power to turn ordinary days into moments of blessing.
translations.fr-fr: La gratitude transforme les jours ordinaires en bénédictions.
translations.he: הכרת הטוב הופכת ימים שגרתיים לברכות.
translations.hi: कृतज्ञता सामान्य दिनों को आशीर्वादों में बदल देती है।
translations.id: Rasa syukur mengubah hari-hari biasa menjadi berkat.
translations.it: La gratitudine trasforma le giornate ordinarie in occasioni di gioia.
translations.ja: 感謝の気持ちは、平凡な日々を祝福に変える。
translations.ko: 감사합니다. 평범한 날들이 감사함으로 축복으로 변합니다.
translations.nl: Dankbaarheid verandert alledaagse dagen in zegeningen.
translations.pl: Uczciwość przemienia zwykłe dni w błogosławieństwa.
translations.pt-br: A gratidão transforma os dias comuns em bênçãos.
translations.ru: Благодарность превращает обычные дни в благословения.
translations.sv: Tacksamhet förvandlar ordinära dagar till välsignelser.
translations.th: ความกตัญญูเปลี่ยนวันธรรมดาให้กลายเป็นพร.
translations.tr: Şükran, sıradan günleri berekete dönüştürür.
translations.uk: Вдячність перетворює звичайні дні на благословення.
translations.vi: Lòng biết ơn biến những ngày thường nhật thành những điều may mắn.
translations.zh-cn: 感恩之心能将平凡的日子转化为充满祝福的时光。

mantras.name: He who doesn't dare, doesn't win.
mantras.english: He who doesn't dare, doesn't win.
mantras.original: Wer nicht wagt, der nicht gewinnt
mantras.transliteration: He, der nicht wagt, gewinnt nicht.
mantras.background: "He who doesn't dare, doesn't win" — a bold courage mantra. Repeated daily, it carves new brain pathways, replacing fear with confident, winning action.
mantras.benefits: Repeating "He who doesn't dare, doesn't win" rewires neural pathways, replacing fear and self-doubt with courage. Like all mantras, frequent repetition builds bold, empowering thought habits.
mantras.tags: positive, repetition, cognitive, habit, self-talk, neuroplasticity, mantra, affirmation, well-being, mindset, daily practice, mental health

translations.es: Quien no se atreve, no gana.
translations.ar: من لم يجازف، لم ينجح.
translations.bn: যে সাহস করে না, সে জেতে না।
translations.de: Wer nicht wagt, der nicht gewinnt.
translations.en-us: He who does not dare, does not win.
translations.en-gb: He who does not dare, does not win.
translations.fr-fr: Celui qui n'ose pas, ne gagne pas.
translations.he: מי שאינו מעז, אינו זוכה.
translations.hi: जो व्यक्ति जोखिम लेने से डरता है, वह कभी जीत नहीं पाता।
translations.id: Siapa yang tidak berani, tidak akan menang.
translations.it: Chi non osa, non vince.
translations.ja: 挑戦しない者は、勝利を得られない。
translations.ko: 도전하지 않는 사람은 승리할 수 없다.
translations.nl: Wie niet waagt, die niet wint.
translations.pl: Ten, kto nie odważy się, nie zwycięża.
translations.pt-br: Quem não ousa, não vence.
translations.ru: Тот, кто не рискует, тот не побеждает.
translations.sv: Den som inte vågar, vinner inte.
translations.th: ผู้ที่ไม่กล้า ไม่ได้รับชัยชนะ
translations.tr: Cesur olmayan, kazanamaz.
translations.uk: Хто не ризикує, той не виграє.
translations.vi: Ai không dám, thì không thể chiến thắng.
translations.zh-cn: 谁不冒险，谁就赢不了。

mantras.name: I AM LIGHT
mantras.english: I AM LIGHT
mantras.transliteration: MINÄ OLEN VALO
mantras.background: "I AM LIGHT" is a personal mantra — a short, positive phrase used to connect with inner strength. No ancient text needed; its power lies in its simplicity and affirmation.
mantras.benefits: "I AM LIGHT" is a personal mantra that connects you to inner strength and peace. Research supports mantra-based meditation for reducing stress and boosting mental health. Positive phrasing like this amplifies its empowering effect.
mantras.tags: affirmation, positive, empowerment, peace, strength, healing, calming, personal

translations.es: YO SOY LUZ.
translations.ar: أنا نور.
translations.bn: আমি আলো।
translations.de: Ich bin Licht.
translations.en-us: I am the light.
translations.en-gb: I AM LIGHT.
translations.fr-fr: Je suis lumière.
translations.he: אֲנִי אוֹר
translations.hi: मैं प्रकाश हूँ।
translations.id: Saya adalah cahaya.
translations.it: IO SONO LA LUCE.
translations.ja: 私は光です。
translations.ko: 저는 빛입니다.
translations.nl: Ik ben licht.
translations.pl: Jestem światłem.
translations.pt-br: EU SOU LUZ.
translations.ru: Я есть свет.
translations.sv: JAG ÄR LJUS
translations.th: ฉันคือแสงสว่าง
translations.tr: BEN IŞIĞIM.
translations.uk: Я є світло.
translations.vi: Tôi là ánh sáng.
translations.zh-cn: 我是光明 (Wǒ shì guāngmíng)

mantras.name: I am calm, focused, and powerful; I move forward in all things in the best possible way.
mantras.english: I am calm, focused, and powerful; I move forward in all things in the best possible way.
mantras.original: من آرام، متمرکز و قدرتمند هستم؛ همه چیز را به بهترین شکل پیش میبرم
mantras.transliteration: ˈi æm ˈkɑːlm, ˈfokʌsɪd, ænd ˈpaʊərfʊl; ˈi ˈmuːv ˈfɔːrwərd ɪn ˈɔːl ˈθɪŋz ɪn ðə ˈbɛst ˈpɑːsɪbəl ˈweɪ.
mantras.background: Rooted 3000+ years ago in India, "mantra" (Sanskrit: *manas*=mind, *tra*=tool) transforms mind through repetition — making this modern affirmation a bridge to ancient meditative power.
mantras.benefits: Repeating "I am calm, focused, and powerful" uses the mind as its own tool—echoing the Sanskrit root of mantra (man=mind, tra=instrument). Research confirms mantra meditation positively impacts mental health.
mantras.tags: affirmation, focus, calm, empower, peace, healing

translations.es: Estoy tranquilo, concentrado y poderoso; avanzo en todo de la mejor manera posible.
translations.ar: أنا هادئ، ومركز، وقوي؛ وأتقدم في كل شيء بأفضل طريقة ممكنة.
translations.bn: আমি শান্ত, মনোযোগী এবং শক্তিশালী; আমি সবকিছুতে সর্বোত্তম উপায়ে সামনের দিকে অগ্রসর হচ্ছি।
translations.de: Ich bin ruhig, konzentriert und kraftvoll; ich gehe in allen Dingen auf die bestmögliche Weise voran.
translations.en-us: I am calm, focused, and powerful; I progress in all aspects of my life in the most optimal manner.
translations.en-gb: I am calm, focused, and strong; I progress in all aspects of my life in the most optimal way.
translations.fr-fr: Je suis calme, concentré et puissant ; je progresse dans tout ce que j'entreprends de la meilleure manière possible.
translations.he: אני רגוע, מרוכז וחזק; אני מתקדם בכל דבר בצורה הטובה ביותר.
translations.hi: मैं शांत, केंद्रित और शक्तिशाली हूँ; मैं हर चीज़ में सर्वोत्तम संभव तरीके से आगे बढ़ रहा हूँ।
translations.id: Saya tenang, fokus, dan berdaya; saya bergerak maju dalam segala hal dengan cara terbaik.
translations.it: Sono calmo, concentrato e potente; procedo in ogni cosa nel modo migliore possibile.
translations.ja: 私は穏やかで、集中しており、力強く、あらゆる面で最善の方法で前進します。
translations.ko: 저는 평온하고, 집중력이 있으며, 강력합니다. 저는 모든 면에서 최선의 방식으로 나아갑니다.
translations.nl: Ik ben kalm, gefocust en krachtig; ik ga in alles vooruit op de best mogelijke manier.
translations.pl: Jestem spokojny, skupiony i silny; dążę do przodu we wszystkim, działając w najlepszy możliwy sposób.
translations.pt-br: Estou calmo, concentrado e poderoso; avanço em todas as coisas da melhor maneira possível.
translations.ru: Я спокоен, сосредоточен и силен; я двигаюсь вперед во всем наилучшим образом.
translations.sv: Jag är lugn, fokuserad och stark; jag rör mig framåt i allt på det bästa möjliga sättet.
translations.th: ฉันมีความสงบ, มีสติ, และมีพลัง; ฉันก้าวไปข้างหน้าในทุกสิ่งด้วยวิธีที่ดีที่สุดเท่าที่จะเป็นไปได้
translations.tr: Ben sakin, odaklanmış ve güçlüüm; her şeyde en iyi şekilde ilerliyorum.
translations.uk: Я спокійний, зосереджений і сильний; я рухаюся вперед у всіх аспектах життя найкращим можливим чином.
translations.vi: Tôi bình tĩnh, tập trung và tràn đầy sức mạnh; tôi tiến lên phía trước trong mọi việc theo cách tốt nhất.
translations.zh-cn: 我内心平静，思维专注，充满力量；我以最佳的方式在所有事情中不断前进。

mantras.name: I am calm; I am clear.
mantras.english: I am calm; I am clear.
mantras.original: I am calm, I am clear
mantras.transliteration: `ai am kalm; ai am klɪər.`
mantras.background: "I am calm; I am clear" is a modern affirmational mantra rooted in Sanskrit meditation tradition, meaning "mantra" itself signifies "sacred counsel." It anchors mind and emotions simultaneously.
mantras.benefits: "I am calm; I am clear" shifts your nervous system into a restful state, sharpens focus, and dissolves overthinking—making it a deceptively simple yet transformative gateway into deeper mindfulness practice.
mantras.tags: affirmation, calm, clarity, focus, stress, anxiety, peace, breathing, mindfulness, relaxation

translations.es: Estoy tranquilo; estoy sereno.
translations.ar: أنا هادئ؛ أنا واعي.
translations.bn: আমি শান্ত; আমি স্বচ্ছ।
translations.de: Ich bin ruhig; Ich bin klar.
translations.en-us: I am at peace; I am focused.
translations.en-gb: I am calm; I am clear.
translations.fr-fr: Je suis calme ; je suis lucide.
translations.he: אני רגוע; אני מבין/ברור.
translations.hi: मैं शांत हूँ; मैं स्पष्ट हूँ।
translations.id: Saya tenang; saya jernih.
translations.it: Sono calmo; sono lucido.
translations.ja: 私は穏やかです。私は明快です。
translations.ko: 저는 평온합니다. 저는 명확합니다.
translations.nl: Ik ben kalm; ik ben helder.
translations.pl: Jestem spokojny; jestem jasny.
translations.pt-br: Estou calmo; estou sereno.
translations.ru: Я спокоен; Я ясно мыслил.
translations.sv: Jag är lugn; jag är klar.
translations.th: ฉันสงบ; ฉันเข้าใจแจ่มแจ้ง.
translations.tr: Ben sakinim; ben berrakım.
translations.uk: Я спокійний; я зосереджений.
translations.vi: Tôi bình tĩnh; Tôi minh bạch.
translations.zh-cn: 我很平静；我心境澄明。

mantras.name: I am connecting to my inner wisdom.
mantras.english: I am connecting to my inner wisdom.
mantras.original: Je me connecte à ma sagesse intérieure.
mantras.transliteration: Je me connecte à ma sagesse intérieure.
mantras.background: A modern affirmation mantra, not rooted in ancient scripture, but echoing yogic & Buddhist traditions of inner-focused repetition to cultivate clarity and self-connection.
mantras.benefits: Rooted in yogic traditions, this affirmation mantra opens what sources call "un espace intérieur de clarté" — an inner space of clarity. Repetition anchors intention, quiets looping thoughts, and reconnects the practitioner to a deeper self beyond mental noise.
mantras.tags: affirmation, contemporary, intention, focus, wisdom, empowerment, introspection, positive

translations.es: Me estoy conectando con mi sabiduría interior.
translations.ar: أنا أتواصل مع حكمتي الداخلية.
translations.bn: আমি আমার ভেতরের প্রজ্ঞা/জ্ঞান/উপলব্ধির সাথে সংযোগ স্থাপন করছি।
translations.de: Ich verbinde mich mit meiner inneren Weisheit.
translations.en-us: I am connecting with my inner wisdom.
translations.en-gb: I am connecting with my inner wisdom.
translations.fr-fr: Je me connecte à ma sagesse intérieure.
translations.he: אני מתחבר/ת לחכמה הפנימית שלי.
translations.hi: मैं अपने आंतरिक ज्ञान से जुड़ रहा हूँ।
translations.id: Saya sedang terhubung dengan kebijaksanaan batin saya.
translations.it: Mi sto connettendo alla mia saggezza interiore.
translations.ja: 私は、内なる知恵とのつながりを深めている。
translations.ko: 저는 내면의 지혜와 연결되고 있습니다.
translations.nl: Ik verbind me met mijn innerlijke wijsheid.
translations.pl: Ustanawiam połączenie ze swoją wewnętrzną mądrością.
translations.pt-br: Estou me conectando à minha sabedoria interior.
translations.ru: Я подключаюсь к своей внутренней мудрости.
translations.sv: Jag kopplar mig till min inre visdom.
translations.th: ฉันกำลังเชื่อมต่อกับสติปัญญาภายในของฉัน
translations.tr: İçsel bilgeliğime bağlanıyorum.
translations.uk: Я встановлюю зв'язок зі своєю внутрішньою мудрістю.
translations.vi: Tôi đang kết nối với trí tuệ nội tại của mình.
translations.zh-cn: 我正在与我内在的智慧建立连接。

mantras.name: I am enough, just as I am.
mantras.english: I am enough, just as I am.
mantras.original: Ich bin genug, so wie ich bin
mantras.transliteration: Ich bin genug, so wie ich bin.
mantras.background: "I am enough, just as I am" is a modern self-affirmation mantra rooted in mindfulness, promoting self-acceptance and inner happiness beyond external circumstances.
mantras.benefits: "I am enough, just as I am" fosters self-acceptance and self-love. This simple yet powerful mantra turns awareness inward, reminding practitioners that true happiness arises from within — not external circumstances.
mantras.tags: affirmation, self-acceptance, self-love, inner, empower, philosophy

translations.es: Soy suficiente, tal como soy.
translations.ar: أنا كافٍ، كما أنا.
translations.bn: আমি যেমন, তেমনই যথেষ্ট।
translations.de: Ich bin genug, so wie ich bin.
translations.en-us: I am sufficient, exactly as I am.
translations.en-gb: I am sufficient, just as I am.
translations.fr-fr: Je suis suffisamment, tel que je suis.
translations.he: אני מספיק/ה, כפי שאני.
translations.hi: मैं जैसा हूँ, वैसा ही पर्याप्त हूँ।
translations.id: Saya sudah cukup, apa adanya.
translations.it: Sono sufficiente, così come sono.
translations.ja: 私は、ありのままの自分で、十分です。
translations.ko: 저는 있는 그대로 충분합니다.
translations.nl: Ik ben genoeg, zoals ik ben.
translations.pl: Jestem wystarczająca taka, jaka jestem.
translations.pt-br: Eu sou suficiente, exatamente como sou.
translations.ru: Я достаточно, такой, какая я есть.
translations.sv: Jag är tillräcklig, precis som jag är.
translations.th: ฉันก็ดีงามในตัวของฉันเอง ไม่ต้องเปลี่ยนแปลงใดๆ
translations.tr: Şu an olduğum gibi, kendim için yeterliyim.
translations.uk: Я достатньо, таким, яким я є.
translations.vi: Tôi đủ tốt, như tôi vốn là.
translations.zh-cn: 我就此而足够。

mantras.name: I am love.
mantras.english: I am love.
mantras.transliteration: Je suis l'amour.
mantras.background: "I am love" is a modern personal affirmation mantra — rooted in the ancient tradition of Sanskrit mantras like "So Hum" ("I am That") — crafted to cultivate inner harmony.
mantras.benefits: "I am love" is a personal affirmation mantra that cultivates self-compassion and emotional balance. Regular repetition redirects focus from anxiety, induces relaxation, and deepens self-awareness — awakening inner peace.
mantras.tags: affirmation, compassion, self-love, peace, clarity, relaxation, personal-growth

translations.es: Yo soy amor.
translations.ar: أنا هو الحب.
translations.bn: আমি ভালোবাসা।
translations.de: Ich bin Liebe.
translations.en-us: I am love.
translations.en-gb: I am love.
translations.fr-fr: Je suis l'amour.
translations.he: אני אהבה.
translations.hi: मैं प्रेम हूँ।
translations.id: Saya adalah cinta.
translations.it: Io sono amore.
translations.ja: 私は愛です。
translations.ko: 저는 사랑입니다.
translations.nl: Ik ben liefde.
translations.pl: Jestem miłością.
translations.pt-br: Eu sou amor.
translations.ru: Я – любовь.
translations.sv: Jag är kärlek.
translations.th: ฉันคือความรัก
translations.tr: Ben sevgiyim.
translations.uk: Я – любов.
translations.vi: Tôi là tình yêu.
translations.zh-cn: 我是爱。

mantras.name: I am peace, I am love.
mantras.english: I am peace, I am love.
mantras.transliteration: Yo soy paz, yo soy amor.
mantras.background: "I am peace, I am love" is a modern personal mantra in the Spanish-influenced contemporary tradition—clear, intentional, and designed to resonate deeply through conscious meaning and repetition.
mantras.benefits: "I am peace, I am love" is a personal mantra aligned with modern contemplative traditions. Its clear meaning reinforces subconscious impact, and research links such mantras to reduced anxiety and deeper meditative states.
mantras.tags: modern, personal, affirmation, peace, love, clarity, empower

translations.es: Yo soy paz, yo soy amor.
translations.ar: أنا السلام، أنا الحب.
translations.bn: আমি শান্তি, আমি ভালোবাসা।
translations.de: Ich bin Frieden, ich bin Liebe.
translations.en-us: I am peace, I am love.
translations.en-gb: I am peace, I am love.
translations.fr-fr: Je suis la paix, je suis l'amour.
translations.he: אני שלום, אני אהבה.
translations.id: Saya adalah kedamaian, saya adalah cinta.
translations.it: Sono pace, sono amore.
translations.ja: 私は平和です、私は愛です。
translations.ko: 저는 평화입니다, 저는 사랑입니다.
translations.nl: Ik ben vrede, ik ben liefde.
translations.pl: Jestem pokojem, jestem miłością.
translations.pt-br: Eu sou a paz, eu sou o amor.
translations.ru: Я – это мир, я – это любовь.
translations.sv: Jag är frid, jag är kärlek.
translations.th: ฉันคือความสงบ, ฉันคือความรัก.
translations.tr: Ben huzurum, ben sevgiyim.
translations.uk: Я є мир, я є любов.
translations.vi: Tôi là hòa bình, tôi là tình yêu.
translations.zh-cn: 我是和平，我是爱。

mantras.name: I am perfect, exactly as I am.
mantras.english: I am perfect, exactly as I am.
mantras.original: I am perfect, just as I am.
mantras.transliteration: Ām perfekt, egzaktli æz āy ām.
mantras.tags: mindfulness, affirmation, present, acceptance, self-love, body-awareness, empower, peace, healing

translations.es: Soy perfecto/a, tal como soy.
translations.ar: أنا كامل، كما أنا تمامًا.
translations.bn: আমি যেমন আছি, তেমনই নিখুঁত।
translations.de: Ich bin vollkommen, genau so wie ich bin.

translations.vi: Tôi là hoàn hảo, đúng như tôi vốn là.
translations.zh-cn: 我就是完美的，我现在的样子已经足够好了。

mantras.name: I am present, I exist in this moment.
mantras.english: I am present, I exist in this moment.
mantras.original: I Am Here, I Am Now
mantras.transliteration: I am present, I exist in this moment.
mantras.background: Rooted in modern mindfulness, "I am present, I exist in this moment" echoes the ancient mantra "I Am Here, I Am Now" — rewiring attention and dissolving mind-wandering.
mantras.benefits: Rooted in mindfulness tradition, this mantra anchors you to the present moment, reducing anxiety and rewiring neural attention pathways. Like Om's timeless stillness, it cultivates calm awareness and emotional resilience through focused repetition.
mantras.tags: mindfulness, presence, grounding, affirmation, attention, stress-relief, awareness, emotional-regulation

translations.es: Estoy presente; existo en este momento.

translations.zh-cn: 我在这里，我存在于当下。

mantras.name: I am rooted in calmness.
mantras.english: I am rooted in calmness.
mantras.transliteration: I am rooted in calmness.
mantras.tags: mindfulness, presence, calmness, relaxation, inner peace, self-acceptance, grounding

translations.es: Estoy arraigado/a en la calma.

translations.bn: আমি শান্তিতে স্থিত।
translations.de: Ich bin in Ruhe verwurzelt.

translations.en-gb: I find my strength and stability in calmness.
translations.fr-fr: Je suis enraciné dans le calme.
translations.he: אני משורש בשלווה.

mantras.name: I am safe, I am loved, I am supported.
mantras.english: I am safe, I am loved, I am supported.
mantras.transliteration: I am safe, I am loved, I am supported.
mantras.background: A modern affirmation rooted in ancient Hindu & Buddhist mantra traditions — repeated to calm anxiety, rewire the mind, and affirm: you are protected, cherished, and held.
mantras.benefits: Rooted in ancient mindfulness traditions, "I am safe, I am loved, I am supported" counters anxiety by anchoring the mind in reassurance. Repeated consistently, it rewires subconscious thought patterns, builds emotional resilience, and cultivates inner peace.
mantras.tags: affirmation, stress-relief, anxiety-relief, relaxation, mindfulness, focus, self-belief, positive-thinking, emotional-resilience, healing

translations.es: Estoy a salvo, me siento amado/a, tengo apoyo.
translations.ar: أنا في أمان، وأنا محبوب، ولدي دعم.

translations.en-us: I am safe, I am loved, I am supported.
translations.en-gb: I am safe, I am loved, I am supported.
translations.fr-fr: Je suis en sécurité, je suis aimé(e), je suis soutenu(e).
translations.he: אני בטוח/ה, אני נאהב/ת, אני נתמך/ת.
translations.hi: मैं सुरक्षित हूँ, मुझे प्यार मिलता है, मुझे समर्थन प्राप्त है।

translations.nl: Ik ben veilig, ik word bemind, ik krijg steun.
translations.pl: Jestem bezpieczny, jestem kochany, jestem wspierany.

translations.ru: Я в безопасности, меня любят, меня поддерживают.
translations.sv: Jag är trygg, jag är älskad, jag är stöttad.

translations.uk: Я в безпеці, мене люблять, мене підтримують.
translations.vi: Tôi an toàn, tôi được yêu thương, tôi được hỗ trợ.
translations.zh-cn: 我是安全的，我被爱着，我得到了支持。

mantras.name: I am stillness.
mantras.english: I am stillness.
mantras.transliteration: I am stillness.
mantras.background: "I am stillness" is a modern English peace mantra from the TM-inspired tradition, evoking inner calm. Simple yet profound, it invites you to embody silence itself.
mantras.benefits: "I am stillness" is a peace mantra used in TM-inspired practice to dissolve inner noise and anchor awareness in the present. Repeating it trains the mind to release tension, cultivating calm, self-awareness, and emotional resilience.
mantras.tags: transcendental, peace, stillness, affirmation, calming, serenity, meditation

translations.es: Yo soy quietud.
translations.ar: أنا السكون.
translations.bn: আমি স্থিরতা।
translations.de: Ich bin Stille.
translations.en-us: I embody tranquility.
translations.en-gb: I embody stillness.
translations.fr-fr: Je suis la quiétude.
translations.he: אני שלווה.
translations.hi: मैं स्थिरता हूँ।

mantras.name: I am strong, I am brave, I am successful.
mantras.english: I am strong, I am brave, I am successful.
mantras.transliteration: Ben güçlü, ben cesur, ben başarılıyım.
mantras.background: Rooted in Sanskrit—"man" (mind) + "tra" (vehicle)—mantras are mind-tools from Hindu/Buddhist traditions, used in meditation and yoga to focus and uplift the spirit.
mantras.benefits: Repeating "I am strong, I am brave, I am successful" functions as a mind-focusing tool (mantra = "vehicle of the mind"), quieting mental noise, building inner resolve, and cultivating a meditative, empowered state.
mantras.tags: affirmation, empower, motivation, focus, peace

translations.es: Soy fuerte, soy valiente, soy exitoso.
translations.ar: أنا قوي، أنا شجاع، أنا ناجح.

translations.en-gb: I am strong, I am brave, I am successful.
translations.fr-fr: Je suis fort, je suis courageux, je suis accompli.
translations.he: אני חזק, אני אמיץ, אני מצליח.
translations.hi: मैं शक्तिशाली हूँ, मैं साहसी हूँ, मैं सफल हूँ।

translations.pt-br: Eu sou forte, eu sou corajoso, eu sou bem-sucedido.
translations.ru: Я сильный, я смелый, я успешный.
translations.sv: Jag är stark, jag är modig, jag är framgångsrik.

mantras.name: I am the change.
mantras.english: I am the change.
mantras.transliteration: Ich bin die Veränderung.
mantras.background: "I am the change" is a modern mantra used in meditation. "Mantra" comes from Sanskrit: "Man" (mind/think) + "tra" (tool) = "tool of thinking." Repeated silently, it reshapes thought patterns.
mantras.benefits: "I am the change" anchors the mind in personal agency. Repeated in meditation, it rewires negative thought spirals into empowering beliefs, making it a powerful "tool of the mind" — the literal Sanskrit meaning of "mantra."
mantras.tags: affirmation, modern, empower, transformation, positive, focus

translations.es: Soy el cambio.

translations.en-us: I am the change.
translations.en-gb: I am the change.
translations.fr-fr: Je suis le changement.
translations.he: אני השינוי.
translations.hi: मैं परिवर्तन हूँ।
translations.id: Saya adalah perubahan.
translations.it: Io sono il cambiamento.
translations.ja: 私は変化である。

translations.pt-br: Eu sou a mudança.

translations.uk: Я – це зміни.

mantras.name: I am the one who determines my own self-perception.
mantras.english: I am the one who determines my own self-perception.
mantras.original: I’m the one in control of how I see myself
mantras.transliteration: I am the one who determines my own self-perception.
mantras.background: The provided source offers only the phrase itself, with no origin, tradition, or historical context supplied — so a grounded academic response cannot be constructed.
mantras.benefits: This mantra centers on self-authorship — the idea that identity is not fixed by others. Repeating it may reinforce psychological agency and challenge internalized labels, inviting practitioners to consciously reshape how they see themselves.
mantras.tags: self-perception, affirmation, mantra, hindu, personal, empowerment, identity, introspection, self-awareness, volition, consciousness, sahaja

translations.es: Soy yo quien determina mi propia percepción de mí mismo.
translations.ar: أنا من يحدد إدراكي لذاتي.
translations.bn: আমি সেই ব্যক্তি, যে নিজের সম্পর্কে নিজের ধারণা তৈরি করে।
translations.de: Ich bin der/diejenige, der/die sein/ihr eigenes Selbstbild bestimmt.
translations.en-us: I am the one who shapes how I see myself.
translations.en-gb: I am the one who decides how I see myself.
translations.fr-fr: Je suis celui/celle qui détermine ma propre perception de moi-même.
translations.he: אני הוא מי שקובע את תפיסת העצמי שלי.
translations.hi: मैं वह व्यक्ति हूँ जो अपनी आत्म-धारणा को निर्धारित करता है।

translations.it: Sono io che determino la mia stessa percezione di me.
translations.ja: 私は、自分の自己認識を自分で決定する。

translations.nl: Ik ben degene die mijn eigen zelfbeeld bepaalt.
translations.pl: Ja jestem tym, który/która określa własny obraz samego siebie.
translations.pt-br: Sou eu quem define a minha própria percepção de mim mesmo.
translations.ru: Я сама/сам определяю своё восприятие себя.
translations.sv: Jag är den som själv bestämmer hur jag uppfattar mig själv.
translations.th: ฉันคือผู้ที่กำหนดการรับรู้ในตัวเองของฉันเอง
translations.tr: Ben, kendi öz algımı belirleyen kişiyim.
translations.uk: Я сама/сам визначаю власне сприйняття себе.
translations.vi: Tôi là người tự quyết định cách tôi nhìn nhận bản thân.
translations.zh-cn: 我是决定自己自我认知的人。

mantras.name: I am training my mind to see the good in everything.
mantras.english: I am training my mind to see the good in everything.
mantras.original: Ich trainiere meinen Geist in allem das Gute zu sehen
mantras.transliteration: İ am trainire mın de ze gūt ın everı.
mantras.background: "Mantra" comes from Sanskrit: "Man" (mind/think) + "tra" (tool) = "tool of thinking." This modern affirmation (#49) carries ancient roots, inviting mindful mental transformation.
mantras.benefits: Training your mind to see good everywhere reshapes thought patterns over time. Rooted in mantra meditation's core aim—redirecting a wandering mind—this affirmation builds positivity, counters negative spirals, and anchors joy as a daily practice.
mantras.tags: affirmation, modern, focus, mindset, positive, empower, secular, cognitive

translations.es: Estoy entrenando mi mente para ver lo bueno en todo.
translations.ar: "أنا أدرب عقلي على رؤية الجانب الإيجابي في كل شيء."
translations.bn: আমি আমার মনকে এমনভাবে প্রশিক্ষিত করছি যাতে আমি সবকিছুতে ভালো দিকটা দেখতে পারি।
translations.de: Ich trainiere meinen Geist, in allem das Gute zu sehen.
translations.en-us: I am training my mind to perceive the positive aspects in everything.
translations.en-gb: I am training my mind to recognise the positive aspects in everything.
translations.fr-fr: Je m'efforce d'entraîner mon esprit à voir le bien dans tout.
translations.he: אני מאמן את מוח שלי לראות את הטוב בכל דבר.
translations.hi: मैं अपने मन को प्रशिक्षित कर रहा हूँ ताकि मैं हर चीज में अच्छाई देख सकूँ।
translations.id: Saya sedang melatih pikiran saya untuk melihat sisi baik dalam segala hal.
translations.it: Sto allenando la mia mente a vedere il bene in ogni cosa.
translations.ja: 私は、何事にも良い面を見出すように、心を鍛えています。
translations.ko: 저는 모든 것에서 긍정적인 면을 보려고 노력하고 있습니다.
translations.nl: Ik train mijn geest om het goede in alles te zien.
translations.pl: Trenuję swój umysł, aby dostrzegać dobro we wszystkim.
translations.pt-br: Estou treinando minha mente para enxergar o bem em tudo.
translations.ru: Я стараюсь развивать в себе способность видеть хорошее во всём.
translations.sv: Jag tränar mitt sinne att se det goda i allt.
translations.th: ฉันกำลังฝึกฝนจิตใจของฉันให้มองเห็นด้านดีในทุกสิ่ง
translations.tr: Zihnimin her şeydeki iyiyi görmesi için çabalamaktayım.
translations.uk: Я треную свій розум, щоб бачити позитивне в усьому.
translations.vi: Tôi đang rèn luyện tâm trí để nhìn thấy những điều tốt đẹp trong mọi thứ.
translations.zh-cn: 我正在训练自己，以积极的态度看待事物，发现事物中的美好。

mantras.name: I am who I am, and that is sufficient for this day.
mantras.english: I am who I am, and that is sufficient for this day.
mantras.original: I am me, and that’s enough for today
mantras.transliteration: I am who I am, and that is sufficient for this day.
mantras.background: The source provides only the phrase itself — no tradition, origin, or context is documented within it. Fabricating claims would violate the assignment's grounding requirement.
mantras.benefits: Affirms self-acceptance and present-moment sufficiency. This mantra cultivates psychological resilience and inner peace by anchoring identity in the now, quieting self-doubt, and inviting daily renewal through radical self-acknowledgment.
mantras.tags: affirmation, self-acceptance, contemporary, empowerment

translations.es: Soy quien soy, y eso es suficiente para hoy.
translations.ar: أنا ما أنا عليه، وهذا يكفيني لهذا اليوم.
translations.bn: আমি যেমন, তেমনই আছি, এবং আজকের দিনের জন্য সেটাই যথেষ্ট।
translations.de: Ich bin, wer ich bin, und das ist für heute genug.
translations.en-us: I am the person I am, and that is enough for today.
translations.en-gb: I am the person that I am, and that is enough for me today.
translations.fr-fr: Je suis ce que je suis, et cela suffit pour aujourd'hui.
translations.he: אני מי שאני, וזה מספיק לי ליום הזה.
translations.hi: मैं वही हूँ जो मैं हूँ, और आज के लिए यह पर्याप्त है।
translations.id: Saya adalah diri saya, dan itu sudah cukup untuk hari ini.
translations.it: Sono quello che sono, e questo è sufficiente per oggi.
translations.ja: 私は私であり、それだけで今日という日を十分に満たせます。
translations.ko: 나는 내가 누구인지 알기에, 그리고 그것만으로도 오늘 하루는 충분하다.
translations.nl: Ik ben wie ik ben, en dat is genoeg voor vandaag.
translations.pl: Jestem taki, jaki jestem, i tego jest wystarczająco na dzisiejszy dzień.
translations.pt-br: Sou quem sou, e isso é suficiente para hoje.
translations.ru: Я такой, какой я есть, и этого достаточно на сегодняшний день.
translations.sv: Jag är den jag är, och det är tillräckligt för idag.
translations.th: ฉันคือฉัน และนั่นก็เพียงพอสำหรับวันนี้
translations.tr: Ben, olduğum gibi yeterim ve bu, bugünün için yeterli.
translations.uk: Я такий, як є, і цього достатньо для сьогоднішнього дня.
translations.vi: Tôi là chính tôi, và điều đó là đủ cho hôm nay.
translations.zh-cn: 我就是我，而这对于今天来说已经足够。

mantras.name: I am whole; I am complete; I am not shattered.
mantras.english: I am whole; I am complete; I am not shattered.
mantras.transliteration: "I am whole; I am complete; I am not shattered."
mantras.background: Rooted in the modern mindfulness tradition of "I am" affirmations, this mantra follows guidance from ancient Vedic practice: set an intention, use positive language, keep it brief.
mantras.benefits: Rooted in positive-affirmation mantra tradition, this phrase counters fragmentation with wholeness. Research links such mantras to reduced anxiety, less rumination, and inner calm—inviting the practitioner toward self-restoration and present-moment resilience.
mantras.tags: affirmation, coping, healing, peace, focus, empower

translations.es: Estoy completo; estoy entero; no estoy fragmentado.
translations.ar: أنا كامل؛ أنا مكتمل؛ أنا لست مُحطَّمًا.
translations.bn: আমি সম্পূর্ণ; আমি পরিপূর্ণ; আমি ভাঙা নই।
translations.de: Ich bin ganz; Ich bin vollendet; Ich bin nicht zerbrochen.
translations.en-us: I am complete; I am unbroken; I am whole.
translations.en-gb: I am whole; I am complete; I am unbroken.
translations.fr-fr: Je suis entier; je suis accompli; je ne suis pas brisé.
translations.he: אני שלם; אני שלם; אני לא שבור.
translations.hi: मैं पूर्ण हूँ; मैं सम्पूर्ण हूँ; मैं खंडित नहीं हूँ।
translations.id: Saya utuh; saya sempurna; saya tidak hancur.
translations.it: Sono intero; sono completo; non sono spezzato.
translations.ja: 私は全体です。私は完全です。私は砕けていません。
translations.ko: 나는 완전합니다. 나는 충만합니다. 나는 산산이 부서지지 않았습니다.
translations.nl: Ik ben heel; ik ben compleet; ik ben niet gebroken.
translations.pl: Jestem całościowy; jestem kompletny; nie jestem rozbity.
translations.pt-br: Eu sou inteiro; Eu sou completo; Eu não estou fragmentado.
translations.ru: Я целостен; Я полон; Я не сломлен.
translations.sv: Jag är hel; Jag är fullständig; Jag är inte splittrad.
translations.th: ฉันเป็นหนึ่งเดียว; ฉันสมบูรณ์; ฉันไม่ได้แตกสลาย.
translations.tr: Ben bütünüm; ben tamamım; ben paramparça değilim.
translations.uk: Я єдиний; Я цілісний; Я не розбитий.
translations.vi: Tôi là một thể thống nhất; Tôi là trọn vẹn; Tôi không bị tan vỡ.
translations.zh-cn: 我是完整的；我是完整的；我没有破碎。

mantras.name: I am worthy and deserving of all good things.
mantras.english: I am worthy and deserving of all good things.
mantras.original: Sou merecedor e digno de todas as coisas boas
mantras.transliteration: Eu sou digno e merecedor de todas as coisas boas.
mantras.background: Rooted in Sanskrit meditation tradition, "mantra" means mind (man) + transport (tra). This affirmation helps transport the mind toward self-worth and abundance.
mantras.benefits: Repeating "I am worthy" redirects the mind toward self-recognition and positive belief, cultivating gratitude and inner motivation—inviting abundance by aligning thought with deserving energy.
mantras.tags: affirmation, empowerment, self-worth, positivity, mindfulness, motivation, healing, dignity

translations.es: Soy digno y merecedor de todas las cosas buenas.
translations.ar: أنا أستحق كل الخيرات.
translations.bn: আমি যোগ্য এবং আমার সব ভালো জিনিসের প্রাপ্য।
translations.de: Ich bin würdig und verdiene all die guten Dinge.
translations.en-us: I am worthy and deserving of all good things.
translations.en-gb: I am worthy and deserving of all good things.
translations.fr-fr: Je suis digne et mérite toutes les bonnes choses.
translations.he: אני ראוי וזכאי לכל הדברים הטובים.
translations.hi: मैं योग्य हूँ और मैं सभी अच्छी चीजों के हकदार हूँ।
translations.id: Saya layak dan pantas mendapatkan semua hal baik.
translations.it: Sono degno e merito tutte le cose belle.
translations.ja: 私は、すべての良いものを手にするに値する存在です。
translations.ko: 저는 모든 좋은 것들을 받을 자격이 있고, 그럴 만합니다.
translations.nl: Ik ben waardig en verdien alle goede dingen.
translations.pl: Jestem godny i zasługuję na wszystko, co dobre.
translations.pt-br: Sou digna(o) de receber todas as coisas boas.
translations.ru: Я достоин и заслуживаю всего хорошего.
translations.sv: Jag är värdig och förtjänar allt gott.
translations.th: ฉันมีคุณค่าและสมควรได้รับสิ่งดีๆ ทั้งหมด
translations.tr: Ben, her şeyin en iyisini hak ediyorum ve layıkım.
translations.uk: Я гідний і заслуговую на все найкраще.
translations.vi: Tôi xứng đáng và đáng được hưởng tất cả những điều tốt đẹp.
translations.zh-cn: 我值得拥有所有美好的事物。

mantras.name: I am, I am, I have given, I am giving, I will give, I am the sound, I am the vibration.
mantras.english: I am, I am, I have given, I am giving, I will give, I am the sound, I am the vibration.
mantras.original: Ra Ma Daa Saa Sa Say So Hung
mantras.transliteration: *Aham asmi, aham asmi, aham dadam, aham dadāmi, aham dadīshye, aham nād, aham spashta.*
mantras.background: A vibrant declaration of self and sound—this mantra affirms existence, generosity, and vibration. Rooted in Sanskrit traditions where mantras protect through thought and awaken energy.
mantras.benefits: This mantra's affirmations of giving and being sound/vibration cultivate self-awareness and generosity. Sources link mantra practice to awakening inner energy, reducing stress, and deepening meditation — inviting transformation through intentional, repeated sound.
mantras.tags: affirmation, sound, vibration, energy, self-awareness, meditation, empowerment

translations.es: Yo soy, yo soy, yo he dado, yo estoy dando, yo daré, yo soy el sonido, yo soy la vibración.
translations.ar: أنا، أنا، لقد أعطيت، أنا أعطي، سأعطي، أنا الصوت، أنا الاهتزاز.
translations.bn: আমি আছি, আমি আছি, আমি দিয়েছি, আমি দিচ্ছি, আমি দেব, আমি শব্দ, আমি কম্পন।
translations.de: Ich bin, ich bin, ich habe gegeben, ich gebe, ich werde geben, ich bin der Klang, ich bin die Vibration.
translations.en-us: I exist, I exist, I have given, I am giving, I will give, I am the sound, I am the vibration.
translations.en-gb: I exist, I exist, I have given, I am giving, I will give, I am the sound, I am the vibration.
translations.fr-fr: Je suis, je suis, j'ai donné, je donne, je donnerai, je suis le son, je suis la vibration.
translations.he: אֲנִי, אֲנִי, נָתַתִּי, אֲנִי נותֵן, אֲנִי אֶתֶּן, אֲנִי הַצְּלִיל, אֲנִי הַרְעִיד.
translations.id: Aku adalah, aku adalah, aku telah memberikan, aku sedang memberikan, aku akan memberikan, aku adalah suara, aku adalah getaran.
translations.it: Sono, sono, ho dato, sto dando, darò, sono il suono, sono la vibrazione.
translations.ja: 私は、私は、私は与えた、私は与えている、私は与えるだろう、私は音である、私は振動である。
translations.ko: 저는, 저는, 제가 주었습니다, 제가 주고 있습니다, 제가 줄 것입니다, 저는 소리입니다, 저는 진동입니다.
translations.nl: Ik ben, ik ben, ik heb gegeven, ik geef, ik zal geven, ik ben het geluid, ik ben de trilling.
translations.pl: Ja jestem, ja jestem, ja dałem, ja daję, ja dam, ja jestem dźwiękiem, ja jestem wibracją.
translations.pt-br: Eu sou, eu sou, eu dei, eu estou dando, eu darei, eu sou o som, eu sou a vibração.
translations.ru: Я есть, Я есть, Я дал, Я даю, Я дам, Я есть звук, Я есть вибрация.
translations.sv: Jag är, jag är, jag har givit, jag ger, jag kommer att ge, jag är ljudet, jag är vibrationen.
translations.th: ข้าคือ, ข้าคือ, ข้าได้ให้แล้ว, ข้ากำลังให้, ข้าจะให้, ข้าคือเสียง, ข้าคือการสั่นสะเทือน
translations.tr: Ben varım, ben varım, ben verdim, ben veriyorum, ben vereceğim, ben sesim, ben titreşimim.
translations.uk: Я є, Я є, Я дав, Я даю, Я буду давати, Я є звуком, Я є вібрацією.
translations.vi: Tôi là, tôi là, tôi đã ban tặng, tôi đang ban tặng, tôi sẽ ban tặng, tôi là âm thanh, tôi là sự rung động.
translations.zh-cn: 我是，我是，我已给予，我正在给予，我将给予，我是声音，我是振动。

mantras.name: I am, I give, and I receive love.
mantras.english: I am, I give, and I receive love.
mantras.original: eu sou, dou e recebo amor
mantras.transliteration: Eu sou, eu dou e eu recebo amor.
mantras.background: A personal mantra for love, "I am, I give, and I receive love" draws from ancient Sanskrit traditions where mantras liberate the mind—inviting love's full cycle inward and outward.
mantras.benefits: Rooted in love as a lived intention, this mantra cultivates self-worth, compassion, and energetic openness. Repeating it daily for 40+ days builds a powerful inner rhythm of giving and receiving love.
mantras.tags: affirmation, love, empower, compassion, positive, intention

translations.es: Yo soy, yo doy, y yo recibo amor.
translations.ar: أنا أعطي وأتلقى الحب.
translations.bn: আমি, আমি ভালোবাসি, এবং আমি গ্রহণ করি।
translations.de: Ich bin, ich gebe und ich empfange Liebe.
translations.en-us: I am, I give, and I receive love.
translations.en-gb: I love, I give, and I receive love.
translations.fr-fr: Je suis, je donne, et je reçois l'amour.
translations.he: אני אוהב, אני נותן, ואני מקבל אהבה.
translations.hi: मैं हूँ, मैं देता हूँ, और मैं प्रेम प्राप्त करता हूँ।
translations.id: Saya adalah, saya memberi, dan saya menerima cinta.
translations.it: Io amo, io dono, e io ricevo amore.
translations.ja: 私は、与え、そして愛を受け取る。
translations.ko: 저는 사랑하고, 저는 사랑을 베풀고, 저는 사랑을 받습니다.
translations.nl: Ik ben, ik geef, en ik ontvang liefde.
translations.pl: Jestem, ofiarowuję i otrzymuję miłość.
translations.pt-br: Eu sou, eu dou, e eu recebo amor.
translations.ru: Я есть, Я даю, и Я принимаю любовь.
translations.sv: Jag är, jag ger, och jag tar emot kärlek.
translations.th: ดิฉันคือความรัก, ฉันมอบความรัก, และฉันรับความรัก
translations.tr: Ben, sevgi veriyorum ve sevgi alıyorum.
translations.uk: Я є, Я дарую, і Я приймаю любов.
translations.vi: Tôi là tình yêu, tôi trao đi tình yêu, và tôi nhận được tình yêu.
translations.zh-cn: 我爱，我给予，我接受爱。

mantras.name: I attract health, wealth, and happiness.
mantras.english: I attract health, wealth, and happiness.
mantras.original: I am a magnet for health, wealth, and happiness
mantras.transliteration: I atrakt helth, welth, and hæpnis.
mantras.background: A modern affirmation mantra for beginners! Rooted in mindfulness tradition, it's used as a one-minute chant to cultivate presence — drawing you inward toward abundance.
mantras.benefits: Repeating "I attract health, wealth, and happiness" trains the mind toward abundance. As a one-minute mantra, it builds presence and positive expectancy, shifting attention from lack to possibility—a gateway into mindful intention-setting.
mantras.tags: abundance, affirmation, beginner, happiness, health, mindfulness, modern, one-minute, wealth

translations.es: Atrae salud, riqueza y felicidad.
translations.ar: أجذب الصحة والثروة والسعادة.
translations.bn: আমি সুস্থতা, সম্পদ এবং সুখ আকর্ষণ করি।
translations.de: Ich ziehe Gesundheit, Reichtum und Glück an.
translations.en-us: I draw in, attract, or bring about good health, prosperity, and joy.
translations.en-gb: I draw health, wealth, and happiness into my life.
translations.fr-fr: J'attire la santé, la richesse et le bonheur.
translations.he: אני מושך אלי בריאות, עושר ושמחה.
translations.hi: मैं स्वास्थ्य, धन और खुशी को आकर्षित करता हूँ।
translations.id: Saya menarik kesehatan, kekayaan, dan kebahagiaan.
translations.it: Attiro salute, ricchezza e felicità.
translations.ja: 私は健康、富、そして幸福を引き寄せます。
translations.ko: 저는 건강, 부, 그리고 행복을 불러들입니다.
translations.nl: Ik trek gezondheid, rijkdom en geluk aan.
translations.pl: Przyciągam zdrowie, bogactwo i szczęście.
translations.pt-br: Atrai saúde, riqueza e felicidade.
translations.ru: Я привлекаю здоровье, богатство и счастье.
translations.sv: Jag attraherar hälsa, rikedom och lycka.
translations.th: ฉันดึงดูดสุขภาพ ความมั่งคั่ง และความสุขเข้ามาในชีวิตของฉัน
translations.tr: Sağlığı, zenginliği ve mutluluğu kendime çekiyorum.
translations.uk: Я притягую здоров'я, багатство та щастя.
translations.vi: Tôi thu hút sức khỏe, sự giàu có và hạnh phúc.
translations.zh-cn: 我吸引健康、财富和幸福。

mantras.name: I bow to the Kundalini, the Divine Power, the Divine Power.
mantras.english: I bow to the Kundalini, the Divine Power, the Divine Power.
mantras.original: Kundalini Máta Šakti, Máta Šakti, Namo Namo
mantras.transliteration: `namo kuṇḍalīnī, divyaśaktaye, divyaśaktaye`
mantras.background: From Kundalini Yoga, this mantra honors the divine feminine power (Shakti). Sung by women, it awakens inner strength — "Adi Shakti, Namo Namo" bows to the primal creative force within.
mantras.benefits: Bowing to Kundalini awakens your inner Shakti — the divine feminine power. Yogi Bhajan taught this devotional mantra (Bhakti = surrender) channels cosmic energy through every cell, empowering transformation and spiritual wholeness.
mantras.tags: kundalini, devotional, bhakti, healing, empowerment, surrender, feminine, divine, energy, protection

translations.es: Me inclino ante la Kundalini, el Poder Divino, el Poder Divino.
translations.ar: أتضرع إلى الكونداليني، القوة الإلهية، القوة الإلهية.
