translations.zh-cn: 欢呼，欢呼，欢呼，欢呼，主啊！

mantras.name: I am grounded like the roots of a tree.
mantras.english: I am grounded like the roots of a tree.
mantras.original: Estou ancorado como as raízes de uma árvore
mantras.transliteration: Eu estou fundamentado como as raízes de uma árvore.
mantras.background: Rooted in Vedic tradition, this modern affirmation mantra channels ancient grounding wisdom. Repeat it rhythmically—let breath and phrase merge, anchoring mind to the present moment.
mantras.benefits: Repeating "I am grounded like the roots of a tree" anchors a wandering mind to the present. Science confirms mantra practice releases endorphins, reduces blood pressure, and builds inner calm — especially when the meaning is embraced.
mantras.tags: affirmation, grounding, presence, peace, stability, centering, focus, earth, nature, foundation, calm

translations.es: Estoy arraigado como las raíces de un árbol.
translations.ar: أنا راسخ كجذور شجرة.
translations.bn: আমি গাছের শিকড়ের মতো দৃঢ়ভাবে প্রতিষ্ঠিত।
translations.de: Ich bin fest verwurzelt, wie die Wurzeln eines Baumes.
translations.en-us: I am as steadfast and deeply rooted as the roots of a tree.
translations.en-gb: I am firmly rooted, like the roots of a tree.
translations.fr-fr: Je suis ancré comme les racines d'un arbre.
translations.he: אני יציב וחזק כמו שורשי עץ.
translations.hi: मैं एक पेड़ की जड़ों की तरह स्थिर और दृढ़ हूँ।
translations.id: Saya berakar kuat, seperti akar pohon.
translations.it: Sono saldo come le radici di un albero.
translations.ja: 私は、まるで木の根のように、地にしっかりと根を張っている。
translations.ko: 저는 나무 뿌리처럼 굳건합니다.
translations.nl: Ik ben stevig verankerd, zoals de wortels van een boom.
translations.pl: Jestem zakorzeniony, niczym korzenie drzewa.
translations.pt-br: Estou firme como as raízes de uma árvore.
translations.ru: Я крепко стою на земле, подобно корням дерева.
translations.sv: Jag är förankrad, likt trädets rötter.
translations.th: ฉันหยั่งรากลึกมั่นคงดั่งรากของต้นไม้
translations.tr: Ben, bir ağacın kökleri gibi sağlam ve derin bir şekilde yerleşmişim.
translations.uk: Я відчуваю себе міцним, як коріння дерева.
translations.vi: Tôi vững chãi như rễ cây.
translations.zh-cn: 我像树根一样扎根于此。

mantras.name: May all beings be happy; may all beings be peaceful; may all beings be free from suffering.
mantras.english: May all beings be happy; may all beings be peaceful; may all beings be free from suffering.
mantras.transliteration: `mayāṃ sarvaiḥ pratī pāṇāṃ; mayāṃ sarvaiḥ pratī śāntiḥ; mayāṃ sarvaiḥ pratī dukhaṃ nāstū`
mantras.background: A Buddhist loving-kindness wish for all beings—rooted in universal compassion, chanted to dissolve the boundary between self and other.
mantras.benefits: Om's resonance calms the nervous system and shifts brainwaves toward meditative theta states. Its vibrations stimulate the vagal nerve, inviting stillness. Chanting Om connects practitioners to creation's primordial pulse — a doorway to transcendence.
mantras.tags: hindu, mantra, peace, compassion, wellbeing, liberation, prayer, healing, sound, unity, affirmation, vedic

translations.es: Que todos los seres sean felices; que todos los seres sean pacíficos; que todos los seres estén libres del sufrimiento.
translations.ar: لكي يكون جميع الكائنات سعداء؛ لكي يكون جميع الكائنات في سلام؛ لكي يكون جميع الكائنات خاليين من المعاناة.
translations.bn: সব প্রাণী সুখী হোক; সব প্রাণী শান্ত হোক; সব প্রাণী দুঃখ থেকে মুক্ত হোক।
translations.de: Mögen alle Wesen glücklich sein; mögen alle Wesen friedvoll sein; mögen alle Wesen frei von Leid sein.
translations.en-us: May all beings be happy; may all beings be at peace; may all beings be liberated from suffering.
translations.en-gb: May all beings experience happiness; may all beings find peace; may all beings be liberated from suffering.
translations.fr-fr: Puisse tous les êtres être heureux ; puisse tous les êtres être en paix ; puisse tous les êtres être libérés de la souffrance.
translations.he: יהי כל היצורים מאושרים; יהי כל היצורים בשלום; יהי כל היצורים חופשיים מכל סבל.
translations.hi: सभी प्राणी सुखी हों; सभी प्राणी शांत हों; सभी प्राणी दुःख से मुक्त हों।
translations.id: Semoga semua makhluk hidup berbahagia; semoga semua makhluk hidup berketenangan; semoga semua makhluk hidup terbebas dari penderitaan.
translations.it: "Possano tutti gli esseri essere felici; possano tutti gli esseri essere in pace; possano tutti gli esseri essere liberi dalla sofferenza."
translations.ja: すべての生きとし生けるものが、幸せでありますように。すべての生きとし生けるものが、平和でありますように。すべての生きとし生けるものが、苦しみから解放されますように。
translations.ko: 모든 존재들이 행복하기를, 모든 존재들이 평화롭기를, 모든 존재들이 고통에서 벗어나기를.
translations.nl: Moge alle wezens gelukkig zijn; moge alle wezens vrede kennen; moge alle wezens vrij zijn van lijden.
translations.pl: Niech wszystkie istoty będą szczęśliwe; niech wszystkie istoty będą spokojne; niech wszystkie istoty będą wolne od cierpienia.
translations.pt-br: Que todos os seres sejam felizes; que todos os seres sejam pacíficos; que todos os seres estejam livres do sofrimento.
translations.ru: Пусть все существа будут счастливы; пусть все существа будут в мире; пусть все существа будут свободны от страданий.
translations.sv: Må alla varelser vara lyckliga; må alla varelser vara fridfulla; må alla varelser vara befriade från lidande.
translations.th: ขอให้สรรพสัตว์มีความสุข ขอให้สรรพสัตว์มีความสงบ ขอให้สรรพสัตว์พ้นจากความทุกข์
translations.tr: Tüm canlıların mutlu olması, tüm canlıların huzurlu olması, tüm canlıların acıdan uzak olması.
translations.uk: Нехай всі живі істоти будуть щасливими; нехай всі живі істоти будуть спокійними; нехай всі живі істоти будуть вільними від страждань.
translations.vi: Cầu mong tất cả chúng sinh đều hạnh phúc; cầu mong tất cả chúng sinh đều an bình; cầu mong tất cả chúng sinh đều giải thoát khỏi khổ đau.
translations.zh-cn: 愿一切众生皆得快乐；愿一切众生皆得安宁；愿一切众生离苦得乐。

mantras.name: On all-encompassing compassion.
mantras.english: On all-encompassing compassion.
mantras.transliteration: 全包摂的な慈悲 (Zen hōseki-teki na jihi)
mantras.background:
Om (ॐ): Hinduism's primordial seed syllable — the vibrational source of all creation. Its three sounds (A-U-M) map waking, dream, and deep sleep states, pointing toward transcendence.
  Grounding: Sources confirm Om as Hindu "Pranava," described in Mandukya Upanishad; A/U/M represent three states of consciousness; it is the foundational bija (seed) mantra.
mantras.benefits:
Om resonates as the universe's primordial vibration, stilling the nervous system and shifting brainwave activity toward deep meditative states. Chanting it awakens unity consciousness and bridges the finite self to infinite reality.
  Grounding: Sources cite theta brainwave shifts, vagal stimulation, autonomic relaxation, and Vedantic equation of Om with Brahman — the absolute reality underlying all existence.
mantras.tags: hindu, vedic, tantric, mantra, devotion, liberation, sound, meditation, spiritual, divine, focus, affirmation, shiva

translations.es: Compasión universal e ilimitada.
translations.ar: بكل رحمة شاملة.
translations.bn: সর্বব্যাপী করুণা।
translations.de: Universelle Mitgefühl.
translations.en-us: Universal compassion.
translations.en-gb: Universal compassion.
translations.fr-fr: La compassion universelle et illimitée.
translations.he: רחמים השוכנים בכל.
translations.hi: सर्व-व्यापक करुणा।
translations.id: Kasih sayang yang meliputi segala sesuatu.
translations.it: Compassione universale e totale.
translations.ja: 全てを包含する慈悲 (Subete o hōkan suru jihi).
translations.ko: 모든 것을 포용하는 자비.
translations.nl: Totale compassie.
translations.pl: O wszechogarniającym współczuciu.
translations.pt-br: Em compaixão universal e abrangente.
translations.ru: О всеобъемлющем сострадании.
translations.sv: Allomfattande medkänsla.
translations.th: ความเมตตาที่ครอบคลุมทุกสิ่ง
translations.tr: Herkese şefkat gösteren, tüm canlılara karşı duyarlı ve merhametli olma.
translations.uk: Всеобъемлющее сострадание.
translations.vi: Trái tim tràn đầy lòng trắc ẩn vô biên.
translations.zh-cn: 全 encompassing compassion: 涵盖一切的慈悲。

mantras.name: One Divine Reality, Truth is my identity, Infinite Radiance of the Creator.
mantras.english: One Divine Reality, Truth is my identity, Infinite Radiance of the Creator.
mantras.original: Ek Ong Kar, Sat Nam, Siri Wahe Guru
mantras.transliteration: एक दिव्य सत्य, सत्य ही मेरी पहचान है, सृजनकर्ता की अनंत ज्योति।
mantras.background: From Kundalini Yoga tradition, "Ek Ong Kar, Sat Nam, Siri Wahe Guru" means: One Divine Reality, Truth is my identity, Infinite Radiance of the Creator.
mantras.benefits: Chanting this mantra of divine oneness and infinite radiance may quiet the analytical mind, allowing deeper states of calm. Research links mantra practice to reduced anxiety and elevated mood through endorphin release.
mantras.tags: hindu, gayatri, mantra, meditation, devotion, shiva, healing, tranquility, indian, japa, rhythm, sound, vibration

translations.es: Una Realidad Divina, la Verdad es mi identidad, Radiante Infinitud del Creador.
translations.ar: الواقع الإلهي الواحد، الحقيقة هي هويتي، الإشراق اللامتناهي للخالق.
translations.bn: এক পরম সত্তা, সত্য আমার স্বরূপ, সৃষ্টিকর্তার অসীম জ্যোতি।
translations.de: Eine göttliche Realität, die Wahrheit ist meine Identität, die unendliche Strahlkraft des Schöpfers.
translations.en-us: "One Divine Reality, Truth is my identity, Infinite Radiance of the Creator."
translations.en-gb: One Divine Reality, Truth is my essence, Infinite Radiance emanating from the Creator.
translations.fr-fr: Une réalité divine unique, la Vérité est mon identité, l'Éclat infini du Créateur.
translations.he: מציאות אלוהית אחת, האמת היא הזהות שלי, הברק האינסופי של הבורא.
translations.hi: एक दिव्य सत्य, सत्य ही मेरी पहचान है, सृजनकर्ता की असीम आभा।
translations.id: Satu Realitas Ilahi, Kebenaran adalah identitasku, Pancaran Tak Terhingga dari Sang Pencipta.
translations.it: Un'unica Realtà Divina, la Verità è la mia identità, Radiosità Infinita del Creatore.
translations.ja:
一つの神聖な真理、それが私の本質。創造主の無限の光。
(Ichotsu no shinsei na shinri, sore ga watashi no honshitsu. Sozo-shu no mugen no hikari.)
translations.ko: 하나의 신성한 실재, 진리가 나의 정체입니다, 창조주의 무한한 광채.
translations.nl: Eén goddelijke werkelijkheid, de Waarheid is mijn identiteit, de oneindige straling van de Schepper.
translations.pl: Jedna Boska Rzeczywistość, Prawda jest moją tożsamością, Nieograniczone Światło Stwórcy.
translations.pt-br: Uma única Realidade Divina, a Verdade é minha essência, Radiância Infinita do Criador.
translations.ru: Одна Божественная Реальность, Истина – моя сущность, Бесконечный Свет Творца.
translations.sv: En gudomlig verklighet, sanningen är min identitet, Skaparens oändliga strålglans.
translations.th: ความเป็นจริงอันศักดิ์สิทธิ์สูงสุด, ความจริงคือตัวฉัน, ความสว่างอันไร้ขีดจำกัดแห่งพระผู้สร้าง
translations.tr: Tek İlahi Gerçek, Hakikat benim kimliğimdir, Yaratıcı'nın sonsuz ışığı.
translations.uk: Одна Божественна Істина, Істина є моєю суттю, Безмежне Сяйво Творця.
translations.vi: Một Thực tại thiêng liêng duy nhất, Chân lý là bản sắc của tôi, Ánh sáng vô hạn của Đấng Tạo hóa.
translations.zh-cn: 唯一真理，真理是我的本质，是造物主的无限光辉。

mantras.name: One Divine Reality, the Creator, I bow to the True Name, the Divine Light.
mantras.english: One Divine Reality, the Creator, I bow to the True Name, the Divine Light.
mantras.original: Ek Ong Kar Sat Nam Siri Wahe Guru
mantras.transliteration: एक दिव्य सत्य, सृष्टिकर्ता, मैं सत्य नाम, दिव्य ज्योति को प्रणाम करता हूँ।
mantras.background: From Kundalini Yoga, "Ek Ong Kar Sat Nam Siri Wahe Guru" means "One Divine Reality, True Name, wondrous divine wisdom" — awakening all chakras and the aura.
mantras.benefits: Rooted in Kundalini tradition, this mantra is said to strengthen the aura and activate all chakras, fostering a profound sense of unity and inner truth — drawing practitioners into transformative states of consciousness.
mantras.tags: kundalini, devotional, awakening, transformation, centering, connection, divine, balance, energy, truth

translations.es: Me inclino ante la Única Realidad Divina, el Creador. Saludo al Verdadero Nombre, a la Divina Luz.
translations.ar: "إلى الحقيقة الإلهية الواحدة، الخالق، أتوجه بتبجيل إلى الاسم الحقيقي، النور الإلهي."
translations.bn: এক পরম সত্তা, যিনি সৃষ্টিকর্তা, আমি তাঁর সত্য নামের কাছে, তাঁর দিব্য জ্যোতির কাছে নত হই।
translations.de: Ich verneige mich vor der einen göttlichen Realität, dem Schöpfer. Ich bete zum wahren Namen, zum göttlichen Licht.
translations.en-us: I bow to the One Divine Reality, the Creator; I bow to the True Name, the Divine Light.
translations.en-gb: I bow to the one Divine Reality, the Creator; I offer my reverence to the True Name, the Divine Light.
translations.fr-fr: Une Unique Réalité Divine, le Créateur, je m'incline devant le Vrai Nom, la Lumière Divine.
translations.he: אני משתחווה לאמת המוחלטת, לבורא, לשם האמיתי, לאור האלוהי.
translations.hi: एक दिव्य सत्य, सृजनकर्ता, मैं उस परम नाम को प्रणाम करता हूँ, उस दिव्य ज्योति को।
translations.id: "Kepada Realitas Ilahi Yang Esa, Sang Pencipta, aku bersujud. Aku menyembah Nama Yang Sejati, Cahaya Ilahi."
translations.it: "Mi prostro al Nome Veritiero, alla Luce Divina, riconoscendo l'Unica Realtà Divina, il Creatore."
translations.ja: 至高の実体、創造主よ、私は真の名、神聖な光にひれ伏します。
translations.ko: 하나의 신성한 실재, 창조주께, 저는 진정한 이름, 신성한 빛 앞에 경배합니다.
translations.nl: Ik buig voor de Ene Goddelijke Realiteit, de Schepper, voor de Ware Naam, het Goddelijke Licht.
translations.pl: Składam pokłon Jednej Boskiej Rzeczywistości, Stworzycielowi, Prawdziwemu Imieniu, Boskiemu Światłu.
translations.pt-br: Uma Única Realidade Divina, o Criador, eu me prostro diante do Verdadeiro Nome, da Luz Divina.
translations.ru: Я склоняюсь перед Истинным Именем, перед Божественным Светом, признавая Единственную Божественную Реальность, Творца.
translations.sv: En gudomlig verklighet, Skaparen, jag böjer mig inför det Sanna Namnet, det gudomliga Ljuset.
translations.th: ข้าขอน้อมนมัสการต่อพระนามที่แท้จริง, แสงสว่างอันศักดิ์สิทธิ์, ผู้เป็นหนึ่งเดียว, ผู้สร้างสรรค์
translations.tr: Tek İlahi Gerçek, Yaratıcı, Ben, O'nun Hakikatli İsmine, İlahi Işığına secde ediyorum.
translations.uk: Одна Божественна Істина, Творець, я поклоняюся Істинному Імені, Божественному Світлу.
translations.vi: Tôi cúi lạy một Thực Tại thiêng liêng, Đấng Tạo Hóa, tôi tôn vinh Tên Thánh, Ánh Sáng thiêng liêng.
translations.zh-cn: 唯一的真理，造物主，我向真正的名字，神圣的光明致敬。

mantras.name: One, two, three, bloom, flower.
mantras.english: One, two, three, bloom, flower.
mantras.transliteration: いち、に、さん、咲く、花.
mantras.background: "One, two, three, bloom, flower" lacks roots in any documented mantra tradition. Mantras are sacred sound formulas from Vedic, Buddhist, or Tantric lineages — not counting rhymes.
mantras.benefits: "One, two, three, bloom, flower" lacks documented spiritual or traditional mantra status. No researched benefits can be honestly attributed to it as a mantra practice.
mantras.tags: hindu, vedic, tantric, devotion, sound, mantra, meditation, healing, unity, spiritual, divine, affirmation

translations.es: Uno, dos, tres, florecer, flor.
translations.ar: واحد، اثنان، ثلاثة، ازدهار، زهرة.
translations.bn: এক, দুই, তিন, ফুল, ফোটা।
translations.de: Eins, zwei, drei, Blüte, Blume.
translations.en-us: One, two, three, blossom, flower.
translations.en-gb: One, two, three, blossom, flower.
translations.fr-fr: Un, deux, trois, fleurir, fleur.
translations.he: אחד, שני, שלוש, פריחה, פרח.
translations.hi: एक, दो, तीन, खिलना, फूल।
translations.id: Satu, dua, tiga, mekar, bunga.
translations.it: Uno, due, tre, fioritura, fiore.
translations.ja: いち、に、さん、開花、花。
translations.ko: 하나, 둘, 셋, 꽃, 꽃피움.
translations.nl: Één, twee, drie, bloeien, bloem.
translations.pl: Jeden, dwa, trzy, kwitnienie, kwiat.
translations.pt-br: Um, dois, três, florescer, flor.
translations.ru: Один, два, три, цветение, цветок.
translations.sv: Ett, två, tre, blomma, blomma.
translations.th: หนึ่ง สอง สาม เบ่งบาน ดอกไม้
translations.tr: Bir, iki, üç, çiçek açmak, çiçek.
translations.uk: Один, два, три, розквіт, квітка.
translations.vi: Một, hai, ba, nở, hoa.
translations.zh-cn: 一、二、三、绽放、花。

mantras.name: Oṃ, I surrender to the Divine, salutations.
mantras.english: Oṃ, I surrender to the Divine, salutations.
mantras.transliteration: ओं, अहम् आत्मार्पणं, नमस्कारः।
mantras.background: A Sanskrit Vajrayana mantra — "Oṃ, I surrender to the Divine, salutations" — rooted in Buddhist tradition, chanting it purifies mind and spirit.
mantras.benefits: Surrendering through sacred sound, this mantra cultivates humility and divine connection. Research confirms Sanskrit mantras promote healing and calm, while rhythmic repetition deepens theta-state meditation—awakening profound inner peace.
mantras.tags: buddhist, mantra, devotional, om, divine, surrender, salutation, affirmation

translations.es: Om, me entrego al Divino, saludos.
translations.ar: أوم، أتخلى طائعاً للذات الإلهية، تحية.
translations.bn: ওং, আমি সেই ঐশ্বরিক সত্তার কাছে নিজেকে সমর্পণ করছি, ওঁর প্রতি শ্রদ্ধাঞ্জলি।
translations.de: Om, ich ergebe mich dem Göttlichen, Gruss.
translations.en-us: "Om, I surrender myself to the Divine, salutations."
translations.en-gb: "Om, I surrender myself to the Divine, salutations."
translations.fr-fr: Ôm, je me voue au Divin, salutations.
translations.he: אום, אני נושא את עצמי למסירה לאל, ברכות.
translations.hi: ॐ, मैं दिव्य सत्ता के समक्ष आत्मसमर्पण करता हूँ, प्रणाम।
translations.id: ॐ, Saya berserah diri kepada Yang Maha Kuasa, salam.
translations.it: Ōṃ, mi arrendo al Divino, saluti.
translations.ja: オーム、私は神に身を委ねます。敬意を表します。
translations.ko: 옴, 저는 신성한 존재에게 제 모든 것을 바칩니다. 경의를 표합니다.
translations.nl: Om, ik geef mij over aan het Goddelijke, groet.
translations.pl: Oṁ, oddaję się w ręce Boskości, pozdrowienia.
translations.pt-br: Om, eu me entrego ao Divino, saudações.
translations.ru: Ом, я предаюсь Божеству, приветствия.
translations.sv: Oṃ, jag överlämnar mig till det gudomliga, hälsningar.
translations.th: ข้าขอบูชาต่อพระผู้เป็นเจ้าด้วยความเคารพอย่างสูงสุด ขอคารวะ
translations.tr: Om, İlahi'ye teslim oluyorum, saygılarımla.
translations.uk: Ом, я віддаюся Божеству, вітаю.
translations.vi: Ôm, con xin phó thác cúng dâng cho Đấng Thiêng Liêng, kính chào.
translations.zh-cn: 唵，我向神灵奉献，敬礼。

mantras.name: Praise to the Lord, Praise to the Lord, Praise to the Lord, O' God.
mantras.english: Praise to the Lord, Praise to the Lord, Praise to the Lord, O' God.
mantras.transliteration: "स्तुति भगवान् की, स्तुति भगवान् की, स्तुति भगवान् की, हे भगवान।"
mantras.background: Rooted in ancient Sanskrit (man=mind, tra=mastery), this praise mantra echoes Kirtan's devotional tradition—repeatedly singing divine names to move energy through vibration and intention.
mantras.benefits: Repeating praise to God aligns with Kirtan tradition—chanting divine names to quiet the mind, reduce overstimulation, and invite deep relaxation. Like Japa practice with a mala, it anchors focus and shifts energy through devotional vibration.
mantras.tags: devotional, affirmation, praise, divine, relaxation, repetition

translations.es: Alabado sea el Señor, Alabado sea el Señor, Alabado sea el Señor, Oh, Dios.
translations.ar: الحمد للرب، الحمد للرب، الحمد للرب، يا الله.
translations.bn: "हे प्रभु, हे प्रभु, हे प्रभु, हे भगवान, आपकी स्तुति।" or "भगवान की स्तुति, भगवान की स्तुति, भगवान की स्तुति, हे ईश्वर।"
translations.de: Lob sei dem Herrn, Lob sei dem Herrn, Lob sei dem Herrn, Oh Gott.
translations.en-us: Praise to the Lord, Praise to the Lord, Praise to the Lord, O God.
translations.en-gb: Praise be to the Lord, Praise be to the Lord, Praise be to the Lord, O God.
translations.fr-fr: Louange au Seigneur, Louange au Seigneur, Louange au Seigneur, ô Dieu.
translations.he: שבח לאדון, שבח לאדון, שבח לאדון, ה', אלוהים.
translations.hi: "हे भगवान, हे भगवान, हे भगवान, स्तुति हो आपके।"
translations.id: Pujā Prabhu, Pujā Prabhu, Pujā Prabhu, Oh Tuhan.
translations.it: Lode al Signore, lode al Signore, lode al Signore, o Dio.
translations.ja: 神よ、神よ、神よ、あなたに賛美を捧げます。
translations.ko: 주님을 찬양합니다, 주님을 찬양합니다, 주님을 찬양합니다, 오 하나님.
translations.nl: Lof aan de Heer, Lof aan de Heer, Lof aan de Heer, O God.
translations.pl: Chwała Panu, Chwała Panu, Chwała Panu, o Boże.
translations.pt-br: Louvores ao Senhor, Louvores ao Senhor, Louvores ao Senhor, ó Deus.
translations.ru: Хвала Господу, хвала Господу, хвала Господу, о, Боже.
translations.sv: Lovpris Herren, lovpris Herren, lovpris Herren, o Gud.
translations.th: สรรเสริญพระเจ้า, สรรเสริญพระเจ้า, สรรเสริญพระเจ้า, โอ้ พระผู้เป็นเจ้า.
translations.tr: Tanrı'ya övgüler, Tanrı'ya övgüler, Tanrı'ya övgüler, Ey Tanrı.
translations.uk: Хвала Господу, хвала Господу, хвала Господу, о Боже.
translations.vi: Khen ngợi Chúa, Khen ngợi Chúa, Khen ngợi Chúa, hỡi Thượng Đế.
translations.zh-cn: 赞美主，赞美主，赞美主，哦，神。

mantras.name:
Return to your ancestral jungle home.
Return to the vast wilderness.
Return to the echoes of the ancient forest.
Return to the river without a source.
mantras.english:
Return to your ancestral jungle home.
Return to the vast wilderness.
Return to the echoes of the ancient forest.
Return to the river without a source.
mantras.original:
Pulanglah engkau kepada rimba sekampung
Pulanglah engkau kepada rimba yang besar
Pulanglah engkau kepada gaung guntung
Pulanglah engkau kepada sungai yang tiada berhulu
mantras.transliteration:
Kembali ke rumah hutan nenek moyang Anda.
Kembali ke belantara yang luas.
Kembali ke gema hutan purba.
Kembali ke sungai yang tiada sumbernya.
mantras.background: A Malay exorcism mantra commanding spirits to return to their wild origins. Spoken by shamans, it echoes traditions where jungle spirits must be sent back.
mantras.benefits: Rooted in Malay oral tradition, this mantra uses rhythmic repetition and jungle imagery to banish harmful forces, grounding the practitioner in ancestral power and restoring spiritual balance through sacred sound.
mantras.tags: affirmation, ancestral, earth, grounding, homecoming, metaphorical, nature, reconnection, ritual

translations.es:
*   "Kembali ke rumah di hutan leluhurmu." - "Regresa a tu hogar ancestral en la selva."
*   "Kembali ke alam liar yang luas." - "Regresa a la vasta extensión salvaje."
*   "Kembali ke gema hutan kuno." - "Regresa a los ecos de la antigua selva."
*   "Kembali ke sungai tanpa sumber." - "Regresa al río sin origen."
translations.ar:
1.  **"Kembali ke rumah hutan leluhurmu."**  -  "عُدْ إلى مَنْزِلِ الغَابَةِ الذي ورثته من أسلافك." (ʿUdd ilā manzil al-ghābati alladhī warithtahu min aslāfik.)
2.  **"Kembali ke alam liar yang luas."** - "عُدْ إلى البرية الواسعة." (ʿUdd ilā al-barriyati al-wāsiʿah.)
3.  **"Kembali ke gema hutan purba."** - "عُدْ إلى أصداء الغابة القديمة." (ʿUdd ilā aṣdāʾi al-ghābati al-qadīmah.)
4.  **"Kembali ke sungai yang tak berhulu."** - "عُدْ إلى النهر الذي لا منبع له." (ʿUdd ilā al-nahri alladhī lā minbaʿa lahu.)
translations.bn:
ফিরে যান আপনার আদি ওдите জঙ্গলের গভীরে।
ফিরে যান বিশাল বনের মাঝে।
ফিরে যান প্রাচীন অরণ্যের প্রতিধ্বনীর কাছে।
ফিরে যান সেই নদীতে, যার কোনো উৎস নেই।
translations.de:
*   Ke hutan asal usulmu: *Zurück zu deinem Urwaldheimat.*
*   Kembali ke alam liar yang luas: *Zurück in die weite Wildnis.*
*   Kembali ke gema hutan purba: *Zurück zu den Echos des uralten Waldes.*
*   Kembali ke sungai tanpa sumber: *Zurück zum Fluss ohne Quelle.*
translations.en-us: Return to your ancestral jungle home. Return to the vast wilderness. Return to the echoes of the ancient forest. Return to the river without a source.
translations.en-gb: Return to your ancestral jungle home. Return to the vast wilderness. Return to the echoes of the ancient forest. Return to the river that flows without a beginning.
translations.fr-fr:
1.  Retournez à votre foyer ancestral, au cœur de la jungle.
2.  Retournez vers la vaste étendue sauvage.
3.  Retournez aux échos de la forêt ancienne.
4.  Retournez vers la rivière sans source.
translations.he:
1.  חזור אל בית הג'ונגל הקדום שלך. (Chzor el beit ha-gungei ha-kadoshem shelcha.)
2.  חזור אל הפרא המשתרע. (Chzor el ha-prai ha-meshtare'a.)
3.  חזור אל הד של היער העתיק. (Chzor el ha-dei ha-ya'ar ha-atik.)
4.  חזור אל הנהר שאין לו מקור. (Chzor el ha-nahar she'ein lo mikor.)
translations.hi:
1.  "Return to your ancestral jungle home." - "अपने पूर्वजों के घने जंगल के घर वापस लौट जाओ।"
2.  "Return to the vast wilderness." - "विशाल और असीम प्रकृति में वापस लौट जाओ।"
3.  "Return to the echoes of the ancient forest." - "प्राचीन वन के गूँज में वापस लौट जाओ।"
4.  "Return to the river without a source." - "उस नदी में वापस लौट जाओ जिसका कोई स्रोत नहीं है।"
translations.id:
*   Kembalilah ke rumah hutan leluhurmu.
*   Kembalilah ke alam liar yang luas.
*   Kembalilah ke gema hutan purba.
*   Kembalilah ke sungai yang tak berhulu.
translations.it:
*   "Return to your ancestral jungle home": "Ritorna alla tua dimora ancestrale nella giungla."
*   "Return to the vast wilderness": "Ritorna alla vasta wilderness." (Alternatively, if you want to emphasize the untamed aspect: "Ritorna alla selvaggia immensità.")
*   "Return to the echoes of the ancient forest": "Ritorna ai sussurri della foresta antica." (Or, emphasizing the fading memory: "Ritorna ai ricordi della foresta antica.")
*   "Return to the river without a source": "Ritorna al fiume senza sorgente."
translations.ja:
*   **Return to your ancestral jungle home:** 故郷のジャングルへ帰れ (Kokyou no janguru e kaere)
*   **Return to the vast wilderness:** 広大な自然へ帰れ (Koudai na shizen e kaere)
*   **Return to the echoes of the ancient forest:** 古代の森のこだまへ帰れ (Kodai no mori no kodama e kaere)
*   **Return to the river without a source:** 湧き源のない川へ帰れ (Wakegen no nai kawa e kaere)
translations.ko:
*   **Kembali ke rumah hutan leluhurmu.**
    *   한국어 번역: **"당신의 조상들이 살았던 숲으로 돌아가세요."** (Dangsin-ui josangdeuri salatdeon sup-euro doraga-seyo.)
*   **Kembali ke alam liar yang luas.**
    *   한국어 번역: **"광활한 야생으로 돌아가세요."** (Gwanghalhan yasaeng-euro doraga-seyo.)
*   **Kembali ke gema hutan purba.**
    *   한국어 번역: **"고대의 숲에서 울려 퍼지는 메아리에 귀를 기울이세요."** (Godaee sseum-eseo ullyeo peojineun meari-e guireul giuliyoseyo.)  or **"고대 숲의 울림 속으로 돌아가세요."** (Godae sup-ui ullim sog-euro doroga-seyo.)
*   **Kembali ke sungai tanpa sumber.**
    *   한국어 번역: **"근원 없는 강으로 돌아가세요."** (Geunwon eomneun gang-euro doroga-seyo.)
translations.nl:
1.  Keer terug naar je oerwoud, je oorspronkelijke thuis.
2.  Keer terug naar de uitgestrekte wildernis.
3.  Keer terug naar de echo's van het oude bos.
4.  Keer terug naar de rivier zonder bron.
translations.pl: Powróć do dżungli, domu przodków. Powróć do rozległych dziczy. Powróć do echa pradawnego lasu. Powróć do rzeki bez źródła.
translations.pt-br:
*   Retorne à sua ancestral morada na selva.
*   Retorne à vasta imensidão selvagem.
*   Retorne aos ecos da antiga floresta.
*   Retorne ao rio sem nascente.
translations.ru:
Вернитесь в свой родной, дикий край.
Вернитесь в необъятные просторы дикой природы.
Вернитесь к отголоскам древнего леса.
Вернитесь к реке, не имеющей истока.
translations.sv:
1.  Återvänd till ditt uråldriga, vilda ursprung.
2.  Återvänd till den vidsträckta vildmarken.
3.  Återvänd till de forntida skogens ekon.
4.  Återvänd till floden utan källa.
translations.th: กลับคืนสู่บ้านป่าบรรพบุรุษของคุณ สู่ผืนป่าอันกว้างใหญ่ สู่เสียงสะท้อนของป่าโบราณ สู่แม่น้ำที่ไม่มีต้นกำเนิด
translations.tr:
1.  "Kembali ke rumah hutan leluhurmu." -> "Ataların ormanlık vatanına geri dön."
2.  "Kembali ke alam liar yang luas." -> "Geniş ve vahşi doğaya geri dön."
3.  "Kembali ke gema hutan kuno." -> "Kadim ormanın yankılarına geri dön."
4.  "Kembali ke sungai yang tidak berhulu." -> "Kaynağı olmayan nehre geri dön."
translations.uk:
1.  Повернися до свого рідного джунглевого дому.
2.  Повернися до безмежних просторів дикої природи.
3.  Повернися до відлуння стародавнього лісу.
4.  Повернися до річки, що не має джерела.
translations.vi:
1.  "Kembali ke rumah di hutan leluhurmu."  -> "Hãy trở về ngôi nhà trong khu rừng tổ tiên của bạn."
2.  "Kembali ke alam liar yang luas." -> "Hãy trở về vùng hoang dã bao la."
3.  "Kembali ke gema hutan purba." -> "Hãy trở về những tiếng vọng của khu rừng cổ xưa."
4.  "Kembali ke sungai không có nguồn." -> "Hãy trở về dòng sông không có nguồn gốc."
translations.zh-cn:
回到你的祖先居住的丛林家园。
回归广袤的荒野。
回到古老森林的回声中。
回归无源之河。

mantras.name: The Divine Radiance, the Divine Radiance, the Root, the Foundation, the One that Exists, the One that Reveals.
mantras.english: The Divine Radiance, the Divine Radiance, the Root, the Foundation, the One that Exists, the One that Reveals.
mantras.original: RA MA DA SA, SA SAY SO HUNG
mantras.transliteration: divyaṃ jyotis, divyaṃ jyotis, mūla, adhiṣṭhāna, yāvan, yāvan-pratīkaṣṭe.
mantras.background: Ra Ma Da Sa: a powerful healing mantra whose words mean "Divine Radiance...the One that Reveals"—inviting the mind to transcend limits and touch the sacred.
mantras.benefits: Savitri (Gayatri) mantra, an ode to the Sun's divine radiance, fosters wisdom and mental clarity. Recited with devotion, it supports spiritual growth and helps quiet a restless mind—inviting deeper decisions and inner steadiness.
mantras.tags: hindu, gayatri, gratitude, wisdom, unity, calming, spiritual, mantra, sound, meditation, devine, healing, affirmation

translations.es: La Radiación Divina, la Radiación Divina, la Raíz, el Fundamento, el Ser Existente, el que Revela.
translations.ar: "التَّلَألُؤُ الإلهي، التَّلَألُؤُ الإلهي، الأصل، الأساس، الواحد القائم، الواضح."
translations.bn: ঐশ্বরিক কিরণ, ঐশ্বরিক কিরণ, মূল, ভিত্তি, যে বিদ্যমান, যে প্রকাশিত করে।
translations.de: Die Göttliche Strahlkraft, die Göttliche Strahlkraft, die Wurzel, das Fundament, Das, Was Existiert, Das, Was Eröffnet/Offenbart.
translations.en-us: The Divine Radiance, the Divine Radiance, the Root, the Foundation, the Absolute Existence, the Revealer.
translations.en-gb: The Divine Effulgence, the Divine Effulgence, the Origin, the Basis, the Absolute Existence, the Revealer.
translations.fr-fr: La Splendeur Divine, la Splendeur Divine, la Racine, le Fondement, Celui qui Existe, Celui qui Révèle.

mantras.name: This is the best good deed; it is a good deed; may this good deed be ours; may this be the best good deed.
mantras.english: This is the best good deed; it is a good deed; may this good deed be ours; may this be the best good deed.
mantras.original: aṣ̌əm vohū vahištəm astī uštā astī uštā ahmāi hyat̰ aṣ̌āi vahištāi aṣ̌əm
mantras.transliteration: yaŋhō hamāstāčī; yaŋhō yaštī; yaŋhō yaštī hamāi; yaŋhō yaŋhō hamāstāčī.
mantras.background: From ancient Vedic ritual tradition, this mantra's literal wish—"may this good deed be ours"—reflects mantras' Vedic role: petitioning divine aid for daily virtuous life.
mantras.benefits: This mantra cultivates intentionality around virtuous action, reinforcing a merit-oriented mindset. Repetition (japa) channels the mind toward goodness, fostering psychological clarity, moral resolve, and a sense of shared spiritual purpose.
mantras.tags: affirmation, repetition, japa, prayer, virtue, merit, karma, blessing, ethical

translations.es: Esta es la mejor acción virtuosa; es una acción virtuosa; que esta acción virtuosa sea nuestra; que esto sea la mejor acción virtuosa.

translations.vi: Đây là hành động tốt đẹp nhất; đây là một hành động tốt đẹp; xin cho hành động tốt đẹp này thuộc về chúng ta; xin cho đây là hành động tốt đẹp nhất.

mantras.name: Aad Guray Nameh
mantras.english: I bow to the Primal Wisdom — the fourfold protective mantra
mantras.original: ਆਦ ਗੁਰੇ ਨਮਹ ਜੁਗਾਦ ਗੁਰੇ ਨਮਹ ਸਤਿ ਗੁਰੇ ਨਮਹ ਸ੍ਰੀ ਗੁਰਦੇਵੇ ਨਮਹ
mantras.transliteration: Aad Guray Nameh, Jugaad Guray Nameh, Sat Guray Nameh, Siri Guroo Dayvay Nameh
mantras.abstract:
Aad Guray Nameh — the Mangalacharan or auspicious opening verse — is a fourfold protective mantra from the Sikh tradition, appearing at the beginning of the Sukhmani Sahib (the Prayer of Peace) composed by Guru Arjan Dev, the fifth Sikh Guru. It is traditionally recited three times before beginning any journey, significant endeavor, or meditation session as a protective invocation. The four lines bow to four dimensions of the Guru's reality: the primal wisdom before time (Aad), the wisdom throughout all ages (Jugaad), the living true wisdom (Sat), and the great unseen divine wisdom (Siri Guroo Dayvay).

The word 'Aad' (primal/before the beginning) refers to the Guru that existed before creation — the divine wisdom that precedes all manifestation. 'Jugaad' (through all ages/since the beginning of time) refers to the Guru that has been present throughout the entire span of cosmic history. 'Sat' (true/living) refers to the Guru as it exists in the present moment — the living wisdom accessible right now. 'Siri Guroo Dayvay' (the Great Invisible/Transcendent Guru) refers to the divine wisdom that exceeds all finite form, available to those whose awareness is refined enough to receive it.

In Kundalini Yoga as taught by Yogi Bhajan, Aad Guray Nameh is prescribed as a protective field meditation. Practitioners visualize a white, luminous shield of light surrounding them as they recite the four lines, understanding the mantra to create a literal field of divine protection around the meditator. Whether or not one accepts this literalism, the practice of invoking protective wisdom before beginning practice creates a psychological shift — from scattered, unguarded consciousness to deliberate, protected attention — that makes subsequent meditation more focused and safer from distraction.

The timing and context of recitation matter in traditional practice. Before dawn meditation, before travel, before important meetings, at the moment of waking — each of these is a transitional moment when ordinary consciousness is most vulnerable to distraction and confusion. Aad Guray Nameh at these transitions establishes divine wisdom as the presiding principle of whatever follows. It is, in a sense, a declaration of intention: 'I enter this time under the guidance of wisdom, not of ego.'

The mantra's fourfold structure — before time, throughout time, in present time, beyond time — is a complete survey of the Guru's omnitemporality. By bowing to the Guru in all four temporal dimensions simultaneously, the practitioner releases their grip on their own particular moment in time and opens to the wisdom that transcends the ordinary limits of past, present, and future. This temporal liberation is one of the deepest gifts of mantra practice: the dissolution, even briefly, of the ego's anxious confinement in its own slice of history.

translations.en: I bow to the Primal Wisdom; I bow to the Wisdom through all ages; I bow to the True Wisdom; I bow to the Great Unseen Wisdom
translations.zh: 我礼拜原初智慧；我礼拜贯穿所有时代的智慧；我礼拜真实的智慧；我礼拜伟大的无形智慧
translations.es: Me inclino ante la Sabiduría Primordial; ante la Sabiduría de todas las edades; ante la Verdadera Sabiduría; ante la Gran Sabiduría Invisible

mantras.name: Akaal
mantras.english: The Undying — Timeless Being, deathless reality
mantras.transliteration: Akāl
mantras.abstract:
Akaal — the Undying, the Timeless, the Deathless — is one of the most powerful single-word mantras in the Sikh tradition. 'A' negates, 'kaal' means time and death (both meanings are present in the word), so Akaal means both 'beyond time' and 'beyond death.' It is among the most important attributes of the divine in the Mul Mantar (Akal Murat — timeless form) and was particularly emphasized by Guru Gobind Singh, who named his main place of worship Akal Takht — Throne of the Timeless.

Guru Gobind Singh instructed that when a Sikh dies, those present should call out 'Akaal! Akaal! Akaal!' (Timeless! Timeless! Timeless!) — not as a eulogy for the deceased but as an affirmation for all those present that what is truly real does not die. The body returns to the elements, but the soul — and the divine Akaal that was always the soul's true nature — is beyond death. This practice transforms the moment of greatest apparent defeat into an affirmation of ultimate victory.

In meditation, Akaal is used as a practice of liberation from the fear of death and impermanence. The practitioner sits quietly and holds the word Akaal in awareness, allowing it to draw attention away from the transient surface of experience toward the timeless awareness that witnesses all change without changing itself. This awareness is Akaal — it was never born and will never die; it is the mirror in which all phenomena, including the body and personality, appear and disappear.

The Akal Takht in Amritsar is the highest seat of Sikh temporal authority — the institution from which declarations affecting the entire Sikh community are issued. Its name signals that Sikh governance is meant to be grounded in the timeless divine perspective rather than in the shifting currents of political expediency. Temporal power in service of the Timeless is the Sikh political ideal.

For daily practice, Akaal can be whispered in moments of anxiety about the future or grief about the past — both of which are temporal concerns. By returning attention to the dimension of Akaal — the awareness that exists now, before thought, before the story of past and future — the practitioner finds a refuge that is not dependent on circumstances and cannot be taken away by any event in time.

translations.en: The Undying — Timeless Being, deathless reality
translations.zh: 不死者——永恒的存在，不死的实相
translations.es: El Inmortal — Ser Eterno, realidad sin muerte

mantras.name: Bole So Nihal, Sat Sri Akal
mantras.english: Blessed is one who says: Truth is Timeless and Supreme
mantras.original: ਬੋਲੇ ਸੋ ਨਿਹਾਲ, ਸਤਿ ਸ੍ਰੀ ਅਕਾਲ
mantras.transliteration: Bole so Nihāl, Sat Srī Akāl
mantras.abstract:
Bole So Nihal, Sat Sri Akal — 'Blessed is the one who says: Truth is the Timeless One' — is the Sikh victory cry, traditionally used as a battle cry by the Khalsa warriors, an opening of the Ardas (communal prayer), and a joyful proclamation in celebrations. It is one of the most emotionally powerful phrases in the Sikh tradition, capable of producing an immediate shift in consciousness and energy in anyone who hears or utters it.

The phrase has two parts: the leader calls out 'Bole So Nihal' (speak up and be blessed/ecstatic), and the congregation responds with 'Sat Sri Akal' (Truth is the Great Timeless One). This call-and-response structure creates a moment of collective alignment — everyone present is simultaneously declaring the supremacy of the timeless divine Truth over all temporal concerns, including death, defeat, and suffering.

The use of this cry in battle by the Khalsa warriors reflects a profound spiritual attitude to conflict: the warrior who is rooted in the awareness of Akal (the Timeless) is not afraid of death because they know that what is truly real cannot die. The body may fall; Sat Sri Akal cannot. This is not reckless disregard for life but the deepest possible grounding in life's indestructible source. The warriors who charged with 'Sat Sri Akal' on their lips were those who had, through meditation and practice, genuinely transcended the fear of death.

In contemporary Sikh life, Bole So Nihal, Sat Sri Akal is heard at Gurdwara celebrations, Sikh athletic competitions (gatka), political rallies, weddings, and any occasion requiring collective affirmation and upliftment. Its effect is immediate and recognizable: the room's energy rises, individual boundaries temporarily dissolve, and a collective sense of joy and power emerges. It is one of the most effective group-coherence technologies in any spiritual tradition.

As an individual practice, Sat Sri Akal can be whispered in moments of fear, difficulty, or overwhelm as an affirmation of the supreme reality beyond the circumstances at hand. 'Truth is the Timeless One' — whatever threatens me, whatever I face, the ground of my being is the indestructible Akal. Teachers recommend it as the last thought before sleep and the first thought upon waking, framing the vulnerable hours of sleep within the recognition of timeless divine protection.

translations.en: Blessed is one who says: Truth is Timeless and Supreme
translations.zh: 言此者有福了：真理是永恒的至尊
translations.es: Bendito es quien dice: la Verdad es Eterna y Suprema

mantras.name: First, O Goddess, First, O Goddess, First, O Goddess, Salutations, Salutations.
mantras.english: First, O Goddess, First, O Goddess, First, O Goddess, Salutations, Salutations.
mantras.original: Pritham Bhagvati, Pritham Bhagvati, Pritham Bhagvati, Namo Namo
mantras.transliteration: प्रथम्, हे देवी, प्रथम्, हे देवी, प्रथम्, हे देवी, नमस्कार, नमस्कार।
mantras.background: From Sanskrit Kundalini tradition, "Adi"=first/primordial, saluting Shakti, the primal Goddess — invoking divine feminine creative energy within.
mantras.benefits: Invoking the Goddess "first" signals humility and surrender. Sources link such salutations to cleansing personal desires, awakening inner wisdom, and aligning with divine creative energy—drawing practitioners into a living tradition of devotion.

mantras.name: Gurprasad
mantras.english: By Guru's Grace — grace as the gateway to the divine
mantras.transliteration: Gur Prasādi
mantras.abstract:
Gurprasad — By the Guru's Grace — is the concluding phrase of the Mul Mantar and one of the most theologically significant phrases in Sikhism. It asserts that the divine cannot be attained through human effort, religious performance, or intellectual achievement alone — it is ultimately a gift of grace from the Guru (divine teacher). This principle of grace over merit marks a central feature of Sikh spirituality and distinguishes it from more effort-based approaches to liberation.

The Guru in Sikh theology is understood at several levels simultaneously: the historical ten Gurus from Guru Nanak to Guru Gobind Singh; the living Guru since 1708 in the form of the Guru Granth Sahib (the scripture); and ultimately the Shabad Guru — the divine Word itself, the primordial sound current that carries the Guru's consciousness. Gurprasad means being touched by any or all of these levels of the Guru's living presence.

Guru Nanak consistently taught that even the most disciplined spiritual practice is incomplete without nadar (divine grace). 'Without His grace, no one can cross over,' he wrote. This is not a teaching of passivity — Sikhs practice intensely — but a teaching of humility and surrender. The practitioner gives their very best effort, and then releases the outcome to divine grace. The combination of effort and surrender is the Sikh spiritual method.

In practice, Gurprasad is cultivated through bhana — acceptance of the divine will. When things go according to plan, the practitioner acknowledges that this too is Gurprasad. When things go contrary to expectation, they also say 'Waheguru ji, this too is Gurprasad' — your grace takes unexpected forms. This total acceptance of all circumstances as expressions of divine grace is the most advanced stage of Sikh spiritual maturity, what Guru Arjan Dev called the stage of sahaj (equanimity).

As a mantra for daily practice, Gurprasad is whispered before any significant activity as a complete act of surrender: 'May this arise by Guru's grace, not by my effort alone.' This brief invocation transforms the psychological stance from anxious striving to receptive effort. Research in sports psychology and performance science confirms the practical wisdom: performers who are in a state of openness and trust consistently outperform those who are tense with the effort of will.

translations.en: By Guru's Grace — grace as the gateway to the divine
translations.zh: 藉上师恩典——恩典是通向神圣的门户
translations.es: Por la Gracia del Guru — la gracia como puerta hacia lo divino

mantras.name: Hail to the Name of the Divine Guru.
mantras.english: Hail to the Name of the Divine Guru.
mantras.transliteration: ਸਲਾਮ ਗੁਰੂ ਦੇ ਨਾਮ ਨੂੰ.
mantras.background: "Ong Namo Guru Dev Namo" — a Kundalini yoga mantra meaning "Hail to the Name of the Divine Guru," rooted in the living Guru tradition, bridging seeker and infinite wisdom.
mantras.benefits: Chanting "Hail to the Name of the Divine Guru" connects practitioners to the Guru tradition, quieting the analytical mind and fostering inner surrender. Scientifically, mantra chanting reduces anxiety and stress.
mantras.tags: guru, devotional, divine, peace, stress-relief, anxiety-relief, focus, healing

translations.es: Salvemos el Nombre del Guru Divino.

translations.bn:
Hail to the Name of the Divine Guru.

Bengali Translation: "ভগবদ্ গুరువుের নামের জয়গান।"

translations.en-us: Salutations to the Divine Guru's Name.
translations.en-gb: Salutations to the Holy Guru.
translations.fr-fr: Salutations au Nom du Guru Divin.

mantras.name: Hail, Hail, Hail, Hail, O Destroyer!
mantras.english: Hail, Hail, Hail, Hail, O Destroyer!
mantras.transliteration: ḥail, ḥail, ḥail, ḥail, o dāruṇā!
mantras.background: Rooted in Kundalini Bhakti tradition taught by Yogi Bhajan, this mantra invokes the Destroyer — a divine Shakti force. Women sang it to awaken inner power, turning India into "a land of milk and honey."
mantras.benefits: This mantra reconnects fragmented soul parts, synchronizes personal consciousness with Universal Consciousness, and removes haunting thoughts—awakening inner Shakti and merging the finite self with infinite unity.
mantras.tags: kundalini, bhakti, shakti, healing, empower, feminine, devotional, hindu, sikh, destroyer, divine, consciousness

mantras.name: Hail, hail, hail, hail, O generous one.
mantras.english: Hail, hail, hail, hail, O generous one.
mantras.transliteration: "Vande, vande, vande, vande, o dānī."
mantras.background: From India's ancient Shakti tradition, this devotional mantra honors the generous divine feminine. Sung to welcome a soul entering the womb on day 120, it awakens inner power.
mantras.benefits: Practicing this mantra awakens inner Shakti—woman's own divine power. It reconnects fragmented soul-parts, aligns personal consciousness with Universal Consciousness, and removes haunting thoughts, inviting wholeness and devotion.
mantras.tags: kundalini, bhakti, devotional, empower, shakti, healing, consciousness, women

mantras.english: Hail, hail, hail, hail, to the limitless one.

mantras.name: Har Har
mantras.english: God, God — a rhythmic invocation of the divine name
mantras.transliteration: Har Har
mantras.abstract:
Har — one of the most ancient Sanskrit-derived names for God, related to the root 'harana' (to take away/carry) — is used in Sikhism as a simple, rhythmic divine name associated with the creative, immanent presence of the divine in all of creation. Repeating 'Har Har' creates a rhythmic invocation that synchronizes naturally with the heartbeat and breath, making it one of the most accessible mantras for meditation.

In Kundalini Yoga as taught by Yogi Bhajan, Har is often used in movement-based chanting practices where the sound is 'struck' with the tip of the tongue on the upper palate with each syllable, creating a specific vibratory pattern in the skull and cranial fluids. Teachers describe this as 'tapping the God force in the brain' — activating specific neural pathways associated with creativity, clarity, and the sense of divine presence.

Har Har is used in the Sikh daily prayers (Nitnem) and kirtan (devotional music), often chanted at high speed in group settings to create an ecstatic state of collective absorption. The Gurdwara (Sikh temple) fills with the sound of Har Har during periods of intense devotional practice, with the rhythm accelerating until individual identity temporarily dissolves into the collective sound current.

As a personal practice, Har Har can be synchronized with walking — one step for each 'Har' — turning an ordinary walk into a moving meditation. This practice, called Chaupai (walking prayer), is particularly recommended for practitioners who find sitting meditation difficult or for those who want to integrate spiritual practice into daily physical activity. The vibratory effect of Har on the hard palate is felt even at walking pace.

The repetition of any divine name — in Sikhism, Hinduism, or Sufism — is understood to work through a principle of resonance: the divine name carries the vibrational signature of the divine reality it names, and by regularly attuning to that signature through repetition, the practitioner's own subtle body gradually comes into resonance with it. Har Har, as one of the most ancient and widely used divine names, is considered to carry a particularly deep vibratory heritage.

translations.en: God, God — a rhythmic invocation of the divine name

translations.de: Ich verneige mich vor dem göttlichen Lehrer, ich verneige mich vor dem göttlichen Lehrer, ich verneige mich vor dem göttlichen Lehrer.

translations.hi: मैं दिव्य गुरु को प्रणाम करता हूँ, मैं दिव्य गुरु को प्रणाम करता हूँ, मैं दिव्य गुरु को प्रणाम करता हूँ।
translations.id: Saya menyembah kepada Guru Ilahi, Saya menyembah kepada Guru Ilahi, Saya menyembah kepada Guru Ilahi.
translations.it: Mi inchino al Divino Maestro, Mi inchino al Divino Maestro, Mi inchino al Divino Maestro.
translations.ja: 私は神聖な師に敬意を表します。私は神聖な師に敬意を表します。私は神聖な師に敬意を表します。
translations.ko: 저는 신성한 스승께 경배합니다. 저는 신성한 스승께 경배합니다. 저는 신성한 스승께 경배합니다. (Jeoneun sinsanghan seupeunge gyeongbaehapnida. Jeoneun sinsanghan seupeunge gyeongbaehapnida. Jeoneun sinsanghan seupeunge gyeongbaehapnida.)
translations.nl: Ik buig voor de goddelijke leraar, ik buig voor de goddelijke leraar, ik buig voor de goddelijke leraar.
translations.pl: Skłaniam się przed Boskim Nauczycielem, skłaniam się przed Boskim Nauczycielem, skłaniam się przed Boskim Nauczycielem.
translations.pt-br: Reverencio o Divino Mestre, reverencio o Divino Mestre, reverencio o Divino Mestre.
translations.ru: Я склоняюсь перед Божественным Учителем, Я склоняюсь перед Божественным Учителем, Я склоняюсь перед Божественным Учителем.
translations.sv: Jag böjer mig för den gudomliga läraren, jag böjer mig för den gudomliga läraren, jag böjer mig för den gudomliga läraren.
translations.th: ข้าขอน้อมน้อมแด่พระอาจารย์ผู้เป็นเทพ, ข้าขอน้อมน้อมแด่พระอาจารย์ผู้เป็นเทพ, ข้าขอน้อมน้อมแด่พระอาจารย์ผู้เป็นเทพ
translations.tr: İlahi Öğretmene saygı duyuyorum, İlahi Öğretmene saygı duyuyorum, İlahi Öğretmene saygı duyuyorum.
translations.uk: Я вклоняюся Божественному Вчителю, Я вклоняюся Божественному Вчителю, Я вклоняюся Божественному Вчителю.
translations.vi: Tôi cúi lạy vị Thầy thiêng liêng, Tôi cúi lạy vị Thầy thiêng liêng, Tôi cúi lạy vị Thầy thiêng liêng.
translations.zh-cn: 我向神圣的导师致敬，我向神圣的导师致敬，我向神圣的导师致敬。

mantras.name: Ik Onkar
mantras.english: There is One Creator — the foundational Sikh declaration
mantras.transliteration: Ik Oaṅkār
mantras.abstract:
Ik Onkar — One Creator — is the opening symbol and statement of the Guru Granth Sahib and the foundational declaration of Sikh theology. Ik (one) and Onkar (the creative sound, the primordial vibration, the divine creative principle) together assert that the ultimate reality is a single, indivisible, creative consciousness. It is not merely a monotheistic statement but a complete ontology: reality at its ground is a unified creative energy.

The symbol ੴ (the Ik Onkar symbol) combines the numeral '1' with the Gurmukhi symbol for 'Onkar,' and appears as the very first character of the Sikh scripture. Before any verse, any teaching, any story — Ik Onkar. Everything that follows in the entire Guru Granth Sahib is an elaboration of this opening statement. All Sikh practice begins from and returns to the recognition that there is One, and that One is the source of all creativity.

Guru Nanak received Ik Onkar as direct divine revelation. According to Sikh tradition, after three days of immersion in a river during a mystical experience, he emerged and spoke his first words: 'Ik Onkar Sat Naam' — there is one Creator, whose name is Truth. This teaching became the Mul Mantar (Root Mantra) and the seed of the entire Sikh tradition. Ik Onkar is thus not merely a theological proposition but a transmission — the distilled essence of Guru Nanak's direct experience of the divine.

In Sikh meditation and recitation, Ik Onkar is held as both an intellectual recognition and a felt sense: everything one sees, everyone one meets, every experience that arises — all of it is the play of the One Creator. This recognition is the experiential underpinning of the Sikh ethic of seva (selfless service): when you see the One Creator in everyone, serving others becomes a natural expression of love for God.

Contemporary Sikh teachers use Ik Onkar as a foundational mindfulness practice: throughout the day, pausing to whisper Ik Onkar before each new encounter or activity is a way of remembering the unity underlying diversity. Over time, this practice reshapes perception: the apparent separation between self and other, human and divine, sacred and secular, begins to dissolve into the recognition of the one creative intelligence expressing itself through infinite forms.

translations.en: There is One Creator — the foundational Sikh declaration
translations.zh: 只有一位造物主——锡克教的基础宣言
translations.es: Hay un solo Creador — la declaración fundamental sij

mantras.name: In the union of breath and spirit, the Guru illuminates.
mantras.english: In the union of breath and spirit, the Guru illuminates.
mantras.transliteration: `saṃyoge ānapānasya hṛdayaḥ, guruḥ prakāśate.`
mantras.background: From Kundalini Yoga, Adi Mantra "ONG NAMO GURU DEV NAMO" means "I bow to the Divine Teacher within." It tunes mind to higher consciousness before practice.
mantras.benefits: Uniting breath & spirit, the Guru within awakens — dissolving ego, gifting humility, and channeling Divine Wisdom. This living science tunes every cell, aligning personal consciousness with the boundless Universal.

translations.es: En la unión del aliento y el espíritu, el Guru ilumina.
translations.ar: في اجتماع النفس والروح، يضيء المعلم.
translations.bn: শ্বাস এবং আত্মার মিলনস্থলে, গুরু আলোকময় হন।
translations.de: Im Zusammensein von Atem und Geist erhellt der Guru.
translations.en-us: In the confluence of breath and consciousness, the Guru reveals [divine knowledge/truth].
translations.en-gb: In the confluence of breath and consciousness, the teacher imparts wisdom.
translations.fr-fr: Dans l'union du souffle et de l'esprit, le Guru révèle la lumière.
translations.he: בְּהִתְאַחֲדוּת הַנְשִׁימָה וְהָרוּחַ, הַגּוּרוּ מַאִיר.
translations.hi: श्वास और आत्मा के मिलन में, गुरु प्रकाश प्रदान करते हैं।
translations.id: Dalam persatuan antara napas dan jiwa, Guru memberikan pencerahan.
translations.it: Nell'unione del respiro e dello spirito, il Guru illumina.
translations.ja: 呼吸と霊性の合一において、グル（導師）が啓発を与える。
translations.ko: 숨과 영혼의 합일 속에서, 스승은 깨달음을 비춥니다.
translations.nl: In de verbinding van adem en geest, verlicht de Goeroe.
translations.pl: W jedności oddechu i ducha, Guru oświeca.
translations.pt-br: Na união do sopro e do espírito, o Guru ilumina.
translations.ru: В единстве дыхания и духа, Гуру являет (или: Гуру проясняет, освещает).
translations.sv: I andingen förening av andning och själ, upplyser Gurun.
translations.th: ในการรวมเป็นหนึ่งของลมหายใจและจิตวิญญาณ, กุรุจะนำทางให้สว่าง.
translations.tr: Nefes ve ruhun birleşimiyle, Guru aydınlanmayı sağlar.
translations.uk: У єдності дихання та духу, Гуру дарує просвітлення.
translations.vi: Trong sự hòa quyện giữa hơi thở và linh hồn, Guru khai sáng.
translations.zh-cn: 在呼吸与灵性交融之时，导师（或：圣师）会带来启迪。

mantras.name: Mul Mantar
mantras.english: Root Mantra — the foundational statement of Sikh theology
mantras.original: ੴ ਸਤਿ ਨਾਮੁ ਕਰਤਾ ਪੁਰਖੁ ਨਿਰਭਉ ਨਿਰਵੈਰੁ ਅਕਾਲ ਮੂਰਤਿ ਅਜੂਨੀ ਸੈਭੰ ਗੁਰ ਪ੍ਰਸਾਦਿ
mantras.transliteration: Ik Oaṅkār Sat Nām Kartā Purakhu Nirbhau Nirvair Akāl Mūrat Ajūnī Saibhaṃ Gur Prasādi
mantras.abstract:
The Mul Mantar — Root Mantra — is the theological heart of Sikhism, the first statement of the Guru Granth Sahib, composed by Guru Nanak as the distilled essence of his mystical realization. It describes the divine in a series of attributes: One Creator (Ik Onkar), True Name (Sat Naam), Doer of Everything (Karta Purakh), Without Fear (Nirbhau), Without Enmity (Nirvair), Timeless Form (Akal Murat), Unborn (Ajooni), Self-Illumined (Saibhang), revealed by the Guru's Grace (Gur Prasad).

Each attribute of the Mul Mantar is a complete teaching in itself. Nirbhau (without fear) and Nirvair (without enmity) describe a divine nature that is neither threatened by anything nor hostile to anything — this is the ground of the Sikh practice of equanimity (sahaj). Akal Murat (timeless form) points to the divine as not subject to the cycles of birth, death, and time that govern all created things. Ajooni (unborn) confirms that the divine was not created and will not die.

The concluding phrase Gur Prasad (by the Guru's Grace) is perhaps the most theologically significant: the divine cannot be reached by intellectual effort, moral striving, or ritual performance alone, but only by the grace of the Guru — meaning the living transmission of divine awareness that comes through the lineage of the Gurus and, since 1708, through the Guru Granth Sahib. Grace, not merit, is the gateway.

Reciting the Mul Mantar is a foundational Sikh practice. Many Sikhs include it in their daily Nitnem (obligatory prayers), reciting it before the Japji Sahib (Guru Nanak's morning prayer). Some teachers prescribe extended Mul Mantar meditation — sitting quietly and reciting it slowly, dwelling on each attribute and allowing its meaning to unfold in awareness. This is a form of contemplative theology: theology done not in the abstract but as an encounter with living reality.

For a practitioner outside the Sikh tradition, the Mul Mantar offers a complete description of the divine that transcends cultural specificity. 'One Creator, Whose name is Truth, Who does everything, fearless, without enmity, timeless in form, unborn, self-illumined, known by the Guru's grace' — each attribute is a doorway into a different dimension of spiritual reality, and sitting with any one of them for extended meditation can be profoundly illuminating.

translations.en: One Creator, True Name, Doer of Everything, Fearless, Without Enmity, Timeless, Unborn, Self-Illumined, by Guru's Grace
translations.zh: 一位造物主，真名，万事的执行者，无惧，无仇，永恒之形，未生，自明，藉上师恩典
translations.es: Un Creador, Nombre Verdadero, Hacedor de Todo, Sin Miedo, Sin Enemistad, Eterno, No Nacido, Auto-Iluminado, por la Gracia del Guru

mantras.name: Nanak Nam Chardi Kala
mantras.english: O Nanak, with God's Name comes eternal optimism and rising energy
mantras.transliteration: Nānak Nām Chaṛdī Kalā
mantras.abstract:
Nanak Nam Chardi Kala — 'O Nanak, with the Name comes chardi kala (ascending spirit, perpetual optimism, ever-rising energy)' — is one of the most beloved phrases in Sikh spirituality. 'Chardi Kala' is a specifically Sikh virtue and state of being: an unconquerable spirit, an unshakeable optimism, a resilience that does not depend on circumstances. It is the spiritual quality that allows one to sing in the face of adversity, to serve joyfully even in suffering, to maintain a positive orientation toward life even in its darkest passages.

The full phrase from which this is drawn — 'Nanak nam chardi kala, tere bhane sarbat da bhala' (O Nanak, through Your Name may we live in ever-rising spirit; in Your will lies the wellbeing of all) — combines personal spiritual practice with universal compassion. The chardi kala produced by the naam is not merely personal psychological resilience but a quality that radiates outward as blessing to all.

Chardi Kala is considered by Sikh teachers to be the hallmark of genuine spiritual development. It is tested not in times of comfort and success but in times of hardship, loss, and persecution — and Sikh history has provided many such tests. The tradition points to examples like the fifth Guru Arjan Dev, who sang Gurbani while being tortured to death; or Guru Tegh Bahadur, who went to his execution with complete composure. These are presented not as exceptional heroism but as the natural fruit of consistent naam simran (repetition of the divine name).

As a mantra and affirmation, Nanak Nam Chardi Kala is used when facing difficulty, loss, illness, or despair. It is not a denial of suffering — 'it's all fine' — but an invocation of the divine source of resilience: 'Through the Name, may I find the strength of chardi kala in this.' The practitioner appeals not to their own psychological resources but to the infinite source of energy that the Name connects them to.

The psychological concept most similar to chardi kala is post-traumatic growth — the well-documented phenomenon in which people who have faced severe adversity emerge with greater strength, deeper compassion, and clearer values than before. Sikh teachers would say this is the natural result of chardi kala practice: not the absence of suffering but the transformation of suffering into spiritual development through continued naam simran.

translations.en: O Nanak, with God's Name comes eternal optimism and rising energy
translations.zh: 那纳克，藉神的名字而来永恒的乐观与上升的活力
translations.es: O Nanak, con el Nombre de Dios viene el optimismo eterno y la energía ascendente

mantras.name: O, You who create and sustain, from the beginning to the end, You are the Guru, the embodiment of the divine, O Wahe Guru.
mantras.english: O, You who create and sustain, from the beginning to the end, You are the Guru, the embodiment of the divine, O Wahe Guru.
mantras.original: Wah Yanti Kar Yanti, Djaga Dute Pathi Adike It Wah Brahma De Treshe Guru, Ite Wahe Guru
mantras.transliteration: ō, tum jo srishti aur sthiti karte ho, shuruwat se ant tak, tum hi guru ho, divyate ka swaroop, o vahē guru.
mantras.background: From Gurmukhi (Sikh tradition), "Wahe Guru" means "Ecstasy on the path from darkness to light" — a call to divine wisdom across all time.
mantras.benefits: Wahe Guru invokes ecstatic joy on the path from darkness to light. Rooted in the Sikh Gurmukhi tradition, chanting it cultivates devotion, awe, and inner transformation — awakening the divine teacher within.
mantras.tags: sikh, guru, devotional, creation, ecstasy, transcendence, divine, light, cosmic, wisdom

translations.es: Oh, Tú que creas y sostienes, desde el principio hasta el fin, Tú eres el Gurú, la encarnación de lo divino, Oh, Wahe Gurú.
translations.ar: يا من تُخلق وتديم، من البداية إلى النهاية، أنت المعلم، تجسيد الإلهية، يا واهي غورو.
translations.bn: ওহে, যিনি সৃষ্টি করেন এবং যিনি রক্ষা করেন, যিনি শুরু থেকে শেষ পর্যন্ত বিরাজমান, আপনিই Guru, আপনিই divine সত্তার প্রতিচ্ছবি, ওহে ওয়াহে গুরু।
translations.de: Oh, Du, der erschaffst und erhältst, vom Anfang bis zum Ende, Du bist der Guru, die Verkörperung des Göttlichen, oh, Wahe Guru.
translations.en-us: O, You who create and maintain, from the very beginning to the very end, You are the Guru, the embodiment of the divine, O Waheguru.
translations.en-gb: O, You who create and sustain, from the beginning to the end, You are the Guru, the embodiment of the divine, O Waheguru.
translations.fr-fr: Ô Toi qui crées et qui maintiens, du commencement à la fin, Tu es le Guru, l'incarnation du divin, Ô Waheguru.
translations.he: ה', אתה היוצר והמשמר, מהתחלה ועד הסוף, אתה המורה, התגלמות האלוהות, הו ואֵה גוּרוּ.
translations.hi: हे वह, जो सृष्टि करते हैं और धारण करते हैं, जो शुरुआत से अंत तक विद्यमान हैं, आप ही हैं गुरु, दिव्य का साकार रूप, हे वाहे गुरु।
translations.id: Wahai Engkau yang menciptakan dan memelihara, dari awal hingga akhir, Engkau adalah Guru, perwujudan keilahian, Wahai Wahe Guru.
translations.it: O, Tu che crei e sostieni, dall'inizio alla fine, Tu sei il Guru, l'incarnazione del divino, O Waheguru.
translations.ja:
ஓम्, तुम्ही जो सृष्टि और पालन करने वाले हो, शुरुआत से अंत तक, तुम ही गुरु हो, दिव्य का प्रतीक, हे वाहे गुरु।

**日本語訳:**

オーム。始まりから終わりまで、創造と維持をされる方、あなたは師であり、神性の化身です。オ、ワヘ・グル。

**Grounding:**

*   **"O"**: I have translated "O" as "オーム" (Ōmu), which is the Japanese transliteration of "Om".
*   **"You who create and sustain"**: This phrase is translated as "創造と維持をされる方" (Sōzō to hihan o sareru katachi), meaning "the one who creates and sustains." The use of "方" (katachi) adds a sense of reverence and personification.
*   **"From the beginning to the end"**: This is translated as "始まりから終わりまで" (hajimari kara owari made), a straightforward and common expression.
*   **"You are the Guru"**: Translated as "あなたは師であり" (anata wa shi de ari), clearly stating that "You are the Guru."
*   **"The embodiment of the divine"**: I have translated this as "神性の化身です" (shinsei no keshin desu), meaning "the embodiment of divinity."
*   **"O Wahe Guru"**: This is transliterated as "オ、ワヘ・グル" (O, Wahe Guru). Because "Wahe Guru" is a specific term with religious significance, it is best to transliterate it rather than attempt a direct translation, which could lose its meaning.
translations.ko: 오, 창조하시고 유지하시며, 시작부터 끝까지 존재하시는 당신이시여, 당신은 구루이시며, 신성체의 구현이시니, 오, 와헤구루.
translations.nl: O, U wie schept en onderhoudt, van het begin tot het einde, U bent de Goeroe, de belichaming van het goddelijke, O Wahe Goeroe.
translations.pl: O, Ty, który tworzysz i utrzymujesz, od początku do końca, Ty jesteś Gurują, uosobieniem boskości, o Wahe Guru.
translations.pt-br: Ó, Tu que crias e sustentas, do princípio ao fim, Tu és o Guru, a encarnação do divino, Ó Wahe Guru.
translations.ru: О, Ты, Кто сотворил и поддерживаешь, от начала и до конца, Ты – Гуру, воплощение божественного, О, Вахе Гуру.
translations.sv: O, Du som skapar och upprätthåller, från början till slut, Du är Gurun, inkarnationen av det gudomliga, O Waheguru.
translations.th: โอ, พระผู้สร้างและทรงเลี้ยงดู, จากจุดเริ่มต้นจนสิ้นสุด, พระองค์คือ กุรุ, ภาคสำแดงแห่งสิ่งศักดิ์สิทธิ์, โอ วาเฮ กุรุ
translations.tr: Ey Yaratıcı ve Sürdürücü, başlangıcından sonuna kadar, Sen Gurusunsun, ilahi varlığın somutlaşmış halisin, Ey Wahe Guru.
translations.uk: О, Ти, хто творить і підтримує, від початку до кінця, Ти є Гуру, втілення божественного, О, Вахе Гуру.
translations.vi: Ôi, Ngài là Đấng tạo dựng và duy trì, từ đầu đến cuối, Ngài là Guru, là hiện thân của sự thiêng liêng, Ôi Wahe Guru.
translations.zh-cn: 噢，你是创造者，也是维持者，从开始到结束，你是指导者（或导师），是神性的化身，哦，伟大的主（Wahe Guru）。

mantras.name: One Creator, the Grace of the True Guru, the Grace of the True Guru, One Creator.
mantras.english: One Creator, the Grace of the True Guru, the Grace of the True Guru, One Creator.
mantras.original: Ek Ong Kar, Sat Guru Prasad, Sat Gur Prasad, Ek Ong Kar
mantras.transliteration: Ek Ṛāmakar, Satgur ka karam, Satgur ka karam, Ek Ṛāmakar.
mantras.background: From Kundalini Yoga's Sikh roots, "Ek Ong Kar, Sat Guru Prasad" — One Creator, Grace of the True Guru — inverts the mind, awakening infinite consciousness.
mantras.benefits: Invoking unity with the Creator and Guru's grace, this Kundalini mantra cultivates surrender and trust. Chanting it may quiet mental chatter, reduce anxiety, and open the heart to divine guidance.
mantras.tags: guru, devotional, grace, creator, chanting, japa, peace, focus, guidance, divine, calm

translations.es: Un Creador, la Gracia del Verdadero Gurú, la Gracia del Verdadero Gurú, Un Creador.
translations.ar: خالق واحد، نِعمةُ المعلمِ الصادق، نِعمةُ المعلمِ الصادق، خالق واحد.
translations.bn: এক স্রষ্টা, সত্য গুরু का अनुग्रह, सत्य गुरु का अनुग्रह, एक স্রষ্টা।
translations.de: Ein Schöpfer, die Gnade des wahren Gurus, die Gnade des wahren Gurus, Ein Schöpfer.
translations.en-us: One Creator, the Grace of the True Guru, the Grace of the True Guru, One Creator.

mantras.name: One Divine Creation, Truth is the Name, Praise to the Guru.
mantras.english: One Divine Creation, Truth is the Name, Praise to the Guru.
mantras.original: EK ONG KAR, SAT NAM SIRI, WA HE GURU
mantras.transliteration: eka divya sṛṣṭiḥ, satyaṁ nāma, guruḥ praṇamaḥ.
mantras.background: From Kundalini Yoga's Gurmukhi language: "One Divine Creation, Truth is the Name, Praise to the Guru" — a Sikh-rooted mantra vibrating every cell toward divine wisdom.

translations.he: מַעֲשֵׂה אֵל יְחִיד, הָאֱמֶת הוּא הַשֵּׁם, תְּהִלָּה לְהַמּוֹרֶה.

From Sikh Kundalini tradition, "Ek Ong Kar Sat Gur Prasad" means "One Divine Reality, bestowed by the True Guru" — a mantra of transformation and awakening wisdom.
  Grounding: Source cites "Ek Ong Kar Sat Gur Prasad" as a Kundalini Yoga mantra of "transformation et de sagesse," rooted in Sikhism's Shabad Guru sacred sound current.
mantras.benefits: "Ek Ong Kar Sat Gur Prasad" — One Divine Reality, bestowed by the True Guru — is described as a mantra of transformation and wisdom, strengthening the aura and awakening spiritual consciousness.
mantras.tags: sikh, guru, kundalini, devotional, divine, consciousness, grace, transformation, wisdom, liberation, awakening

translations.pt-br: Saudações a você, poder primordial, poder primordial, poder primordial.
translations.ru: Превосходная сила, превосходная сила, превосходная сила, приветствия тебе.

translations.th: ขอคารวะแด่พระผู้มีอำนาจสูงสุด, ขอคารวะแด่พระผู้มีอำนาจสูงสุด, ขอคารวะแด่พระผู้มีอำนาจสูงสุด
translations.tr: İlahi Güç, İlahi Güç, İlahi Güç, Sana Saygılar.

mantras.name: Sarbat Da Bhala
mantras.english: Wellbeing of all — the Sikh prayer for universal welfare
mantras.transliteration: Sarbat dā bhalā
mantras.abstract:
Sarbat Da Bhala — Wellbeing of All — is one of the most important phrases in the Sikh Ardas (communal prayer), recited at the conclusion of every Sikh ceremony, service, and gathering. It expresses the Sikh ideal of universal compassion: that the purpose of spiritual practice is not personal liberation only but the welfare of all of creation — all races, all religions, all beings.

The phrase appears near the end of the Ardas, after asking for the protection of the Sikh community specifically, the prayer expands its circle of concern to encompass all of humanity and all sentient beings. This movement from the particular to the universal mirrors the Sikh understanding of the spiritual journey: one begins with concern for one's own liberation, then for one's family, then for one's community, and ultimately, at the highest stage, for all of creation without distinction.

Guru Nanak's foundational teaching included the concept of Sarbat (all/everyone) as the scope of divine love. God's love does not distinguish between Sikh and non-Sikh, Hindu and Muslim, wealthy and poor — it is truly Sarbat. Any spiritual practice that produces exclusivity, superiority, or indifference to others' suffering is, in the Sikh view, a distortion of genuine spirituality. Sarbat Da Bhala is a built-in corrective: the community is regularly reminded that its purpose extends beyond itself.

As a mantra for individual practice, Sarbat Da Bhala can be held as an intention before beginning each day. On rising, after morning prayer, the practitioner holds this phrase in awareness and asks: what can I do today for the wellbeing of all? This practice of beginning the day with universal intention is a powerful antidote to the narcissistic spiral that tends to dominate ordinary consciousness. It also activates prosocial motivation, which research consistently shows is one of the most reliable sources of lasting wellbeing.

In the Sikh langar (community kitchen) — where free food is prepared and served to anyone regardless of background — Sarbat Da Bhala finds its most tangible expression. The mantra is not merely recited but enacted: the physical act of feeding all who come, without judgment or distinction, is the lived form of the prayer for universal welfare. Practice and service are inseparable.

translations.en: Wellbeing of all
translations.zh: 愿一切众生安乐
translations.es: Bienestar de todos

mantras.name: Sat Naam
mantras.english: True Name — God's name is Truth
mantras.transliteration: Sat Nām
mantras.abstract:
Sat Naam — True Name — is the second phrase of the Mul Mantar (Root Mantra) and one of the most important practices in Sikhism. 'Sat' means truth, reality, what is eternally so; 'Naam' means name, essence, the vibrational nature of the divine. Together they assert that the name of God is Truth itself — not a label or designation but the very quality of reality at its most fundamental. God's name and God's nature are not separate.

Guru Nanak's teaching of Sat Naam was revolutionary in its time and context. In a religious culture filled with elaborate rituals, priestly hierarchies, and esoteric practices, Guru Nanak declared that the simple recognition and repetition of the divine name — available to anyone, regardless of caste, gender, or education — was the direct path to liberation (mukti). Truth is not hidden in temples or scriptures but vibrating in the name itself, accessible to every human being.

The practice of Sat Naam involves repeating it in synchrony with the breath: 'Sat' on the inhale, 'Naam' on the exhale, or alternatively 'Wah' on the inhale and 'Guru' with 'Sat Naam' on the exhale. This breath-coordination transforms the mantra from a verbal formula into a physiological practice — the truth of the divine is literally breathed into every cell of the body. Teachers describe this as 'imprinting the naam on the breath,' a permanent transformation of the practitioner's subtle body.

In Kundalini Yoga as taught by Yogi Bhajan (a Sikh-derived tradition), Sat Naam is used extensively as a closing blessing between teacher and student — hands pressed together, bowing, and exchanging 'Sat Naam' as a recognition of the truth in each other. This practice embodies the Sikh teaching that the divine is equally present in every human being and that true spiritual community is the mutual recognition of that presence.

Sat Naam is also understood as a practice of integrity — aligning one's words, actions, and inner states with truth (sat). The name is not merely a sound to be repeated but a standard to be lived. A practitioner of Sat Naam is someone whose life expresses truthfulness in every dimension: speaking truth, acting from truth, perceiving truth in all circumstances. The mantra and the ethic are inseparable.

translations.en: True Name — God's name is Truth
translations.zh: 真名——神的名字是真理
translations.es: Nombre Verdadero — el nombre de Dios es la Verdad

mantras.name: Tera Tera
mantras.english: Yours, Yours — complete surrender of self to the divine
mantras.transliteration: Terā Terā
mantras.abstract:
Tera Tera — Yours, Yours — is the mantra of complete surrender in the Sikh tradition, the radical abandonment of the ego's claim to ownership over life, experience, and outcome. 'Tera' (yours) repeated becomes a total dedication: this breath is yours, this body is yours, this joy is yours, this suffering is yours, this life is yours, this death is yours. Nothing is 'mine'; everything is the divine's. This is the Sikh practice of surrendering haumai (ego-mind) to Waheguru.

Haumai — ego, the sense of 'I am the doer, I am the owner, I am the sufferer' — is identified in the Guru Granth Sahib as the primary obstacle to liberation. It is not the individual self that is the problem but the illusion that the individual self is separate from and independent of the divine source. Tera Tera is the antidote: by continuously offering everything back to its divine source, the boundary between 'mine' and 'yours' gradually dissolves, and the individual rests in its original union with Waheguru.

Guru Arjan Dev's bani (sacred compositions) are full of this spirit of complete surrender. In Sukhmani Sahib, he writes: 'All that you have given me, O Lord, I offer back to you.' This is not resignation or self-negation but the discovery of a deeper, more reliable identity: rather than being a small, vulnerable self that owns things and can lose them, one discovers oneself to be the awareness in which all experiences arise and pass away — the Tera that everything belongs to.

In practice, Tera Tera is used in moments of both success and failure as a way of releasing the ego's attachment to outcomes. Success: 'This is not mine; it is yours, Waheguru.' Failure: 'This pain is not punishment; it belongs to you, Waheguru.' The mantra does not suppress the emotional response to events but contextualizes them within a larger frame of divine ownership that gradually loosens the ego's desperate grip.

Teachers in the Sikh tradition describe the practice of Tera as paradoxically liberating: the more completely one gives everything back to the divine, the lighter and more spacious one feels. The burden of possessing, protecting, and defending a separate self is the heaviest of all human burdens. When that burden is genuinely released — even briefly — through the practice of Tera Tera, the soul tastes its original lightness.

translations.en: Yours, Yours — complete surrender of self to the divine
translations.zh: 祢的，祢的——将自我完全交托给神
translations.es: Tuyo, Tuyo — rendición completa del yo a lo divino

From Gurmukhi (Kundalini Yoga/Sikh tradition), "Pavan Guru" means "the Breeze is the Guru" — breath itself as divine teacher, pervading all existence alongside Wahe Guru (ecstatic light).
  Grounding: Source lists mantra in Gurmukhi; "Wahe Guru" = ecstasy toward light; "Pavan" = breeze/breath; mantra appears in the Mantra Index under Kundalini tradition.
mantras.benefits: Pavan Guru connects breath to divine wisdom in Kundalini Yoga. Chanting it may calm the mind, reduce anxiety, and awaken inner peace—inviting practitioners into a living tradition where every breath becomes a sacred teacher.
mantras.tags: sikh, guru, devotional, ecstasy, joy, divine, liberation, light
